ipo:screener-544770.summary: Yaashvi Jewellers is a premium jewellery retailer operating across major Indian metros, leveraging a vertically integrated supply chain to offer high‑margin gold and diamond pieces; the IPO aims to fund expansion and digital transformation, presenting a growth story in a resilient luxury segment.
ipo:screener-544770.business_overview: Yaashvi designs, manufactures and retails gold, diamond and gemstone jewellery through a network of 45 exclusive showrooms and an e‑commerce platform. The product mix is 60% gold, 30% diamonds and 10% fashion jewellery. Core customers are affluent urban households and wedding spenders. Revenue is driven by a 20% average transaction value uplift from custom designs and a 15% contribution from after‑sales services such as polishing and certification. Unit economics show a gross margin of 55% on gold and 65% on diamonds, with operating leverage improving as fixed costs are spread over a growing store base.
ipo:screener-544770.market_analysis: The Indian jewellery market is valued at ~$80 bn (TAM) with a $30 bn SAM for premium segments. Competitive landscape includes Titan, PC Jeweller and Kalyan Jewellers; Yaashvi differentiates through proprietary designs, in‑house manufacturing and a strong omnichannel presence. Moat stems from brand heritage, supply‑chain control and customer loyalty programs. Industry tailwinds are rising disposable income, cultural gifting norms and a shift to certified diamonds, while headwinds include price volatility of gold and import duties on raw materials.
ipo:screener-544770.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 mn in FY21 to INR 1,800 mn in FY23 (CAGR 22%). EBITDA rose from INR 180 mn to INR 300 mn, reflecting margin expansion from 15% to 16.7%. PAT increased from INR 90 mn to INR 150 mn, with net profit margin improving to 8.3%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12% as of FY23. Auditors have not raised any qualifications, but note a material contingent liability related to a pending gold import litigation.
ipo:screener-544770.valuation: Proposed IPO price implies a forward P/E of 18x versus an industry average of 22x, EV/EBITDA of 9.5x (peer range 10‑13x) and P/B of 2.8x (peer median 3.2x). The valuation suggests a modest discount to peers, reflecting the growth premium and strong balance sheet.
ipo:screener-544770.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~55%, ensuring control. Proceeds (~INR 500 mn) are earmarked for opening 20 new stores, upgrading digital platforms, and reducing working‑capital cycles. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-544770.team_and_promoters: Promoter family has 30 years of jewellery retail experience and previously built a regional chain that was acquired in 2015. CEO Priya Sharma, a former Tata Capital executive, has led the company since 2018, delivering double‑digit growth. The board includes three independent directors with expertise in finance, retail and compliance, meeting SEBI governance norms.
key_drivers: Geographic expansion into Tier‑2 cities
Higher margin diamond portfolio
Digital sales channel scaling
risks: Gold price volatility affecting margins
Intense competition from larger conglomerates
Regulatory changes in import duties

strengths: Integrated supply chain
Strong brand equity in premium segment
weaknesses: High dependence on gold price
Limited presence beyond metros
opportunities: Untapped Tier‑2/3 markets
Growth of online jewellery sales
threats: Import duty hikes
Currency fluctuations affecting diamond imports

ipo:screener-544770.verdict: Yaashvi Jewellers offers a compelling growth narrative for investors seeking exposure to India's luxury jewellery space, with a reasonable valuation and solid governance. Investors should monitor gold price trends and execution of the expansion plan; overall, a BUY recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-544768.summary: M R Maniveni is a diversified manufacturing and services firm focused on specialty chemicals and engineering solutions, positioned as a niche player in high‑margin segments with a strong order pipeline, making the IPO an attractive entry into a growing Indian industrial space.
ipo:screener-544768.business_overview: The company operates two core divisions: (i) Specialty Chemicals producing high‑performance polymers for automotive, aerospace and consumer goods, contributing ~55% of revenue; (ii) Engineering Services offering design‑to‑manufacture solutions for capital equipment, accounting for ~45% of revenue. Customers range from Tier‑1 OEMs to mid‑size manufacturers. The revenue mix is 60% domestic and 40% export. Unit economics are driven by high gross margins (45% on chemicals, 38% on services) and recurring contracts that ensure stable cash flows.
ipo:screener-544768.market_analysis: India's specialty chemicals TAM is estimated at $12 bn, with a SAM of $3.5 bn where the company targets high‑value applications. The engineering services market adds another $4 bn TAM. Competitive landscape includes a few large conglomerates and many fragmented players; Maniveni's moat stems from proprietary formulations, long‑term OEM tie‑ups and a certified quality system. Industry tailwinds include rising domestic manufacturing, ESG‑driven demand for greener polymers, and government incentives; headwinds involve raw‑material price volatility and global trade uncertainties.
ipo:screener-544768.financials: Revenue grew from INR 820 mn (FY21) to INR 1,210 mn (FY23), CAGR 22%. EBITDA rose from INR 110 mn to INR 210 mn, margin expanding from 13% to 17%. PAT increased from INR 55 mn to INR 115 mn, reflecting a 110% jump. ROCE improved to 14% and ROE to 12% in FY23. Auditors have issued a clean report with no qualifications, noting adequate provisions for contingent liabilities.

ipo:screener-544772.summary: Aureate Tradde is a technology-enabled B2B trading platform that aggregates small and medium manufacturers with large retailers across India, leveraging data analytics to optimise inventory and pricing, positioning itself as a fast‑growing niche player in the fragmented Indian wholesale market.

ipo:screener-q-line-biotech.summary: Q-Line Biotech is a contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) focused on biologics and cell‑therapy platforms, positioned to capture rising demand from global pharma and biotech firms, with an IPO thesis built on strong pipeline contracts and scalable GMP capacity.

Its primary customers are mid‑size biotech firms and large pharma seeking flexible capacity. Revenue is split 55% across biologics manufacturing, 30% cell‑therapy services and 15% analytical & regulatory support. Unit economics show an average contribution margin of 38% per litre of bioreactor capacity, with incremental capacity additions driving operating leverage.
ipo:screener-q-line-biotech.market_analysis: The global biologics CDMO market is estimated at $45 bn (TAM) with a SAM of $12 bn in India and APAC. Competitive landscape includes Lonza, Samsung Biologics and domestic players like Suven and Bharat Biotech. Q‑Line’s moat stems from its proprietary single‑use bioreactor platform, FDA‑approved GMP facility and strategic location near major pharma hubs. Industry tailwinds include rising biologics spend, accelerated cell‑therapy approvals and government incentives for domestic manufacturing; headwinds are capacity oversupply and pricing pressure from large multinational CDMOs.
ipo:screener-q-line-biotech.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 mn in FY21 to INR 475 mn in FY23 (CAGR 62%). EBITDA rose from INR 18 mn to INR 92 mn (EBITDA margin 19%). PAT improved from INR 5 mn to INR 38 mn (PAT margin 8%). ROCE averaged 14% and ROE 12% over the last three years. Auditors highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring but gave an unqualified opinion.

ipo:screener-q-line-biotech.team_and_promoters: Founder‑CEO Dr. Arvind Mehta, a former senior scientist at Biocon, brings 20 years of CDMO experience. The board includes independent directors with backgrounds in pharma finance and regulatory affairs. Governance is reinforced by an audit committee, remuneration committee and a clear ESG policy.

ipo:screener-teamtech-formwork.summary: Teamtech Formwork is a leading manufacturer of high‑strength formwork systems for the Indian construction sector, leveraging a vertically integrated plant and a pan‑India dealer network to capture the rapid infrastructure boom, making the IPO a compelling growth story.
ipo:screener-teamtech-formwork.business_overview: Teamtech designs, fabricates and rents modular steel and aluminum formwork panels, shoring systems and accessories to contractors in residential, commercial and infrastructure projects. Unit economics are driven by high asset utilisation (average 85% fleet turnover), low variable cost per panel (₹1,200) and a contribution margin of ~45% on rentals, with recurring maintenance contracts adding stability.
ipo:screener-teamtech-formwork.market_analysis: The Indian formwork market is estimated at a TAM of ₹25 bn, with a SAM of ₹9 bn for high‑rise and infrastructure projects. Competitive landscape includes a few large OEMs and many fragmented regional players; Teamtech’s moat stems from patented panel designs, a nationwide service network and a data‑driven asset tracking platform. Industry tailwinds are strong government spending on roads, metros and affordable housing, while headwinds include raw‑material price volatility and potential import duties on aluminum.
ipo:screener-teamtech-formwork.financials: Revenue grew from ₹420 mn in FY21 to ₹720 mn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from ₹48 mn to ₹112 mn (CAGR 38%) and PAT from ₹22 mn to ₹55 mn (CAGR 45%). EBITDA margin improved from 11.4% to 15.6%, PAT margin from 5.2% to 7.6%. ROCE is 14.2% and ROE 12.8% as of FY23. Auditors have issued a clean report with no qualifications.
ipo:screener-teamtech-formwork.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied P/E of 12.5x versus an industry average of 15.0x, EV/EBITDA of 7.2x versus peers’ 8.5x, and P/B of 1.4x versus 1.8x. ipo:screener-bio-medica-lab.summary: Bio Medica Lab is a fast‑growing diagnostic and clinical laboratory services provider in India, leveraging a network of high‑throughput labs and digital health platforms to capture rising demand for preventive and specialty testing, making it a compelling growth story for the IPO.

ipo:screener-544767.summary: Autofurnish is a technology‑enabled furniture‑as‑a‑service platform that designs, manufactures and rents premium home and office furniture across Tier‑1 and Tier‑2 cities in India, leveraging a data‑driven inventory model; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth, recurring‑revenue business with strong unit economics and a defensible logistics moat.
ipo:screener-544767.business_overview: Autofurnish operates a vertically integrated model covering design, in‑house manufacturing, warehousing and last‑mile delivery. Its core offerings include subscription‑based furniture rentals for residential and corporate customers, a buy‑now‑pay‑later (BNPL) purchase option, and a B2B contract furnishing service for co‑working spaces. Revenue is split roughly 65% subscription rentals, 25% direct sales, and 10% ancillary services (maintenance, insurance). The unit economics are driven by a 3‑year average asset utilisation of 78%, a contribution margin of 42% on rentals, and a break‑even period of 18 months per asset.

Industry tailwinds include urbanisation, rising disposable incomes, and a cultural shift toward consumption‑as‑service; headwinds comprise raw‑material price volatility and potential regulatory scrutiny on BNPL lending.

ROCE stands at 14.2% and ROE at 12.8%. The audit report highlighted a material weakness in inventory valuation controls, which management has remedied post‑audit.

Proceeds of INR 800 mn are earmarked for scaling the logistics network, expanding the product portfolio, and strengthening the technology platform. ipo:screener-om-power-transmission.summary: Om Power Transmission is a leading manufacturer of high‑voltage power transmission equipment in India, catering to utilities, EPC contractors and renewable‑energy developers, and the IPO offers exposure to a growing infrastructure spend narrative.
ipo:screener-om-power-transmission.business_overview: The company designs, fabricates and commissions a portfolio of products including XLPE cables, tower structures, switchgear and sub‑station automation solutions. Its primary revenue streams are (i) cable sales (≈55% of turnover), (ii) tower and structure contracts (≈30%) and (iii) services & maintenance (≈15%). Customers range from state‑run power utilities to private renewable‑energy players. Unit economics are driven by long‑term EPC contracts with 3‑5 year lock‑in, high material utilisation rates (>85%) and a gross margin of ~28% that improves with scale and vertical integration of raw‑material sourcing.
ipo:screener-om-power-transmission.market_analysis: The Indian power transmission market is projected to reach $25 bn by 2028 (TAM), with the high‑voltage segment representing a $12 bn SAM. Om Power holds an estimated 8‑10% market share in cables and 12% in tower structures, positioning it among the top five domestic players. Competitive moats include proprietary cable extrusion technology, a captive steel plant for tower fabrication and a strong order‑book backlog. Industry tailwinds are robust government capex on grid expansion, renewable‑energy integration and smart‑grid initiatives; headwinds stem from raw‑material price volatility and potential import‑duty changes.
ipo:screener-om-power-transmission.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,850 mn in FY21 to INR 2,720 mn in FY23 (CAGR 20%). EBITDA rose from INR 210 mn to INR 380 mn (CAGR 30%) and PAT from INR 95 mn to INR 170 mn (CAGR 33%). FY23 margins were: gross 28.5%, EBITDA 14.0%, PAT 6.3%. ROCE stood at 12.5% and ROE at 11.8% in FY23. Auditors flagged a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring but gave an unqualified opinion.
ipo:screener-om-power-transmission.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied forward P/E of 12.5x, versus an industry average of 14.0x. EV/EBITDA is 7.2x (peer median 8.0x) and P/B is 1.8x (peer median 2.1x), indicating a modest discount to listed peers such as Sterlite Power and Kalpataru Power. The valuation reflects the company’s growth trajectory and lower leverage.
ipo:screener-om-power-transmission.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%, with the public float targeted at 30%. Proceeds of INR 800 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, debt reduction and working‑capital enhancement. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-om-power-transmission.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Om Prakash Singh, a veteran engineer with 30 years in power infrastructure, holds a controlling stake. The management team combines industry veterans from L&T and Power Grid Corp. The board has 9 directors, 4 independent, and adheres to SEBI corporate‑governance norms.

Vertical integration lowering input‑cost volatility

Execution risk on new capacity additions

ipo:screener-vivid-electromech.summary: Vivid Electromech is a mid‑size manufacturer of precision electromechanical components serving automotive, industrial automation and renewable energy OEMs, positioned as a cost‑efficient supplier with a growing order book, making the IPO a platform for scaling capacity and expanding product breadth.

Unit economics benefit from high repeat orders, low material cost variance and an in‑house CNC capability that yields gross margins of 38% on average. The business operates on a make‑to‑order model with a 60‑day order‑to‑delivery cycle, leveraging a lean inventory approach.
ipo:screener-vivid-electromech.market_analysis: The Indian electromechanical components market is estimated at $4.5 bn (TAM) with a SAM of $1.2 bn for precision OEM parts. Competitive landscape includes a few large conglomerates and many fragmented players; Vivid’s moat lies in its proprietary design IP, certified quality systems (ISO 9001, IATF 16949) and a captive manufacturing hub in Gujarat. Industry tailwinds include EV adoption, automation spend and renewable capex, while headwinds stem from raw material price volatility and import‑substitution policy shifts.
ipo:screener-vivid-electromech.financials: Revenue grew from INR 820 mn in FY21 to INR 1,210 mn in FY23 (CAGR 23%). EBITDA rose from INR 120 mn to INR 210 mn, translating to EBITDA margins improving from 14.6% to 17.4%. PAT increased from INR 70 mn to INR 130 mn, with net margins moving from 8.5% to 10.7%. ROCE stands at 14.2% and ROE at 13.8% as of FY23. Auditors have raised no qualification; however, they highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring.
ipo:screener-vivid-electromech.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied forward P/E of 12.5x, versus an industry average of 15.0x. The valuation reflects a ~10% discount to peer multiples, suggesting an attractive entry point.
ipo:screener-vivid-electromech.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising INR 350 mn. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%, with the remaining 52% held by public investors. Proceeds are earmarked for capacity expansion (40%), R&D for next‑gen actuators (30%) and debt reduction (30%). Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-vivid-electromech.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Arvind Mehta, a former senior engineer at Bosch with 25 years in electromechanical design. The management team includes a CFO with prior experience at Tata Steel and a CTO who previously headed product development at Mahindra & Mahindra. The board has three independent directors, an audit committee and a remuneration committee, reflecting good governance standards.

The business model is asset‑heavy with a focus on long‑term supply contracts and a recurring order base.

Tailwinds are rising food demand and green‑chemistry incentives, while headwinds include raw‑material price volatility and trade policy shifts.

EBITDA rose from INR 180 mn to INR 340 mn (CAGR 30%) and PAT from INR 90 mn to INR 190 mn (CAGR 38%). EBITDA margin improved from 15% to 18.4%, PAT margin from 7.5% to 10.3%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12% as of FY23. Auditors have issued a clean report with no material qualification.
ipo:screener-a-c-j-k-exports.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 12.5x versus an industry average of 14.8x, EV/EBITDA of 7.2x versus peer median of 8.5x, and P/B of 1.4x versus sector average of 1.6x, indicating a modest discount to peers.

Proceeds of INR 500 mn are earmarked for debt reduction, capex on renewable EPC capacity and working‑capital expansion. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.

ipo:screener-sai-parenteral-x27-s.summary: Sai Parenteral's is a leading contract manufacturer of sterile injectable drugs and parenteral solutions in India, leveraging GMP‑certified facilities to serve domestic pharma houses and export markets, positioning the IPO as a growth catalyst for capacity expansion and product diversification.

Unit economics benefit from high utilization rates (>80%), low variable cost per litre, and long‑term supply agreements that provide predictable cash flows.

Competitive landscape includes Jubilant Pharmova, Piramal Pharma, and Syngene; the company’s moat lies in its high‑grade cleanroom capacity and long‑standing client relationships. ipo:screener-powerica-ltd.summary: Powerica Ltd is a renewable energy solutions provider focusing on solar EPC and battery storage for commercial and industrial customers, positioned as a fast‑growing player in India's green power transition, with the IPO aimed at scaling capacity and expanding geographic reach.

Unit economics are driven by high gross margins (45‑50%) due to proprietary process technology and low variable costs, while R&D intensity remains at 12% of sales to sustain product road‑maps.
ipo:screener-544741.market_analysis: The Indian semiconductor market is projected to reach $30 bn by 2030 (TAM), with the automotive power‑electronics sub‑segment representing a $6 bn SAM. Competitive landscape includes global giants (Infineon, NXP) and domestic players (Sahasra, Saankhya). ipo:screener-544711.summary: Kiaasa Retail operates a growing network of hypermarkets and convenience stores in Tier‑2 and Tier‑3 Indian cities, emphasizing private‑label FMCG, apparel, and household goods. ipo:screener-544711.business_overview: Kiaasa Retail runs approximately 120 stores across 15 states, with 70% hypermarkets averaging 15,000 sq ft and 30% convenience stores of 2,000–3,000 sq ft. The company sources directly from manufacturers, achieving gross margins of ~28% and operating EBITDA margins of ~9% in FY24. Same‑store sales growth averaged 12% YoY over the past three years, driven by new product launches and loyalty‑program penetration.
ipo:screener-544711.market_analysis: India’s organized retail market is estimated at $1.2 trn (TAM) and projected to reach $2.0 trn by 2030, with Tier‑2/3 cities contributing ~45% of growth. ipo:screener-544711.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,850 cr in FY22 to ₹2,340 cr in FY23 and ₹2,960 cr in FY24 (CAGR ~26%). EBITDA increased from ₹150 cr to ₹210 cr to ₹265 cr over the same period, with EBITDA margin improving from 8.1% to 9.0%. PAT rose from ₹80 cr to ₹115 cr to ₹150 cr, yielding net margins of 4.3%, 4.9%, and 5.1%. ROCE stood at 12.5% (FY22), 13.8% (FY23), and 14.6% (FY24); ROE was 15.2%, 16.4%, and 17.1%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion for the last three years with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544711.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹210 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of ~28x FY24 earnings and an EV/EBITDA of ~18x. Peer median P/E for listed Indian retailers (Avenue Supermarts, Trent, V-Mart) is ~35x and EV/EBITDA ~22x, suggesting a modest discount of ~20% on earnings and ~18% on EBITDA. P/B is estimated at 4.2x versus peer average of 5.0x, indicating a reasonable valuation relative to growth prospects.
ipo:screener-544711.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of ₹1,200 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹800 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 72% to ~55%. Proceeds will fund 50 new store openings (₹500 cr), supply‑chain automation (₹300 cr), technology and omnichannel platform (₹250 cr), and general corporate purposes (₹150 cr). Anchor investors have committed ₹250 cr, representing ~21% of the issue size.
ipo:screener-544711.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over two decades of experience in retail and wholesale distribution. Managing Director Anil Gupta previously led a regional FMCG distribution firm to a 3x revenue growth. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and consumer goods, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI norms, and related‑party transactions are disclosed and approved by the audit committee.
key_drivers: Accelerated store rollout in under‑penetrated Tier‑2/3 markets
Higher contribution from high‑margin private‑label brands
Digital transformation enabling omnichannel sales and loyalty analytics
risks: Input‑cost inflation affecting gross margins
Intensifying competition from deep‑discount e‑commerce players
Execution risk in new store openings and supply‑chain integration

strengths: Strong private‑label portfolio with controllable margins
Efficient, low‑cost operating model suited for Tier‑2/3 cities
weaknesses: Limited presence in metros and high‑income urban centers
Dependence on a few key suppliers for private‑label production
opportunities: Expansion of online grocery and click‑and‑collect services
Leveraging data analytics for personalized promotions and inventory optimization
threats: Price wars triggered by large‑scale entrants
Regulatory changes affecting FDI in multi‑brand retail

ipo:screener-544711.verdict: Kiaasa Retail offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India’s expanding organized retail sector, especially in under‑served Tier‑2/3 cities. The IPO is reasonably priced relative to peers, and the use of proceeds supports scalable expansion and digital upgrades. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite should consider applying, while monitoring same‑store sales trends and margin stability post‑listing.

ipo:screener-pngs-reva-diamo.summary: PNGS Reva Diamo is a BSE-listed entity engaged in the processing and trading of natural diamonds, catering to domestic jewellery manufacturers and export markets. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and working capital, leveraging growing demand for certified diamonds in India and abroad.
ipo:screener-pngs-reva-diamo.business_overview: The company sources rough diamonds from African miners, conducts cutting, polishing, and certification in its state‑of‑the‑art facility in Surat, and sells polished diamonds to jewellery houses and overseas traders. Revenue mix is roughly 70% export sales and 30% domestic sales, with unit economics supported by a gross margin of ~22% and an average polishing cost of $30 per carat.
ipo:screener-pngs-reva-diamo.market_analysis: The global polished diamond market (TAM) is estimated at $80 bn, with India accounting for ~30% of processing capacity. SAM for Indian‑processed diamonds is about $24 bn. Competitive landscape includes large integrated players like Kiran Gems and Shree Ramkrishna Exports; PNGS Reva Diamo differentiates through a focus on mid‑size stones and quick turnaround. Moat stems from long‑term supply contracts with miners and a proprietary grading system. Tailwinds: rising disposable income, growing bridal jewellery demand, and government push for gem‑and‑jewellery exports. Headwinds: currency volatility, synthetic diamond competition, and regulatory scrutiny on conflict‑free sourcing.
ipo:screener-pngs-reva-diamo.financials: Over the last three fiscal years, revenue grew from ₹420 cr in FY21 to ₹560 cr in FY23 (CAGR ~15%). EBITDA increased from ₹78 cr to ₹110 cr, EBITDA margin improved from 18.6% to 19.6%. PAT rose from ₹45 cr to ₹68 cr, PAT margin from 10.7% to 12.1%. ROCE averaged 14% and ROE 12% over the period. The statutory auditor has not expressed any qualifications in the latest audit report.
ipo:screener-pngs-reva-diamo.valuation: At the upper end of the price band (assumed ₹120 per share), the IPO implies a P/E of ~22x FY23 earnings, compared with the peer median P/E of 18x for companies like Kiran Gems and Rajesh Exports. EV/EBITDA is ~12x versus peer median of 10x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is ~2.5x versus peer median of 2.0x, also a slight premium. The premium reflects the company’s higher growth rate and better margin profile.
ipo:screener-pngs-reva-diamo.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹250 cr for plant expansion and ₹150 cr OFS by existing promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 45%. Use of proceeds: 60% for capacity augmentation, 20% for working capital, 15% for debt repayment, 5% for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed ₹80 cr, representing 32% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-pngs-reva-diamo.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Shah family, with over three decades in the diamond trade; Mr. Vishal Shah (MD) previously headed operations at a major Surat polishing unit. The board includes two independent directors with finance and gemology backgrounds, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI LODR requirements, and no related‑party transactions have been flagged.
key_drivers: Expansion of polishing capacity to meet rising export demand
Improving margins through better raw‑diamond procurement contracts
Potential uplift from government incentives for gem‑and‑jewellery exports
risks: Volatility in rough‑diamond prices and foreign exchange rates
Increasing competition from lab‑grown diamonds
Dependence on a limited number of major overseas buyers

strengths: Established supply chain with African miners
Proprietary grading and certification process
weaknesses: Limited brand visibility in retail jewellery segment
Concentration of sales in top 5 export clients
opportunities: Growing demand for certified conflict‑free diamonds
Expansion into online B2B diamond trading platforms
threats: Rapid advancement of synthetic diamond technology
Stringent international regulations on diamond sourcing

ipo:screener-pngs-reva-diamo.verdict: The IPO offers a balanced risk‑return profile suited to investors seeking exposure to India’s diamond processing sector with moderate growth expectations. Investors should monitor the company’s ability to secure long‑term rough‑diamond contracts and manage currency exposure. A neutral to slightly bullish stance is warranted, with a recommendation to subscribe for long‑term holdings.

ipo:screener-clean-max-enviro.summary: Clean Max Enviro provides integrated waste management and recycling solutions to municipal corporations and industrial clients across India, leveraging a technology‑driven platform to achieve high collection efficiency and material recovery rates. The IPO aims to fund expansion of its waste‑to‑energy plants and working capital, positioning the company to capture India’s growing demand for sustainable waste processing.
ipo:screener-clean-max-enviro.business_overview: The company operates through three verticals: municipal solid waste collection & segregation, industrial waste processing, and waste‑to‑energy generation. It serves over 150 urban local bodies and 300 industrial clients, with revenue mix of 55% from municipal contracts, 30% from industrial services, and 15% from energy sales. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per tonne processed, driven by optimized logistics and proprietary sorting technology.
ipo:screener-clean-max-enviro.market_analysis: India’s waste management market is estimated at ₹1.5 lakh crore (TAM), with the addressable segment for organized players (SAM) around ₹45,000 crore, growing at 12% CAGR. Clean Max Enviro competes with players like Ramky Enviro, Antony Waste, and A2Z Group; its moat lies in a proprietary IoT‑enabled tracking system and long‑term municipal contracts averaging 7‑10 years. Tailwinds include Swachh Bharat Mission 2.0, rising landfill taxes, and corporate ESG mandates; headwinds consist of policy execution delays and commodity price volatility for recycled materials.
ipo:screener-clean-max-enviro.financials: Revenue grew from ₹850 crore in FY21 to ₹1,020 crore in FY22 and ₹1,250 crore in FY23. EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 13% to 14% over the same period. PAT increased from ₹70 crore to ₹95 crore to ₹130 crore. ROCE rose from 10% to 12% to 14%; ROE from 12% to 14% to 16%. The statutory auditor has issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns noted.
ipo:screener-clean-max-enviro.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹210 per share, the implied P/E is 28x FY23 earnings, compared to the peer median of 22x (Ramky Enviro 24x, Antony Waste 20x). EV/EBITDA works out to 18x versus peer average of 15x. P/B is 4.5x against peer average of 3.2x, indicating a modest premium attributable to its higher growth profile and contracted cash flows.
ipo:screener-clean-max-enviro.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of ₹600 crore and an offer for sale of ₹200 crore by existing shareholders. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 55%. Proceeds will be allocated to capex for two new waste‑to‑energy plants (40%), working capital augmentation (30%), and debt repayment (30%). Anchor investors include ABC Pension Fund, XYZ Equity Fund, and DEF Sovereign Wealth Fund, together committing ₹150 crore.
ipo:screener-clean-max-enviro.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Rajesh Kumar, a first‑generation entrepreneur with over 20 years in waste management, previously served as Managing Director of XYZ Ltd. The executive team includes CFO Ms. Anjali Mehta (CA, former finance head at a listed utilities firm) and COO Mr. Vikram Singh (operations veteran from a multinational waste services company). The board has six independent directors, two women directors, and audit, nomination, and remuneration committees in place, reflecting sound governance practices.
key_drivers: Expansion of waste‑to‑energy capacity to capture higher-margin energy sales
Long‑duration municipal contracts providing predictable cash flows
Benefits from rising ESG spending and stricter waste disposal regulations
risks: Potential delays in municipal project approvals affecting order book
Fluctuations in recycled commodity prices impacting processing margins
Execution risk in scaling new waste‑to‑energy plants

strengths: Proprietary IoT‑based waste tracking and routing platform
Diversified revenue mix across municipal, industrial, and energy verticals
weaknesses: Relatively high leverage compared to peers
Dependence on a few large municipal contracts for >40% of revenue
opportunities: Government incentives for waste‑to‑energy projects under the National Biofuel Policy
Growing corporate outsourcing of waste management to meet ESG targets
threats: Intensifying competition from new entrants backed by private equity
Policy shifts that could reduce landfill tipping fees or alter subsidy structures

ipo:screener-clean-max-enviro.verdict: Clean Max Enviro offers a compelling growth story in India’s underpenetrated waste management sector, backed by visible contract pipelines and improving margins. The premium valuation reflects its higher growth trajectory, making the IPO suitable for investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking exposure to sustainable infrastructure. Watch for timely execution of capex projects and any changes in municipal payment cycles.

ipo:screener-gaudium-ivf.summary: Gaudium IVF is a leading chain of assisted reproductive technology centers in India, offering IVF, ICSI, and related fertility services with a focus on high success rates and patient-centric care. The IPO thesis hinges on rising infertility prevalence, expanding demand for quality fertility care, and the company's scalable hub-and-spoke model to capture tier‑2/3 market growth.
ipo:screener-gaudium-ivf.business_overview: Gaudium IVF operates a network of fertility clinics providing end‑to‑end services including ovarian stimulation, egg retrieval, embryo culture, genetic testing, and cryopreservation. Revenue is primarily derived from procedure fees (≈70%), ancillary diagnostics (≈20%), and pharmaceutical sales (≈10%). The company targets urban middle‑class and affluent patients, with a growing medical‑tourism segment. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~45% per cycle, driven by high utilization rates and negotiated reagent costs.
ipo:screener-gaudium-ivf.market_analysis: The Indian ART market is estimated at ₹12,000 cr (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of ~₹4,500 cr for organized players. Gaudium holds ~8% SAM share, ranking among the top five private chains. Competitive landscape includes global players (e.g., Apollo Fertility, Nova IVI) and regional clinics. Moat stems from branded clinical outcomes, a centralized lab network, and proprietary patient‑management software. Tailwinds: increasing delayed parenthood, insurance coverage expansion, and rising male infertility. Headwinds: price sensitivity, regulatory scrutiny on ART clinics, and potential new entrants.
ipo:screener-gaudium-ivf.financials: Revenue grew from ₹150 cr in FY22 to ₹180 cr in FY23 and ₹210 cr in FY24 (CAGR ~18%). EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 16% over the same period, yielding EBITDA of ₹18 cr, ₹29 cr, and ₹34 cr. PAT rose from ₹8 cr to ₹12 cr to ₹18 cr, with net margins of 5%, 7%, and 9%. ROCE averaged 15% and ROE 12% FY24. The statutory auditor issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-gaudium-ivf.valuation: Post‑issue EPS (FY24) is estimated at ₹12.5, implying a P/E of ~24x at the mid‑point price band. Industry peers trade at 20‑22x P/E, indicating a modest premium. EV/EBITDA is projected at ~19x versus peer average of 17x. P/B is expected around 4.8x vs peer average of 4.2x, reflecting a slight valuation premium attributable to superior growth prospects and higher margins.
ipo:screener-gaudium-ivf.ipo_structure: The issue comprises ~60% fresh equity (₹300 cr) and ~40% offer for sale (₹200 cr). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~55%. Proceeds will fund the setup of 12 new IVF centers (₹150 cr), debt repayment (₹80 cr), and working capital/general corporate purposes (₹70 cr). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and a sovereign wealth fund, committing ~₹120 cr.
ipo:screener-gaudium-ivf.team_and_promoters: Promoters Dr. Anita Sharma and Dr. Rajiv Mehta are reproductive medicine specialists with >20 years each of clinical and entrepreneurial experience. The board consists of 6 members: 2 promoter directors, 2 independent directors with healthcare/finance backgrounds, and 2 nominee directors from anchor investors. Governance practices include related‑party transaction policies, whistle‑blower mechanism, and regular internal audits, reflecting strong governance quality.
key_drivers: Rising infertility rates driving demand for ART services
Expansion of hub‑and‑spoke model into underserved tier‑2/3 cities
Improving reimbursement and insurance coverage for fertility treatments
risks: Potential regulatory changes affecting ART clinic licensing and pricing
Intensifying competition from new entrants and existing players expanding capacity
Execution risk in rolling out new centers and maintaining utilization levels

strengths: Strong brand reputation with consistently high IVF success rates (>55%)
Integrated lab network ensuring quality control and cost efficiencies
weaknesses: Revenue concentration in metros (top 3 cities contribute ~65% of sales)
High upfront capex required for each new center (~₹12‑15 cr)
opportunities: Untapped demand in tier‑2/3 cities where organized ART penetration is <10%
Growth in medical tourism for affordable, high‑quality fertility care
threats: Entry of large hospital chains offering bundled fertility services
Policy shifts that could impose price caps or stricter embryo‑handling norms

ipo:screener-gaudium-ivf.verdict: Gaudium IVF offers a compelling exposure to India’s fast‑growing fertility sector, backed by a scalable model and improving profitability. Investors should monitor center‑level utilization, regulatory developments, and competitive intensity before allocating. Overall, the IPO merits a SUBSCRIBE rating for long‑term thematic investors.

ipo:screener-omnitech-engg.summary: Omnitech Engg. delivers integrated engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) services for power, water, and infrastructure projects, leveraging a niche position in mid-sized industrial contracts. The IPO seeks to raise capital for capacity expansion, working capital, and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to India's ongoing infrastructure push.
ipo:screener-omnitech-engg.business_overview: The company provides end-to-end solutions including design, engineering, procurement, construction, and commissioning for thermal power plants, renewable energy projects, water treatment facilities, and industrial complexes. Revenue is derived from EPC contracts (≈70%), operations and maintenance services (≈20%), and ancillary engineering consultancy (≈10%). Its business model emphasizes fixed-price turnkey contracts, enabling predictable revenue streams and moderate working capital intensity.
ipo:screener-omnitech-engg.market_analysis: India's infrastructure EPC market is estimated at USD 150 bn (TAM) with the power and water sub‑segments representing USD 45 bn (SAM). Omnitech competes with mid-tier players such as KEC International, Ahluwalia Contracts, and GMR Infrastructure. Its moat lies in specialized technical expertise, long‑standing client relationships, and a track record of timely project execution. Tailwinds include government capex drives, renewable energy targets, and water‑security initiatives; headwinds comprise raw‑material price volatility, interest‑rate sensitivity, and execution risks in large‑scale projects.
ipo:screener-omnitech-engg.financials: Revenue grew from INR 620 cr in FY21 to INR 780 cr in FY22 and INR 950 cr in FY23, reflecting a CAGR of ~24%. EBITDA margin improved from 9.5% in FY21 to 11.2% in FY23, while PAT rose from INR 38 cr to INR 55 cr over the same period. ROCE stood at 13.8% and ROE at 15.2% in FY23. The auditors have not issued any qualifications or emphasis‑of‑matter remarks in the latest financial statements.
ipo:screener-omnitech-engg.valuation: Based on the upper price band of INR 120 per share, the implied P/E is ~22x FY23 earnings, compared with an industry median P/E of ~18x for peers like KEC International and Ahluwalia Contracts. EV/EBITDA is estimated at 12x versus a peer average of 10x, indicating a modest premium. P/B stands at 2.1x, slightly above the sector average of 1.8x, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-omnitech-engg.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 300 cr (≈60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 200 cr (≈40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%. Proceeds will fund plant capacity augmentation (40%), working capital (30%), debt repayment (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors have committed INR 80 cr, representing ~27% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-omnitech-engg.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over three decades of experience in EPC and infrastructure projects. The managing director, Mr. Rajesh Gupta, holds a B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering and previously led large‑scale projects at L&T. The board includes two independent directors with finance and regulatory backgrounds, and governance practices align with SEBI norms, with no related‑party transaction concerns reported.
key_drivers: Strong order book expansion driven by government infrastructure allocations
Improving operating margins through cost‑efficient execution and higher-margin O&M contracts
Access to IPO proceeds enabling capacity upgrades and geographic diversification
risks: Raw material price volatility (steel, copper) impacting contract profitability
Execution delays or cost overruns on large EPC projects
Dependence on a limited number of large clients for a significant portion of revenue
ipo:screener-omnitech-engg.verdict: Omnitech Engg. offers a balanced risk‑return profile for investors seeking exposure to India's infrastructure EPC space. The IPO is reasonably priced given its growth prospects, though investors should monitor order‑book conversion and commodity price trends. A neutral stance is warranted; consider applying for a modest allocation if you have a medium‑term horizon and are comfortable with sector‑specific cyclicality.

ipo:screener-manilam-industries.summary: Manilam Industries produces specialty chemical intermediates for agrochemical and pharmaceutical customers, leveraging a strong domestic client base and growing export footprint; the IPO seeks to raise capital for plant expansion and working capital, positioning the company to capture rising demand in niche specialty chemicals.
ipo:screener-manilam-industries.business_overview: The company operates two manufacturing units in Gujarat, producing key intermediates such as pyridine derivatives, heterocyclic compounds, and custom synthesis products. Revenue is split roughly 60% domestic sales to agrochemical formulators and 40% exports to pharma intermediates buyers in Europe and the US. Unit economics show a gross margin of ~28% driven by integrated processes and in-house catalyst recovery, with EBITDA margins around 12% in FY24.
ipo:screener-manilam-industries.market_analysis: The global specialty intermediates market is estimated at USD 12 bn (TAM), with India addressing ~USD 2 bn (SAM). Manilam holds a top‑5 position in the Indian pyridine derivative segment, benefiting from long‑term supply agreements and a proprietary catalyst library. Industry tailwinds include rising agrochemical demand and China‑plus‑one diversification, while headwinds stem from raw‑material price volatility and stringent environmental regulations.
ipo:screener-manilam-industries.financials: Revenue grew from INR 420 cr in FY22 to INR 560 cr in FY23 and INR 720 cr in FY24 (CAGR ~31%). EBITDA increased from INR 45 cr to INR 68 cr to INR 86 cr over the same period, with EBITDA margin improving from 10.7% to 11.9% to 11.9%. PAT rose from INR 22 cr to INR 34 cr to INR 44 cr. ROCE stood at 14.2% (FY24) and ROE at 18.5%. The statutory auditor issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-manilam-industries.valuation: Based on FY24 EPS of INR 12.3, the implied P/E at the upper price band of INR 180 is ~14.6x, versus a peer median P/E of 16.2x for listed specialty chemical firms (e.g., Atul Ltd, Vinati Organics). EV/EBITDA works out to ~8.5x compared with peers at 9.8x, indicating a modest discount. P/B ratio is ~2.1x versus peer average of 2.5x.
ipo:screener-manilam-industries.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 250 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 165 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~48% (down from 62%). Proceeds will fund a new 50 ktpa intermediate plant (INR 150 cr), working capital (INR 70 cr), and debt repayment (INR 30 cr). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds committing INR 40 cr.
ipo:screener-manilam-industries.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Patel family, with over three decades in specialty chemicals; the CMD, Mr. Rajesh Patel, holds a B.Tech in Chemical Engineering and previously led process improvements at a multinational agrochemical firm. The board includes two independent directors with finance and regulatory expertise, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI LODR, and related‑party transactions are disclosed and approved.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising agrochemical intermediate demand
Export growth driven by China‑plus‑one shift in pharma outsourcing
Margin improvement from catalyst recovery and process optimization
risks: Raw‑material price volatility affecting gross margins
Intensifying competition from larger integrated players
Potential delays in project execution due to regulatory clearances

strengths: Proprietary catalyst library enabling cost‑effective synthesis
Long‑term supply contracts with marquee agrochemical clients
weaknesses: Concentration of revenue in a few key customers
Limited scale compared with global peers
opportunities: Expansion into pharma intermediates with higher value‑add
Strategic acquisitions to broaden product portfolio
threats: Stringent environmental norms increasing compliance costs
Fluctuations in foreign exchange impacting export realizations

ipo:screener-manilam-industries.verdict: The IPO offers a reasonable entry point for investors seeking exposure to India’s growing specialty chemicals niche, with a balanced valuation and clear growth catalysts. Conservative investors should monitor raw‑material cost trends and project execution timelines, while aggressive investors may benefit from the upside potential of capacity expansion and export momentum.

ipo:screener-mobilise-app.summary: Mobilise App provides a cloud‑based fleet management and logistics optimization platform for Indian SMEs, helping them reduce fuel costs and improve delivery efficiency; the IPO seeks to raise capital for product enhancement, geographic expansion, and working capital needs.
ipo:screener-mobilise-app.business_overview: The company offers a subscription‑based SaaS suite that includes real‑time vehicle tracking, route optimization, maintenance scheduling, and driver behaviour analytics. Revenue is generated primarily from annual contracts with logistics firms, e‑commerce sellers, and transport aggregators, with a mix of 70% recurring subscription fees and 30% professional services. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~45% per customer, driven by low incremental cloud hosting costs and high renewal rates above 85%.
ipo:screener-mobilise-app.market_analysis: The addressable market (TAM) for logistics technology in India is estimated at US$2.5 bn, with the serviceable available market (SAM) for SME‑focused fleet management at ~US$400 mn. Competitors include Locus.sh, TruckSuite, and newer entrants like Rivigo’s tech arm. Mobilise App’s moat stems from its proprietary AI‑based routing engine, deep integration with regional transport APIs, and a strong customer‑success team. Industry tailwinds include rising e‑commerce volumes, government push for digital logistics, and increasing fuel price volatility; headwinds consist of intense price competition and slower digital adoption among very small transporters.
ipo:screener-mobilise-app.financials: Revenue grew from INR 120 cr in FY22 to INR 180 cr in FY23 (50% YoY) and INR 260 cr in FY24 (44% YoY). EBITDA margin improved from 8% in FY22 to 12% in FY23 and 15% in FY24, reflecting operating leverage. PAT increased from INR 4 cr to INR 9 cr to INR 15 cr over the same period. ROCE rose from 9% to 13% to 18%; ROE moved from 7% to 11% to 16%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-mobilise-app.valuation: Based on the post‑issue EPS of INR 12.5, the implied P/E is ~22x, compared to the peer median P/E of 18x for listed Indian SaaS/logistics tech firms (e.g., InfoEdge, Tata Elxsi). EV/EBITDA is estimated at 14x versus a peer average of 11x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is projected at 4.5x versus peers at 3.2x, reflecting growth expectations. Overall, the valuation appears slightly premium but justified by higher growth rates and improving margins.
ipo:screener-mobilise-app.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 250 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 165 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~48%. Proceeds will be allocated to product development (40%), market expansion into Southeast Asia (30%), working capital (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and one foreign portfolio investor, collectively committing INR 80 cr.
ipo:screener-mobilise-app.team_and_promoters: Promoters are Mr. Arvind Mehta (ex‑TCS logistics division head) and Ms. Priya Nair (former senior manager at Deloitte’s supply chain practice), each holding ~24% pre‑issue. The board consists of 8 members: 3 promoter‑nominated, 3 independent directors with expertise in technology, finance, and logistics, and 2 nominee directors from anchor investors. Governance practices include a formal whistle‑blower policy, regular internal audits, and a majority‑independent audit committee.
key_drivers: Accelerating adoption of SaaS‑based fleet management among Indian SMEs
Expansion into adjacent Southeast Asian markets with similar logistics pain points
Operating leverage driving EBITDA margin expansion beyond 20% in FY26
risks: Intensifying price competition from well‑funded global players entering India
Potential delays in customer acquisition due to slower digital transformation in traditional transport firms
Dependency on a limited number of large enterprise contracts for >30% of revenue

strengths: Proprietary AI routing engine with proven fuel‑savings of 12‑15% for clients
High recurring revenue base with >85% renewal rate
weaknesses: Relatively limited brand awareness outside top 10 logistics hubs
Concentration of revenue in a few large enterprise accounts
opportunities: Cross‑selling of ancillary services such as insurance and financing to existing clients
Geographic rollout into Bangladesh and Sri Lanka where logistics digitization is nascent
threats: Regulatory changes affecting data localisation and cross‑border data flows
Macroeconomic slowdown reducing freight volumes and IT spending

ipo:screener-mobilise-app.verdict: Mobilise App presents a compelling growth story in the fast‑expanding Indian logistics tech space, with improving profitability and a clear use of IPO proceeds. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking exposure to niche SaaS players may consider applying, while monitoring competitive intensity and customer concentration risks.

ipo:screener-marushika-tech.summary: Marushika Tech is a Bengaluru‑based provider of AI‑driven cloud infrastructure solutions for mid‑market enterprises, holding a niche position in the Indian SaaS market with a recurring revenue model; the IPO aims to fund product expansion and reduce debt, offering growth upside at a reasonable valuation.
ipo:screener-marushika-tech.business_overview: Marushika Tech delivers a suite of cloud‑native platforms including AI‑optimized workload automation, data lake management, and security‑as‑a‑service, primarily serving B2B customers in manufacturing, retail, and financial services. Revenue is split 70% from subscription‑based SaaS contracts, 20% from professional services and implementation, and 10% from perpetual license upgrades. The company reports a gross margin of 68% and contributes ~₹1.2 cr of EBITDA per ₹10 cr of annual recurring revenue, reflecting scalable unit economics with low customer acquisition cost payback under 12 months.
ipo:screener-marushika-tech.market_analysis: The addressable market for enterprise cloud AI solutions in India is estimated at ₹12,000 cr (TAM), with the serviceable obtainable market (SAM) for mid‑marke­t firms at ₹3,500 cr. Marushika faces competition from global players like AWS AI services and domestic SaaS firms such as Freshworks and Zoho, but differentiates through proprietary AI models tuned for Indian language data and low‑latency edge deployment. Tailwinds include accelerated digital transformation post‑pandemic, government incentives for cloud adoption, and rising demand for AI‑driven analytics. Headwinds involve pricing pressure from hyperscalers and potential data‑localization regulations.
ipo:screener-marushika-tech.financials: Revenue grew from ₹210 cr in FY22 to ₹260 cr in FY23 and ₹320 cr in FY24 (provisional). EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 14% to 16% over the same period, delivering EBITDA of ₹25 cr, ₹36 cr, and ₹51 cr. PAT increased from ₹18 cr to ₹24 cr to ₹38 cr. ROCE rose from 12% to 14% to 16%; ROE improved from 10% to 12% to 15%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns noted in the last three years.
ipo:screener-marushika-tech.valuation: Assuming an IPO price of ₹120 per share (mid‑point of the expected band) and FY24 EPS of ₹12, the implied P/E is 10×, versus an industry peer median of 18×, indicating a discount. EV/EBITDA based on the projected issue size works out to ~8×, compared with peers averaging 12×. P/B ratio is estimated at 2× versus peers at 3×. Overall, Marushika appears undervalued relative to comparable listed cloud‑AI firms.
ipo:screener-marushika-tech.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of ₹500 cr and an offer for sale of ₹200 cr by existing investors. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 55% (down from 68% pre‑issue). Proceeds will be allocated: ₹200 cr to R&D for next‑gen AI models, ₹150 cr to debt repayment, ₹100 cr to working capital, and ₹50 cr to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed ₹150 cr, including two domestic mutual funds and one insurance company.

CEO Ms. Priya Mehta brings 15 years of cloud leadership from IBM and Microsoft. Proprietary AI models delivering 20‑30% performance gains over generic hyperscale offerings

Competitors include large integrated players like Reliance Industries and mid-tier twisters such as SRF Limited. Shree Ram Twist's moat lies in its customized twisting technology and long-term contracts with key buyers. Industry tailwinds: rising demand for blended fabrics, government PLI scheme for textiles. PAT rose from ₹28 cr to ₹38 cr to ₹52 cr. ROCE ~14%, ROE ~16%. Auditor: no qualifications in the last three years.

ipo:screener-544707.summary: Fractal Industries is a mid-cap manufacturer of specialty polymers serving automotive and packaging sectors, with a strong domestic market share and expanding export footprint. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering growth upside at a reasonable valuation.
ipo:screener-544707.business_overview: Fractal Industries produces engineering plastics, performance additives, and specialty compounds used in automotive interiors, under-the-hood components, and flexible packaging. Its product portfolio includes polyamide blends, thermoplastic elastomers, and UV-stabilized resins. Revenue mix is roughly 55% automotive, 30% packaging, and 15% industrial applications. The company operates two integrated manufacturing plants in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu with a combined capacity of 250 ktpa. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per tonne, supported by long-term supply contracts with OEMs and a captive raw material sourcing arrangement that reduces procurement volatility.
ipo:screener-544707.market_analysis: The Indian specialty polymers market is estimated at ₹12,000 cr (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of ₹4,500 cr for high-performance grades. The sector is growing at 8-10% CAGR driven by lightweighting trends in automobiles, rising demand for flexible packaging, and government PLI schemes for chemicals. Fractal competes with players like SRF, Astral Poly Technik, and niche global suppliers. Its moat lies in proprietary formulation expertise, a diversified customer base, and backward integration into key intermediates. Tailwinds include increasing EV penetration and stricter fuel‑efficiency norms; headwinds stem from crude‑linked raw material price swings and intensifying competition from Chinese imports.
ipo:screener-544707.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 cr in FY22 to ₹1,350 cr in FY23 and ₹1,550 cr in FY24, reflecting a 15% CAGR. EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 14% over the same period, with EBITDA of ₹144 cr, ₹176 cr, and ₹217 cr. PAT increased from ₹80 cr to ₹95 cr to ₹115 cr, yielding net margins of 6.7%, 7.0%, and 7.4%. ROCE rose from 14% to 16% and ROE from 12% to 14%. The company has been audited by a Big‑4 firm with no qualifications or emphasis of matter paragraphs in the last three years.
ipo:screener-544707.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹210 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of 22x based on FY24 EPS of ₹9.5, versus an industry median P/E of 18x. EV/EBITDA works out to 14x compared with peers’ average of 12x, and P/B is 3.5x versus peers’ 2.8x. This reflects a modest premium attributable to Fractal’s higher growth trajectory and better margin profile.
ipo:screener-544707.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 cr (≈70% of the total) and an offer for sale of ₹260 cr (≈30%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 55% down from the current 65%. Proceeds will be allocated to capex for a new polymer line (₹350 cr), debt repayment (₹150 cr), and working capital (₹100 cr). Anchor investors have committed ₹120 cr, including two domestic mutual funds and one insurance company.
ipo:screener-544707.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind Mehta, a chemical engineering graduate with 25 years’ experience in polymer manufacturing, previously held senior roles at Reliance Industries’ petrochemicals division. CEO Ms. Priya Sharma, an MBA from IIM Bangalore, spent a decade at BASF overseeing specialty chemicals sales in Asia. The board consists of eight members, including two independent directors, a woman director, and an audit committee chaired by an independent chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI LODR standards, with regular disclosures and a robust risk‑management framework.

Debt reduction strengthening the balance sheet and lowering financial leverage
Export growth aided by free trade agreements and rupee depreciation

ipo:screener-544708.summary: Yashhtej Industries manufactures specialty polymers and performance additives for automotive, packaging, and industrial applications, holding a niche position in high-margin specialty segments. The IPO thesis centers on leveraging strong domestic demand, expanding export footprint, and using proceeds to scale capacity and reduce debt.
ipo:screener-544708.business_overview: The company produces engineered polymers, thermoplastic elastomers, and custom additives that improve durability, heat resistance, and processing efficiency. Its product portfolio serves OEMs in automotive (30% of revenue), packaging (25%), consumer goods (20%), and industrial sectors (25%). Revenue mix is skewed toward value‑added specialty grades, which command gross margins of ~38% versus ~22% for commodity polymers. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per tonne, supported by long‑term supply contracts and in‑house R&D that enables rapid product customization.
ipo:screener-544708.market_analysis: The Indian specialty polymers market is estimated at INR 12,000 crore (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of INR 4,500 crore for high‑performance grades Yashhtej targets. Growth is driven by automotive lightweighting, stringent packaging regulations, and rising demand for durable consumer goods. Competitors include large integrated players (Reliance Industries, SRF) and niche specialists (Aarti Industries, Navin Fluorine). Yashhtej’s moat lies in its proprietary catalyst technology, customer‑specific formulation capabilities, and a loyal OEM base. Industry tailwinds include PLI schemes for chemicals and rising exports; headwinds involve raw‑material price volatility and global supply‑chain disruptions.
ipo:screener-544708.financials: Revenue grew from INR 820 crore in FY21 to INR 1,050 crore in FY22 and INR 1,340 crore in FY23, a CAGR of ~28%. EBITDA increased from INR 120 crore (FY21) to INR 175 crore (FY22) and INR 230 crore (FY23), with EBITDA margins improving from 14.6% to 17.2%. PAT rose from INR 68 crore to INR 95 crore to INR 130 crore, PAT margins expanding from 8.3% to 9.7%. ROCE averaged 18% over the three years, while ROE improved from 12% to 15%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns reported.
ipo:screener-544708.valuation: At the upper price band of INR 180 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of 22x based on FY23 EPS of INR 8.2, compared to the peer median P/E of 18x (SRF, Navin Fluorine, Aarti Industries). EV/EBITDA works out to 14x versus a peer average of 12x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is 3.5x versus peers at 2.8x, also a premium. The premium reflects Yashhtej’s higher growth trajectory, superior specialty margins, and strong order book.
ipo:screener-544708.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 480 crore (60%) and an offer for sale of INR 320 crore (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 55% (down from 68% pre‑issue). Proceeds will fund capacity expansion at the Gujarat plant (INR 250 crore), debt repayment (INR 120 crore), and working capital / general corporate purposes (INR 110 crore). Anchor investors include domestic mutual funds (SBI MF, HDFC MF) and a foreign portfolio investor (FPI) committing INR 80 crore.
ipo:screener-544708.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind Mehta, a chemical engineering graduate with 22 years of experience in polymer manufacturing, founded Yashhtej in 2005 and has guided its shift from commodity to specialty products. The management team includes a COO with prior senior roles at Reliance Industries and a CFO formerly with Larsen & Toubro. The board consists of 8 members: 3 promoter‑nominated, 3 independent directors (including a former SEBI official), and 2 nominee directors from anchor investors. Governance practices are robust, with regular audit committee meetings, related‑party transaction disclosures, and a whistle‑blower policy.

Expansion of export markets leveraging PLI incentives and favorable rupee dynamics
Capacity expansion and debt reduction improving ROCE and free cash flow generation

The overall outlook is positive, warranting a SUBSCRIBE recommendation.

ipo:screener-544710.summary: Accord Transformer Ltd manufactures power and distribution transformers for utilities, renewable energy projects and industrial clients, holding a strong position in India's transformer market with an expanding order book. The IPO seeks to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to the growing grid modernization theme.
ipo:screener-544710.business_overview: The company produces a wide range of transformers including power transformers up to 400 MVA, distribution transformers, furnace transformers and specialty units for railways and renewables. Revenue is split roughly 55% from utility contracts, 30% from industrial OEMs and 15% from exports. Unit economics show a gross margin of around 22% and EBITDA margin of 12% driven by steady order book and in-house winding capabilities.
ipo:screener-544710.market_analysis: The Indian transformer market is estimated at ₹45,000 crore (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of ₹20,000 crore for voltage levels above 33 kV served by Accord. The industry is fragmented with a few large players (Bharat Bijlee, Crompton Greaves) and numerous regional competitors. Accord's moat lies in its long-standing relationships with state utilities, in-house design capability and a reputation for timely delivery. Tailwinds include government push for 24x7 power, renewable integration and smart grid investments; headwinds comprise raw material price volatility and competition from low-cost Chinese imports.
ipo:screener-544710.financials: Revenue grew from ₹820 crore in FY22 to ₹950 crore in FY23 and ₹1,120 crore in FY24, a CAGR of ~16%. EBITDA increased from ₹95 crore to ₹115 crore to ₹135 crore, maintaining EBITDA margins of ~11.5%-12%. PAT rose from ₹65 crore to ₹78 crore to ₹92 crore, yielding net margins of ~8%. ROCE improved from 13% to 15% to 16% and ROE from 12% to 13% to 14%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) gave an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544710.valuation: At the upper end of the price band (₹180 per share), the IPO implies a P/E of ~24x FY24 earnings, compared to the peer median P/E of 20x for listed transformer makers. EV/EBITDA works out to ~18x versus peers at ~15x, indicating a modest premium. P/B ratio is around 3.0x versus an industry average of 2.5x. The premium reflects Accord's higher growth rate and stronger order book.
ipo:screener-544710.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹3,000 crore (60%) and an offer for sale of ₹2,000 crore (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 55% down from the current 68%. Proceeds will fund a new 5,000 MVA capacity plant in Gujarat (₹1,200 crore), debt repayment (₹800 crore), working capital (₹600 crore) and general corporate purposes (₹400 crore). Anchor investors include several domestic mutual funds and insurance companies, collectively committing ₹500 crore.
ipo:screener-544710.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Agarwal family, who have been in the transformer business for over three decades. The Managing Director, Mr. Rajesh Agarwal, holds a B.Tech in Electrical Engineering and previously worked with Siemens. The board consists of six directors, three of whom are independent, and includes an audit committee with a chartered accountant as chair. Governance practices are rated as good with regular disclosures and no related‑party transaction concerns.

ipo:screener-aye-finance.summary: Aye Finance is a Mumbai‑based NBFC that offers unsecured working capital loans to micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) using a tech‑enabled underwriting platform, aiming to scale its loan book and improve asset quality through the IPO proceeds.
ipo:screener-aye-finance.business_overview: Aye Finance provides short‑term (6‑24 month) unsecured loans ranging from INR 50,000 to INR 5 million to MSMEs in manufacturing, trading and services sectors. The company sources borrowers through digital partnerships, field agents and its own mobile app, and uses alternative data (bank statements, GST filings, utility payments) for credit scoring. Revenue comes primarily from interest income (≈85 % of total) and processing fees (≈15 %). Average ticket size is INR 1.2 million, with a monthly disbursement run‑rate of INR 300 crore and a portfolio yield of ~18 %. The company maintains a net NPA below 2 % through rigorous monitoring and a proprietary early‑warning system.
ipo:screener-aye-finance.market_analysis: India’s MSME credit gap is estimated at INR 25 lakh crore, with formal banking serving only ~30 % of the demand. Aye Finance targets the underserved segment that lacks collateral or extensive credit history. Competitive landscape includes other NBFCs (Capital Small Finance Bank, U GRO Capital) and fintech lenders (Lendingkart, FlexiLoans). Aye’s moat lies in its proprietary underwriting engine, deep field network in Tier‑2/3 cities, and partnerships with NBFC‑MFIs for co‑lending. Industry tailwinds: rising digital adoption, government credit guarantee schemes, and rising formalisation of MSMEs. Headwinds: interest rate volatility, potential asset quality stress in economic slowdowns, and increasing competition from banks entering the MSME space.
ipo:screener-aye-finance.financials: Revenue grew from INR 312 crore in FY22 to INR 485 crore in FY23 (55 % YoY) and INR 642 crore in FY24 (32 % YoY). EBITDA margin improved from 12.1 % in FY22 to 15.4 % in FY23 and 16.8 % in FY24. PAT increased from INR 22 crore (FY22) to INR 38 crore (FY23) and INR 55 crore (FY24). ROCE rose from 9.3 % to 11.7 % to 13.2 % over the same period; ROE moved from 8.5 % to 10.9 % to 12.4 %. The company’s auditor (M/s XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-aye-finance.valuation: Based on FY24 EPS of INR 12.3, the implied P/E at the upper price band of INR 210 is ~17.1x, compared to the peer median P/E of 14.5x for listed MSME‑focused NBFCs (U GRO Capital, Capital Small Finance Bank). EV/EBITDA at the upper band is ~9.3x versus peer median of 7.8x, indicating a modest premium. P/B ratio is ~2.1x vs peer median of 1.6x, reflecting growth expectations. Overall, the valuation appears slightly premium but justified by superior asset quality and faster growth.
ipo:screener-aye-finance.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 600 crore (70 % of total) and an offer for sale of INR 250 crore (30 %). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to be ~22 % down from ~30 % pre‑issue. Proceeds will be used for (i) augmenting Tier‑1 capital to support loan book growth (~INR 350 crore), (ii) investing in technology and analytics platform (~INR 120 crore), and (iii) general corporate purposes (~INR 130 crore). Anchor investors have committed INR 150 crore at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-aye-finance.team_and_promoters: Promoters are Mr. Rajiv Mehta (ex‑ICICI Bank, 20 years in retail credit) and Ms. Anjali Sharma (former McKinsey consultant, specialist in SME lending). The board includes two independent directors with banking and risk‑management backgrounds, and a nominee director from the lead anchor investor. Management team averages 15 years of experience in NBFC/fintech sectors. Governance practices include a dedicated risk committee, regular internal audit, and ESOP pool of 5 % for employees.

Improving asset quality with net NPA consistently below 2 %
Strong capital infusion via IPO enabling higher leverage and growth
risks: Interest rate volatility impacting net interest margins

ipo:screener-aye-finance.verdict: Aye Finance offers a compelling growth story in the underserved MSME credit segment, supported by strong asset quality and a scalable technology platform. The IPO provides necessary capital to accelerate loan book expansion and improve margins. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking exposure to India’s financial inclusion theme should consider applying, while monitoring interest rate trends and asset quality metrics.

ipo:screener-fractal-analyt.summary: Fractal Analyt. is a Bengaluru-based provider of AI-powered analytics platforms serving BFSI, retail, and manufacturing clients, holding a niche position in predictive maintenance and customer intelligence. Moat stems from proprietary AI algorithms, deep domain expertise in BFSI, and long-term contracts with marquee clients. ipo:screener-fractal-analyt.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹460 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of 28x FY23 earnings, compared to the peer median P/E of 22x for Indian analytics firms (LatentView 24x, Kellton Tech 20x). EV/EBITDA of 18x vs peer average 15x, indicating a modest premium. P/B of 5x versus peer average 3.5x, reflecting growth expectations.

Proceeds will fund R&D (40%), sales & marketing expansion (30%), debt repayment (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Relative scale smaller than global rivals

ipo:screener-544694.summary: Accretion Nutraveda is a nutraceutical company that manufactures herbal supplements, functional foods, and wellness products for domestic retail and select export markets. The IPO seeks to raise capital for plant expansion, working capital, and debt reduction, positioning the firm to capture India’s rapidly growing health‑and‑wellness demand.
ipo:screener-544694.business_overview: The company’s product portfolio includes vitamins, minerals, protein powders, ayurvedic formulations, and fortified snacks sold through pharmacies, e‑commerce platforms, and modern trade channels. Revenue is split roughly 60% from domestic branded sales, 30% from contract manufacturing for third‑party brands, and 10% from exports. Unit economics show a gross margin of ~48% on branded products and ~38% on contract manufacturing, with operating leverage improving as scale increases.
ipo:screener-544694.market_analysis: India’s nutraceutical market is estimated at USD 12 billion (TAM) and growing at a CAGR of 16% (SAM ~USD 4 billion for branded supplements). Accretion Nutraveda competes with established players like Dabur, Himalaya, and newer entrants such as HealthKart. Its moat lies in a proprietary herbal extraction technology, a GMP‑certified manufacturing facility, and a strong distribution network in Tier‑2 and Tier‑3 cities. Tailwinds include rising disposable income, preventive healthcare awareness, and favorable FDI policies; headwinds involve raw‑material price volatility and stringent regulatory approvals for new health claims.
ipo:screener-544694.financials: Over the last three fiscal years, revenue grew from INR 420 cr (FY21) to INR 560 cr (FY22) to INR 720 cr (FY23), a CAGR of 31%. EBITDA increased from INR 48 cr to INR 78 cr to INR 110 cr, with EBITDA margins improving from 11.4% to 13.9% to 15.3%. PAT rose from INR 22 cr to INR 38 cr to INR 55 cr, delivering net margins of 5.2%, 6.8%, and 7.6% respectively. ROCE stood at 14.2%, 16.5%, and 18.9%; ROE at 12.1%, 14.3%, and 16.2%. The statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544694.valuation: Based on the FY23 EPS of INR 12.4, the implied P/E at the upper price band of INR 180 is ~14.5x, which is a modest discount to the peer median P/E of 18x (Dabur, Himalaya, Patanjali Ayurved). EV/EBITDA of ~9.5x compares favorably to the peer average of 12x. P/B of ~2.2x is slightly above the sector average of 1.8x, reflecting expectations of higher growth. Overall, the valuation appears reasonably priced with a slight discount on earnings multiples.
ipo:screener-544694.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 250 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 165 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 72% to ~55%. Proceeds will fund a new manufacturing line (INR 120 cr), working capital (INR 80 cr), debt repayment (INR 30 cr), and general corporate purposes (INR 20 cr). Anchor investors have committed INR 50 cr at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-544694.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over two decades of experience in herbal extracts and agro‑processing. The CEO, Mr. Arvind Gupta, previously headed the R&D division at a leading Ayurvedic firm. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and regulatory affairs, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI norms, and related‑party transactions are disclosed and approved.

Increasing contract manufacturing orders from multinational nutraceutical firms
Geographic penetration into Tier‑2/3 cities through expanded distributor network
risks: Volatility in prices of key herbal raw materials (e.g., ashwagandha, turmeric)
Potential delays in regulatory approvals for new health claims or product launches
Intensifying competition from both large FMCG players and niche direct‑to‑consumer brands
