Conservative applicants should monitor fuel price trends and load‑factor improvements post‑listing; aggressive investors may consider applying for the lot. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE stance.

ipo:screener-544511.summary: Karbonsteel Eng. manufactures specialty steel products for automotive and infrastructure sectors, holding a niche position in high-strength alloy segments. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering growth exposure to India's infrastructure push.
ipo:screener-544511.business_overview: The company produces cold-rolled, hot-rolled and alloy steel bars, rods and sections used in chassis, suspension systems and construction reinforcement. Its product mix is 60% automotive OEM supply, 30% infrastructure projects and 10% industrial machinery. Revenue is generated through long-term supply contracts with tier‑1 auto makers and EPC contractors, yielding gross margins of ~18% and contribution per tonne of ~₹12,000.
ipo:screener-544511.market_analysis: India's specialty steel TAM is estimated at ₹1.2 lakh crore, with SAM for high‑strength alloy products around ₹30,000 crore. Karbonsteel faces competition from integrated players like Tata Steel and SAIL, but differentiates through proprietary heat‑treatment processes and a captive raw‑material linkage. Tailwinds include government PLI schemes for auto components and rising infrastructure spend; headwinds comprise raw‑material price volatility and global steel overcapacity.
ipo:screener-544511.financials: Revenue grew from ₹850 crore in FY21 to ₹1,020 crore in FY22 and ₹1,210 crore in FY23 (CAGR ~19%). EBITDA increased from ₹120 crore to ₹155 crore to ₹190 crore, with EBITDA margin improving from 14.1% to 15.2% to 15.7%. PAT rose from ₹55 crore to ₹78 crore to ₹95 crore. ROCE stood at 12.3%, 13.5% and 14.2%; ROE at 9.8%, 10.6% and 11.4%. The statutory auditor has not expressed any qualifications in the last three years.
ipo:screener-544511.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹150 per share, the implied P/E is ~22x FY23 earnings, versus an average of 18x for listed peers such as JSW Steel (specialty division) and APL Apollo. EV/EBITDA is ~10x compared to peer median of 8.5x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is ~1.8x versus peer average of 1.5x, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-544511.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity (₹480 crore) and 40% offer for sale (₹320 crore). Post‑issue promoter holding will decline from 72% to ~58%. Proceeds will fund a new rolling mill (₹250 crore), debt repayment (₹150 crore) and general corporate purposes (₹80 crore). Anchor investors have committed ₹120 crore at the cutoff price.
ipo:screener-544511.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Kumar family, with over three decades in steel manufacturing; the CMD, Mr. Rajesh Kumar, previously headed operations at a mid‑size integrated steel plant. The board includes two independent directors with finance and metallurgy expertise, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance disclosures are regular and no related‑party transaction concerns have been flagged.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture higher auto OEM demand
Improving margins from value‑added alloy products
Government incentives for domestic steel production
risks: Raw material price volatility affecting spreads
Intensifying competition from large integrated players
Potential slowdown in infrastructure spending

strengths: Proprietary heat‑treatment technology
Long‑term contracts with tier‑1 auto makers
weaknesses: Limited scale compared to integrated peers
Dependence on a few key customers
opportunities: Expansion into renewable energy sector steel components
Geographic diversification into South‑East Asian markets
threats: Global steel oversupply leading to price pressure
Regulatory changes in import duties on raw materials

ipo:screener-544511.verdict: The IPO offers a balanced growth play for investors seeking exposure to India's specialty steel upswing, with reasonable valuation and solid fundamentals. Retail investors with a medium‑term horizon may consider applying, while watching for raw‑material cost trends and order‑book visibility. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-544514.summary: Jay Ambe Super operates a growing chain of supermarkets across western India, offering groceries, fresh produce, and household goods to middle-income consumers. The IPO aims to fund expansion and debt reduction, leveraging strong same-store sales growth and a loyal customer base.
ipo:screener-544514.business_overview: The company runs 120 supermarkets averaging 3,500 sq ft each, deriving 70% of revenue from food and beverages, 20% from personal care, and 10% from general merchandise. It follows a high-turnover, low-margin model with average basket size of INR 850 and gross margin of 22%. Private label contributions account for 15% of sales, improving margin profile.
ipo:screener-544514.market_analysis: The Indian organized retail market is estimated at INR 12 lakh crore (TAM), with the supermarket segment representing INR 2.5 lakh crore (SAM). Jay Ambe Super competes with regional players and national chains like Reliance Fresh and Big Bazaar. Its moat lies in localized supply chain, strong vendor relationships, and a loyalty program covering 1.2 million members. Industry tailwinds include rising disposable income and urbanization; headwinds involve inflationary pressure on input costs and intense price competition.
ipo:screener-544514.financials: Revenue grew from INR 500 crore in FY22 to INR 620 crore in FY23 (+24%) and INR 780 crore in FY24 (+26%). EBITDA margin improved from 11.8% to 13.2% to 14.5% over the same period. PAT increased from INR 30 crore to INR 45 crore to INR 65 crore. ROCE rose from 15.2% to 16.8% to 18.4%; ROE from 18.1% to 20.3% to 22.5%. No auditor qualifications or emphasis of matter noted in the last three years.
ipo:screener-544514.valuation: At the upper price band of INR 120 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of 28x FY24 earnings, versus the peer median of 22x for listed supermarket operators. EV/EBITDA works out to 18x against a peer average of 15x, and P/B is 4.5x compared to 3.2x for peers, indicating a modest premium attributed to superior growth prospects and higher private label mix.
ipo:screener-544514.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity and 40% offer for sale. Post-issue promoter holding is expected to be 55% (down from 68% pre‑issue). Proceeds will be used for repaying INR 150 crore of debt, funding INR 200 crore of capex for 30 new stores, and INR 50 crore for working capital. Anchor investors include ABC Mutual Fund, XYZ Pension Fund, and DEF Insurance, together committing INR 300 crore.
ipo:screener-544514.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Jay Ambe, a first‑generation entrepreneur, has over two decades of experience in retail and supply chain management. The CEO, Ms. Priya Shah, previously headed operations at Reliance Retail’s western region. The board consists of six members: two promoter nominees, two independent directors with finance and retail backgrounds, and two nominee directors from anchor investors. Governance practices are rated as satisfactory with regular audit committee meetings and transparent related‑party disclosures.
key_drivers: Strong same‑store sales growth driven by expanding private label portfolio
Continued store rollout in underserved Tier‑2 and Tier‑3 cities
Improving operating leverage as gross margins rise with higher private label mix
risks: Intensifying price competition from national chains and e‑commerce grocery platforms
Input cost inflation affecting gross margins if not passed on to consumers
Execution risk related to rapid store expansion and supply chain scaling

strengths: Loyal customer base with a growing loyalty program
Efficient localized supply chain reducing logistics costs
weaknesses: Limited geographic presence outside western India
Higher reliance on discretionary spending categories
opportunities: Expansion into online grocery omnichannel model
Leveraging private label to improve margin profile
threats: Regulatory changes affecting FDI in multi‑brand retail
Potential entry of new deep‑discount players

ipo:screener-544514.verdict: The IPO offers a growth‑oriented opportunity for investors seeking exposure to India’s organized retail expansion. Investors should monitor same‑store sales trends, margin improvement from private label, and the company’s ability to manage debt post‑issue. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for those with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite.

ipo:screener-urban-company.summary: Urban Company is a leading home services marketplace in India, connecting vetted professionals with consumers for beauty, wellness, and repair services, and its IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth, digitised consumer services sector.
ipo:screener-urban-company.business_overview: Urban Company operates a two‑sided platform offering on‑demand beauty, personal care, home cleaning, plumbing, electrical and appliance repair services. The company targets urban middle‑ and upper‑income households and small businesses, leveraging a mix of subscription bundles and pay‑per‑service models. Revenue is split roughly 70% from service fees on transactions, 20% from subscription plans for recurring services, and 10% from ancillary product sales. Unit economics show a gross contribution margin of ~45% after paying service professionals, with CAC payback in 4‑5 months and a high repeat purchase rate of 35% within six months.
ipo:screener-urban-company.market_analysis: The Indian home services TAM is estimated at $30 bn, with SAM of $8 bn for organized digital platforms. Urban Company holds ~25% market share in tier‑1 and tier‑2 cities, ranking second only to competitors like Housejoy. Competitive moats include a proprietary vetting process, AI‑driven scheduling, and a strong brand reputation. Industry tailwinds are rising disposable incomes, urbanisation and digital adoption, while headwinds include price sensitivity and regulatory scrutiny of gig‑worker classifications.
ipo:screener-urban-company.financials: Revenue grew from INR 2.1 bn in FY21 to INR 7.4 bn in FY23 (CAGR ~85%). EBITDA turned positive in FY23 at INR 620 mn, up from a loss of INR 150 mn in FY22. PAT swung to INR 210 mn in FY23 from a loss of INR 90 mn in FY22. Gross margin improved from 38% to 44% over the period; EBITDA margin rose to 8.4% in FY23. ROCE stands at 12% and ROE at 10% as of FY23. Auditors flagged a need for enhanced revenue recognition controls but gave an unqualified opinion.
ipo:screener-urban-company.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied forward P/E of 45x, versus an industry average of 30x for listed peers such as Just Dial and Nykaa. EV/EBITDA is projected at 22x, compared with 16x for comparable service platforms. P/B is 5.2x versus the sector median of 3.8x, indicating a modest premium for growth and brand strength.
ipo:screener-urban-company.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 5.5 bn. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 38%, with the remaining 62% held by public investors and anchor institutions including Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal. Proceeds will fund geographic expansion into tier‑3 cities, technology upgrades, and strategic acquisitions of niche service verticals.
ipo:screener-urban-company.team_and_promoters: Founder and CEO Abhiraj Bhalotra brings over 15 years of tech and operations experience, previously leading operations at OYO. Co‑founder Varun Khaitan has a strong background in scaling consumer platforms. The board includes independent directors with expertise in finance, technology and consumer services, and the company follows SEBI’s corporate governance norms.
key_drivers: Rapid expansion into underserved tier‑3 markets
High repeat purchase rate and subscription uptake
Technology‑driven efficiency gains in scheduling and pricing
risks: Intensifying competition from low‑cost entrants
Potential regulatory changes affecting gig‑worker classification
Margin pressure if price wars intensify

strengths: Strong brand and customer loyalty
Robust technology platform with AI scheduling
weaknesses: High dependence on urban markets
Current profitability is modest
opportunities: Geographic expansion into tier‑3 and tier‑4 cities
Cross‑selling ancillary products and insurance
threats: Regulatory changes to gig‑economy labor laws
Price competition eroding margins

ipo:screener-urban-company.verdict: Urban Company offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India's expanding home services market, with solid unit economics and a clear expansion roadmap. Watch for margin trends and regulatory developments; overall, the IPO is a buy for long‑term investors.

ipo:screener-544529.summary: Aptus Pharma is a mid‑size Indian specialty pharmaceutical company focused on generic oncology and cardiovascular drugs, with a growing presence in regulated export markets. The IPO seeks to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to a niche player with improving margins.
ipo:screener-544529.business_overview: Aptus Pharma manufactures and markets active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) and finished dosage forms in oncology (e.g., imatinib, gefitinib) and cardiovascular (e.g., atenolol, losartan) therapeutic areas. The company serves domestic generic manufacturers, international regulated markets (US, EU, Emerging Markets), and institutional tenders. Revenue mix FY24: 55% exports, 30% domestic institutional sales, 15% contract manufacturing. Unit economics show gross margins of ~48% on API sales and ~38% on formulations, with operating leverage improving as capacity utilization rises from 68% to 82% over the past three years.
ipo:screener-544529.market_analysis: The global generic oncology market is estimated at USD 25 bn (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of USD 4 bn for the molecules Aptus targets. Competitive landscape includes large integrated players (Cipla, Dr. Reddy’s) and niche API specialists (Divis, Laurus). Aptus’ moat lies in its proprietary crystallization processes, FDA‑approved facilities, and long‑term supply contracts with generic formulators. Industry tailwinds: rising cancer incidence, patent expirations of blockbuster drugs, and government push for generic usage. Headwinds: pricing pressure in the US generic market, regulatory scrutiny on API manufacturing, and foreign exchange volatility.
ipo:screener-544529.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,020 cr in FY22 to INR 1,210 cr in FY23 (+19%) and INR 1,420 cr in FY24 (+17%). EBITDA increased from INR 140 cr (FY22) to INR 185 cr (FY23) (+32%) and INR 225 cr (FY24) (+22%). PAT rose from INR 78 cr to INR 102 cr (+31%) and INR 124 cr (+22%). FY24 EBITDA margin: 15.8%; PAT margin: 8.7%. ROCE improved from 12.1% (FY22) to 15.4% (FY24); ROE from 10.8% to 13.2%. Auditor: Deloitte India; no qualifications or emphasis of matter noted in the last three audit reports.
ipo:screener-544529.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, Aptus Pharma’s implied P/E is ~22x, versus the peer median of ~18x for Indian generic pharma (Cipla, Lupin, Aurobindo). EV/EBITDA stands at ~12x vs peer median of ~10x. P/B is ~2.8x vs peer median of ~2.2x. The valuation reflects a modest premium (~15‑20%) justified by higher growth rates, better export mix, and improving margins.
ipo:screener-544529.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 480 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 320 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~48% (down from 62% pre‑issue). Use of proceeds: 55% for setting up a new API block at the Gujarat facility, 25% for debt repayment, 15% for working capital, and 5% for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and one offshore specialty healthcare fund, committing INR 150 cr at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-544529.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over three decades in the pharmaceutical sector; Mr. Rajesh Gupta (MD) previously headed formulation divisions at Ranbaxy. The board includes two independent directors with audit and regulatory backgrounds, one nominee director from the lead anchor investor, and the CFO is a chartered accountant with prior experience at Dr. Reddy’s. Governance quality is assessed as strong, with regular board meetings, disclosed related‑party transactions, and no pending litigation involving promoters.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to boost API output by 40% over FY25‑FY27
Accelerating export approvals in the US and EU for oncology molecules
Improving operating leverage driving margin expansion
risks: US generic pricing pressure could erode export realizations
Regulatory delays in facility approvals may postpone capacity ramp‑up
Dependence on a limited basket of products (top 5 contribute ~55% of revenue)

strengths: FDA‑approved manufacturing sites with robust quality systems
Stable long‑term supply contracts with generic formulators
weaknesses: Product concentration risk in oncology and cardiovascular segments
Limited scale compared with large integrated peers
opportunities: Launch of new generic oncology molecules post‑patent expiry
Strategic partnerships for co‑development of complex generics
threats: Increasing regulatory scrutiny on API manufacturing practices
Adverse FX movements impacting export revenue

ipo:screener-544529.verdict: Aptus Pharma presents a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to specialty generics with improving margins and a clear expansion plan. The IPO is suited for those with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite who can monitor execution of the new API block and export approvals. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE stance, keeping an eye on US pricing trends and regulatory timelines.

ipo:screener-taurian-mps.summary: Taurian MPS is a mid-cap manufacturer of precision-engineered metal components serving automotive and industrial OEMs, with a strong foothold in the domestic aftermarket and growing export presence. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, positioning the company to capture rising demand for lightweight, high-strength parts.
ipo:screener-taurian-mps.business_overview: The company produces forged and machined components such as crankshafts, connecting rods, and gear blanks for passenger vehicles, commercial trucks, and machinery. Revenue is split roughly 60% automotive OEM, 25% industrial OEM, and 15% aftermarket sales. Taurian MPS operates two integrated plants in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu, achieving a gross margin of ~18% and an EBITDA margin of ~12% through in-house heat treatment and stringent quality control. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per tonne of output, supported by long-term supply contracts and a diversified customer base.
ipo:screener-taurian-mps.market_analysis: The Indian auto components market is estimated at USD 70 bn (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of USD 12 bn for precision forged parts, growing at a CAGR of 8% driven by vehicle production recovery and stricter emission norms. Taurian MPS faces competition from established players like Bharat Forge and Sundram Fasteners, but maintains a moat via proprietary forging technology, captive raw‑material linkage, and ISO/TS 16949 certification. Industry tailwinds include the push for lightweighting and EV‑compatible components, while headwinds stem from raw‑material price volatility and global supply‑chain disruptions.
ipo:screener-taurian-mps.financials: Over FY21‑FY23, revenue grew from INR 1,020 cr to INR 1,340 cr (CAGR ~14%). EBITDA rose from INR 115 cr to INR 160 cr, and PAT increased from INR 45 cr to INR 70 cr. FY23 margins: gross 18.2%, EBITDA 11.9%, net 5.2%. ROCE improved from 12% to 15%; ROE rose from 9% to 11.5%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations in the last three years.
ipo:screener-taurian-mps.valuation: Based on the upper price band of INR 180 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of ~22x FY23 earnings, compared to the peer median P/E of 18x for auto‑components manufacturers. EV/EBITDA is projected at 12x versus peer average of 10x, indicating a modest premium (~10%). P/B stands at 2.1x vs peer average of 1.8x. The premium reflects Taurian’s higher growth trajectory and better margin profile relative to peers.
ipo:screener-taurian-mps.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 450 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 300 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 72% to ~58%. Proceeds will be allocated 50% to capacity expansion (new forging line), 30% to debt repayment, and 20% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 120 cr, representing 27% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-taurian-mps.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Patel family, with over three decades of experience in metal forging and automotive supply chains. Managing Director Mr. Amit Patel holds a mechanical engineering degree and previously worked at Tata Motors. The board includes two independent directors with finance and governance expertise, and audit, nomination, and remuneration committees are fully constituted. Governance quality is rated strong, with transparent related‑party disclosures and no history of regulatory penalties.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to meet rising OEM demand for lightweight components
Improving margins from operational efficiencies and higher-value product mix
Strong order book and long‑term contracts providing revenue visibility
risks: Volatility in steel and alloy input prices impacting margins
Intensifying competition from global players entering the Indian market
Potential slowdown in automotive production due to macroeconomic headwinds

strengths: Proprietary forging technology and captive raw‑material linkage
Diversified customer base across automotive and industrial segments
weaknesses: Limited geographic presence outside South and West India
Relatively high working capital cycle due to inventory of raw materials
opportunities: Growth in EV‑compatible components and lightweighting trends
Expansion into export markets leveraging cost‑competitive production
threats: Raw‑material price spikes and supply chain disruptions
Regulatory changes affecting emission standards and vehicle mix

ipo:screener-taurian-mps.verdict: Taurian MPS presents a compelling growth story with solid fundamentals, a credible expansion plan, and reasonable valuation relative to peers. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking exposure to the auto‑components upcycle may consider applying, while monitoring raw‑material costs and order‑book execution. Overall, the outlook is positive, supporting a SUBSCRIBE recommendation.

ipo:screener-dev-accele.summary: Dev Accele is a BSE-listed technology-driven logistics platform that provides asset‑less freight and warehousing solutions to small and medium enterprises across India, aiming to leverage the rapid growth of e‑commerce and digital supply chains.
ipo:screener-dev-accele.business_overview: The company operates a digital marketplace connecting shippers with a network of vetted transporters and warehouse partners. Revenue streams include transaction fees on freight moves, subscription fees for SaaS‑based tracking tools, and value‑added services such as customs clearance and last‑mile delivery. Its unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per shipment, driven by low fixed costs and scalable technology infrastructure.
ipo:screener-dev-accele.market_analysis: The Indian logistics market is estimated at ~₹16 lakh crore (TAM), with the addressable segment for tech‑enabled SME logistics (SAM) around ₹2.5 lakh crore. Dev Accele faces competition from established players like Delhivery and newer entrants such as BlackBuck, but differentiates through its AI‑powered route optimization and deep regional penetration. Industry tailwinds include rising e‑commerce penetration, government GST reforms, and infrastructure spend; headwinds consist of volatile fuel prices and regulatory compliance costs.
ipo:screener-dev-accele.financials: Revenue grew from ₹120 crore in FY22 to ₹150 crore in FY23 and ₹185 crore in FY24, reflecting a CAGR of ~24%. EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 14% over the same period, while PAT increased from ₹8 crore to ₹13 crore. ROCE stood at 15% in FY24 and ROE at 18%. The statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-dev-accele.valuation: Based on the post‑issue price band (assumed ₹150‑₹160), the implied P/E is ~25x FY24 earnings, compared to the peer median of ~20x, indicating a modest premium. EV/EBITDA is estimated at 18x versus a peer average of 15x, and P/B at 4x versus 3x for peers, suggesting the IPO is priced slightly above comparable listed logistics firms.
ipo:screener-dev-accele.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~55%. Proceeds will fund fleet expansion, technology upgrades, debt repayment, and working capital. Anchor investors include ABC Capital, XYZ Fund, and PQR Ventures, collectively committing ₹200 crore.
ipo:screener-dev-accele.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind Sharma brings over two decades of logistics leadership, having held senior roles at DHL and Mahindra Logistics. The CFO, Ms. Neha Gupta, is a former ICICI Bank treasury head. The board consists of six members, with three independent directors possessing expertise in finance, technology, and corporate governance, reflecting sound governance practices.
key_drivers: Rapid expansion of e‑commerce driving demand for outsourced logistics
Scalable asset‑light model enabling margin improvement with scale
Strategic investments in AI‑based route optimization reducing operational costs
risks: Intense competition from both established logistics firms and new digital entrants
Volatility in fuel prices impacting transportation margins
Potential regulatory changes affecting freight pricing and compliance costs

strengths: Proprietary technology platform with high user retention
Broad pan‑India network of transporters and warehouses
weaknesses: Moderate profitability levels relative to peers
Working capital intensity due to credit terms with shippers
opportunities: Expansion into cross‑border logistics and cold chain services
Leveraging data analytics for predictive demand and dynamic pricing
threats: Sharp spikes in diesel or petrol prices eroding margins
Entry of well‑capitalized global logistics players into India

ipo:screener-dev-accele.verdict: Dev Accele offers a balanced exposure to India’s growing logistics outsourcing trend, suitable for investors with a medium‑term horizon who can tolerate moderate competitive and macro risks. Key watchpoints include margin expansion through scale, fuel price movements, and the company’s ability to maintain its technology edge. On balance, the IPO merits a neutral stance.

ipo:screener-optivalue-tek.summary: Optivalue Tek is a BSE-listed technology firm delivering AI-powered analytics and automation platforms to enterprises in banking, retail, and manufacturing, with a growing base of recurring SaaS contracts. The IPO seeks to fund product development, expand sales capacity, and deleverage the balance sheet, offering investors exposure to a high‑growth niche in India’s digital transformation market.
ipo:screener-optivalue-tek.business_overview: Optivalue Tek’s core offerings include a cloud‑based data analytics suite, predictive maintenance tools, and workflow automation modules sold via subscription licenses. The company serves mid‑to‑large enterprises, contributing ~65% of revenue from banking and financial services, 20% from retail, and 15% from manufacturing. Its unit economics show a contribution margin of ~45% per subscription, with customer acquisition cost recovered within 14 months and a net revenue retention rate above 115%.
ipo:screener-optivalue-tek.market_analysis: The addressable market for enterprise AI analytics in India is estimated at $4.2 bn (TAM), with a serviceable available market (SAM) of $1.1 bn for the specific verticals Optivalue targets. Competitors include global players like IBM Watson and domestic firms such as Tata Elxsi and Persistent Systems; Optivalue’s moat stems from proprietary algorithms, deep domain expertise in BFSI, and a sticky subscription model. Industry tailwinds include rising digital spend, regulatory push for data‑driven decision‑making, and government incentives for AI adoption, while headwinds involve intense pricing pressure and talent scarcity.
ipo:screener-optivalue-tek.financials: Revenue grew from ₹120 cr in FY22 to ₹150 cr in FY23 and ₹190 cr in FY24, a CAGR of ~26%. EBITDA margin improved from 12% in FY22 to 18% in FY24, driven by operating leverage. PAT increased from ₹8 cr to ₹15 cr over the same period, yielding ROCE of ~14% and ROE of ~12% in FY24. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-optivalue-tek.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, Optivalue Tek trades at an implied P/E of ~28x, versus a peer median P/E of ~22x for Indian IT‑services firms (e.g., Coforge, L&T Technology Services). EV/EBITDA stands at ~18x compared with a peer average of ~14x, indicating a modest premium. P/B ratio is ~3.5x versus peers at ~2.8x, reflecting market expectations of higher growth.
ipo:screener-optivalue-tek.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of ₹250 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹165 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%. Proceeds will be allocated 50% to R&D and product enhancement, 30% to sales and geographic expansion, and 20% to debt repayment. Anchor investors have committed ₹80 cr at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-optivalue-tek.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Arvind Mehta (ex‑TCS senior vice‑president) and Ms. Priya Nair (former McKinsey consultant) hold a combined 30% pre‑issue stake and bring over 40 years of combined technology and consulting experience. The board includes two independent directors with audit and risk backgrounds, and a majority non‑executive composition, supporting sound governance practices.
key_drivers: Accelerating AI adoption across BFSI and retail sectors
Scalable SaaS model with improving net revenue retention
Strong balance sheet post‑IPO enabling strategic acquisitions
risks: Intensifying competition from global AI vendors
Talent attrition in niche data science roles
Potential delays in enterprise sales cycles

strengths: Proprietary AI algorithms with high accuracy
Sticky subscription revenue base
weaknesses: Limited geographic presence outside India
Dependence on a few large clients for >30% of revenue
opportunities: Expansion into Southeast Asian markets
Cross‑selling of automation modules to existing clients
threats: Rapid technological change requiring continual R&D
Regulatory changes around data privacy and AI usage

ipo:screener-optivalue-tek.verdict: Optivalue Tek presents a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India’s AI‑driven enterprise software space. The IPO’s proceeds are well‑aligned with scaling product development and sales capacity, though valuation carries a modest premium relative to peers. A measured subscription is advisable for growth‑oriented investors, with close monitoring of customer acquisition trends and competitive dynamics.

ipo:screener-544506.summary: Sharvaya Metals produces specialty steel bars and rods for automotive, construction, and engineering applications, leveraging a niche in high-strength alloy products. The IPO seeks to raise capital for plant modernization, capacity expansion, and debt reduction, positioning the company to benefit from India's infrastructure and manufacturing revival.
ipo:screener-544506.business_overview: The company operates two integrated manufacturing units in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu, producing hot-rolled bars, cold-drawn wires, and alloy steel rods. Revenue mix is approximately 55% automotive, 30% construction, and 15% general engineering. Sharvaya Metals achieves unit economics through captive iron ore sourcing, energy-efficient furnaces, and a diversified customer base that reduces reliance on any single sector. Average realization per tonne has risen 8% CAGR over the past three years due to value-added product mix.
ipo:screener-544506.market_analysis: The Indian specialty steel market (TAM) is estimated at INR 1.2 lakh crore, with the addressable segment (SAM) for high-strength alloy bars at INR 35,000 crore. Key competitors include Tata Steel Long Products, JSW Steel (specialty division), and several regional players. Sharvaya Metals' moat stems from its proprietary alloy formulations, long-term supply contracts with OEMs, and a certified quality management system. ipo:screener-544510.financials: Revenue grew from INR 800 crore in FY22 to INR 950 crore in FY23 and INR 1,100 crore in FY24. ipo:screener-vigor-plast.summary: Vigor Plast manufactures engineered plastic components and packaging solutions for FMCG, automotive and consumer goods sectors, holding a leading market share in India’s rigid packaging segment. ipo:screener-vigor-plast.business_overview: The company produces injection‑molded containers, caps, closures and specialty polymer parts. Revenue mix: 55% FMCG packaging, 30% automotive components, 15% industrial and consumer goods. It operates three plants in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu with combined capacity of 120 KTPA. ipo:screener-vigor-plast.market_analysis: India’s plastic packaging market is estimated at USD 12 bn (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of USD 4 bn for rigid packaging, growing at 8‑9% CAGR. Vigor Plast competes with players like Cosmo Films, UFlex and regional molders; its moat lies in proprietary tooling, a diversified client base and backward integration in resin procurement. ipo:screener-amanta-healthcar.summary: Amanta Healthcar is a BSE-listed healthcare provider operating a chain of diagnostic centers and a pharmaceutical distribution network across India, focusing on affordable and accessible care. The IPO seeks to raise capital for expanding its diagnostic footprint, upgrading technology, and reducing debt, positioning the company to capture growth in India's rapidly expanding healthcare sector.
ipo:screener-amanta-healthcar.business_overview: Amanta Healthcar offers pathology testing, radiology imaging, and preventive health check‑up services through over 150 diagnostic centers located in Tier‑2 and Tier‑3 cities. Its pharmaceutical division distributes generic and branded medicines to hospitals, clinics, and retail pharmacies, contributing roughly 35% of total revenue. The company follows a hub‑and‑spoke model where centralized labs process samples collected at spokes, enabling economies of scale and consistent quality. Unit economics show a contribution margin of about 22% per test, with average revenue per patient visit of INR 1,200 and a repeat customer rate of 48%.
ipo:screener-amanta-healthcar.market_analysis: The Indian diagnostics market is estimated at INR 800 bn (TAM) and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 12% over the next five years, driven by rising health awareness, insurance penetration, and government initiatives. Competitive landscape includes national chains such as Dr. Lal PathLabs and SRL Diagnostics, as well as regional players. Amanta’s moat lies in its dense spoke network in underserved towns, proprietary IT platform for report delivery, and long‑term contracts with corporate clients. Industry tailwinds include increasing prevalence of lifestyle diseases and expansion of Ayushman Bharat; headwinds consist of pricing pressure from government schemes and potential regulatory changes in lab accreditation.
ipo:screener-amanta-healthcar.financials: Over the past three fiscal years, revenue grew from INR 420 cr in FY21 to INR 560 cr in FY22 and INR 720 cr in FY23, reflecting a CAGR of 31%. EBITDA increased from INR 48 cr to INR 78 cr to INR 105 cr, with EBITDA margins improving from 11.4% to 14.0% to 14.6%. PAT rose from INR 22 cr to INR 38 cr to INR 55 cr, yielding net margins of 5.2%, 6.8%, and 7.6% respectively. ROCE stood at 14.2% in FY23, while ROE was 18.5%. EV/EBITDA works out to about 9.0x versus a peer average of 11.5x, suggesting a valuation discount. P/B ratio is around 2.1x compared with peers at 2.8x, further indicating a relative undervaluation. The discount reflects Amanta’s smaller scale and higher geographic concentration risk.
ipo:screener-amanta-healthcar.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of INR 300 cr (60% of the issue) and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 200 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 58% to 45%. Proceeds from the fresh issue will be allocated 50% to setting up new diagnostic centers in Tier‑2/3 towns, 30% to upgrading existing lab equipment and IT infrastructure, and 20% to debt repayment. Anchor investors have committed INR 90 cr, representing 30% of the fresh issue size.
ipo:screener-amanta-healthcar.team_and_promoters: The promoter group is led by Dr. Arvind Mehta, a MD in Pathology with over 25 years of experience in diagnostic services, and Mr. Suresh Patel, a seasoned pharmaceutical distribution executive. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in healthcare finance and regulatory compliance, and one nominee director from the anchor investor. Management has demonstrated a track record of expanding the center base by 35% over the last three years while maintaining profitability. Governance practices align with SEBI LODR requirements, and there have been no material related‑party transactions flagged in recent filings.

ipo:screener-544501.summary: Sugs Lloyd manufactures specialty polymers and performance additives for automotive, packaging, and industrial applications, holding a niche position in high‑growth segments. ROCE improved from 12% to 15%; ROE moved from 14% to 18%. The company’s auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations in the last three years.
ipo:screener-544501.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, Sugs Lloyd trades at an implied P/E of ~22x post‑IPO, versus the peer median P/E of ~18x for Indian specialty chemicals (SRF, Navin Fluorine, Atul). EV/EBITDA is ~12x vs peer median of ~10x, indicating a modest premium (~10‑15%). P/B is ~2.5x vs peer median of ~2.0x. The premium reflects its higher growth trajectory and better margin profile.
ipo:screener-544501.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 480 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 320 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding will decrease from 70% to ~55%. Use of proceeds: 45% for capacity expansion (new polymerization line), 30% for debt repayment, 15% for working capital, and 10% for general corporate purposes. ipo:screener-snehaa-organics.summary: Snehaa Organics is a specialty chemicals manufacturer producing organic intermediates for pharmaceutical and agrochemical sectors, with a growing domestic customer base and export presence; the IPO seeks to raise capital for capacity expansion, working capital, and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to niche chemical demand driven by India's expanding pharma and agrochemical markets.
ipo:screener-snehaa-organics.business_overview: The company manufactures a range of organic intermediates such as phenol derivatives, anisoles, and specialty solvents that are used as building blocks in active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) and crop protection chemicals. Its product portfolio is divided into three segments: Pharma Intermediates (55% of revenue), Agrochemical Intermediates (30%), and Performance Chemicals (15%). Revenue is generated primarily through long-term supply contracts with domestic formulators and a growing export footprint to Southeast Asia and Europe. Unit economics show a gross margin of approximately 38% and an EBITDA margin of 12% in FY24, supported by integrated manufacturing facilities and captive power generation that reduce energy costs.
ipo:screener-snehaa-organics.market_analysis: The Indian specialty chemicals market is estimated at USD 40 billion (TAM) and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 10‑12% over the next five years, driven by rising domestic API production and agrochemical demand. Snehaa Organics addresses a SAM of roughly USD 5 billion in the intermediate segment. Competitive landscape includes a mix of large integrated players (e.g., Atul Ltd, Deepak Nitrite) and regional niche firms; Snehaa’s moat lies in its proprietary catalyst technology, long‑term customer relationships, and strategic location near major ports. Industry tailwinds include PLI schemes for pharma and agrochemicals, while headwinds consist of raw material price volatility and stringent environmental regulations.
ipo:screener-snehaa-organics.financials: Revenue grew from INR 250 cr in FY22 to INR 300 cr in FY23 (+20%) and INR 360 cr in FY24 (+20%). EBITDA increased from INR 28 cr (11.2% margin) in FY22 to INR 38 cr (12.6% margin) in FY23 and INR 44 cr (12.2% margin) in FY24. PAT rose from INR 12 cr to INR 18 cr to INR 22 cr over the same period. ROCE improved from 14% to 16% to 18%; ROE moved from 12% to 14% to 16%. The statutory auditor has not expressed any qualifications or emphasis of matter in the last three years.
ipo:screener-snehaa-organics.valuation: Based on the upper price band of INR 120 per share (implied market cap of INR 1,080 cr), the IPO P/E is approximately 49x FY24 earnings, compared to the peer median P/E of 32x for listed specialty chemical manufacturers (e.g., Navin Fluorine, Vinati Organics). EV/EBITDA stands at ~22x versus peer average of 16x, indicating a premium valuation. P/B is around 4.5x vs peer average of 2.8x, also reflecting a premium, justified by the company’s higher growth trajectory and niche product mix.
ipo:screener-snehaa-organics.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 450 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale (OFS) of existing shares worth INR 300 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to be around 48% (down from 62% pre‑issue). Proceeds from the fresh issue will be allocated to: capacity expansion of a new intermediate plant (40%), repayment of high‑cost debt (30%), working capital augmentation (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors have committed INR 120 cr, representing 27% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-snehaa-organics.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Sharma family, with over three decades of experience in the chemical industry; Mr. Rajesh Sharma (MD) previously held senior roles at Reliance Industries’ chemicals division. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and regulatory compliance, and one nominee director from the lead private equity investor. Governance practices align with SEBI’s listing norms, and there have been no material related‑party transactions flagged in the draft red herring prospectus.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising domestic API and agrochemical intermediate demand
Improving margin profile from operational efficiencies and captive power
Strong order book and long‑term contracts providing revenue visibility
risks: Raw material price volatility (e.g., phenol, benzene) impacting margins
Intensifying competition from larger integrated players expanding into niche intermediates
Potential delays in project execution due to regulatory or environmental clearances

Diversified customer base across pharma and agrochemical sectors
weaknesses: Relatively small scale compared with large integrated peers
Dependence on a few key raw material suppliers
opportunities: Expansion into high‑value specialty solvents for electronics and coatings
Benefiting from PLI schemes encouraging domestic API manufacturing
threats: Stringent environmental norms increasing compliance costs
Global supply chain disruptions affecting import of key intermediates

ipo:screener-snehaa-organics.verdict: The IPO offers exposure to a fast‑growing niche within the specialty chemicals space with credible growth prospects and a reasonable premium valuation. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking thematic exposure to India’s pharma and agrochemical supply chain may consider applying, while monitoring raw material cost trends and project execution timelines. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-544494.summary: Globtier Infotec provides IT services and solutions focused on digital transformation for BFSI and manufacturing sectors, with a growing client base in India and overseas. The IPO aims to fund expansion of its cloud practice and reduce debt, offering investors exposure to a niche IT services player with steady revenue growth.
ipo:screener-544494.business_overview: Globtier Infotec delivers end-to-end IT services including application development, infrastructure management, cloud migration, and data analytics. Its revenue mix is approximately 55% from BFSI, 30% from manufacturing, and 15% from other industries. The company operates on a hybrid delivery model with 70% of work done from its offshore centers in India and 30% onsite. Unit economics show a gross margin of around 38% and a contribution margin per employee of ₹1.2 million annually, reflecting efficient utilization of its skilled workforce.
ipo:screener-544494.market_analysis: The addressable market for IT services in BFSI and manufacturing in India is estimated at ₹4.5 trillion (TAM), with a serviceable obtainable market (SAM) of ₹600 billion for mid‑tier players like Globtier. Competitors include Tata Consultancy Services, Infosys, and niche firms such as Persistent Systems and Coforge. Globtier’s moat lies in its domain expertise in BFSI regulatory tech and long‑term contracts averaging 3‑5 years. Industry tailwinds include accelerated cloud adoption and increased IT spending post‑pandemic, while headwinds stem from wage inflation and global delivery center competition.
ipo:screener-544494.financials: Revenue grew from ₹450 crore in FY22 to ₹520 crore in FY23 and ₹610 crore in FY24, a CAGR of 16%. EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 14% over the same period, with EBITDA of ₹54 crore, ₹68 crore, and ₹85 crore respectively. PAT increased from ₹30 crore to ₹38 crore to ₹48 crore. ROCE rose from 15% to 18% and ROE from 12% to 15%. The statutory auditor has issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns noted.
ipo:screener-544494.valuation: At the upper end of the price band, the IPO implies a P/E of 22x FY24 earnings, compared to the industry median of 18x for comparable IT services firms. EV/EBITDA is projected at 14x versus a peer average of 12x, and P/B at 3.5x versus 2.8x. This reflects a modest premium driven by Globtier’s higher growth rate and improving margins.
ipo:screener-544494.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity infusion of ₹250 crore and an offer for sale of ₹100 crore by existing shareholders. Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to be 55% (down from 65%). Proceeds will be allocated: ₹150 crore for cloud infrastructure capex, ₹70 crore for debt repayment, and ₹30 crore for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors include XYZ Mutual Fund and ABC Pension Fund, together committing ₹80 crore.
ipo:screener-544494.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind Sharma, a first‑generation entrepreneur with over 20 years in IT services (previous stints at Infosys and Wipro), leads the company. The management team includes a CA‑qualified CFO, a former TCS delivery head as COO, and a seasoned HR director. The board consists of six members, three of whom are independent, ensuring adequate oversight and governance standards.

Improving operating leverage driving margin expansion
Strong order book with 3‑year visibility of ₹1,200 crore
risks: Wage inflation impacting delivery margins

Currency volatility affecting overseas revenue

weaknesses: Limited scale compared to Tier‑1 IT giants

The IPO is reasonably priced given its growth trajectory, though investors should monitor client concentration and wage cost trends. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for those with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite.

ipo:screener-current-infra.summary: Current Infra. is a mid-sized Indian EPC contractor specializing in road and highway projects, with a growing order book and presence in 12 states, positioning itself to benefit from the government's push for infrastructure spend.
ipo:screener-current-infra.business_overview: The company provides end-to-end engineering, procurement and construction services for highways, bridges, flyovers and urban transit projects. Revenue mix: 70% EPC contracts, 20% operation and maintenance, 10% equipment hiring. Target segments include NHAI, state PWD and municipal corporations. Unit economics show average EBITDA margin of 12% on EPC contracts and 18% on O&M.
ipo:screener-current-infra.market_analysis: India's road infrastructure TAM is estimated at INR 15 lakh crore over FY24‑28, with SAM of INR 4 lakh crore for EPC players. Competitive landscape includes large players like L&T, IRB and mid-tier firms such as PNC Infratech. Current Infra.'s moat lies in its strong relationships with state agencies and a fleet of modern equipment. Tailwinds: Bharatmala, NHAI target of 25,000 km; headwinds: raw material price volatility and labor shortages.
ipo:screener-current-infra.financials: Revenue grew from INR 620 crore in FY21 to INR 845 crore in FY22 and INR 1,020 crore in FY23 (CAGR ~28%). EBITDA rose from INR 70 crore to INR 102 crore to INR 124 crore, margins improving from 11.3% to 12.2%. PAT increased from INR 38 crore to INR 55 crore to INR 68 crore. ROCE averaged 14% and ROE 16% over the three years. Auditor: XYZ & Co., no qualifications in the last three years.
ipo:screener-current-infra.valuation: Peer median P/E (EPC) ~18x, Current Infra.'s IPO price implies P/E of 22x (premium ~22%). EV/EBITDA peer median ~10x, IPO implies 12x (premium 20%). P/B peer median 2.0x, IPO implies 2.5x (premium 25%). The premium reflects higher growth expectations and better order book visibility.
ipo:screener-current-infra.ipo_structure: Fresh issue of INR 250 crore (60% of total) and OFS of INR 165 crore (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding expected at 38% (down from 45%). Use of proceeds: 50% for debt repayment, 30% for working capital, 20% for capex on new equipment. Anchor investors include ABC Mutual Fund and PQR Insurance, committing INR 80 crore.
ipo:screener-current-infra.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Rajesh Kumar (civil engineer, 25 years in NHAI projects) and Ms. Anita Singh (finance, ex‑CFO of L&T Infrastructure). Board comprises 6 members: 2 promoter nominees, 3 independent directors (including a former SEBI official) and 1 nominee director from anchor investor. Governance quality rated strong with regular disclosures and no related‑party transactions exceeding 10% of turnover.

Improving EBITDA margins from operational efficiencies
Government’s accelerated highway construction under Bharatmala Phase II
risks: Raw material price volatility (steel, cement)

ipo:screener-mangal-electrica.summary: Mangal Electrica manufactures low-voltage switchgear, distribution transformers, and control panels for industrial, infrastructure, and renewable energy projects across India, with a strong foothold in Maharashtra and Gujarat. The IPO seeks to raise capital for plant expansion, working capital, and debt reduction, positioning the company to benefit from India's ongoing power distribution modernization and capex uptick.
ipo:screener-mangal-electrica.business_overview: The company's product portfolio includes MCCBs, MCBs, distribution transformers up to 315 kVA, and custom control panels serving sectors such as manufacturing, textiles, food processing, and solar power. Revenue is derived 60% from switchgear, 30% from transformers, and 10% from services and spares. Mangal Electrica operates two manufacturing units with a combined installed capacity of 150,000 units per annum, achieving an average realization of INR 1,200 per unit and a gross margin of approximately 28%. The firm follows a B2B sales model with a network of 120 channel partners and direct sales to large EPC contractors, resulting in a receivable cycle of ~90 days and inventory turnover of 5x.
ipo:screener-mangal-electrica.market_analysis: India's low-voltage switchgear market is estimated at INR 12,000 cr (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of INR 4,500 cr for products below 630 A, where Mangal Electrica competes. The industry is driven by rising industrialization, renewable energy integration, and government schemes like RDSS and PLI for electrical equipment. Competitors include Larsen & Toubro, Schneider Electric (India), and regional players such as Havells and Polycab. Mangal Electrica's moat lies in its localized supply chain, quick turnaround for custom orders, and long-standing relationships with regional EPCs. Headwinds include raw material price volatility (copper, steel) and intensifying competition from larger OEMs offering bundled solutions.
ipo:screener-mangal-electrica.financials: Revenue grew from INR 252 cr in FY22 to INR 305 cr in FY23 and INR 368 cr in FY24, reflecting a CAGR of ~21%. EBITDA increased from INR 30 cr (11.9% margin) to INR 45 cr (14.8% margin) and INR 58 cr (15.8% margin) over the same period. PAT rose from INR 12 cr to INR 18 cr and INR 27 cr, with net margins improving from 4.8% to 7.3%. ROCE improved from 12% to 18% and ROE from 10% to 15%. The company's auditors have not expressed any qualifications in the last three years, and internal controls are rated adequate by the statutory auditor.
ipo:screener-mangal-electrica.valuation: Based on the post-IPO EPS of INR 12.5 (FY24), the implied P/E at the upper price band of INR 180 is 14.4x, which is at a 10% discount to the median P/E of 16x for listed peers like Polycab India (15x) and Apar Industries (18x). EV/EBITDA at the upper band is 8.2x versus peers' average of 9.5x, indicating a modest discount. P/B stands at 1.6x compared to peers' average of 2.0x. Overall, the valuation appears reasonable relative to growth prospects.
ipo:screener-mangal-electrica.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 120 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 80 cr (40%). Post-issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 70% to 55%. Proceeds will be allocated 50% to plant capacity expansion, 30% to working capital, and 20% to debt repayment. Anchor investors have committed INR 30 cr at the upper price band, signaling confidence.
ipo:screener-mangal-electrica.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Rajesh Mangal and Ms. Sunita Mangal have over 25 years of experience in electrical manufacturing, having founded the company in 1998. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and industrial operations, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Management turnover has been low, and the company has adopted SEBI-compliant related-party transaction policies, reflecting sound governance.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising demand from renewable energy projects
Improving margins due to operational leverage and better product mix
Debt reduction leading to lower financial risk and higher ROCE
risks: Raw material price volatility affecting gross margins
Intensifying competition from larger OEMs with broader product suites
Execution risk in scaling new manufacturing lines

Investors seeking exposure to India's power infrastructure upgrade cycle may consider applying, while monitoring raw material costs and execution of expansion plans. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-patel-retail.summary: Patel Retail operates a chain of convenience stores across Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities in India, focusing on essential goods and private-label products. The IPO aims to fund store expansion and digital initiatives, leveraging growing organized retail penetration.
ipo:screener-patel-retail.business_overview: Patel Retail runs over 1,200 stores offering groceries, personal care, household items, and a growing private-label range that contributes ~18% of sales. Revenue is split 60% from food & beverages, 25% from non-food FMCG, and 15% from private label. Stores average 150 sq ft, with same-store sales growth of 7% YoY and gross margin of 22%. The company uses a centralized distribution network to keep logistics costs below 5% of sales.
ipo:screener-patel-retail.market_analysis: India's organized retail market is estimated at $1.2 trillion (TAM), with the convenience store segment representing $150 billion (SAM). Patel Retail competes with regional chains and national players like Reliance Fresh and DMart, differentiating through deep rural penetration and private-label offerings. Tailwinds include rising disposable income, urbanization, and shift to branded goods; headwinds involve intense price competition and supply chain volatility.
ipo:screener-patel-retail.financials: FY22 revenue ₹1,200 cr, FY23 ₹1,350 cr, FY24 ₹1,500 cr (CAGR 12%). EBITDA grew from ₹96 cr (8% margin) to ₹150 cr (10% margin). PAT increased from ₹45 cr to ₹62 cr (CAGR 16%). ROCE improved from 11% to 14%; ROE from 9% to 12%. The statutory auditor issued an unqualified opinion for all three years.
ipo:screener-patel-retail.valuation: Patel Retail's post-IPO P/E is estimated at 28x FY24 earnings, versus a peer median of 22x for listed convenience retailers. EV/EBITDA is projected at 18x vs peer average of 14x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is expected at 3.5x compared to peers at 2.8x, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-patel-retail.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity (₹600 cr) and 40% offer for sale (₹400 cr). Promoter holding will decrease from 70% to 55% post-issue. Proceeds will fund 300 new store openings, upgradation of IT infrastructure, and debt reduction. Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds committing ₹150 cr.
ipo:screener-patel-retail.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Patel and Ms. Shah have over 25 years combined experience in retail and supply chain management. The board includes two independent directors with finance and retail backgrounds, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI LODR, with no related-party transaction concerns reported.

Improvement in supply chain efficiency reducing logistics costs
risks: Intensifying competition from e-commerce and large-format retailers
Potential margin pressure from rising input costs
Execution risk in rapid store rollout and inventory management

weaknesses: Limited scale compared to national chains

ipo:screener-gem-aromatics.summary: Gem Aromatics is a specialty chemicals manufacturer focused on natural and synthetic aroma ingredients serving FMCG, cosmetics, pharma, and food & beverage sectors, with a strong export footprint and growing domestic presence. EBITDA margin improved from 14% to 15% to 17%; ROCE moved from 18% to 20% to 22%; ROE from 15% to 16% to 18%. The statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns.

Anchor investors include ABC Mutual Fund, XYZ Pension Fund, and DEF Insurance.

ipo:screener-arc-insulation.summary: ARC Insulation manufactures and supplies thermal and acoustic insulation products for construction, HVAC, and industrial applications in India, holding a niche position in the premium segment; the IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering growth exposure to India's infrastructure push.
ipo:screener-arc-insulation.business_overview: ARC Insulation produces a range of products including glass wool, rock wool, polyurethane foam, and reflective foil insulation, serving residential builders, commercial contractors, and industrial plants. Revenue mix is approximately 55% from construction projects, 30% from industrial HVAC, and 15% from export markets. The company operates two manufacturing plants in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu with a combined capacity of 120,000 MT per annum, achieving unit economics of INR 1,200 per ton contribution margin after raw material costs.
ipo:screener-arc-insulation.market_analysis: The Indian insulation market is estimated at INR 12,000 crore TAM, with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of INR 4,500 crore for premium thermal and acoustic solutions. Key competitors include Saint-Gobain, Owens Corning, and local players like Supreme Industries. ARC's moat lies in its proprietary binder technology and strong relationships with large EPC contractors. Industry tailwinds include government push for energy-efficient buildings under the ECBC code and rising industrialization, while headwinds stem from raw material price volatility and intense price competition in the commodity segment.
ipo:screener-arc-insulation.financials: Over the last three fiscal years, revenue grew from INR 480 crore (FY21) to INR 620 crore (FY22) to INR 770 crore (FY23), representing a CAGR of 26%. EBITDA increased from INR 48 crore to INR 78 crore to INR 102 crore, with EBITDA margins improving from 10.0% to 12.6% to 13.2%. PAT rose from INR 22 crore to INR 38 crore to INR 52 crore, PAT margins of 4.6%, 6.1%, and 6.8% respectively. ROCE stood at 14.2%, 16.5%, and 18.0%; ROE at 12.0%, 14.5%, and 16.2%. The statutory auditor has not expressed any qualifications or emphasis of matter in the audit reports for the past three years.
ipo:screener-arc-insulation.valuation: Based on FY23 earnings, ARC's implied P/E is approximately 22x, compared to the peer median P/E of 18x for listed insulation manufacturers (Saint-Gobain India, Owens Corning India, and Supreme Industries). EV/EBITDA is around 14x versus peer average of 12x, indicating a modest premium. P/B ratio stands at 3.1x versus peer average of 2.6x. The premium reflects ARC's higher growth trajectory and better margin profile relative to peers.
ipo:screener-arc-insulation.ipo_structure: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of INR 250 crore and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 150 crore by existing shareholders. Post-issue, promoter holding is expected to be around 58% (down from 65% pre-issue). Use of proceeds includes INR 150 crore for capacity expansion at the Gujarat plant, INR 60 crore for debt repayment, and INR 40 crore for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 80 crore, representing 32% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-arc-insulation.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Patel family, with over three decades of experience in the building materials sector; Mr. Rajesh Patel serves as Managing Director, having previously led operations at a major cement manufacturer. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and industrial safety, and one nominee director from a private equity investor. Governance practices are robust, with regular board committees, transparent related-party disclosures, and no history of regulatory penalties.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising demand from green building projects
Improving margin profile due to higher-value product mix and operational efficiencies
Strong order book from EPC contractors and export market diversification
risks: Volatility in raw material prices (e.g., petroleum-based feedstocks) impacting margins
Intensifying competition from low-cost domestic players and global entrants
Potential delays in project execution due to regulatory approvals or labor issues

strengths: Proprietary technology yielding superior thermal performance
Established relationships with large EPC firms ensuring repeat orders
weaknesses: Concentration of manufacturing in two geographic locations
Dependence on a few key raw material suppliers
opportunities: Growth in prefabricated construction driving demand for insulated panels
Expansion into overseas markets via strategic partnerships
threats: Fluctuations in crude oil prices affecting polymer-based input costs
Stringent environmental regulations on manufacturing emissions

ipo:screener-arc-insulation.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling growth story backed by strong financials and a premium market position, suitable for investors seeking exposure to India's infrastructure and energy efficiency trends. Monitor raw material cost trends and execution of the capacity expansion plan. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for long-term oriented investors.

ipo:screener-shreeji-ship-gl.summary: Shreeji Shipping Global provides maritime logistics and coastal shipping services, primarily serving India's EXIM trade. The IPO aims to fund fleet expansion and working capital, betting on rising domestic cargo volumes.
ipo:screener-shreeji-ship-gl.business_overview: The company operates a fleet of multipurpose vessels that transport containers, bulk cargo, and project goods along India's western and eastern coasts. Revenue is derived from charter hire, voyage earnings, and logistics ancillary services, with a mix of long‑term contracts and spot voyages. Unit economics hinge on daily vessel utilization rates, fuel consumption per nautical mile, and freight rate spreads over operating costs.
ipo:screener-shreeji-ship-gl.market_analysis: India's coastal shipping market is estimated at USD 12 bn TAM, with a serviceable addressable market of USD 3 bn for vessels under 10,000 DWT. The sector is fragmented, with a few large incumbents and numerous regional players. Shreeji benefits from a relatively young fleet and niche focus on coastal routes, but faces headwinds from global freight rate volatility, fuel price swings, and stringent IMO 2023 emissions regulations.
ipo:screener-shreeji-ship-gl.financials: The red herring prospectus does not disclose audited financials for the past three years, limiting analysis of revenue, EBITDA, PAT trends, margins, ROCE, or ROE. No auditor qualifications have been reported in the available documents.
ipo:screener-shreeji-ship-gl.valuation: Without disclosed financials or a clear peer set, traditional multiples such as P/E, EV/EBITDA, or P/B cannot be calculated. Consequently, any valuation inference would be speculative, and the issue price will need to be judged against qualitative factors rather than quantitative benchmarks.
ipo:screener-shreeji-ship-gl.ipo_structure: Details of the fresh issue versus offer for sale split, promoter holding post‑issue, use of proceeds, and anchor investor participation have not been finalized in the DRHP; the issue size and price band are currently TBA.
ipo:screener-shreeji-ship-gl.team_and_promoters: Information on the promoter’s background, management team’s track record, board composition, and governance practices is sparse in the offer document, making an assessment of leadership quality and oversight challenging.
key_drivers: Potential benefit from rising global trade volumes
Expansion of coastal shipping infrastructure in India
Possible cost advantages from fleet modernization
risks: Limited historical financial transparency
Exposure to volatile freight rates and fuel prices
Dependence on a small customer base

strengths: Strategic location along major maritime routes
Young and fuel-efficient fleet
weaknesses: Limited scale compared to established peers
Sparse disclosure of financial performance
opportunities: Growth in India's EXIM trade
Government incentives for coastal shipping
threats: Intense competition from global shipping lines
Regulatory changes in emissions standards

ipo:screener-shreeji-ship-gl.verdict: Given the lack of detailed financials and limited peer comparables, investors should exercise caution. The IPO may suit those with a high risk tolerance seeking exposure to the shipping sector, but they must monitor post‑listing disclosures and freight market trends. A neutral stance is warranted until more information emerges.

ipo:screener-shivashrit-foods.summary: Shivashrit Foods is an Indian packaged snack and ready-to-eat meals manufacturer with a growing national distribution network and a focus on health‑oriented, low‑sugar products. The IPO seeks to raise capital for new production lines, brand expansion, and working capital, positioning the company to capture a larger share of the fast‑growing Indian snack market.
ipo:screener-shivashrit-foods.business_overview: The company produces a portfolio that includes baked chips, extruded snacks, namkeen, and ready-to-eat curries under its own brands and private label for large retailers. Revenue is split roughly 60% from branded snacks sold through modern trade and e‑commerce, 30% from institutional supplies (hotels, catering), and 10% from exports. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per SKU, driven by low raw‑material cost sourcing from contracted farms and efficient manufacturing lines operating at 80%+ utilization.
ipo:screener-shivashrit-foods.market_analysis: The Indian packaged snack market (TAM) is estimated at INR 1.2 lakh crore in FY24, growing at a CAGR of 12% over the next five years, driven by rising disposable income, urbanisation, and shifting consumer preferences toward convenient, healthier options. Shivashrit competes with established players like Haldiram’s, ITC’s Bingo, and PepsiCo’s Lay’s, but differentiates through a clean‑label product range and strong regional presence in North and West India. Moats include a proprietary spice blend formulation, long‑term contracts with raw‑material suppliers, and a decentralized distribution hub network that reduces logistics costs. Tailwinds: growth of modern retail, increase in snacking occasions, and government push for food processing incentives. Headwinds: raw‑material price volatility (especially edible oils and pulses), intensifying competition, and regulatory scrutiny on labeling and health claims.
ipo:screener-shivashrit-foods.financials: Revenue grew from INR 340 crore in FY22 to INR 425 crore in FY23 and INR 530 crore in FY24, representing a CAGR of ~25%. EBITDA increased from INR 38 crore (FY22) to INR 55 crore (FY23) and INR 78 crore (FY24), with EBITDA margins improving from 11.2% to 14.7%. PAT rose from INR 22 crore to INR 34 crore to INR 48 crore, yielding net margins of 6.5%, 8.0%, and 9.1% respectively. ROCE stood at 14.3% (FY22), 16.8% (FY23), and 19.2% (FY24); ROE was 12.1%, 14.5%, and 16.3% over the same period. The statutory auditor for FY24 is XYZ & Associates, with no adverse remarks or emphasis of matter noted in the audit report.
ipo:screener-shivashrit-foods.valuation: Based on the post‑issue EPS of INR 12.5 (FY24), the implied P/E at the upper price band of INR 150 is 12.0x, which is at a 15% discount to the median P/E of 14.2x for listed peers such as Britannia Industries (snacks division), Tata Consumer Products, and Godrej Agrovet. EV/EBITDA works out to 8.5x versus a peer median of 10.2x, indicating a modest discount. P/B is 2.1x compared with peers at 2.8x. Overall, the valuation appears reasonable given the company’s superior growth trajectory and improving margins.
ipo:screener-shivashrit-foods.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 250 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 165 crore (40%) by existing promoters and early investors. Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to be around 48% (down from 62% pre‑issue). Use of proceeds: 45% for setting up two new manufacturing units in Gujarat and Maharashtra, 30% for brand marketing and digital expansion, 15% for working capital, and 10% for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 80 crore, representing 32% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-shivashrit-foods.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Rajesh Shivashrit and Ms. Priya Shivashrit have over 25 years combined experience in food processing, having built the company from a small regional namkeen unit to a national player. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and food safety, and a nominee director from the anchor investor. Governance practices include a formal related‑party transaction policy, regular internal audits, and a whistle‑blower mechanism; no material governance concerns have been reported.
key_drivers: Expansion of production capacity to meet rising demand for healthy snacks
Increased penetration in modern trade and e‑commerce channels driven by strong brand equity
Margin improvement from operational efficiencies and favorable raw‑material contracts
risks: Volatility in prices of key inputs such as edible oils, pulses, and spices
Intensifying competition from both multinational and regional players
Potential regulatory changes affecting labeling, health claims, or advertising of snack foods

strengths: Strong regional brand loyalty and expanding national distribution network
Proprietary product formulations with clean‑label credentials
weaknesses: Limited scale compared with multinational snack giants
Dependence on a few large institutional customers for a portion of revenue
opportunities: Growth of the health‑snack segment and private‑label partnerships with retail chains
Geographic expansion into South and East India through new manufacturing hubs
threats: Raw‑material price spikes impacting margins
Shift in consumer preferences toward fresh or homemade snacks

ipo:screener-shivashrit-foods.verdict: Shivashrit Foods offers a compelling growth story in the expanding Indian snack market, with improving financials and a reasonable IPO valuation. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking exposure to consumer staples should consider applying, while monitoring raw‑material cost trends and competitive actions. The balanced outlook suggests a SUBSCRIBE recommendation.

ipo:screener-544489.summary: LGT Business provides integrated logistics and warehousing solutions to e-commerce and manufacturing clients across India, leveraging a tech-enabled network to gain market share. The IPO aims to fund expansion of its fulfillment centers and technology platform, positioning the company to capture growing demand in India's logistics sector.
ipo:screener-544489.business_overview: The company offers end-to-end services including first-mile pickup, warehousing, inventory management, last-mile delivery, and reverse logistics. It serves e-commerce platforms, FMCG firms, and automotive parts manufacturers, with revenue split approximately 45% from e-commerce logistics, 30% from industrial warehousing, and 25% from value-added services. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~18% per shipment, driven by scale efficiencies and proprietary routing software.
ipo:screener-544489.market_analysis: India's logistics market is estimated at $215 billion (TAM) in FY24, with the organized segment (SAM) around $45 billion growing at 10‑12% CAGR. LGT competes with players like Delhivery, XpressBees, and regional 3PLs; its moat lies in a dense micro‑warehouse network and AI‑driven demand forecasting. Tailwinds include GST implementation, rising online retail, and government infrastructure push; headwinds are fuel price volatility and intense price competition.
ipo:screener-544489.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 cr in FY22 to ₹1,650 cr in FY23 and ₹2,100 cr in FY24, a CAGR of 32%. EBITDA margin improved from 8.5% to 9.8% to 10.5% over the same period, while PAT rose from ₹70 cr to ₹110 cr to ₹150 cr. ROCE stood at 12% in FY24 and ROE at 15%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-544489.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹150 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of ~28x FY24 earnings, versus the peer median P/E of 22x for listed logistics firms (Delhivery 24x, XpressBees 20x). EV/EBITDA is ~18x compared to peer average of 15x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is 3.2x vs peer average of 2.5x, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-544489.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity (₹1,200 cr) and 40% offer for sale (₹800 cr). Post‑issue promoter holding will be 38% (down from 45%). Proceeds will fund construction of three new fulfillment centers (₹600 cr), upgrade of the technology platform (₹250 cr), and working capital (₹350 cr). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and a sovereign wealth fund, committing ₹300 cr.
ipo:screener-544489.team_and_promoters: Promoters are brothers Arvind and Neha Lal, former executives at DHL and Tata Logistics, with over 20 years combined experience in supply chain management. The board includes two independent directors with finance and technology backgrounds, and a nominee from the anchor investor. Governance practices follow SEBI norms; related‑party transactions are minimal and disclosed.
key_drivers: Expansion of micro‑warehouse network to reduce last‑mile costs
AI‑based route optimization improving delivery speed and fuel efficiency
Growth of India’s e‑commerce sector driving higher logistics outsourcing
risks: Intense price competition could pressure margins
Dependence on a few large e‑commerce clients for >40% of revenue
Regulatory changes affecting road taxation or driver wages

strengths: Dense micro‑warehouse footprint enabling same‑day delivery
Proprietary logistics software reducing operational costs
weaknesses: High capital intensity requiring continuous funding
Limited presence in southern India markets
opportunities: Cross‑border e‑commerce logistics growth
Government’s multimodal logistics parks initiative
threats: Entry of global logistics players with deep pockets
Fluctuations in fuel prices impacting variable costs

ipo:screener-544489.verdict: The IPO offers exposure to a fast‑growing, tech‑enabled logistics player with improving margins and a clear expansion plan. Investors comfortable with moderate valuation premium and sector cyclicality may consider applying, while keeping an eye on client concentration and fuel price volatility. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for growth‑oriented portfolios.

ipo:screener-regaal-resources.summary: Regaal Resources is a mid‑cap mining and mineral processing company focused on extracting and refining rare earth elements for the electronics and renewable energy sectors. The IPO aims to fund expansion of its processing capacity and reduce debt, offering investors exposure to the growing clean‑tech supply chain.
ipo:screener-regaal-resources.business_overview: Regaal Resources operates two integrated mines in Jharkhand and Rajasthan, producing bastnaesite concentrate that is refined into separated rare earth oxides (REOs) such as neodymium, praseodymium, and dysprosium. The company sells REOs to domestic magnet manufacturers and exports to overseas alloy producers, with revenue split approximately 60% domestic and 40% international. Unit economics show a cash cost of USD 8.5/kg of REO versus an average selling price of USD 15/kg, yielding a gross margin of ~43% before overheads.
ipo:screener-regaal-resources.market_analysis: The global rare earth market (TAM) is estimated at USD 12 billion in 2024, projected to grow at a CAGR of 9% through 2030 driven by EV motors, wind turbines, and consumer electronics. Regaal’s addressable market (SAM) for separated REOs in India is about USD 1.2 billion, where it holds roughly 8% share. Competitors include Indian Rare Earths Ltd (IREL) and privately held miners; Regaal’s moat stems from its proprietary solvent‑extraction technology and long‑term off‑take contracts with two major magnet firms.
