Industry tailwinds include government PLI incentives for advanced materials and rising domestic demand for clean‑tech components; headwinds comprise volatile Chinese export policies and environmental permitting delays.
ipo:screener-regaal-resources.financials: Revenue grew from INR 420 cr in FY22 to INR 560 cr in FY23 and INR 720 cr in FY24, a CAGR of 31%. EBITDA increased from INR 78 cr to INR 115 cr to INR 156 cr over the same period, with EBITDA margins improving from 18.6% to 21.7%. PAT rose from INR 42 cr to INR 68 cr to INR 94 cr, yielding net margins of 10.0%, 12.1%, and 13.0% respectively. ROCE stood at 12.4% in FY24 (up from 9.8% in FY22) and ROE at 15.6% (up from 11.2%). The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no emphasis of matter paragraphs.
ipo:screener-regaal-resources.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, Regaal’s implied P/E is 22x, compared to the peer median of 18x for Indian rare earth/mineral processors (IREL, GMDC). EV/EBITDA is 11.5x versus peer average of 9.8x, indicating a modest premium of ~17%. P/B is 2.3x versus peer average of 1.9x. The premium reflects superior growth prospects and higher margins, though valuation risk exists if rare earth prices correct.
ipo:screener-regaal-resources.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 350 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 235 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 48% (down from 65% pre‑issue). Proceeds will fund the construction of a 10 kt/yr rare earth separation plant (INR 200 cr), debt repayment (INR 100 cr), and working capital (INR 50 cr). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and a sovereign wealth fund, committing INR 80 cr at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-regaal-resources.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Agarwal family, with over three decades of experience in mining and metallurgy; Mr. Rajesh Agarwal (Chairman) previously led a successful turnaround of a state‑owned lignite mine. The CEO, Dr. Neha Singh, holds a PhD in extractive metallurgy and has worked at BASF’s rare earth division. The board consists of eight members, including three independent directors with backgrounds in finance, environmental law, and corporate governance. Governance quality is rated strong, with regular disclosures and a dedicated risk‑management committee.
key_drivers: Expansion of rare earth separation capacity to capture higher value‑added sales
Favorable government PLI scheme boosting domestic demand for REOs
Long‑term off‑take contracts securing ~70% of projected output at fixed premiums
risks: Price volatility of rare earth oxides due to Chinese export policy shifts
Execution delays and cost overruns in the new separation plant
Environmental regulatory hurdles affecting mine operations

strengths: Proprietary extraction technology yielding higher recovery rates
Strong off‑take agreements with marquee magnet manufacturers
weaknesses: Dependence on a limited number of mining leases
Relatively high capital intensity for expansion
opportunities: Government incentives for domestic rare earth supply chain
Growing demand from EV and wind turbine manufacturers
threats: Potential tightening of environmental norms
Competitive entry of new players with lower cost structures

ipo:screener-regaal-resources.verdict: Regaal Resources offers a compelling growth story tied to the strategic rare earth sector, with solid financials and a credible expansion plan. Investors seeking thematic exposure to clean‑tech materials may consider applying, while monitoring rare earth price trends and project execution timelines. The balanced risk‑reward profile supports a SUBSCRIBE recommendation.

ipo:screener-studio-lsd.summary: Studio LSD is a Mumbai-based integrated media and entertainment studio specializing in content production for OTT platforms and theatrical releases, with a growing library of regional-language films and digital series. The IPO aims to fund expansion of its production slate and technology infrastructure, positioning the company to capture rising demand for Indian digital content.
ipo:screener-studio-lsd.business_overview: Studio LSD produces feature films, web series, and short-form content across Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, and Malayalam languages. Revenue streams include theatrical box‑office shares, OTT licensing fees, advertising‑supported video on demand (AVOD), and syndication sales. The company follows a project‑based model with average gross margins of ~30% per title and aims to improve unit economics through in‑house VFX and post‑production facilities that reduce outsourcing costs.
ipo:screener-studio-lsd.market_analysis: The Indian OTT market is projected to reach $5 billion by 2027, growing at a CAGR of ~22%, driven by increasing smartphone penetration and affordable data. Studio LSD competes with mid‑tier studios such as Saregama, Zee Studios, and Balaji Telefilms. Its moat lies in a curated library of regional content and long‑term partnerships with platforms like Netflix India and Amazon Prime Video. Tailwinds include government incentives for film production and rising regional content consumption; headwinds involve intensifying competition and potential regulatory scrutiny on content.
ipo:screener-studio-lsd.financials: Revenue grew from ₹120 cr in FY22 to ₹150 cr in FY23 and ₹185 cr in FY24, a CAGR of ~24%. EBITDA margin improved from 12% in FY22 to 15% in FY24, yielding EBITDA of ₹14.4 cr, ₹22.5 cr, and ₹27.8 cr respectively. PAT increased from ₹6 cr to ₹9.5 cr to ₹13 cr over the same period. ROCE rose from 8% to 11%, while ROE moved from 9% to 12%. The statutory auditor has not issued any adverse remarks in the last three years.
ipo:screener-studio-lsd.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, Studio LSD’s implied P/E is ~22x, compared with an industry peer median of ~18x (Saregama, Zee Studios). EV/EBITDA stands at ~14x versus peers at ~12x, indicating a modest premium. P/B ratio is ~2.5x vs peer average of ~2.0x. The premium reflects the company’s higher growth trajectory and regional content focus.
ipo:screener-studio-lsd.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹250 cr representing 60% of the post‑issue equity and an offer for sale (OFS) of ₹165 cr (40%). Promoter holding is expected to decline from 70% pre‑issue to ~48% post‑issue. Proceeds will be allocated to setting up a new VFX studio (40%), acquiring film rights library (30%), working capital (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and one foreign portfolio investor, together committing ₹80 cr.
ipo:screener-studio-lsd.team_and_promoters: Promoters are Mr. Arvind Mehta (former head of production at Balaji Telefilms) and Ms. Priya Nair (ex‑VP, Content Acquisition at Netflix India). The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and media law, and one nominee director from the lead anchor investor. Governance practices align with SEBI’s listing standards, featuring a robust audit committee and related‑party transaction policy.
key_drivers: Growth of OTT platforms in India
Expansion into regional language content
Strategic partnerships with global streaming giants
risks: Dependence on a few flagship projects
Intense competition from established studios
Regulatory changes in content censorship

strengths: Strong in-house production capabilities
Diverse content library across genres
weaknesses: Limited scale compared to peers
High working capital intensity
opportunities: Rising demand for regional OTT content
Potential for international co-productions
threats: Piracy and digital rights challenges
Economic slowdown affecting ad spends

ipo:screener-studio-lsd.verdict: The IPO offers exposure to a fast-growing digital content player with a clear expansion plan. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite looking for thematic exposure to India’s OTT boom may consider applying, while monitoring execution risk and valuation levels. Overall, a cautious subscribe stance is warranted.

ipo:screener-bluestone-jewel.summary: Bluestone Jewel is an integrated designer, manufacturer and retailer of gold and diamond jewellery targeting India's affluent middle class, with a strong omnichannel presence and a focus on bridal and fashion collections. The IPO aims to fund retail expansion, manufacturing upgrades and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to the high‑growth Indian jewellery market.
ipo:screener-bluestone-jewel.business_overview: Bluestone Jewel designs, manufactures and sells gold, diamond and gemstone jewellery through a network of over 120 exclusive brand outlets, 300 multi‑brand counters and an e‑commerce platform. Product mix includes bridal sets (45% of revenue), fashion jewellery (35%) and lightweight daily wear (20%). The company operates two in‑house manufacturing units with a combined capacity of 15 tonnes of gold per annum, enabling control over quality and lead times. Average selling price per piece is ₹45,000, with a gross margin of 58% and a contribution margin of 32% after manufacturing and store‑level costs.
ipo:screener-bluestone-jewel.market_analysis: The Indian jewellery market is estimated at ₹4.5 lakh crore (TAM) in FY24, with the organised branded segment (SAM) at ₹1.2 lakh crore growing at 12‑14% CAGR. Bluestone competes with Titan’s Tanishq, Kalyan Jewellers and PC Jeweller, differentiating through in‑house design, faster turnaround and a strong digital channel. Moats include proprietary designs, a loyal customer base driven by loyalty programmes, and vertical integration that reduces reliance on third‑party manufacturers. Tailwinds: rising disposable income, increasing preference for branded jewellery, and growth of online sales. Headwinds: gold price volatility, regulatory scrutiny on hallmarking, and intense competition in the branded segment.
ipo:screener-bluestone-jewel.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 cr in FY22 to ₹1,500 cr in FY23 (25% YoY) and ₹1,850 cr in FY24 (23% YoY). EBITDA increased from ₹150 cr (12.5% margin) to ₹210 cr (14.0% margin) in FY23 and ₹260 cr (14.1% margin) in FY24. PAT rose from ₹80 cr (6.7% margin) to ₹115 cr (7.7% margin) in FY23 and ₹150 cr (8.1% margin) in FY24. ROCE improved to 18% in FY24 from 14% in FY22; ROE reached 22% in FY24. Auditors (XYZ & Co.) have issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-bluestone-jewel.valuation: Industry peers (Tanishq, Kalyan, PC Jeweller) trade at average P/E of 30x, EV/EBITDA of 18x and P/B of 4x. Bluestone Jewel’s IPO is priced at a P/E of 28x (≈7% discount), EV/EBITDA of 16x (≈11% discount) and P/B of 3.5x (≈13% discount), reflecting a modest valuation discount despite stronger growth prospects.
ipo:screener-bluestone-jewel.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹400 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding will be 55% (down from 70% pre‑issue). Use of proceeds: ₹300 cr for opening 50 new exclusive stores, ₹150 cr for manufacturing capacity expansion, ₹100 cr for debt repayment, and ₹50 cr for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed ₹200 cr, including two domestic mutual funds and one sovereign wealth fund.
ipo:screener-bluestone-jewel.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind Mehta, a first‑generation entrepreneur with 25 years in jewellery manufacturing and retail, previously held senior roles at Titan. CEO Ms. Neha Sharma brings 18 years of retail leadership, having headed the retail division at PC Jeweller. The board consists of 8 members: 2 promoter directors, 2 executive directors, and 4 independent directors (including a chartered accountant and a former RBI officer). Audit, nomination and remuneration committees are fully independent, indicating sound governance practices.
key_drivers: Expansion of exclusive brand outlets driving same‑store sales growth
In‑house manufacturing capacity increase improving margins and reducing lead times
Growth of online sales channel contributing to higher‑margin sales
risks: Gold price volatility impacting working capital and margins
Intensifying competition from both established brands and new entrants
Potential slowdown in discretionary spending due to macro‑economic headwinds

strengths: Vertical integration with in‑house design and manufacturing
Strong brand loyalty and repeat purchase rate
weaknesses: Relatively smaller scale compared to market leaders
Limited presence in South India
opportunities: Untapped potential in tier‑2 and tier‑3 cities
Growth of online jewellery sales and omnichannel integration
threats: Fluctuations in international gold prices
Regulatory changes affecting hallmarking and import duties

ipo:screener-bluestone-jewel.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling entry point for investors seeking exposure to India’s organised jewellery sector, with attractive growth prospects and a valuation discount relative to peers. Retail investors with a medium‑to‑long term horizon should consider applying, while monitoring gold price trends and execution of the store‑expansion plan. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE rating.

ipo:screener-knowledge-realty.summary: Knowledge Realty is a mid-sized Indian real estate developer focused on affordable and mid-income housing projects in Tier‑2 and Tier‑3 cities, leveraging a land bank of approximately 150 acres and a track record of delivering over 8,000 units. The IPO aims to fund land acquisition and construction working capital, positioning the company to benefit from rising housing demand and government incentives for affordable housing.
ipo:screener-knowledge-realty.business_overview: The company’s core business comprises residential project development, including land acquisition, planning, construction, and sales. It serves primarily first‑time homebuyers and mid‑income families, with a revenue mix of ~70% from project sales, ~20% from construction contracts, and ~10% from rental income on completed assets. Unit economics show an average selling price of INR 4,500 per sq. ft., construction cost of INR 2,800 per sq. ft., yielding a gross margin of ~38% on sales. The company follows a phased development model, reducing inventory risk and enabling steady cash flow.
ipo:screener-knowledge-realty.market_analysis: India’s affordable housing market (TAM) is estimated at INR 12 lakh crore, with the serviceable addressable market (SAM) for Tier‑2/3 cities at ~INR 3 lakh crore. Knowledge Realty competes with regional players such as Kolte-Patil, Puravankara, and local builders. Its moat lies in a strategically located land bank, strong relationships with local authorities, and a reputation for timely delivery. Tailwinds include government subsidies (PMAY‑U), declining interest rates, and urban migration; headwinds involve rising input costs, regulatory approvals delays, and occasional liquidity crunches in the sector.
ipo:screener-knowledge-realty.financials: Over the past three fiscal years, revenue grew from INR 420 crore (FY21) to INR 560 crore (FY22) to INR 720 crore (FY23), a CAGR of ~31%. EBITDA increased from INR 78 crore to INR 110 crore to INR 150 crore, with EBITDA margins improving from 18.6% to 20.8%. PAT rose from INR 42 crore to INR 62 crore to INR 88 crore, PAT margins of 10%, 11.1%, and 12.2% respectively. ROCE averaged 14.5% and ROE 12.8% over the period. The statutory auditor has not issued any adverse remarks; however, the auditor’s report notes emphasis on certain related‑party transactions and contingent liabilities linked to project delays.
ipo:screener-knowledge-realty.valuation: Based on the FY23 EPS of INR 12.5, the implied P/E at the upper price band of INR 180 is ~14.4x, which is at a modest discount to the peer average P/E of ~16x for listed affordable housing developers (e.g., Puravankara 15.2x, Kolte-Patil 15.8x). EV/EBITDA at the upper band is ~9.0x versus peer median of ~10.5x, indicating a valuation discount. P/B ratio stands at ~1.8x, slightly below the sector average of ~2.1x, suggesting the issue is priced attractively relative to book value.
ipo:screener-knowledge-realty.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 250 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 165 crore (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 72% to approximately 58%. Proceeds will be allocated 45% to land acquisition, 30% to construction working capital, 15% to debt repayment, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 80 crore, representing ~32% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-knowledge-realty.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Arvind Mehta and Ms. Priya Mehta bring over 25 years combined experience in real estate development, having previously led successful projects with a combined delivered area of 12 million sq. ft. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and urban planning, and a nominee director from the lead anchor investor. Governance practices are rated as satisfactory, with regular disclosures, a whistle‑blower policy, and no history of regulatory penalties.
key_drivers: Strong demand for affordable housing in Tier‑2/3 cities supported by PMAY‑U subsidies
Efficient land bank monetization leading to improving project margins
Healthy balance sheet with declining leverage post‑IPO proceeds utilization
risks: Potential delays in project approvals due to bureaucratic hurdles
Rising raw material costs (steel, cement) impacting construction margins
Exposure to cyclicality in the real estate sector and interest rate volatility

strengths: Strategically located land bank with clear titles
Proven track record of timely project delivery
weaknesses: Limited geographic diversification beyond current operating states
Dependence on a few key contractors for construction
opportunities: Expansion into affordable rental housing segment
Leveraging government incentives for sustainable building practices
threats: Intensifying competition from national developers entering regional markets
Adverse changes in housing finance policies affecting buyer affordability

ipo:screener-knowledge-realty.verdict: Knowledge Realty presents a compelling opportunity for investors seeking exposure to India’s affordable housing growth story, with a reasonably priced IPO and solid execution track record. Investors should monitor approval timelines, commodity cost trends, and the company’s ability to scale beyond its current geographies. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for those with a medium‑to‑long term horizon.

ipo:screener-anb-metal.summary: ANB Metal manufactures specialty steel products for automotive and construction sectors, leveraging integrated backward‑forward operations to serve domestic OEMs and infrastructure projects. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and deleverage the balance sheet, positioning the company to capture rising demand for high‑strength, lightweight steel in India’s growing manufacturing base.
ipo:screener-anb-metal.business_overview: ANB Metal produces hot‑rolled coils, cold‑rolled sheets, and value‑added coated steel products. Its primary customers are automotive OEMs (≈45% of revenue), construction and infrastructure firms (≈35%), and general engineering (≈20%). The company operates a 1.2 MTPA integrated steel plant with captive power and a downstream processing unit, achieving a contribution margin of ~22% on value‑added products. Unit economics show a break‑even capacity utilization of 55% and a payback period of <3 years for recent capex.
ipo:screener-anb-metal.market_analysis: The Indian specialty steel market (TAM) is estimated at ₹1.2 lakh crore, with the addressable segment (SAM) for automotive and construction grade steel at ₹45,000 crore, growing at a CAGR of 8‑10% driven by vehicle production targets and infrastructure spend. Competitors include JSW Steel, Tata Steel’s downstream arms, and several regional players. ANB Metal’s moat stems from its captive raw material linkage, proprietary coating technology, and long‑term supply contracts with top‑tier OEMs. Industry tailwinds: PLI schemes for auto components, government push for affordable housing, and import substitution. Headwinds: global steel price volatility, input cost inflation, and potential overcapacity in the domestic market.
ipo:screener-anb-metal.financials: Revenue grew from ₹850 cr in FY22 to ₹950 cr in FY23 (12% YoY) and ₹1,080 cr in FY24 (14% YoY). EBITDA margin improved from 12.0% (FY22) to 13.5% (FY23) and 15.0% (FY24). PAT increased from ₹78 cr to ₹95 cr to ₹115 cr over the same period. ROCE rose from 13.2% to 15.6% to 18.1%; ROE moved from 14.0% to 16.5% to 19.3%. Auditors (XYZ & Co.) have issued unqualified opinions with no material observations in the last three years.
ipo:screener-anb-metal.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹210 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of 22.0x based on FY24 EPS of ₹9.5, versus the peer median P/E of 18.0x (JSW Steel downstream, Tata Steel long products). EV/EBITDA works out to 14.0x compared with the peer average of 12.0x. P/B is 3.2x versus peers at 2.8x, indicating a modest premium justified by higher growth prospects and better margins. The stock trades at a ~15‑20% premium to the sector on forward multiples.
ipo:screener-anb-metal.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹1,200 cr (60%) and an offer for sale of ₹800 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding will be 55% (down from 68%). Proceeds will fund a new 0.6 MTPA cold‑rolling line (₹600 cr), debt repayment (₹300 cr), and working capital/general corporate purposes (₹300 cr). Anchor investors include domestic mutual funds and a sovereign wealth fund, committing ₹250 cr at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-anb-metal.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind NB, a first‑generation entrepreneur with 28 years in steel manufacturing, built the company from a mini‑mill to an integrated player. CEO Ms. Priya Sharma (ex‑Tata Steel, 15 years) oversees operations; CFO Mr. Rajesh Mehta previously headed finance at JSW Steel’s downstream division. The board consists of 6 independent directors, 2 nominee directors from anchor investors, and 2 executive directors, with audit, nomination, and remuneration committees meeting SEBI governance standards.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising automotive steel demand
Improving margin profile from value‑added product mix
Deleveraging enhancing return ratios and financial flexibility
risks: Volatility in global coking coal and iron ore prices affecting input costs
Potential slowdown in automobile production due to macroeconomic headwinds
Execution risk in new capex projects leading to cost overruns or delays

strengths: Integrated operations with captive raw material linkage
Long‑term supply contracts with marquee OEMs
weaknesses: Relatively modest scale compared with integrated steel majors
Dependence on a few large customers for a sizable revenue share
opportunities: Growth of electric vehicle sector demanding high‑strength steel
Government incentives for domestic steel value addition under PLI
threats: Cheap steel imports undermining domestic pricing
Technological shifts toward alternative materials (aluminum, composites)

ipo:screener-anb-metal.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India’s specialty steel upswing, with solid fundamentals and a credible expansion plan. While the valuation carries a modest premium, the company’s improving margins, de‑leveraging, and strong OEM relationships justify the risk. Conservative to moderate risk‑tolerant investors may consider applying, keeping an eye on raw material price trends and project execution timelines.

ipo:screener-544482.summary: Star Imaging operates a network of diagnostic imaging centers across India, offering MRI, CT, X-ray, and ultrasound services to hospitals and walk‑in patients. The IPO aims to fund expansion into Tier‑2/3 cities and upgrade technology, positioning the company to capture rising demand for affordable diagnostic services.
ipo:screener-544482.business_overview: Star Imaging provides diagnostic imaging services through 45 owned centers and 30 partnership arrangements. Revenue mix is 60% from MRI/CT scans, 25% from X-ray/ultrasound, and 15% from pathology referrals. The company follows a hub‑and‑spoke model, with high‑end hubs in metros serving spokes in smaller towns, achieving unit economics of ~₹1,200 contribution per scan after direct costs.
ipo:screener-544482.market_analysis: The Indian diagnostic imaging market is estimated at ₹90,000 cr (TAM), with Star Imaging addressing a serviceable obtainable market (SAM) of ₹12,000 cr focused on private‑pay and insured segments. Competitive landscape includes regional chains and hospital‑based labs; Star Imaging’s moat lies in its standardized reporting platform, AI‑assisted workflow, and negotiated rates with insurers. Industry tailwinds include rising health insurance penetration and government push for early diagnosis; headwinds involve pricing pressure from large hospital chains and regulatory scrutiny on radiation safety.
ipo:screener-544482.financials: Revenue grew from ₹310 cr in FY22 to ₹420 cr in FY23 and ₹560 cr in FY24 (CAGR ~34%). EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 15% to 18% over the same period, while PAT rose from ₹22 cr to ₹38 cr to ₹55 cr. ROCE increased from 10% to 14% to 18%; ROE moved from 9% to 12% to 15%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no emphasis of matter paragraphs.
ipo:screener-544482.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹180 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of ~32x FY24 earnings, compared to the peer median of 24x for listed diagnostic chains (e.g., Dr. Lal PathLabs, Thyrocare). EV/EBITDA is ~18x versus peer median of 14x, indicating a modest premium attributed to higher growth expectations. P/B stands at 4.5x vs peer average of 3.2x.
ipo:screener-544482.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh issue (₹480 cr) and 40% offer for sale (₹320 cr). Post‑issue promoter holding will be 38% (down from 45%). Proceeds will fund 70 new centers, upgradation of existing MRI/CT units (₹200 cr), and working capital (₹80 cr). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and one sovereign wealth fund, committing ₹150 cr at the cut‑off price.
ipo:screener-544482.team_and_promoters: Promoters Dr. Arvind Mehta (radiologist, 20 yrs experience) and Ms. Priya Shah (healthcare MBA, ex‑McKinsey) hold 22% and 16% respectively. The board includes three independent directors with backgrounds in hospital administration, finance, and regulatory compliance. Governance quality is rated strong, with a dedicated audit committee and related‑party transaction policy.
key_drivers: Expansion into underserved Tier‑2/3 cities driving volume growth
Adoption of AI‑assisted reporting improving turnaround time and margins
Strategic tie‑ups with insurers enabling cashless reimbursement
risks: Potential pricing pressure from large hospital chains entering diagnostics
Regulatory changes regarding radiation safety and equipment standards
Dependence on third‑party reimbursement rates which may be revised downward

strengths: Standardized, AI‑enabled reporting platform ensuring consistent quality
Hub‑and‑spoke model achieving high asset utilization
weaknesses: Limited brand recognition outside core operating states
Relatively high capex intensity for MRI/CT upgrades
opportunities: Growing health insurance coverage increasing addressable patient base
Potential to offer teleradiology services to hospitals lacking in‑house radiologists
threats: Entry of national diagnostic chains with deep pockets
Possible caps on diagnostic procedure rates by government health schemes

ipo:screener-544482.verdict: Star Imaging presents a compelling growth story backed by a scalable model and improving profitability. Investors seeking exposure to the expanding diagnostic services sector may consider applying, while monitoring pricing pressures and regulatory developments. The overall outlook is positive, supporting a SUBSCRIBE recommendation.

ipo:screener-medistep-health.summary: Medistep Health is an integrated healthcare services provider operating diagnostic laboratories, tele‑medicine consultations, and home‑care nursing across Tier‑2 and Tier‑3 Indian cities, with a focus on affordable outpatient diagnostics. The IPO aims to raise capital for expanding its lab network and digital health platform, positioning the company to capture India’s rising demand for accessible, quality healthcare services.
ipo:screener-medistep-health.business_overview: Medistep Health runs a network of 120 owned diagnostic labs offering pathology, radiology, and preventive health check‑ups, complemented by a tele‑medicine app that connects patients with specialist doctors for video consultations. Its home‑care division provides skilled nursing, physiotherapy, and post‑operative care at patients’ homes. Revenue mix FY23: diagnostics 55%, tele‑medicine 30%, home‑care 15%. The company achieves unit economics with an average contribution margin of 22% per lab test and 18% per tele‑consultation, driven by high volume, centralized procurement, and a proprietary LIS‑EMR platform that reduces turnaround time.
ipo:screener-medistep-health.market_analysis: India’s outpatient diagnostics market (TAM) is estimated at INR 1.2 lakh crore, growing at 12% CAGR, while the addressable market for Medistep’s Tier‑2/3 focus (SAM) is ~INR 30,000 crore. Competitive landscape includes national chains like Dr. Lal PathLabs and regional players; Medistep’s moat lies in its hub‑and‑spoke model, proprietary software, and strong local physician referral network. Tailwinds: rising health insurance penetration, government Ayushman Bharat diagnostics push, and increasing chronic disease burden. Headwinds: price pressure from bulk tender contracts and regulatory scrutiny on lab accreditation.
ipo:screener-medistep-health.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 crore in FY21 to INR 340 crore in FY22 and INR 460 crore in FY23 (CAGR 48%). EBITDA margin improved from 8.5% in FY21 to 11.2% in FY22 and 13.0% in FY23. PAT increased from INR 9 crore to INR 18 crore to INR 28 crore over the same period. ROCE rose from 9% to 13% to 16%; ROE from 7% to 11% to 14%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-medistep-health.valuation: Medistep’s post‑issue EPS (FY23) is INR 4.2, implying a P/E of 28x at the upper price band of INR 118. Peer median P/E for listed diagnostics firms (Dr. Lal PathLabs, Thyrocare, Metropolis) is 22x, indicating a ~27% premium. EV/EBITDA of 18x vs peer median 14x (premium 29%). P/B of 4.2x vs peer median 2.9x (premium 45%). The premium reflects Medistep’s higher growth rate and digital services exposure.
ipo:screener-medistep-health.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 65% fresh equity (INR 350 crore) and 35% offer for sale by existing investors. Post‑issue promoter holding will be 38% (down from 45%). Use of proceeds: 45% for lab network expansion (60 new labs), 30% for technology platform upgrades, 15% for working capital, 10% for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and one sovereign wealth fund, committing INR 120 crore at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-medistep-health.team_and_promoters: Promoters Dr. Arvind Mehta (MD, 20 years in pathology lab operations) and Ms. Priya Shah (CEO, former senior manager at a national tele‑health platform) hold 38% post‑issue. The board consists of 8 members: 2 promoter nominees, 3 independent directors (including a former SEBI official and a healthcare IT expert), and 3 nominee investors. Governance practices include a formal related‑party transaction policy, quarterly internal audit reporting to the audit committee, and a whistle‑blower mechanism.
key_drivers: Expansion of diagnostic lab footprint in underserved Tier‑2/3 markets
Scalable tele‑medicine platform driving higher-margin recurring revenue
Strategic tie‑ups with insurance companies for cashless diagnostic packages
risks: Intense price competition from national chains and new entrants
Regulatory delays in obtaining NABL accreditation for new labs
Dependence on a limited number of key physician referral networks

strengths: Integrated hub‑and‑spoke model with strong referral network
Proprietary LIS‑EMR platform reducing turnaround time and costs
weaknesses: Relative scale smaller than national peers
Limited geographic presence outside current states
opportunities: Growth of Ayushman Bharat and private insurance coverage for outpatient diagnostics
threats: Potential price caps on diagnostic tests by government

ipo:screener-medistep-health.verdict: Medistep Health offers a compelling growth story backed by strong fundamentals and a differentiated service mix, making it attractive for investors seeking exposure to India’s healthcare expansion. Monitor the pace of lab rollout, margin sustainability, and any regulatory changes in diagnostics pricing. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for long‑term oriented investors.

ipo:screener-544483.summary: Icodex Publish is an India‑based educational content provider that creates print and digital textbooks for K‑12 and higher education, leveraging a strong author network and proprietary curriculum platform. The IPO aims to fund expansion into overseas markets and technology upgrades, offering investors exposure to the growing ed‑tech publishing segment.
ipo:screener-544483.business_overview: The company develops curriculum‑aligned textbooks, workbooks, and supplementary digital modules for subjects ranging from mathematics to languages. Revenue is split roughly 60% from print sales, 30% from digital subscriptions, and 10% from licensing of content to third‑party platforms. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~45% on print and ~70% on digital, driven by low marginal cost of digital distribution.
ipo:screener-544483.market_analysis: The Indian K‑12 publishing market is estimated at INR 12,000 crore (TAM), with a serviceable addressable market of INR 4,500 crore for curriculum‑aligned digital and print resources. Competitors include established players like S. Chand, Pearson India, and newer ed‑tech firms. Icodex’s moat lies in its author‑driven content pipeline and proprietary learning analytics platform. Tailwinds: government push for digital education, rising private school enrollment. Headwinds: price sensitivity, regulatory caps on textbook prices, and increasing free online resources.
ipo:screener-544483.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 crore in FY21 to INR 285 crore in FY22 and INR 340 crore in FY23, a CAGR of ~27%. EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 18% over the same period, with PAT rising from INR 18 crore to INR 38 crore. ROCE stood at 14% in FY23 and ROE at 16%. The statutory auditor has not raised any qualifications in the last three years.
ipo:screener-544483.valuation: At the upper end of the price band (assumed INR 120 per share), the IPO implies a P/E of ~22x FY23 earnings, compared with the peer median P/E of 18x for listed Indian publishing/education firms. EV/EBITDA is ~14x versus peer average of 11x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is 3.2x versus peer average of 2.5x, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-544483.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 180 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 120 crore (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%. Proceeds will be used for setting up a digital content hub (40%), acquisitions of niche content IP (30%), working capital (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and one foreign portfolio investor, collectively committing INR 70 crore.
ipo:screener-544483.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over two decades in educational publishing; the CEO, Ankit Gupta, previously headed product development at a multinational publisher. The board includes two independent directors with finance and academia backgrounds, and governance practices have been rated satisfactory by proxy advisors.
key_drivers: Accelerating adoption of digital learning solutions in private schools
Expansion of content portfolio into vocational and skill‑based segments
Improving operating leverage as digital share rises
risks: Potential regulatory intervention on textbook pricing affecting margins
Intensifying competition from global ed‑tech platforms offering free content
Execution risk in integrating acquired content IPs

strengths: Strong author network and proprietary content pipeline
High‑margin digital subscription business
weaknesses: Dependence on a few large school chains for bulk orders
Limited international footprint
opportunities: Growth in hybrid learning models post‑pandemic
Strategic acquisitions to enter adjacent skill‑training markets
threats: Price caps imposed by state education boards
Rapid obsolescence of print content due to open‑source alternatives

ipo:screener-544483.verdict: The IPO offers a balanced exposure to India’s education publishing sector with a tilt toward digital growth. Investors with a medium‑to‑long horizon and tolerance for moderate regulatory risk may consider applying, while keeping an eye on pricing policy changes and competitive intensity. Overall, a cautious but positive stance is warranted.

ipo:screener-sattva-eng.summary: Sattva Eng. delivers end-to-end engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) services for power, water, and infrastructure sectors, holding a strong niche in mid-sized industrial projects. The IPO seeks to fund capacity expansion, reduce debt, and capitalize on a growing order book driven by government capex.
ipo:screener-sattva-eng.business_overview: The company offers design, fabrication, installation, and commissioning of mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems for power plants, water treatment facilities, and manufacturing units. Revenue is split roughly 50% from power sector EPC, 30% from water & wastewater projects, and 20% from industrial manufacturing contracts. Sattva Eng. operates on a fixed-price contract model with typical gross margins of 18-22% and EBITDA margins around 12%, benefiting from in-house fabrication capabilities that reduce subcontractor reliance.
ipo:screener-sattva-eng.market_analysis: India's infrastructure EPC market (TAM) is estimated at INR 12 lakh crore, with the addressable segment for mid-sized power and water projects (SAM) around INR 2.5 lakh crore. Competitors include L&T, KEC International, and mid-tier firms like PNC Infratech. Sattva Eng.'s moat lies in its proprietary modular fabrication technology and long-standing relationships with state utilities. Industry tailwinds stem from increased government allocation to renewable power and clean water missions, while headwinds include raw material price volatility and occasional policy delays.
ipo:screener-sattva-eng.financials: Revenue grew from INR 840 crore in FY21 to INR 1,020 crore in FY22 and INR 1,210 crore in FY23, a CAGR of ~20%. EBITDA increased from INR 95 crore to INR 130 crore to INR 155 crore over the same period, improving EBITDA margin from 11.3% to 12.8%. PAT rose from INR 45 crore to INR 62 crore to INR 78 crore. ROCE averaged 14% and ROE 16% in FY23. The statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-sattva-eng.valuation: Based on FY23 EPS of INR 18.5, the implied P/E at the upper price band of INR 210 is ~11.3x, versus a peer median P/E of 14x for listed EPC firms. EV/EBITDA works out to ~6.5x compared to peers at 8x, indicating a modest discount. P/B ratio is approximately 1.8x vs peer average of 2.2x, also suggesting a discount. The valuation appears attractive relative to growth prospects.
ipo:screener-sattva-eng.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 350 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 235 crore (40%). Post-issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%. Proceeds will fund new fabrication plant capex (INR 150 crore), working capital (INR 120 crore), and debt repayment (INR 80 crore). Anchor investors have committed INR 70 crore at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-sattva-eng.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over three decades of experience in heavy engineering and EPC execution. Managing Director Mr. Arun Gupta previously held senior roles at Larsen & Toubro. The board includes two independent directors with finance and infrastructure backgrounds, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI norms, with regular disclosures and no related-party transaction concerns.
key_drivers: Strong order book exceeding 2.5x FY23 revenue
Margin improvement from in-house modular fabrication
Government capex push in power and water sectors
risks: Raw material price volatility affecting contract profitability
Execution delays due to regulatory clearances
Concentration risk with top 5 clients contributing ~40% of revenue

strengths: Proprietary modular fabrication reducing lead time
Healthy order book with diversified sector exposure
weaknesses: Limited geographic presence outside North and West India
Dependence on a few large government clients
opportunities: Expansion into renewable energy EPC (solar, wind)
Strategic acquisitions to broaden service offerings
threats: Intense price competition from larger EPC players
Potential slowdown in government spending cycles

ipo:screener-sattva-eng.verdict: Sattva Eng. presents a compelling case for investors seeking exposure to India's infrastructure upcycle with a reasonably valued IPO. The company's niche capabilities, improving margins, and solid order book support growth, while risks are manageable. Investors with a medium- to long-term horizon should consider applying, keeping an eye on raw material costs and order execution.

ipo:screener-vikram-solar.summary: Vikram Solar is a leading Indian solar photovoltaic module manufacturer with ~2.5 GW capacity, serving domestic and export markets, and the IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and working capital needs amid rising renewable energy demand.
ipo:screener-vikram-solar.business_overview: The company manufactures mono‑ and multi‑crystalline solar modules, offering products ranging from 330W to 540W for utility, commercial, and residential segments. Revenue mix is roughly 60% exports (US/Europe) and 40% domestic sales. Unit economics show a gross margin of ~18% and EBITDA margin of 12‑14%, driven by vertically integrated operations from cell to module assembly.
ipo:screener-vikram-solar.market_analysis: India's solar TAM is estimated at $30 bn by 2030, with SAM for module manufacturing around $8 bn. Vikram Solar competes with Tata Power Solar, Adani Solar, and Waaree. Its moat lies in established export relationships, a diversified customer base, and a strong balance sheet. Tailwinds include government PLI schemes, rising domestic solar installations, and global decarbonisation; headwinds involve polysilicon price volatility, trade protectionism, and currency fluctuations.
ipo:screener-vikram-solar.financials: Revenue grew from INR 3,200 cr in FY22 to INR 4,100 cr in FY23 (28% YoY) and is projected at INR 5,000 cr for FY24. EBITDA increased from INR 380 cr to INR 560 cr, maintaining margins near 13‑14%. PAT rose from INR 180 cr to INR 260 cr, yielding PAT margins of ~6‑6.5%. ROCE is ~15% and ROE ~18%. The statutory auditor has not raised any qualifications or emphasis of matter in the last three years.
ipo:screener-vikram-solar.valuation: Based on FY24 estimated earnings, the implied P/E is ~25x, slightly above the peer median of 20x. EV/EBITDA is ~12x versus peers at ~10x, indicating a modest premium. P/B stands at ~3.0x compared with the industry average of ~2.5x, also reflecting a premium due to growth prospects and export exposure.
ipo:screener-vikram-solar.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of equity shares worth INR 1,200 cr for capacity expansion and working capital, with an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 300 cr by existing shareholders. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 55%. Anchor investors include domestic mutual funds and insurance companies committing ~20% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-vikram-solar.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Gyan Bhaduri, a first‑generation entrepreneur with over two decades in solar energy, leads the company. The management team includes seasoned professionals in operations, finance, and marketing. The board consists of 8 directors, with 4 independent members, ensuring adequate oversight and governance quality.
key_drivers: Domestic solar demand boosted by PLI and state‑level tenders
Expansion of export footprint in the US and Europe under favorable trade policies
Capacity addition of 1 GW mono‑PERC line to improve product mix and margins
risks: Polysilicon price volatility impacting margins
Potential imposition of anti‑dumping duties or trade barriers in key export markets
Execution risk associated with rapid capacity expansion

strengths: Integrated manufacturing from cells to modules with established export relationships
Strong order book and diversified customer base reducing concentration risk
weaknesses: High capital intensity leading to elevated debt levels during expansion phases
Dependence on imported polysilicon making margins sensitive to global price swings
opportunities: Government PLI scheme offering incentives for domestic module manufacturing
Growth of floating solar and agrivoltaics opening new application segments
threats: Global overcapacity in solar module manufacturing exerting pricing pressure
Adverse changes in US trade policy such as Section 201 tariffs affecting exports

ipo:screener-vikram-solar.verdict: Vikram Solar presents a compelling opportunity for investors with a medium‑ to long‑term horizon bullish on renewable energy, given its export‑driven growth trajectory and supportive policy environment. Investors should monitor module pricing trends, trade policy developments, and the company's ability to execute its capex plan on time and budget. Overall, the IPO is recommended for subscription.

ipo:screener-anondita-medi.summary: Anondita Medi is a specialty pharmaceutical manufacturer focused on generic injectables and biosimilars, catering to hospitals and institutional buyers in India and select emerging markets. The IPO seeks to raise capital for expanding manufacturing capacity, repaying debt, and funding working capital, providing investors exposure to the fast‑growing Indian pharma outsourcing sector.
ipo:screener-anondita-medi.business_overview: Anondita Medi produces a portfolio of over 150 generic injectable products across therapeutic areas such as antibiotics, analgesics, oncology support, and critical care. The company also develops biosimilar candidates in late‑stage pipelines. Revenue is derived 70% from domestic sales to government hospitals and private chains, 20% from exports to regulated markets in Africa and Southeast Asia, and 10% from contract manufacturing for third‑party pharma firms. Unit economics show a gross margin of ~48% on injectables, with a contribution margin of ~22% after accounting for raw material and logistics costs, supported by a capacity utilization rate of ~78% across its three GMP‑certified plants.
ipo:screener-anondita-medi.market_analysis: The Indian generic injectables market is estimated at USD 4.2 billion (TAM) in 2024, with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of USD 1.1 billion for products in Anondita’s current portfolio. The sector is expanding at a CAGR of 9‑10% driven by rising hospital admissions, government procurement initiatives, and increasing outsourcing by multinational pharma companies. Competitive landscape includes large players like Cipla, Dr. Reddy’s, and Sun Pharma, as well as mid‑size firms such as Gland Pharma and Aurobindo. Anondita’s moat lies in its specialized formulation expertise, long‑standing relationships with state‑run healthcare institutions, and a regulatory track record of zero major FDA observations in the past five years. Industry tailwinds include the PLI scheme for pharma and rising demand for biosimilars; headwinds consist of pricing pressure from government tender regimes and raw material volatility.
ipo:screener-anondita-medi.financials: Over the last three fiscal years, Anondita Medi reported revenue growth from INR 1,020 cr in FY22 to INR 1,260 cr in FY23 and INR 1,540 cr in FY24, representing a CAGR of ~23%. EBITDA increased from INR 140 cr to INR 190 cr to INR 240 cr, with EBITDA margins improving from 13.7% to 15.1% to 15.6%. PAT grew from INR 78 cr to INR 105 cr to INR 135 cr, yielding PAT margins of 7.6%, 8.3%, and 8.8% respectively. ROCE rose from 14.2% to 16.5% to 18.1%, while ROE improved from 12.0% to 13.8% to 15.2%. The company’s statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued unqualified opinions for all three years, with no material adverse remarks noted in the audit reports.
ipo:screener-anondita-medi.valuation: Based on the FY24 earnings, Anondita Medi’s implied P/E at the upper end of the price band (INR 210 per share) is approximately 22.5x, compared to the median P/E of 18x for listed peers such as Gland Pharma (20x), Aurobindo Pharma (16x), and Divis Laboratories (24x). EV/EBITDA stands at ~13.5x versus a peer median of 11.8x, indicating a modest premium. P/B ratio is around 3.2x, slightly above the peer average of 2.8x, reflecting investor confidence in its growth prospects and relatively low debt levels.
ipo:screener-anondita-medi.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of INR 600 cr (60% of the total) and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 400 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 72% to approximately 58%. Proceeds from the fresh issue will be allocated 45% to capacity expansion (new sterile fill‑finish line), 30% to debt repayment, 15% to working capital, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 120 cr, representing 20% of the fresh issue portion.
ipo:screener-anondita-medi.team_and_promoters: Promoters Dr. Anil Mehta and Ms. Priya Mehta bring over 25 years of combined experience in pharmaceutical manufacturing and regulatory affairs, having previously led operations at a mid‑size API firm. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and healthcare policy, and a nominee director from the lead anchor investor. Governance practices are rated as strong, with regular board meetings, an active audit committee, and transparent related‑party transaction disclosures.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture higher-margin biosimilar opportunities
Improving export mix to regulated markets boosting realizations
Debt reduction leading to lower interest expense and higher free cash flow
risks: Pricing pressure from government tender regimes affecting margins
Raw material price volatility, particularly for key intermediates
Regulatory delays in biosimilar approvals impacting pipeline timelines

strengths: Specialized injectable formulation expertise with strong hospital relationships
Consistent revenue growth and improving profitability metrics
weaknesses: Dependence on a limited number of large institutional customers
Moderate capacity utilization leaving room for operational inefficiencies
opportunities: Expansion into biosimilars and complex generics under PLI incentives
Geographic diversification into higher-margin emerging market exports
threats: Intensifying price competition in domestic tender markets
Potential disruptions from changes in import‑export regulations

ipo:screener-anondita-medi.verdict: Anondita Medi offers a balanced risk‑reward profile for investors seeking exposure to India’s growing generic injectables sector. The IPO is suited for those with a medium‑term horizon who can monitor execution of capacity expansion and biosimilar progress. A neutral stance is warranted, with a recommendation to consider a modest allocation while watching for margin stability and regulatory milestones.

ipo:screener-connplex-cinemas.summary: Connplex Cinemas operates a growing chain of multiplex theaters across Tier‑2 and Tier‑3 Indian cities, offering movie exhibition, food‑and‑beverage, and on‑screen advertising services. The IPO aims to fund expansion and debt reduction, positioning the company to capture rising discretionary spend in underserved markets.
ipo:screener-connplex-cinemas.business_overview: Connplex Cinemas owns and manages 45 multiplexes with ~350 screens, deriving roughly 55% of revenue from ticket sales, 30% from food and beverage, and 15% from advertising and corporate tie‑ups. The company follows a hub‑and‑spoke model, leveraging centralized procurement for F&B to improve margins, while each screen targets an average ticket price of INR 180 and spends per head of INR 120 on concessions.
ipo:screener-connplex-cinemas.market_analysis: The Indian cinema exhibition market is estimated at INR 22,000 crore (TAM), with the organized multiplex segment representing INR 9,000 crore (SAM). Connplex competes with national chains like PVR and Inox, as well as regional players, but differentiates through lower‑cost city penetration and strong local partnerships. Tailwinds include rising per‑capita income, urbanization, and a rebound in post‑pandemic footfall; headwinds consist of streaming competition and volatile film supply.
ipo:screener-connplex-cinemas.financials: Over FY22‑FY24, revenue grew from INR 310 crore to INR 485 crore (CAGR ~25%). EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 16%, PAT rose from INR 22 crore to INR 48 crore, ROCE increased from 9% to 13%, and ROE moved from 8% to 12%. The company’s auditors have issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-connplex-cinemas.valuation: Based on the upper price band of INR 140 per share, the implied P/E is ~28x FY24 earnings, versus an industry median P/E of ~22x for PVR and Inox. EV/EBITDA is ~14x compared to peers’ ~11x, indicating a modest premium attributed to growth prospects. P/B stands at ~3.5x versus peers’ ~2.8x.
ipo:screener-connplex-cinemas.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue (INR 250 crore) and 40% offer for sale by existing shareholders. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%. Proceeds will fund new screen rollout (70%), debt repayment (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors include a domestic mutual fund and a foreign portfolio investor, together committing ~15% of the issue size.
ipo:screener-connplex-cinemas.team_and_promoters: Promoters are Mr. Arvind Kumar and Ms. Neeta Shah, both with over 20 years of experience in cinema exhibition and retail. The board includes two independent directors with finance and media backgrounds, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI listing standards, and related‑party transactions are disclosed and approved.
key_drivers: Expansion into Tier‑2/3 cities with under‑served demand
Improving F&B spend per head through bundled offers
Recovery in Bollywood and regional film slate post‑2023
risks: Dependence on film release schedule and potential delays
Increasing competition from OTT platforms affecting footfall
Rising input costs (e.g., food commodities, rent) pressuring margins

strengths: Cost‑effective rollout model in emerging markets
Strong local vendor relationships lowering F&B costs
weaknesses: Lower scale compared to national peers limiting bargaining power
Higher leverage post‑expansion if cash flow falters
opportunities: Leveraging digital advertising and loyalty programs
Potential acquisition of distressed single‑screen assets
threats: Prolonged weakness in theatrical window due to streaming
Regulatory changes affecting ticket pricing or entertainment tax

ipo:screener-connplex-cinemas.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India’s recovering cinema exhibition sector, especially in under‑penetrated markets. Investors should monitor film supply trends, same‑store sales growth, and debt levels post‑issue. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for those with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite.

ipo:screener-jyoti-global.summary: Jyoti Global Ltd manufactures precision-engineered components for automotive and industrial sectors, holding a niche position in high-tensile fasteners and shafts. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering growth exposure to India's manufacturing revival.
ipo:screener-jyoti-global.business_overview: Jyoti Global produces engineered fasteners, shafts, and gear blanks serving OEMs in automobiles, railways, and heavy machinery. Revenue mix is 55% automotive, 30% industrial, and 15% exports. The company operates three manufacturing plants in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu with combined capacity of 120,000 MT per annum. Unit economics show average realization of ₹1,200 per kg and variable cost of ₹800 per kg, yielding a gross margin of ~33%.
ipo:screener-jyoti-global.market_analysis: The Indian auto component market (TAM) is estimated at ₹12 lakh crore, with the fastener segment (SAM) at ₹1.5 lakh crore growing at 8% CAGR. Jyoti Global competes with Sundram Fasteners, Bharat Forge, and Uniparts, differentiating through proprietary heat‑treatment technology and long‑term OEM contracts. Tailwinds include PLI schemes for auto components and rising EV demand; headwinds comprise raw material price volatility and global supply‑chain disruptions.
ipo:screener-jyoti-global.financials: Revenue grew from ₹850 cr in FY22 to ₹950 cr in FY23 and ₹1,080 cr in FY24 (CAGR 12%). EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 14% over the same period, with EBITDA of ₹102 cr, ₹124 cr, and ₹151 cr. PAT increased from ₹45 cr to ₹55 cr to ₹68 cr. ROCE rose from 15% to 18%; ROE from 12% to 15%. No auditor qualifications or adverse remarks in the last three years.
ipo:screener-jyoti-global.valuation: Post‑issue EPS (FY24) is ₹12.2, implying a P/E of 22x versus the peer median of 18x (premium ~22%). EV/EBITDA is 14x vs peer median 12x (premium ~17%). P/B stands at 3.2x against peer average 2.8x (premium ~14%). The valuation reflects growth premium but leaves limited upside margin of safety.
ipo:screener-jyoti-global.ipo_structure: Fresh issue constitutes ₹600 cr (70% of total ₹860 cr) and OFS ₹260 cr (30%). Post‑issue promoter holding declines from 65% to 55%. Use of proceeds: ₹350 cr for capex (new press lines and heat‑treatment furnace), ₹150 cr for debt repayment, ₹100 cr for working capital, and ₹60 cr for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed ₹120 cr from domestic mutual funds and insurance companies.
ipo:screener-jyoti-global.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Rajesh Jyoti, a second‑generation entrepreneur with a B.Tech from IIT Delhi, has over 30 years in engineering manufacturing. The board comprises 6 members: 2 executive promoters, 2 independent directors, and 2 nominee directors from anchor investors. Governance practices include a formal audit committee, related‑party transaction policy, and ESG reporting aligned with SEBI guidelines.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising fastener demand from EV and EV‑related auto segments
Improving operating leverage as higher‑margin export sales scale
Debt reduction leading to lower interest cost and higher free cash flow
risks: Raw material (steel) price volatility impacting margins
Intensifying competition from low‑cost imports and larger integrated players
Potential slowdown in domestic auto OEM capex cycles

strengths: Proprietary heat‑treatment technology yielding superior product performance
Long‑term supply agreements with top‑tier OEMs ensuring revenue visibility
weaknesses: Customer concentration with top 5 clients contributing ~45% of sales
Limited geographic diversification beyond South and West India
opportunities: Growth in EV‑specific fastener requirements opening new product lines
Expansion into adjacent markets such as railway and defense components
threats: Steel price spikes due to global supply constraints
Trade protectionism affecting export markets

ipo:screener-jyoti-global.verdict: The IPO offers a growth‑oriented play on India’s auto component revival with a credible management team and improving financials. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking sector exposure may consider applying, while monitoring raw material cost trends and order book momentum. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-jsw-cement.summary: JSW Cement is a fast‑growing integrated cement producer in India, operating 5 integrated plants and 2 grinding units with a capacity of ~14 MTPA, targeting infrastructure and housing demand; the IPO seeks to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to a high‑margin cement play backed by the JSW Group.
ipo:screener-jsw-cement.business_overview: JSW Cement manufactures Ordinary Portland Cement (OPC), Portland Pozzolana Cement (PPC), Portland Slag Cement (PSC), ready‑mix concrete (RMC) and aggregates. Its revenue mix is roughly 70% cement sales, 20% RMC and 10% other products. The company serves infrastructure, housing and industrial segments across South and West India. Average realization stands at ~₹5,200/ton with variable cost ~₹3,200/ton, delivering an EBITDA margin of around 20% and a contribution margin of ~₹2,000/ton.
ipo:screener-jsw-cement.market_analysis: The Indian cement market TAM is approximately ₹8 lakh crore (~$100 bn) in FY24, with JSW’s serviceable addressed market (SAM) in the South and West regions estimated at ₹2 lakh crore. Key competitors include UltraTech, Shree Cement, ACC and Ambuja. JSW’s moat derives from integrated logistics, captive power plants, proximity to ports and synergies with the JSW Group’s steel and energy businesses. Tailwinds: sustained government infrastructure spend, housing demand and urbanization. Headwinds: volatility in coal/petcoke prices, industry overcapacity and stricter environmental norms.
ipo:screener-jsw-cement.financials: Revenue grew from ₹7,200 cr in FY21 to ₹8,500 cr in FY22 and ₹9,800 cr in FY23 (CAGR ~16%). EBITDA rose from ₹1,200 cr (16.7% margin) to ₹1,500 cr (17.6%) and ₹1,800 cr (18.4%) over the same period. PAT increased from ₹600 cr to ₹750 cr to ₹950 cr. ROCE improved from 12% to 13% to 14%; ROE from 15% to 16% to 17%. Audited by Deloitte with no qualifications or emphasis of matter paragraphs.
ipo:screener-jsw-cement.valuation: Based on trailing twelve‑month earnings, JSW Cement commands a P/E of ~25× versus the peer average of ~20×, indicating a modest premium. EV/EBITDA is around 12× compared with the industry average of 10×, also a slight premium. P/B stands at ~3× versus peers at ~2.5×, reflecting a premium valuation due to growth expectations and strong parentage.
ipo:screener-jsw-cement.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹4,500 cr for capacity expansion and an offer for sale (OFS) of ₹1,500 cr from promoters and private‑equity investors. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~58% (down from the current ~65%). Proceeds will be allocated: ~60% to capex (new grinding unit, waste‑heat recovery systems), ~20% to debt repayment and ~20% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed ~₹2,000 cr from domestic mutual funds and insurance companies.
ipo:screener-jsw-cement.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the JSW Group, led by Chairman Sajjan Jindal. The CEO is Jayant Acharya, formerly with UltraTech Cement. The board includes a mix of executive, non‑executive and independent directors with robust audit and nomination committees. Governance practices are strong, with no material related‑party concerns and high disclosure standards.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to 18 MTPA by FY26
Improving realizations from premium products (PSC, PPC)
Cost savings from captive renewable power and logistics
risks: Raw material price volatility (coal, petcoke)
Intensifying competition in South/West markets
Regulatory delays in environmental clearances

strengths: Integrated operations with captive power and port access
Strong parent brand and financial backing
weaknesses: Geographic concentration in South/West India
Higher leverage compared to peers
opportunities: Govt’s ₹100 lakh crore infrastructure push
Growth in affordable housing and green building
threats: Input cost spikes
Environmental regulations limiting capacity expansion

ipo:screener-jsw-cement.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling growth story for long‑term investors seeking exposure to India’s infrastructure‑driven cement demand, though the premium valuation requires confidence in execution of expansion plans. Conservative investors may wait for post‑listing price stability, while aggressive investors can subscribe for the growth upside. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-parth-electrical.summary: Parth Electrical manufactures low-voltage switchgear, distribution transformers, and related electrical components for industrial, infrastructure, and renewable energy projects in India, with a growing export presence; the IPO seeks to raise capital for plant expansion, working capital, and debt reduction, positioning the company to benefit from India's power infrastructure and electrification push.
ipo:screener-parth-electrical.business_overview: Parth Electrical's product portfolio includes MCCBs, MCBs, distribution transformers, panel boards, and custom-engineered solutions. The company serves utilities, EPC contractors, industrial manufacturers, and renewable energy developers. Revenue mix FY24: 55% switchgear, 30% transformers, 15% services and exports. Unit economics show gross margins of ~22% and contribution margins of ~15% on core products, supported by in-house tooling and a supplier base concentrated in Gujarat and Maharashtra.
ipo:screener-parth-electrical.market_analysis: The Indian low-voltage switchgear market is estimated at USD 4.5 bn (TAM) and the distribution transformer market at USD 2.2 bn, together forming a SAM of ~USD 3.0 bn for Parth's addressable segments. Key competitors include Larsen & Toubro, Schneider Electric India, and Havells, but Parth differentiates through localized manufacturing, shorter lead times, and strong relationships with state utilities. Tailwinds: government push for 24×7 power, renewable integration, and smart grid investments. Headwinds: raw material price volatility (copper, steel) and intensifying competition from Chinese imports.
ipo:screener-parth-electrical.financials: Revenue: FY22 ₹312 cr, FY23 ₹378 cr (+21% YoY), FY24 ₹452 cr (+19% YoY). EBITDA: FY22 ₹38 cr, FY23 ₹48 cr (+26%), FY24 ₹58 cr (+21%). PAT: FY22 ₹22 cr, FY23 ₹28 cr (+27%), FY24 ₹35 cr (+25%). Margins: EBITDA margin 12.2% (FY24), PAT margin 7.7%. ROCE: 18% (FY24), ROE: 15%. Auditor: Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu India LLP; no qualifications in the last three years.
ipo:screener-parth-electrical.valuation: Peer median P/E (L&T Electricals, Havells, Siemens) ~28x FY24 earnings; Parth's implied IPO P/E at the upper price band of ₹120 is ~22x, a ~20% discount. EV/EBITDA peer median ~16x; Parth's implied EV/EBITDA ~12x, a ~25% discount. P/B peer median ~3.5x; Parth's implied P/B ~2.1x, reflecting a discount due to lower scale and higher leverage.
ipo:screener-parth-electrical.ipo_structure: Fresh issue of ₹250 cr (60% of total) and OFS of ₹165 cr (40%). Post-issue promoter holding expected at 48% (down from 62%). Use of proceeds: ₹150 cr for new manufacturing line in Vadodara, ₹70 cr for working capital, ₹30 cr for debt repayment. Anchor investors include HDFC Mutual Fund, ICICI Prudential, and SBI Life, committing ~₹80 cr.
ipo:screener-parth-electrical.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Parthiv Shah (MD, 20+ years in electrical engineering) and Ms. Neeta Shah (CFO, CA, former senior finance officer at Crompton Greaves). Board comprises 6 members: 2 promoter directors, 2 independent directors with backgrounds in power sector and finance, and 2 nominee directors from anchor investors. Governance practices include related-party transaction policy, whistleblower mechanism, and regular internal audits; no material governance issues reported.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising switchgear demand from renewable projects
Improving margin profile via operational efficiencies and product mix shift to higher-margin transformers
Strong order book (~₹620 cr) providing revenue visibility for next 18‑24 months
risks: Raw material price volatility impacting gross margins
Intensifying competition from low-cost imports and larger multinational players
Execution risk in scaling new plant and potential delays in commissioning

strengths: Established relationships with state utilities and EPC contractors
In-house tooling and flexible manufacturing enabling quick turnaround
weaknesses: Limited scale compared to multinational peers
Higher working capital intensity due to inventory of copper and steel
opportunities: Government's ₹2.5 trn allocation for power distribution upgrades under Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme
Growth in solar and wind projects requiring customized switchgear solutions
threats: Potential imposition of anti‑dumping duties on Chinese electrical imports altering competitive landscape
Currency fluctuations affecting import costs for raw materials

ipo:screener-parth-electrical.verdict: Parth Electrical offers a compelling play on India's power infrastructure expansion with reasonable valuation discounts to peers. Investors with a medium‑to‑long term horizon and tolerance for cyclical commodity exposure should consider applying, while monitoring raw material costs and execution of the new capex program.

ipo:screener-highway-infra.summary: Highway Infra Ltd is an Indian EPC and O&M player focused on highway construction, maintenance, and toll-operate-transfer projects, with a strong order book and growing presence in expressway contracts. The IPO thesis hinges on secular government spending on roads, margin improvement from O&M toll assets, and deleveraging post‑issue.
ipo:screener-highway-infra.business_overview: The company executes engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) contracts for national and state highways, undertakes operation and maintenance (O&M) of completed projects, and holds equity in select toll-operate-transfer (TOT) assets. Revenue mix FY24: ~70% EPC contracts, ~20% O&M services, ~10% toll revenue from TOT holdings. It serves primarily government clients (NHAI, state PWDs) and private developers. Unit economics show EBITDA margins of 12‑13% on EPC work and 18‑20% on O&M/toll assets, with a cash conversion cycle averaging 90 days due to milestone‑based billing.
ipo:screener-highway-infra.market_analysis: India’s highway construction market (TAM) is estimated at INR 12 lakh crore over FY24‑FY29, with the addressable segment (SAM) for EPC/O&M players of INR 3 lakh crore. The sector benefits from sustained budgetary allocation (NHAI capex ~INR 2.5 lakh crore FY24) and policy push for expressways and TOT models. Competitive landscape includes large integrated firms (L&T, IRB) and niche EPC players. Highway Infra’s moat lies in its executed project track record, relationships with NHAI, and in‑house O&M capabilities. Tailwinds: Gati Shakti, NHAI’s 2025‑30 roadmap, rising private participation. Headwinds: raw material price volatility, interest rate sensitivity, and occasional land acquisition delays.
ipo:screener-highway-infra.financials: Revenue: FY22 INR 1,200 Cr (+0% YoY), FY23 INR 1,400 Cr (+16.7%), FY24 INR 1,650 Cr (+17.9%). EBITDA: FY22 INR 150 Cr (12.5% margin), FY23 INR 180 Cr (12.9% margin), FY24 INR 210 Cr (12.7% margin). PAT: FY22 INR 80 Cr (6.7% margin), FY23 INR 100 Cr (7.1% margin), FY24 INR 120 Cr (7.3% margin). ROCE averaged 15% over FY22‑FY24; ROE averaged 18%. No auditor qualifications or emphasis of matter in the last three annual reports.
ipo:screener-highway-infra.valuation: Based on FY24E earnings, the implied P/E is ~20x versus the peer group median of 18x (L&T, IRB, PNC Infratech), indicating a modest premium. EV/EBITDA is ~12x vs peers ~10x, also a slight premium. P/B stands at ~3.0x compared with peers ~2.5x, reflecting market confidence in growth prospects. The premium is justified by superior O&M margins and a healthier balance sheet post‑IPO.
ipo:screener-highway-infra.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of INR 500 Cr and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 200 Cr, totaling INR 700 Cr. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~55% (down from ~65% pre‑issue). Proceeds will fund new EPC capex (~INR 250 Cr), debt reduction (~INR 150 Cr), and general corporate purposes (~INR 100 Cr). Anchor investors include HDFC Mutual Fund, SBI Life Insurance, and Kotak Mahindra AMC, together committing ~INR 120 Cr.
ipo:screener-highway-infra.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Rajesh Kumar, a civil engineering graduate with 30 years’ experience in highway EPC, previously held senior roles at L&T Construction. The management team comprises a CA‑qualified CFO, a COO ex‑NHAI project director, and a head of O&M with toll‑road expertise. The board has nine members, six of whom are independent, with active audit, nomination, and risk committees. Governance disclosures are robust, with no related‑party transaction concerns flagged in recent filings.
key_drivers: Strong order book of INR 4,500 Cr providing 2‑3 years revenue visibility
Government’s sustained highway capex under Bharatmala and Gati Shakti initiatives
Margin accretion from growing O&M/Toll portfolio and cost‑control measures
risks: Execution delays due to land acquisition or clearance bottlenecks
Input cost volatility (steel, cement, bitumen) impacting EPC margins
Interest rate risk given debt‑financed working capital and equipment loans

strengths: Experienced promoter and management team with deep highway sector knowledge
Diversified revenue mix across EPC, O&M, and toll assets reducing cyclicality
weaknesses: High working capital intensity typical of EPC contracts
Revenue concentration in government‑funded projects (~80% of order book)
opportunities: Expansion into expressway and TOT projects offering higher margin toll revenues
Geographic diversification into neighboring states and potential overseas bidding
threats: Sharp increase in raw material prices squeezing EPC margins
Policy slowdown or budget reallocation affecting highway outlays

ipo:screener-highway-infra.verdict: The IPO provides access to a well‑positioned highway EPC/O&M player with a solid order book, improving margins, and a deleveraging balance sheet post‑issue. It suits long‑term investors comfortable with cyclical infrastructure exposure, while monitoring order book execution and commodity price trends. Directional call: Subscribe.

ipo:screener-aaradhya-dis.summary: Aaradhya Dis. is a niche manufacturer of specialty chemicals serving the agro‑chemical and pharmaceutical sectors, with a growing presence in export markets and a focus on high‑margin custom synthesis. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, positioning the company to capture rising demand for green chemistry solutions.
ipo:screener-aaradhya-dis.business_overview: The company produces intermediates, active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), and specialty agro‑chemicals across three product lines: custom synthesis (45% of revenue), bulk intermediates (35%), and formulation additives (20%). It serves domestic Indian manufacturers and exports to Europe and North America, leveraging a multi‑product plant with flexible batch reactors. Unit economics show gross margins of ~38% and EBITDA margins of ~12% on average order sizes of INR 2–5 crore, supported by long‑term supply contracts.
ipo:screener-aaradhya-dis.market_analysis: The Indian specialty chemicals market is estimated at USD 40 bn (TAM), with the addressable segment for custom synthesis and APIs at USD 8 bn (SAM). Aaradhya Dis. competes with mid‑size players like Navin Fluorine and Fine Organic, differentiating through proprietary catalysts and faster turnaround times. Industry tailwinds include rising outsourcing by global pharma firms and government PLI schemes for chemicals, while headwinds stem from raw‑material price volatility and stringent environmental regulations.
ipo:screener-aaradhya-dis.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 cr in FY21 to INR 285 cr in FY22 and INR 340 cr in FY23 (CAGR ~27%). EBITDA increased from INR 22 cr to INR 30 cr to INR 41 cr over the same period, with EBITDA margin improving from 10.5% to 12.1%. PAT rose from INR 12 cr to INR 18 cr to INR 26 cr. ROCE averaged 14% and ROE 16% in FY23. The statutory auditor has not raised any qualifications in the last three years.
ipo:screener-aaradhya-dis.valuation: Based on FY23 earnings, the implied P/E at the upper price band (INR 120) is ~22x, compared to the peer median P/E of 18x for listed specialty chemical firms. EV/EBITDA stands at ~14x versus peer average of 12x, indicating a modest premium. P/B ratio is ~2.8x vs peer average of 2.2x, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-aaradhya-dis.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 70% fresh equity (INR 250 cr) and 30% offer for sale by existing shareholders. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%. Proceeds will fund a new 50 kt/year plant (60%), debt repayment (25%), and general corporate purposes (15%). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and one foreign portfolio investor, committing INR 75 cr.
ipo:screener-aaradhya-dis.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Agarwal family, with over two decades of experience in chemical manufacturing; the CMD previously headed a division at a listed agro‑chemical firm. The board includes two independent directors with finance and regulatory backgrounds, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI norms, and related‑party transactions are disclosed and approved.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to meet rising export demand
Margin improvement from higher‑value custom synthesis
Benefit from government PLI incentives for specialty chemicals
risks: Raw‑material price volatility impacting gross margins
Intensifying competition from larger integrated players
Potential delays in environmental clearances for new plant

strengths: Proprietary catalyst technology enabling faster turnaround
Diversified customer base across pharma and agro‑chemical sectors
weaknesses: Limited scale compared with global peers
Dependence on a few key raw‑material suppliers
opportunities: Growth in outsourcing of API manufacturing to India
Expansion into green chemistry and bio‑based intermediates
threats: Stringent effluent treatment norms increasing capex
Global supply chain disruptions affecting import of intermediates

ipo:screener-aaradhya-dis.verdict: The IPO offers a reasonable entry point for investors seeking exposure to India’s growing specialty chemicals niche, with solid growth prospects and manageable risks. Conservative investors should monitor raw‑material cost trends and project execution timelines, while aggressive investors may benefit from the expected margin expansion. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-mahendra-realtor.summary: Mahendra Realtor is a mid-sized Indian real estate developer specializing in affordable residential and mixed‑use projects in western India, aiming to capture growth from urbanization and government housing incentives.
ipo:screener-mahendra-realtor.business_overview: The company develops and sells residential apartments, plotted developments, and small commercial spaces.
