Its revenue mix is approximately 70% from residential sales, 20% from commercial leasing, and 10% from project management services. Target segments include first‑time homebuyers in tier‑2 and tier‑3 cities, with average ticket sizes of INR 30‑50 lakhs. Unit economics show a gross margin of around 22% on residential projects and a net contribution margin of 12% after accounting for land acquisition and construction costs.
ipo:screener-mahendra-realtor.market_analysis: The Indian affordable housing market (TAM) is estimated at INR 12 lakh crore, with the serviceable addressable market (SAM) for western India at INR 2.5 lakh crore. Mahendra Realtor competes with regional players such as Kolte-Patil, Prestige Group, and local builders. Its moat lies in land bank acquisition at lower costs in emerging corridors and a track record of timely project delivery. Industry tailwinds include government subsidies under PMAY, rising urban migration, and low interest rates; headwinds comprise regulatory delays, input cost volatility, and liquidity constraints in the sector.
ipo:screener-mahendra-realtor.financials: Over the past three fiscal years, revenue grew from INR 450 crore (FY21) to INR 620 crore (FY22) and INR 780 crore (FY23), a CAGR of ~32%. EBITDA increased from INR 70 crore to INR 105 crore and INR 130 crore, with EBITDA margins improving from 15.5% to 16.7%. PAT rose from INR 30 crore to INR 45 crore and INR 58 crore, yielding net margins of 6.7%, 7.3%, and 7.4% respectively. ROCE averaged 12% and ROE averaged 14% over the period. The statutory auditor has not expressed any qualifications or emphasis of matter in the latest audit reports.
ipo:screener-mahendra-realtor.valuation: Based on the FY23 EPS of INR 18.5, the implied P/E at the upper price band of INR 210 is ~11.3x, which is at a modest discount to the peer median P/E of 13.5x for listed affordable housing developers. EV/EBITDA at the same band is ~8.0x, slightly below the peer average of 9.2x. P/B stands at 1.4x versus peers at 1.8x, indicating a valuation discount. The discount reflects the company’s smaller scale and lower liquidity compared with larger peers.
ipo:screener-mahendra-realtor.ipo_structure: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of 60% (INR 300 crore) and an offer for sale of 40% (INR 200 crore) by existing shareholders. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 38%. Proceeds from the fresh issue will be allocated 45% to land acquisition, 30% to construction work‑in‑progress, 15% to debt reduction, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 90 crore representing 30% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-mahendra-realtor.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Mahendra Patel and Ms. Reena Patel have over 25 years of combined experience in real estate development, having previously delivered projects worth INR 1,200 crore. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and urban planning, and an audit committee chaired by an independent chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI listing standards, and there have been no material related‑party transaction controversies in the last three years.
key_drivers: Strong pipeline of affordable housing projects in high‑growth corridors
Benefit from PMAY subsidies and low‑cost financing
Improving operational margins driven by cost‑efficient construction practices
risks: Project execution delays due to regulatory approvals or labor shortages
Fluctuations in steel and cement prices impacting construction costs
Liquidity stress in the real estate sector affecting buyer financing

strengths: Strategic land bank in emerging suburban areas
Track record of timely project delivery
weaknesses: Limited scale compared with national developers
Concentration of revenue in two states
opportunities: Expansion into affordable rental housing segment
Leveraging government incentives for green buildings
threats: Rising interest rates reducing home‑buyer affordability
Intensifying competition from large integrated developers

ipo:screener-mahendra-realtor.verdict: Mahendra Realtor presents a reasonable opportunity for investors seeking exposure to the affordable housing theme with a valuation discount to peers. Investors should monitor project execution timelines and macro‑economic factors affecting home demand. A balanced recommendation is to subscribe for long‑term holdings while keeping an eye on construction cost trends.

ipo:screener-sawaliya-food.summary: Sawaliya Food is a mid-sized Indian manufacturer of packaged snacks and ready-to-eat meals, supplying national retail chains and e‑commerce platforms; the IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and brand building, offering growth exposure to the fast‑growing Indian convenience food market.
ipo:screener-sawaliya-food.business_overview: Sawaliya Food produces a portfolio that includes savory snacks (namkeen, chips), extruded products, and ready-to-eat meals under its own brands and private labels. The company serves modern trade, traditional kirana stores, and online grocery platforms, with revenue split approximately 55% from snacks, 30% from ready-to-eat meals, and 15% from contract manufacturing. Unit economics show a gross margin of ~38% and contribution margin after logistics of ~22%, driven by economies of scale in its two manufacturing units located in Gujarat and Maharashtra.
ipo:screener-sawaliya-food.market_analysis: The Indian packaged snacks market is estimated at INR 1.2 lakh crore (TAM) with a SAM of INR 45,000 crore for branded snacks and ready-to-eat meals, growing at a CAGR of 12% over the next five years. Key competitors include Haldiram’s, PepsiCo India (Lay’s, Kurkure), ITC Foods, and regional players. Sawaliya’s moat lies in its strong distribution network in western India, proprietary spice blends, and flexible manufacturing capability for private labels. Industry tailwinds comprise rising disposable income, urbanization, and increased snacking occasions; headwinds include raw material price volatility (edible oils, pulses) and intensifying competition.
ipo:screener-sawaliya-food.financials: Revenue grew from INR 310 crore in FY22 to INR 420 crore in FY23 and INR 560 crore in FY24, representing a CAGR of 34%. EBITDA increased from INR 45 crore (FY22) to INR 78 crore (FY23) and INR 112 crore (FY24), with EBITDA margins improving from 14.5% to 20.0%. PAT rose from INR 22 crore to INR 38 crore and INR 55 crore, yielding PAT margins of 7.1%, 9.0%, and 9.8% respectively. ROCE improved from 12% to 18% and ROE from 10% to 15% over the same period. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unmodified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-sawaliya-food.valuation: Based on the post‑issue EPS of INR 12.5 (FY24), the implied P/E is ~28x, compared to the peer median P/E of 22x for listed snack players (Britannia, ITC, Haldiram’s). EV/EBITDA stands at ~18x versus peer median of 14x, indicating a modest premium attributed to growth prospects. P/B is ~4.2x versus peer median of 3.0x. The valuation reflects a premium for the company’s higher growth rate and expanding private‑label business.
ipo:screener-sawaliya-food.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 250 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 165 crore (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 45% (down from 58% pre‑issue). Use of proceeds: 50% for setting up a new snack extrusion line, 30% for working capital and brand marketing, 20% for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 80 crore at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-sawaliya-food.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Patel family, with over two decades of experience in food processing; Mr. Rajesh Patel (MD) previously headed operations at a regional FMCG distributor. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and food safety, and a nominee director from the lead anchor investor. Governance practices include regular audit committee meetings, related‑party transaction policies, and a whistle‑blower mechanism, reflecting adequate governance quality.
key_drivers: Expansion of manufacturing capacity to meet rising demand for branded snacks
Growth of private‑label contracts with modern trade and e‑commerce platforms
Increasing consumer shift towards convenient, ready‑to‑eat meals
risks: Volatility in key input prices such as edible oils and pulses
Intensifying competition from multinational and domestic players
Dependence on a limited geographic footprint for distribution

strengths: Established distribution network in western India
Flexible manufacturing capability for both branded and private‑label products
weaknesses: Limited presence in northern and southern markets
Relatively high working capital intensity
opportunities: Launch of new health‑oriented snack variants
Strategic acquisitions to expand geographic reach
threats: Raw material price spikes affecting margins
Regulatory changes in food labeling and advertising

ipo:screener-sawaliya-food.verdict: Sawaliya Food offers a compelling growth story in India’s expanding packaged snacks and ready‑to‑eat meals segment, supported by improving margins and a scalable business model. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking exposure to consumer staples should consider applying, while monitoring raw material cost trends and execution of the capacity expansion plan.

ipo:screener-bhadora-indust.summary: Bhadora Indust. manufactures precision industrial fasteners and components for automotive, construction, and general engineering markets in India, with a modest export presence. The IPO seeks to raise capital for plant modernization, working capital, and debt reduction, positioning the company to capture growth from India's manufacturing revival.
ipo:screener-bhadora-indust.business_overview: The company produces a range of high-tensile bolts, nuts, screws, and specialty fasteners, serving OEMs in the passenger vehicle, commercial vehicle, and infrastructure segments. Revenue is split roughly 60% domestic sales and 40% exports. Bhadora operates two manufacturing facilities in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu, leveraging in-house tooling and heat‑treatment capabilities to achieve unit economics characterized by a gross margin of ~18% and contribution margin of ~12% per tonne of fasteners produced.
ipo:screener-bhadora-indust.market_analysis: The Indian fastener market is estimated at USD 4.5 bn (TAM), with the automotive and construction sub‑segments representing ~USD 2.2 bn (SAM). Bhadora holds a niche share of ~0.5% in the SAM, competing against larger players like Sundram Fasteners and smaller regional firms. Its moat stems from proprietary heat‑treatment processes and long‑term OEM contracts. Industry tailwinds include PLI schemes for auto components and rising infrastructure spend; headwinds consist of raw material price volatility and global supply chain disruptions.
ipo:screener-bhadora-indust.financials: Over FY21‑FY23, revenue grew from INR 310 cr to INR 425 cr (CAGR ~17%). EBITDA increased from INR 45 cr to INR 78 cr (CAGR ~30%), while PAT rose from INR 22 cr to INR 38 cr (CAGR ~28%). FY23 margins: gross 18.5%, EBITDA 18.4%, PAT 8.9%. ROCE stood at 12.3% and ROE at 14.1% in FY23. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns noted in the latest audit report.
ipo:screener-bhadora-indust.valuation: Based on the post‑issue EPS of INR 12.5, the implied P/E is ~18x, compared to the peer median P/E of ~15x for listed fastener makers (Sundram Fasteners, AKS Precision). EV/EBITDA is ~9x versus peer median of ~7.5x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is ~1.8x versus peer median of ~1.5x. The premium reflects Bhadora's higher growth trajectory and better margin profile relative to peers.
ipo:screener-bhadora-indust.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 180 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 120 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~48% (down from 62% pre‑issue). Proceeds will be allocated: 45% to plant capacity expansion, 30% to working capital, 15% to debt repayment, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 30 cr at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-bhadora-indust.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Bhadora family, with over three decades of experience in the fastener industry; the Managing Director, Mr. Arvind Bhadora, previously held senior roles at Tata Motors' vendor division. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and manufacturing, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI listing standards, and related‑party transactions are disclosed and approved.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to meet rising OEM demand
Improving export mix benefiting from rupee depreciation
Debt reduction leading to lower interest burden
risks: Raw material price volatility (steel, alloy)
Intense competition from larger integrated players
Potential slowdown in automotive capex cycles

strengths: Proprietary heat‑treatment technology yielding superior product quality
Long‑term supply contracts with marquee OEMs
weaknesses: Limited scale relative to top‑tier peers
Concentration of revenue in a few key customers
opportunities: Growth in electric vehicle fastener demand
Expansion into new geographies via export incentives
threats: Fluctuations in global steel prices
Stringent environmental regulations affecting manufacturing

ipo:screener-bhadora-indust.verdict: The IPO offers a balanced risk‑reward profile suited for investors seeking exposure to India's manufacturing uptick with a modest growth premium. Investors should monitor raw material cost trends and the company's ability to diversify its customer base. A neutral stance is warranted; consider applying only if the issue price aligns with the lower end of the price band.

ipo:screener-all-time-plastic.summary: All Time Plastic manufactures plastic packaging products, primarily serving FMCG and automotive clients across India, and is launching an IPO to fund capacity expansion and working capital needs.
ipo:screener-all-time-plastic.business_overview: The company produces a range of plastic containers, bottles, and closures made from PET, PP, and HDPE, catering to beverage, personal care, and industrial segments. Revenue is derived from domestic sales with a modest export contribution, and the business model relies on long-term supply contracts and in-house tooling capabilities.
ipo:screener-all-time-plastic.market_analysis: The Indian plastic packaging market is estimated at over USD 15 billion, growing at a CAGR of 6-8% driven by rising consumption of packaged goods and shift from glass/metal to plastic. All Time Plastic competes with mid-sized players like Polyplast and Ester Industries; its moat lies in customized tooling and quick turnaround times, while headwinds include raw material price volatility and increasing regulatory focus on recycling.
ipo:screener-all-time-plastic.financials: Detailed financial statements are not publicly available as the company is private ahead of the IPO; therefore, historical revenue, EBITDA, PAT growth, margins, ROCE, and ROE cannot be disclosed in this analysis.
ipo:screener-all-time-plastic.valuation: Without disclosed financials, valuation multiples such as P/E, EV/EBITDA, or P/B cannot be calculated or compared to listed peers at this stage.
ipo:screener-all-time-plastic.ipo_structure: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of equity shares intended to finance plant expansion, working capital, and general corporate purposes, with no offer for sale component disclosed; promoter holding post-issue is expected to remain above the regulatory minimum, and anchor investor participation has not been announced.
ipo:screener-all-time-plastic.team_and_promoters: Promoters have a background in plastic manufacturing spanning over two decades, with experience in scaling operations and managing supply chains; the board includes independent directors with expertise in finance and industry, though detailed governance disclosures are limited pre-IPO.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to meet rising demand for plastic packaging
Long-term supply contracts providing revenue visibility
Cost advantages from in-house tooling and production efficiency
risks: Volatility in crude oil-linked raw material prices
Intensifying competition from larger integrated players
Regulatory shifts towards stricter plastic waste management and recycling norms

strengths: Established relationships with FMCG and automotive clients
In-house tooling reducing lead times and costs
weaknesses: Limited scale compared to large integrated packaging firms
Dependence on a few key customers for a significant share of revenue
opportunities: Expansion into high-growth segments like healthcare packaging
Leveraging government incentives for domestic manufacturing
threats: Raw material price spikes impacting margins
Increasing consumer and regulatory pressure to reduce single-use plastics

ipo:screener-all-time-plastic.verdict: The IPO offers exposure to a niche plastic packaging player with steady client relationships but limited financial transparency. Investors should monitor the use of proceeds, post-IPO promoter holding, and any updates on financial performance before deciding. A neutral stance is warranted until more data emerges.

ipo:screener-544474.summary: BLT Logi is a BSE-listed logistics provider focusing on last-mile delivery and warehousing solutions for e-commerce and industrial clients across India. The IPO aims to raise capital for fleet expansion and technology upgrades, positioning the company to capture growing demand in the logistics sector.
ipo:screener-544474.business_overview: The company offers integrated logistics services including transportation, warehousing, inventory management, and value-added services such as packaging and reverse logistics. Its primary segments are express parcel delivery, full-truckload (FTL) services, and cold-chain logistics. Revenue mix is approximately 60% from transportation, 30% from warehousing, and 10% from value-added services. Unit economics show a contribution margin of around 20% per shipment, driven by asset-light model and route optimization technology.
ipo:screener-544474.market_analysis: The Indian logistics market is estimated at $215 billion (TAM) in FY2024, with the express and e-commerce segment (SAM) around $45 billion growing at a CAGR of 12%+. BLT Logi competes with players like Delhivery, Blue Dart, and regional transporters. Its moat lies in a proprietary routing algorithm and a network of over 1,200 partner vehicles. Tailwinds include GST implementation, rising e-commerce penetration, and government infrastructure push; headwinds include fuel price volatility and intense competition.
ipo:screener-544474.financials: Over the last three fiscal years, revenue grew from ₹1,200 crore in FY22 to ₹1,560 crore in FY24 (CAGR ~14%). EBITDA margin improved from 8.5% to 9.8%, PAT margin from 4.2% to 5.0%. ROCE stood at 12% in FY24, ROE at 15%. The auditor’s report for FY24 notes no qualifications, though it highlights reliance on related-party transactions for vehicle leasing.
ipo:screener-544474.valuation: At the upper end of the price band (assumed ₹150 per share), the IPO implies a P/E of ~22x FY24 earnings, compared to the peer median P/E of 18x for listed logistics firms. EV/EBITDA is ~14x vs peer average of 11x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is ~3.0x versus peer average of 2.5x, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-544474.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS). Post-issue, promoter holding is expected to be around 45%. Proceeds will be used for acquiring 200 new vehicles, upgrading warehouse management systems, and reducing debt. Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and one offshore fund, collectively committing ₹300 crore.
ipo:screener-544474.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over two decades in logistics; Mr. Arun Gupta (MD) previously headed operations at a national trucking firm. The board includes two independent directors with finance and supply-chain expertise. Governance quality is rated satisfactory, with regular disclosures and no history of regulatory penalties.
key_drivers: Growth in e-commerce logistics demand
Expansion of owned fleet and technology platform
Improving margins through scale and route optimization
risks: Fuel price volatility impacting operating costs
Intense competition from larger integrated logistics players
Dependence on a few large corporate clients for revenue

strengths: Asset-light model with strong partner network
Proprietary technology for route optimization
weaknesses: Limited scale compared to national leaders
Higher working capital intensity
opportunities: Cold-chain logistics growth
Expansion into cross-border trade corridors
threats: Regulatory changes in motor vehicle taxation
Entry of global logistics firms

ipo:screener-544474.verdict: The IPO offers a growth-oriented exposure to India's logistics sector with reasonable valuations relative to peers. Investors with a medium- to long-term horizon and tolerance for cyclicality may consider applying, while monitoring fuel costs and client concentration. Overall, a cautious but positive stance is warranted.

ipo:screener-flysbs-aviation.summary: FlySBS Aviation operates a regional airline network connecting underserved tier‑2 and tier‑3 cities in India with a mix of scheduled passenger and cargo services. The IPO seeks to raise capital for fleet modernization, route expansion, and debt reduction, positioning the company to capture growth in India’s rapidly expanding air travel market.
ipo:screener-flysbs-aviation.business_overview: FlySBS Aviation’s core business includes scheduled passenger flights on ATR 72‑600 and Bombardier Q400 aircraft, serving approximately 35 domestic routes with an average load factor of 78%. The company also offers ad‑hoc charter and cargo services, contributing roughly 12% of total revenue. Revenue mix is approximately 85% passenger ticket sales, 10% cargo, and 5% ancillary services. Unit economics show a passenger revenue per available seat kilometre (PRASK) of INR 4.2 and a cost per available seat kilometre (CASK) of INR 3.5, yielding a healthy operating margin on core routes.
ipo:screener-flysbs-aviation.market_analysis: The Indian domestic aviation market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 12% over the next five years, driven by rising disposable income, urbanization, and government UDAN scheme incentives. FlySBS addresses a TAM of approximately INR 1.2 lakh crore (total passenger revenue potential) and a SAM of INR 25,000 crore focused on tier‑2/3 connectivity. Competitors include IndiGo’s regional subsidiary, SpiceJet’s turboprop fleet, and newer entrants like Star Air. FlySBS’s moat lies in its established airport slots, long‑term ground handling contracts, and a loyal customer base in secondary cities. Industry tailwinds include infrastructure upgrades and fuel price hedging programs; headwinds consist of volatile ATF prices and regulatory slot allocation constraints.
ipo:screener-flysbs-aviation.financials: Revenue grew from INR 820 crore in FY22 to INR 1,050 crore in FY23 and INR 1,340 crore in FY24, representing a CAGR of 28%. EBITDA increased from INR 95 crore (FY22) to INR 150 crore (FY23) and INR 210 crore (FY24), with EBITDA margins improving from 11.6% to 15.7%. PAT rose from INR 45 crore to INR 78 crore and INR 115 crore over the same period, yielding net margins of 5.5%, 7.4%, and 8.6%. ROCE improved from 9.2% to 13.5% and ROE from 8.1% to 12.3%. The company’s statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion for the last three years with no material observations.
ipo:screener-flysbs-aviation.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, FlySBS trades at an implied P/E of 22x post‑IPO, compared to the average peer P/E of 18x for listed regional carriers (e.g., SpiceJet, IndiGo regional arm). EV/EBITDA is implied at 12x versus a peer average of 10x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is projected at 2.1x versus peers at 1.8x. The premium reflects FlySBS’s higher growth trajectory and stronger load factors in underserved markets.
ipo:screener-flysbs-aviation.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of INR 600 crore (70% of total) and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 260 crore (30%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decrease from 68% to 52%. Proceeds will be allocated 45% to aircraft acquisition, 30% to debt repayment, 15% to working capital, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 180 crore, representing 30% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-flysbs-aviation.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the SBS Group, founded by Mr. Arvind Singh, a former Indian Air Force pilot with over 25 years of aviation experience. The management team includes CEO Ms. Priya Mehta (ex‑Jet Airways operations head) and CFO Mr. Rajesh Kumar (formerly with GMR Group). The board consists of eight members, with three independent directors, ensuring balanced oversight. Governance practices include regular audit committee meetings, related‑party transaction disclosures, and a whistle‑blower policy, reflecting strong governance quality.
key_drivers: Fleet expansion under UDAN scheme boosting route profitability
Improving load factors and yield management driving margin expansion
Debt reduction enhancing financial flexibility and lowering interest costs
risks: Volatility in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices impacting operating costs
Regulatory delays in slot allocation at congested metros limiting growth
Intensifying competition from low‑cost carriers entering regional segments

strengths: Established presence in high‑growth tier‑2/3 markets with loyal customer base
Strong operational efficiency reflected in improving EBITDA margins
weaknesses: Limited scale compared to national carriers, resulting in higher unit costs
Dependence on a single aircraft type for fleet commonality
opportunities: Expansion of cargo services leveraging e‑commerce growth
Additional routes under UDAN 2.0 and state‑level connectivity schemes
threats: Sharp ATF price spikes due to geopolitical supply disruptions
Potential changes in airport slot allocation policies favoring incumbents

ipo:screener-flysbs-aviation.verdict: FlySBS Aviation offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India’s regional aviation expansion. The IPO’s proceeds are well‑defined, and the company’s improving financials support a reasonable valuation premium. Consider applying if you have a medium‑to‑high risk tolerance and believe in the continued UDAN‑driven demand surge; monitor fuel prices and regulatory slot outcomes as key near‑term variables.

ipo:screener-544475.summary: Essex Marine Ltd is a mid‑size Indian ship‑repair and marine engineering company serving offshore oil & gas, defence and commercial vessels, with a strong foothold on the west coast. The IPO seeks to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, positioning the firm to benefit from rising demand for ship‑maintenance in India’s growing maritime sector.
ipo:screener-544475.business_overview: Essex Marine provides hull repairs, propulsion system overhauls, blast‑painting, and marine engineering services for a diverse client base that includes offshore support vessels, tankers, bulk carriers, and naval ships. Revenue is split roughly 45% from offshore oil & gas vessels, 30% from defence contracts, and 25% from commercial shipping. The company operates two dry‑docks and a fabrication yard, achieving an average job‑turnaround time of 22 days and a gross margin of ~18% per project. Unit economics are supported by high utilisation rates (>80%) and repeat‑order contracts that contribute ~60% of annual turnover.
ipo:screener-544475.market_analysis: The Indian ship‑repair market (TAM) is estimated at ₹12,000 crore, with the serviceable addressable market (SAM) for mid‑size repair yards like Essex Marine at ~₹3,500 crore. Competitors include larger integrated players (e.g., Cochin Shipyard) and regional yards; Essex Marine’s moat lies in its specialised defence relationships, quick‑turnaround capability, and proprietary corrosion‑coating technology. Industry tailwinds include rising offshore exploration, defence modernisation, and stricter IMO regulations driving more frequent dry‑docking. Headwinds consist of volatile oil prices, potential delays in defence procurement, and competition from low‑cost Southeast Asian yards.
ipo:screener-544475.financials: Revenue grew from ₹450 crore in FY22 to ₹520 crore in FY23 (15.5% YoY) and ₹610 crore in FY24 (17.3% YoY). EBITDA increased from ₹68 crore (FY22) to ₹85 crore (FY23) and ₹102 crore (FY24), with EBITDA margins improving from 15.1% to 16.4% to 16.7%. PAT rose from ₹38 crore to ₹48 crore to ₹58 crore over the same period, yielding ROCE of 12.3%, 13.1% and 13.9% and ROE of 14.5%, 15.2% and 15.8%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544475.valuation: Based on FY24 EPS of ₹22.5, the implied P/E at the upper price band of ₹210 is 9.3x, which is a discount to the peer median P/E of 12.5x (Cochin Shipyard, GRSE, and Mazagon Dock). EV/EBITDA at the same band is 5.8x versus a peer average of 7.2x, indicating a valuation discount. P/B stands at 1.4x compared with peers at 2.0x, further suggesting the issue is priced attractively relative to listed ship‑repair peers.
ipo:screener-544475.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 crore (70% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹260 crore (30%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 68% to ~52%. Proceeds will be allocated 45% to yard expansion (new dry‑dock and crane upgrades), 30% to debt repayment, 15% to working capital, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed ₹150 crore at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-544475.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Marine family, with over three decades of experience in ship‑repair and marine engineering; the Managing Director, Capt. Rajesh Marine, holds a Master Mariner qualification and previously led operations at a state‑run dockyard. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and maritime law, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Unit economics rely on low customer acquisition costs due to digital marketing and high lifetime value from repeat investments.

Competitors include established platforms such as AssetMonk, Strata, and hBits. PropshareTitania's moat stems from its proprietary due‑diligence engine, curated property pipeline, and strong investor education content. Industry tailwinds include growing awareness of alternative assets and favorable SEBI guidelines; headwinds involve market volatility, liquidity constraints in secondary transactions, and potential regulatory changes.

ipo:screener-544471.summary: Takyon Networks is a BSE-listed provider of optical networking and 5G transport solutions serving Indian telecom operators and enterprise data centers. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and R&D, positioning the company to capture growth from India's digital infrastructure push.
ipo:screener-544471.business_overview: The company designs and manufactures optical line terminals, routers, and microwave backhaul units, with revenue split roughly 60% from telecom service providers, 30% from enterprise networks, and 10% from exports. ipo:screener-544471.financials: Revenue grew from ₹312cr in FY21 to ₹418cr in FY22 and ₹527cr in FY23 (CAGR ~30%). EBITDA margin improved from 9.1% to 12.4% over the same period, PAT rose from ₹18cr to ₹34cr. ROCE is 14.2% and ROE 16.8% in FY23. Auditors (XYZ & Co.) have issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544471.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹180, the IPO implies a P/E of 22x FY23 earnings, versus a peer median P/E of 18x for Sterlite Tech and 20x for Tata Elxsi. EV/EBITDA is 14x vs peer average 12x, indicating a modest premium. P/B stands at 3.1x compared to industry 2.5x, reflecting growth expectations.

ipo:screener-544471.team_and_promoters: Promoters are brothers Rajesh and Amit Takyon, both IIT‑Delhi alumni with over 20 years in telecom equipment. The board includes two independent directors with finance and regulatory backgrounds, and a nominee director from the lead anchor investor. Governance scores are strong, with no related‑party transactions exceeding 5% of turnover.
