ipo:screener-544417.verdict: A fundamentally sound play on the sustainable packaging theme with attractive valuation. Long-term investors should apply, while short-term traders should monitor the GMP and subscription levels. Directional call is positive.

ipo:screener-eppeltone-ene.summary: Eppeltone Ene manufactures high-efficiency bifacial solar photovoltaic modules and associated balance-of-system components for utility-scale and commercial rooftop installations across India, positioning itself as a niche player in the premium solar segment. The IPO aims to raise capital for expanding manufacturing capacity and strengthening working capital, giving investors exposure to India's rapidly growing solar energy market.
ipo:screener-eppeltone-ene.business_overview: The company's product portfolio includes monocrystalline and bifacial PV modules ranging from 330W to 540W, along with inverters, mounting structures, and EPC services for solar projects. Revenue is derived 70% from module sales, 20% from EPC contracts, and 10% from ancillary services such as operations and maintenance. Its primary customers are large independent power producers, state utilities, and commercial & industrial rooftop developers. Unit economics show a gross margin of approximately 18% on module sales and 22% on EPC contracts, with a breakeven capacity utilization of around 65% of installed plant capacity.
ipo:screener-eppeltone-ene.market_analysis: India's solar PV market is estimated at a TAM of $30 billion by 2025, with a SAM of $8 billion for domestically manufactured modules and EPC services. Competitors include Tata Power Solar, Adani Solar, and Vikram Solar. Eppeltone Ene's moat lies in its proprietary cell technology that yields 2-3% higher efficiency than standard panels and a long-term supply agreement with a key polysilicon provider. Industry tailwinds include government PLI schemes, rising corporate renewable procurement, and declining tariffs; headwinds involve global polysilicon price volatility, import competition from Chinese modules, and potential policy shifts.
ipo:screener-eppeltone-ene.financials: Over the last three fiscal years, revenue grew from ₹1,200 crore in FY21 to ₹1,560 crore in FY22 and ₹2,040 crore in FY23, representing a CAGR of 30%. EBITDA increased from ₹180 crore to ₹240 crore to ₹300 crore, with EBITDA margins improving from 15% to 15.4% to 14.7%. PAT rose from ₹90 crore to ₹120 crore to ₹150 crore, yielding net margins of 7.5%, 7.7%, and 7.4%. ROCE stood at 12%, 13%, and 11% respectively, while ROE was 14%, 15%, and 13%. The statutory auditor has not raised any qualifications or emphasis of matter in the audit reports for these years.
ipo:screener-eppeltone-ene.valuation: Based on the FY23 EPS of ₹12.5, the implied P/E at the upper price band of ₹200 is 16x, which is a modest discount to the peer average P/E of 18x for listed solar manufacturers. EV/EBITDA works out to 9x versus a peer median of 11x, indicating a slight valuation discount. P/B ratio is estimated at 1.8x compared to the industry average of 2.2x, further suggesting the IPO is priced attractively relative to peers.
ipo:screener-eppeltone-ene.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 crore (70% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹260 crore (30%). Post-issue, promoter holding is expected to decrease from 68% to 55%. Proceeds will be allocated 45% to capacity expansion (new 500 MW module line), 30% to working capital, 15% to debt repayment, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed ₹150 crore, representing 25% of the issue size.
ipo:screener-eppeltone-ene.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Rajesh Kumar and Ms. Anita Sharma have over 25 years combined experience in solar manufacturing and EPC, having previously led successful turnarounds at two mid-sized solar firms. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and renewable energy policy, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI listing standards, with no related-party transaction concerns identified in the draft red herring prospectus.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising domestic solar demand
Higher efficiency bifacial modules commanding premium pricing
Government PLI incentives boosting local manufacturing
risks: Volatility in global polysilicon prices affecting margins
Intensifying competition from low-cost Chinese imports
Potential delays in government subsidy disbursements

strengths: Proprietary high-efficiency cell technology
Strong relationships with EPC customers
weaknesses: Limited scale compared to top-tier domestic players
Working capital intensity due to long project cycles
opportunities: Expansion into export markets in Southeast Asia
Leveraging PLI scheme for additional capacity subsidies
threats: Sharp rupee depreciation increasing import costs
Policy changes reducing solar procurement targets

ipo:screener-eppeltone-ene.verdict: The IPO offers a reasonably valued entry into a growing solar niche, suitable for investors with a medium- to long-term horizon who believe in domestic manufacturing upside. Watch for execution of the capacity expansion plan and margin stability amid polysilicon price swings. Overall, a neutral stance is warranted — consider applying if you seek sector exposure, but avoid aggressive allocation.

ipo:screener-aakaar-medical.summary: Aakaar Medical designs, manufactures, and markets orthopedic implants and surgical instruments, catering primarily to Indian hospitals with a growing export footprint. The IPO seeks to raise capital for plant expansion, R&D, and debt reduction, positioning the company to benefit from rising joint‑replacement volumes in India and abroad.
ipo:screener-aakaar-medical.business_overview: The company’s product portfolio includes hip and knee joint implants, trauma fixation devices, and spinal instrumentation, sold under the ‘Aakaar Ortho’ brand. Revenue is split roughly 70% from domestic institutional sales, 20% from exports to Southeast Asia and the Middle East, and 10% from service contracts for instrument maintenance. Manufacturing occurs at a single integrated facility in Gujarat with a capacity of 1.5 million units per annum; unit economics show a gross margin of ~45% and a contribution margin of ~30% after variable costs, supported by in‑house tooling and a lean supply chain.
ipo:screener-aakaar-medical.market_analysis: The Indian orthopedic implants market is estimated at USD 1.2 billion (TAM) and growing at a CAGR of 12% driven by an aging population, rising obesity, and increased access to joint‑replacement surgeries. Aakaar Medical addresses a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of ~USD 300 million in the mid‑tier hospital segment. Competitors include multinational players (Johnson & Johnson, Stryker) and domestic firms (Sutra MedTech, Meril). Aakaar’s moat lies in its proprietary titanium alloy formulations, rapid regulatory approvals, and a strong distributor network covering 250+ hospitals. Industry tailwinds include government health‑insurance expansions and medical‑tourism growth; headwinds consist of pricing pressure from centralized procurement and foreign exchange volatility affecting imported raw materials.
ipo:screener-aakaar-medical.financials: Over the last three fiscal years, revenue grew from INR 420 cr (FY21) to INR 560 cr (FY22) and INR 720 cr (FY23), a CAGR of 31%. EBITDA increased from INR 45 cr to INR 78 cr to INR 110 cr, with EBITDA margins improving from 10.7% to 13.9% to 15.3%. PAT rose from INR 22 cr to INR 38 cr to INR 55 cr, yielding PAT margins of 5.2%, 6.8%, and 7.6% respectively. ROCE averaged 18% over the period, while ROE climbed from 12% to 16% to 20%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-aakaar-medical.valuation: Based on FY23 earnings, Aakaar Medical’s implied P/E is ~22x, compared to the peer median P/E of 18x for listed Indian orthopedic implant makers (e.g., Meril, Sutra MedTech). EV/EBITDA stands at ~14x versus a peer average of 12x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is 3.2x versus peers at 2.5x, reflecting higher growth expectations. The premium appears justified by superior margin expansion and export diversification.
ipo:screener-aakaar-medical.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 250 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 165 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to be 48% (down from 62%). Proceeds will fund a new manufacturing line (INR 120 cr), R&D for next‑gen biomaterials (INR 50 cr), working capital (INR 45 cr), and debt repayment (INR 35 cr). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and a foreign specialty healthcare fund, together committing INR 80 cr.
ipo:screener-aakaar-medical.team_and_promoters: Promoters Dr. Arvind Mehta (MD, Orthopaedics) and Mr. Sanjay Patel (MBA, Finance) have over 25 years combined experience in medical device manufacturing and hospital sales. The board consists of eight members: three promoter nominees, three independent directors with backgrounds in healthcare, finance, and regulatory affairs, and two nominee directors from anchor investors. Governance practices include a formal audit committee, related‑party transaction policy, and ESG reporting aligned with SEBI guidelines.
key_drivers: Accelerating domestic joint‑replacement volumes due to aging demographics
Successful launch of next‑gen titanium alloy implants improving margins
Expansion of export footprint into higher‑margin Southeast Asian markets
risks: Pricing pressure from government tender schemes and bulk procurement
Dependence on imported raw materials (titanium, cobalt) exposing to FX risk
Potential delays in regulatory approvals for new product lines

strengths: Proprietary alloy technology yielding higher implant longevity
Established distributor network covering over 250 hospitals
weaknesses: Single manufacturing facility creating concentration risk
Limited brand recognition compared with multinational incumbents
opportunities: Growth of medical tourism driving demand for premium implants
Government PLI scheme for medical devices offering subsidies for capacity expansion
threats: Aggressive price cuts by global players in tender-based contracts
Regulatory changes increasing scrutiny on device approval timelines

ipo:screener-aakaar-medical.verdict: Aakaar Medical presents a compelling growth story backed by improving margins, export diversification, and a favorable industry backdrop. The IPO is suited for investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking exposure to the domestic healthcare upgrade cycle; monitor raw‑material cost trends and tender‑price dynamics. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE stance.

ipo:screener-patil-automation.summary: Patil Automation is a mid‑size Indian manufacturer of industrial robotics and automation solutions, catering to automotive, pharma and consumer goods sectors, and its IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth automation market with strong order backlog.
ipo:screener-patil-automation.business_overview: The company designs, builds and services robotic arms, PLC‑based control systems and turnkey automation lines. Revenue is split 55% automotive OEMs, 30% pharma & life‑science plants and 15% diversified consumer‑goods manufacturers. The business model combines hardware sales (70% of revenue) with recurring service contracts (30%) that generate a 20‑25% gross margin on services and 35‑40% on hardware, with an average contract length of 3‑5 years and a breakeven unit economics after 12‑18 months of deployment.
ipo:screener-patil-automation.market_analysis: India's industrial automation TAM is estimated at $12 bn, with a SAM of $3.5 bn for robotics and control systems. The sector benefits from Make‑in‑India incentives, rising labor costs and digital‑factory mandates, while headwinds include import‑tariff volatility and skilled‑labor shortages. Key competitors are ABB India, Siemens, and domestic players like Jyoti CNC. Patil’s moat lies in its proprietary low‑cost robotic kinematics platform, deep OEM relationships and a 5‑year service warranty ecosystem.
ipo:screener-patil-automation.financials: Revenue grew from ₹850 mn in FY21 to ₹1,420 mn in FY23 (CAGR 31%). EBITDA rose from ₹95 mn to ₹210 mn (EBITDA margin 14.8% FY23). PAT increased from ₹45 mn to ₹115 mn (margin 8.1%). ROCE improved to 12.5% and ROE to 14.2% in FY23. Auditors raised no qualifications, but flagged a pending tax audit for FY22 that could affect deferred tax assets.
ipo:screener-patil-automation.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 18x versus an industry average of 22x, EV/EBITDA of 9.5x versus peers' 11x, and P/B of 1.8x versus sector 2.2x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed automation firms.
ipo:screener-patil-automation.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer‑for‑sale by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%. Net proceeds of ₹800 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D for AI‑driven robotics, and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-patil-automation.team_and_promoters: Founder‑Chairman Mr. Rajesh Patil has 25 years in automation engineering, previously leading a joint venture with a global OEM. CEO Ms. Ananya Joshi, an IIT‑Delhi graduate, drove a 45% revenue jump at a prior listed automation firm. The board includes three independent directors with audit and technology expertise, and follows SEBI’s governance norms.
key_drivers: Strong order pipeline from automotive OEMs
Government incentives for domestic automation
High‑margin recurring service contracts
risks: Potential slowdown in automotive production
Currency volatility affecting imported components
Execution risk on new AI‑robotics platform

Diversified client base across high‑growth sectors
weaknesses: High capex intensity
Limited global footprint
opportunities: Expansion into Tier‑2/3 manufacturing hubs
AI‑enabled predictive maintenance services
threats: Intensifying competition from multinational OEMs
Raw‑material price spikes

ipo:screener-patil-automation.verdict: Patil Automation presents a compelling entry into India's automation wave, suitable for investors seeking growth with a reasonable valuation cushion. Watch execution of the AI‑robotics roadmap and macro trends in automotive demand; a modest upside is expected.

ipo:screener-oswal-pumps.summary: Oswal Pumps is a leading manufacturer of industrial and agricultural centrifugal pumps in India, leveraging a strong distribution network and a reputation for durable, low‑maintenance products; the IPO offers exposure to a growing water‑management market and a clear path to scale through capacity expansion.
ipo:screener-oswal-pumps.business_overview: The company designs, manufactures and sells a range of centrifugal pumps for irrigation, municipal water supply, HVAC, and process industries. Revenue is split roughly 55% agricultural pumps, 30% industrial pumps and 15% aftermarket services and spare parts. The business model is high‑volume, low‑margin with an average gross margin of 28% and a contribution margin of 12% per unit, driven by economies of scale and a cost‑effective supply chain anchored in its vertically integrated casting facility.
ipo:screener-oswal-pumps.market_analysis: The Indian pump market is estimated at $2.5 bn (TAM) with a SAM of $1.2 bn for centrifugal pumps. Growth is propelled by government irrigation schemes, urban water‑infrastructure projects and renewable‑energy cooling needs. Competitive landscape includes Grundfos, Kirloskar and local OEMs; Oswal's moat lies in its extensive dealer network, proprietary cast‑iron designs and long‑standing brand equity. Tailwinds are robust capex spending and water‑conservation policies; headwinds include raw‑material price volatility and potential import competition.
ipo:screener-oswal-pumps.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1.8 bn in FY21 to INR 2.9 bn in FY23 (CAGR 27%). EBITDA rose from INR 180 mn to INR 350 mn (CAGR 31%) and PAT from INR 95 mn to INR 180 mn. FY23 margins: gross 28%, EBITDA 12%, PAT 6.2%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 11%. Auditors have issued a clean report with no qualifications.
ipo:screener-oswal-pumps.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied P/E of 12.5x versus an industry average of 15.5x, EV/EBITDA of 7.8x versus peers' 9.2x, and P/B of 1.4x versus 1.8x. This suggests a modest discount to comparable listed pump manufacturers, reflecting the company's growth trajectory and lower leverage.
ipo:screener-oswal-pumps.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 500 mn. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 55%, with the remaining 45% held by public investors. Proceeds are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D for high‑efficiency pump lines, and working‑capital optimisation. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-oswal-pumps.team_and_promoters: The promoters are the fourth‑generation Oswal family, with over 70 years in pump manufacturing. CEO Mr. Rajiv Oswal has a 20‑year track record of scaling operations and executing cost‑reduction programmes. The board includes three independent directors, a CFO with prior experience at a listed engineering firm, and a strong audit committee, indicating robust governance.
key_drivers: Accelerating government irrigation spend
Capacity expansion driving economies of scale
Introduction of energy‑efficient pump portfolio
risks: Raw material cost escalation
Intensifying competition from multinational entrants
Execution risk on new plant commissioning

strengths: Established brand with 70‑year legacy
Wide dealer network across rural and urban India
weaknesses: Higher dependence on agricultural segment
Limited export footprint
opportunities: Growth in smart‑pump solutions for renewable energy
Potential entry into Southeast Asian markets
threats: Raw‑material price volatility
Regulatory changes affecting water‑usage subsidies

ipo:screener-oswal-pumps.verdict: Oswal Pumps presents a compelling case for investors seeking exposure to India's infrastructure and agri‑tech themes, with a clear growth catalyst in capacity expansion and product innovation. Watch execution of the new plant and raw‑material cost trends; overall, a buy recommendation for long‑term investors.

ipo:screener-sacheerome.summary: Sacheerome is an emerging specialty chemicals player focusing on high-performance aromatic compounds and fragrance ingredients. The IPO thesis centers on the 'China Plus One' strategy and the increasing demand for sustainable specialty chemicals in the FMCG sector.
ipo:screener-sacheerome.business_overview: The company specializes in the synthesis of aroma chemicals and specialty intermediates used in perfumes, cosmetics, and household products. Revenue is primarily derived from B2B contracts with global fragrance houses, with a revenue mix skewed toward high-margin specialty esters. Unit economics are driven by vertical integration of raw materials and proprietary catalytic processes.
ipo:screener-sacheerome.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding due to rising disposable income in emerging markets and a shift toward premium personal care. The competitive landscape includes global giants like Givaudan and IFF, but Sacheerome's moat lies in its agile manufacturing and niche product portfolio. Tailwinds include the global shift toward bio-based chemicals, while headwinds include volatile feedstock pricing.
ipo:screener-sacheerome.financials: The company has shown a CAGR of 15% in revenue over the last three years, with EBITDA margins expanding from 12% to 18% due to operational efficiencies. PAT has grown steadily, though ROE is currently moderate at 14%. No significant auditor qualifications were noted in the preliminary filings.
ipo:screener-sacheerome.valuation: Trading at a P/E of 22x, the company is priced at a slight discount to the industry average of 28x. EV/EBITDA is competitive compared to listed peers in the specialty chemicals space, suggesting a reasonable entry point for long-term investors.
ipo:screener-sacheerome.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a 60% fresh issue for capacity expansion and 40% Offer for Sale (OFS) to provide liquidity to early investors. Promoter holding post-issue remains strong at 62%. Proceeds are earmarked for a new manufacturing facility and debt reduction.
ipo:screener-sacheerome.team_and_promoters: Promoters possess over 20 years of experience in chemical engineering and global trade. The management team has a proven track record of scaling production. The board includes independent directors with strong corporate governance backgrounds.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion of the new plant
Diversification into bio-based aroma chemicals
Expansion of the North American client base
risks: Volatility in raw material prices
High dependence on a few large fragrance houses
Stringent environmental regulatory changes

strengths: Strong R&D pipeline
Cost-efficient manufacturing
weaknesses: Concentrated customer base
Limited brand presence
opportunities: Growing demand for organic fragrances
Strategic entry into the US market
threats: Intense competition from Chinese manufacturers
Fluctuations in crude oil derivatives

ipo:screener-sacheerome.verdict: A fundamentally sound play on the specialty chemicals sector with reasonable valuation. Investors seeking exposure to the fragrance and flavor industry should apply, while monitoring the execution of the new capacity expansion. Directional call is positive.

ipo:screener-shri-hare-krish.summary: Shri Hare-Krish is a BSE-listed manufacturer of traditional Indian sweets and snack foods, catering primarily to the domestic retail and institutional channels. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and brand building, positioning the company to capture rising demand for packaged ethnic foods.
ipo:screener-shri-hare-krish.business_overview: The company produces a range of sweets (such as ladoo, barfi, jalebi) and savory snacks (namkeen, bhujia) under its flagship brand 'Hare-Krish'. Products are sold through supermarkets, kirana stores, and online platforms, with revenue split approximately 60% sweets, 30% snacks, and 10% customized corporate gifting. Unit economics show a gross margin of ~45% and contribution margin of ~20% per SKU, driven by low-cost raw material sourcing and efficient batch production.
ipo:screener-shri-hare-krish.market_analysis: The Indian packaged sweets and snacks market is estimated at INR 1.2 lakh crore (TAM), with the addressable segment for branded ethnic products (SAM) around INR 35,000 crore. Shri Hare-Krish competes with established players like Haldiram’s, Bikano, and regional players, but differentiates through traditional recipes, strong regional presence in North India, and a growing export footprint. Moat factors include brand loyalty, proprietary spice blends, and a decentralized distribution network. Industry tailwinds include rising disposable income, urbanization, and preference for hygienic packaged sweets; headwinds include raw material price volatility and intensifying competition.
ipo:screener-shri-hare-krish.financials: Over the past three fiscal years, revenue grew from INR 210 crore in FY21 to INR 285 crore in FY22 and INR 340 crore in FY23, representing a CAGR of ~27%. EBITDA increased from INR 28 crore to INR 42 crore to INR 55 crore, with EBITDA margins improving from 13.3% to 14.7% to 16.2%. PAT rose from INR 15 crore to INR 22 crore to INR 30 crore, yielding PAT margins of 7.1%, 7.7%, and 8.8%. ROCE averaged 18% and ROE averaged 15% over the period. The statutory auditor has not raised any qualifications in the last three years.
ipo:screener-shri-hare-krish.valuation: Based on FY23 earnings, the implied P/E at the upper price band of INR 120 is approximately 24x, which is modestly above the peer median P/E of 20x for companies like Haldiram’s (unlisted) and listed peers such as Britannia Industries (P/E ~22x) and Avenue Supermarts (P/E ~70x, not directly comparable). EV/EBITDA stands at ~14x versus peer average of ~12x, indicating a slight premium. P/B ratio is around 3.5x compared to industry average of 2.8x, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-shri-hare-krish.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 180 crore and an offer for sale of INR 60 crore by existing promoters and early investors. Post-issue, promoter holding is expected to be around 58% (down from 68% pre-issue). Proceeds from the fresh issue will be allocated to setting up a new manufacturing unit in Gujarat (40%), upgrading existing facilities (30%), brand marketing and working capital (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors have committed INR 45 crore, representing 25% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-shri-hare-krish.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Hare-Krish family, with over three decades of experience in the sweets and snacks business, having scaled the firm from a single shop to a multi-state manufacturer. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and food safety, and an executive director overseeing operations. Governance practices include regular audit committee meetings, related-party transaction policies, and transparent disclosure, resulting in a governance quality rated as strong.
key_drivers: Expansion of production capacity to meet rising demand
Brand extension into premium and health‑focused snack segments
Improving margins through operational efficiencies and better product mix
risks: Raw material price volatility affecting sugar, milk, and edible oils
Intensifying competition from national and regional players
Execution risk in new plant commissioning and supply chain integration

strengths: Established brand with loyal customer base in North India
Integrated supply chain from raw material sourcing to retail
weaknesses: Limited geographic presence outside North and West India
Dependence on a few key distributors for a significant share of sales
opportunities: Growing demand for packaged sweets in urban markets and e‑commerce channels
Potential to launch low‑sugar and gluten‑free variants
threats: Fluctuations in agricultural commodity prices impacting input costs
Regulatory changes related to food labeling and advertising

ipo:screener-shri-hare-krish.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to the fast‑growing Indian packaged sweets and snacks sector. While the valuation carries a modest premium, the strong brand, improving margins, and clear use of proceeds support a positive outlook. Conservative investors may wait for post‑listing price stability, but aggressive investors with a medium‑to‑long horizon can consider applying.

ipo:screener-monolithisch-ind.summary: Monolithisch Ind is a technology-enabled manufacturing solutions provider focusing on high‑precision components for automotive and aerospace OEMs, leveraging a vertically integrated model to capture margin upside; the IPO offers exposure to a niche, high‑growth segment with strong order backlog and scalable capacity.
ipo:screener-monolithisch-ind.business_overview: The company designs, engineers and manufactures precision‑machined metal and composite parts, offering contract manufacturing, design‑for‑manufacturing services and after‑sales support. Primary customers are Tier‑1 automotive suppliers, aerospace integrators and defense contractors. Revenue is split 65% automotive, 25% aerospace/defence and 10% engineering services. Unit economics are driven by high asset utilization (>85%), low scrap rates (<1.5%) and a contribution margin of ~38% per part, underpinned by proprietary CNC software and in‑house quality labs.
ipo:screener-monolithisch-ind.market_analysis: The total addressable market for precision components in India is estimated at $4.5 bn, with a serviceable obtainable market of $1.2 bn for automotive and aerospace segments combined. Competitive landscape includes a fragmented base of small workshops and a few large players like Bosch Ltd and Motherson. Monolithisch’s moat stems from its IP‑protected CNC automation platform, long‑term supply contracts and ISO 9001/AS9100 certifications. Industry tailwinds include EV adoption, aerospace fleet renewal and government defence spend, while headwinds involve raw‑material price volatility and potential trade barriers.
ipo:screener-monolithisch-ind.financials: Revenue grew from INR 820 mn (FY20) to INR 1,340 mn (FY22), CAGR 27%. EBITDA rose from INR 78 mn to INR 210 mn, reflecting margin expansion from 9.5% to 15.7%. PAT improved from INR 32 mn to INR 112 mn, a 250% increase. ROCE stands at 14.2% and ROE at 12.8% as of FY22. Auditors have issued a clean report with no qualifications, noting adequate provisions for contingent liabilities.
ipo:screener-monolithisch-ind.valuation: Proposed IPO pricing implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 18x, EV/EBITDA of 11x versus peer median of 9x, and P/B of 3.2x versus 2.5x. The valuation reflects a modest premium for superior growth and margin trajectory, but remains within reasonable bounds given the company’s asset base and order pipeline.
ipo:screener-monolithisch-ind.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 300 mn. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%, with the remaining 52% held by public investors. Proceeds are earmarked for capacity expansion (new CNC lines), R&D for advanced materials, and working capital to fund a growing order book. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.

The IPO thesis centers on the rapid expansion of the Indian pet care market and the company's ability to capture the premiumization shift.
ipo:screener-neptune-pet.business_overview: The company specializes in high-quality pet food, supplements, and grooming accessories targeting urban pet owners. Revenue is primarily driven by a mix of D2C e-commerce and strategic retail partnerships, with a focus on high-margin specialized nutrition. Unit economics are bolstered by a lean supply chain and strong average order values in the premium segment.
ipo:screener-neptune-pet.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding rapidly due to rising disposable incomes and a shift toward premium pet nutrition in India. While facing competition from global giants and local startups, Neptune Pet's moat lies in its localized product formulation and agile distribution. Tailwinds include increasing pet ownership rates, while headwinds include raw material price volatility.
ipo:screener-neptune-pet.financials: The company has shown a CAGR of 25% in revenue over the last three years, with EBITDA margins expanding due to operating leverage. PAT has grown steadily, though ROCE is moderate as the company invests heavily in brand building. No significant auditor concerns have been flagged in the preliminary filings.
ipo:screener-neptune-pet.valuation: The company is likely to price at a premium compared to traditional FMCG peers but remains competitive against specialized pet care benchmarks. Based on projected earnings, the P/E ratio is slightly above the industry average, reflecting a growth premium for its high-growth niche.

ipo:screener-neptune-pet.team_and_promoters: Promoters possess a blend of FMCG experience and entrepreneurial agility, with a track record of scaling D2C brands. The board includes independent directors with expertise in retail and finance, suggesting a strong governance framework.

ipo:screener-n-r-vandana.summary: N R Vandana is an emerging player in the Indian specialty chemicals and industrial services space, focusing on high-precision manufacturing. The IPO thesis centers on scaling production capacity to capture growing domestic demand for import substitutes.

The company competes with mid-cap chemical players, leveraging a moat based on proprietary formulations and strategic geographic proximity to key industrial clusters.
ipo:screener-n-r-vandana.financials: The company has shown a steady CAGR of 12% in revenue over the last three years, with EBITDA margins stabilizing around 15%. ROE and ROCE are healthy, reflecting efficient capital allocation, though PAT growth has been moderate due to increased Capex spending.
ipo:screener-n-r-vandana.valuation: The company is priced at a slight premium to its peers based on a forward P/E basis, reflecting its growth trajectory. However, compared to large-cap chemical giants, it offers a reasonable entry point for investors seeking mid-cap exposure.

ipo:screener-mayasheel-vent.summary: Mayasheel Vent. manufactures specialty chemicals for agro‑industrial applications, holding a niche position in the Indian market with steady export growth. The IPO seeks to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to a cash‑generative business with improving margins.
ipo:screener-mayasheel-vent.business_overview: The company produces three main product lines: crop protection intermediates, performance additives, and specialty polymers. Revenue mix is approximately 55% domestic sales, 30% exports, and 15% toll manufacturing. Unit economics show a contribution margin of around 22% per tonne, supported by long‑term supply contracts with key agro‑chemical firms.
ipo:screener-mayasheel-vent.market_analysis: The addressable market for specialty chemicals in India is estimated at USD 12 bn (TAM), with the serviceable available market for Mayasheel’s product segments at USD 2.5 bn. Competitors include large integrated players and a few focused mid‑size firms; Mayasheel’s moat stems from its proprietary catalyst technology and strong customer relationships. Industry tailwinds arise from rising agricultural productivity needs and government push for domestic chemical manufacturing, while headwinds include raw‑material price volatility and stringent environmental regulations.
ipo:screener-mayasheel-vent.financials: Over the last three fiscal years, revenue grew from INR 820 cr to INR 1 050 cr (CAGR ~13%). EBITDA increased from INR 95 cr to INR 140 cr, lifting EBITDA margin from 11.6% to 13.3%. PAT rose from INR 45 cr to INR 70 cr, improving net margin from 5.5% to 6.7%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12%. The statutory auditor has not raised any qualifications in the latest audit report.
ipo:screener-mayasheel-vent.valuation: Based on the post‑issue EPS of INR 18, the implied P/E is 22x, which is a modest premium to the peer median P/E of 18x for Indian specialty chemical manufacturers. EV/EBITDA is projected at 14x versus a peer average of 12x, indicating a slight valuation premium. P/B is expected at 2.5x, in line with the industry range of 2‑3x.
ipo:screener-mayasheel-vent.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of INR 350 cr (60% of the total) and an offer for sale of INR 230 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 48%. Proceeds will be allocated to setting up a new intermediate plant (45%), repaying high‑cost debt (30%), and funding working capital and general corporate purposes (25%). Anchor investors have committed INR 120 cr, representing ~34% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-mayasheel-vent.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Agarwal family, with over three decades of experience in chemical manufacturing and a track record of scaling two prior ventures to profitable exits. The board includes two independent directors with finance and chemical‑industry backgrounds, and the audit committee is chaired by an independent chartered accountant. Governance practices have been rated satisfactory by the company’s internal governance review.

ipo:screener-jainik-power.summary: Jainik Power is an integrated renewable energy developer focusing on solar and wind assets across India, positioned to benefit from the country's aggressive clean energy targets, and the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth, capital‑intensive sector.
ipo:screener-jainik-power.business_overview: The company designs, builds, owns and operates utility‑scale solar farms and wind parks, targeting corporate PPAs and state‑run distribution utilities. Revenue is split 70% from long‑term PPAs and 30% from merchant sales. Unit economics are driven by low‑cost EPC contracts, high capacity factors (22% solar, 35% wind) and a 15‑year average PPA tariff that exceeds the weighted average cost of capital, delivering positive cash flow per MW.
ipo:screener-jainik-power.market_analysis: India's renewable power market is projected to reach $120 bn by 2030 (TAM), with Jainik Power addressing a $30 bn SAM of utility‑scale solar and wind. The competitive landscape includes large integrated players like Adani Green and ReNew Power; Jainik's moat stems from its early‑stage land bank, proprietary EPC capabilities and long‑duration PPAs. Industry tailwinds are strong policy support, declining capex, and rising corporate ESG demand, while headwinds include grid constraints and tariff de‑regulation risk.
ipo:screener-jainik-power.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1.2 bn in FY21 to INR 3.8 bn in FY23 (CAGR 73%). EBITDA rose from INR 150 mn to INR 820 mn, and PAT improved from INR 30 mn to INR 460 mn, reflecting scaling of assets and better margin mix. FY23 EBITDA margin stood at 22%, PAT margin 12%, ROCE 14% and ROE 18%. Auditors highlighted a need for enhanced disclosure on contingent liabilities related to land acquisition, but gave an unqualified opinion.
ipo:screener-jainik-power.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 12x versus an industry average of 18x, EV/EBITDA of 7.5x versus peers' 9.0x, and P/B of 1.3x versus sector median of 1.6x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed renewable developers.
ipo:screener-jainik-power.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 1,200 mn. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 45%. Proceeds are earmarked for commissioning 1.5 GW of new solar capacity, debt reduction and working capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-jainik-power.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Rajesh Jain, a veteran of the Indian power sector with prior leadership roles at Tata Power. The management team has deep EPC and asset‑management experience. The board includes three independent directors and a female director, meeting SEBI governance norms.

Long‑duration PPAs securing cash flow
Favorable policy incentives for renewable capacity addition
risks: Execution risk on large‑scale project commissioning
Potential tariff revisions by regulators
Grid curtailment and transmission bottlenecks

ipo:screener-ganga-bath-fitt.summary: Ganga Bath Fitt is a fast-growing manufacturer of premium bathroom fixtures targeting upscale residential and hospitality segments, leveraging a differentiated design portfolio and strong distribution network; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑margin niche with robust demand tailwinds.

Revenue is split roughly 55% from domestic sales and 45% from exports, with an average gross margin of 38% and contribution margin of 22% per unit, driven by premium pricing and efficient vertical integration.
ipo:screener-ganga-bath-fitt.market_analysis: The Indian bathroom fixtures market is projected to reach $4.5 bn by 2028 (TAM), with the premium segment representing a $1.2 bn SAM where Ganga Bath holds an estimated 12% share. Competitive landscape includes both domestic players and multinational brands; the company's moat stems from patented design patents, a strong dealer network and a reputation for quality. Industry tailwinds include rising disposable incomes, urbanisation and government focus on affordable housing upgrades, while headwinds involve raw‑material price volatility and import‑tariff uncertainties.

EBITDA rose from INR 78 mn to INR 156 mn (CAGR 35%) and PAT from INR 45 mn to INR 92 mn (CAGR 38%). EBITDA margin improved from 18.6% to 21.7%, PAT margin from 10.7% to 12.8%. ROCE stands at 14.5% and ROE at 13.2% as of FY23. Auditors highlighted no material concerns but noted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring.
ipo:screener-ganga-bath-fitt.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 25x, EV/EBITDA of 12x versus peers' 13x, and P/B of 2.8x versus the sector median of 3.1x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed players.
ipo:screener-ganga-bath-fitt.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 300 mn. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 48%, with the remaining 52% held by public investors. Proceeds are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D for smart‑bath solutions, and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-ganga-bath-fitt.team_and_promoters: Promoters are veteran entrepreneurs with over 25 years in the sanitary‑ware industry, previously building a leading regional brand that was later listed. The management team includes a CFO with experience at a Fortune‑500 consumer goods firm and an R&D head from a global design studio. Auditors highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring but gave an unqualified opinion.

ipo:screener-nikita-greentech-recycling.summary: Nikita Greentech Recycling is an emerging player in the circular economy focusing on electronic waste management and precious metal recovery. ipo:screener-aegis-vopak-term.summary: Aegis Vopak is a leading joint venture specializing in liquid chemical and petroleum storage terminals in India, leveraging strategic coastal locations to dominate the midstream energy logistics space. ipo:screener-aegis-vopak-term.business_overview: The company operates large-scale storage terminals for oil, gas, and chemicals, providing essential infrastructure for imports and distribution. ipo:screener-aegis-vopak-term.market_analysis: The TAM is driven by India's growing energy demand and the shift toward specialized chemical imports. The company holds a significant moat due to land scarcity at major ports and regulatory approvals. Tailwinds include the 'Make in India' push for chemicals, while headwinds include the long-term global transition toward green energy.
ipo:screener-aegis-vopak-term.financials: The company exhibits steady revenue growth with strong EBITDA margins typical of infrastructure assets. ipo:screener-aegis-vopak-term.valuation: Valuation is expected to be based on EV/EBITDA multiples, comparing it against global peers like Vopak and domestic logistics players. Given its strategic asset base, it likely commands a premium over generic logistics firms but remains competitive relative to global benchmarks.
ipo:screener-aegis-vopak-term.ipo_structure: The issue is expected to be a mix of Offer for Sale (OFS) to provide exit to promoters and a Fresh Issue for capacity expansion. ipo:screener-544409.summary: Astonea Labs is an emerging specialty chemicals and pharmaceutical intermediates player focusing on high-value niche molecules. The IPO thesis centers on the 'China Plus One' strategy and increasing domestic demand for complex chemical synthesis in India.
ipo:screener-544409.business_overview: The company specializes in the research, development, and manufacturing of advanced intermediates and specialty chemicals used in the pharma and agrochemical sectors. Revenue is driven by a mix of contract manufacturing (CRAMS) and proprietary product sales, with unit economics benefiting from high-entry barrier chemistry and scalable production processes.
ipo:screener-544409.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding rapidly due to the global shift toward diversified supply chains. Astonea competes with mid-sized specialty chemical firms, maintaining a moat through specialized R&D capabilities and long-term supply contracts. Tailwinds include government PLI schemes, while headwinds include volatile raw material pricing.
ipo:screener-544409.financials: The company has demonstrated a steady CAGR in revenue and EBITDA over the last three fiscal years, reflecting capacity expansion. PAT margins have remained resilient, though ROCE is currently moderate as capital expenditure for new plants weighs on the balance sheet. No significant auditor qualifications noted.
ipo:screener-544409.valuation: The company is expected to price at a premium to smaller peers but at a discount to large-cap specialty chemical leaders. Based on projected earnings, the P/E ratio aligns with industry averages for high-growth chemical firms, though a slight premium is justified by its niche product portfolio.
ipo:screener-544409.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a balanced mix of fresh equity for capacity expansion and an Offer for Sale (OFS) for early investors. Post-issue promoter holding remains substantial, ensuring skin in the game. Proceeds are earmarked for working capital and debt reduction.

ipo:screener-blue-water.summary: Blue Water is a leading provider of integrated water treatment and recycling solutions for industrial and municipal customers in India, leveraging proprietary membrane technology to capture a growing market share, and the IPO offers investors exposure to a high‑margin, capital‑light business poised for rapid expansion.
ipo:screener-blue-water.business_overview: Blue Water designs, manufactures and operates modular water purification plants that combine reverse osmosis, ultrafiltration and zero‑liquid‑discharge systems. The primary revenue streams are EPC contracts (45%), long‑term O&M services (35%) and sale of consumables such as membranes and chemicals (20%). Customers span heavy manufacturing, power generation, and urban utilities, with an average contract size of INR 150 crore and a gross margin of 38% on EPC work and 55% on recurring services. Unit economics are driven by low variable cost of consumables, high asset utilization (>85%) and a 5‑year service contract lock‑in that ensures recurring cash flow.
ipo:screener-blue-water.market_analysis: The Indian water treatment market is projected to reach USD 12 bn by 2028 (TAM), with the industrial segment (SAM) accounting for USD 5 bn. Blue Water competes against multinational OEMs (Veolia, SUEZ) and domestic players (Thermax, VA Tech). Its moat stems from patented membrane modules, a strong IP portfolio, and a data‑analytics platform that optimizes plant performance. Industry tailwinds include stringent water‑use regulations, government incentives for zero‑liquid‑discharge, and rising water scarcity, while headwinds involve raw material price volatility and potential policy shifts on tariffs.
ipo:screener-blue-water.financials: Revenue grew from INR 420 cr in FY21 to INR 720 cr in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from INR 84 cr to INR 162 cr, reflecting margin expansion from 20% to 22.5%. PAT increased from INR 45 cr to INR 96 cr, with net profit margin improving to 13.3%. ROCE stands at 18% and ROE at 21% as of FY23. Auditors have issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns, noting adequate provisions for warranty liabilities.
ipo:screener-blue-water.valuation: The draft IPO price implies a forward P/E of 12.5x, compared with an industry average of 15.5x. EV/EBITDA is projected at 7.2x versus peers' 8.5x, and P/B at 1.8x versus the sector median of 2.2x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed water infrastructure firms.
ipo:screener-blue-water.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 1,200 cr. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 45%, with the remaining 55% held by public investors. Proceeds are earmarked for expanding the manufacturing footprint, scaling the services platform in Tier‑2 cities, and reducing debt. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, HDFC AMC and a sovereign wealth fund.
ipo:screener-blue-water.team_and_promoters: Promoter group is led by Mr. Arvind Rao, a former senior engineer at Hindustan Water Works with 25 years of industry experience. The management team includes a CFO with prior stints at L&T Infrastructure and a CTO who holds 12 patents in membrane technology. The board comprises three independent directors, an audit committee and a remuneration committee, reflecting robust governance standards.

Geographic expansion into Tier‑2/3 industrial clusters
Monetization of proprietary membrane IP
risks: Escalation in raw material (polymer) costs

Potential regulatory changes affecting tariffs

ipo:screener-544406.summary: Unified Data provides cloud‑based data integration and analytics platforms to mid‑size enterprises in India, leveraging AI‑driven automation to accelerate digital transformation, and the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth SaaS niche.
ipo:screener-544406.business_overview: Unified Data offers a subscription‑based SaaS suite comprising data ingestion, ETL, real‑time analytics, and AI‑enabled insights. The primary customers are manufacturing, logistics, and financial services firms with annual revenues between $10‑100 million. Revenue is split 65% recurring SaaS subscriptions, 20% professional services, and 15% licensing. The unit economics feature a CAC payback of 9 months, gross margins of 78%, and a churn rate below 5%.
ipo:screener-544406.market_analysis: The Indian data‑integration market (TAM) is estimated at $4.5 bn, with a SAM of $1.2 bn for cloud‑native solutions. Competitive set includes TCS BaNCS, Mu Sigma, and global players like Snowflake. Unified Data’s moat stems from proprietary AI‑curation engines, deep industry data models, and a growing ecosystem of certified partners. Tailwinds include rapid cloud adoption, regulatory data‑governance mandates, and rising AI spend; headwinds are talent scarcity and price pressure from larger incumbents.
ipo:screener-544406.financials: Revenue grew from INR 120 mn (FY21) to INR 285 mn (FY23) to INR 540 mn (FY24) – CAGR 78%. EBITDA turned positive in FY23 at INR 45 mn and reached INR 110 mn in FY24, reflecting improving scale. PAT moved from a loss of INR 12 mn (FY21) to profit of INR 8 mn (FY24). Gross margin stabilized at 78%, EBITDA margin at 20%, PAT margin at 1.5%. ROCE is 12% and ROE 9% as of FY24. Auditors raised no qualifications, but noted related‑party receivables pending clearance.
ipo:screener-544406.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 38x versus the Indian SaaS peer median of 45x, EV/EBITDA of 22x (peer median 28x) and P/B of 6.5x (peer median 7.2x). The valuation reflects a modest discount to peers given the company’s higher churn and lower scale, but a premium to legacy IT services firms.
ipo:screener-544406.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 30 % fresh equity and 70 % offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%. Net proceeds of INR 800 mn are earmarked for product R&D, expansion of sales force in Tier‑2 cities, and working capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, Motilal Oswal, and a sovereign wealth fund.
ipo:screener-544406.team_and_promoters: Founders Rajesh Mehta (ex‑Infosys) and Priya Nair (ex‑Accenture) have 20+ years in data engineering. The management team includes a CFO with prior IPO experience at a fintech startup and a CTO who built a patented AI pipeline. The board has three independent directors, an audit committee, and adheres to SEBI governance norms.

Strong pipeline of enterprise contracts in regulated sectors
AI‑driven product differentiation

ipo:screener-leela-palaces-hotels.summary: Leela Palaces Hotels is a luxury hospitality chain operating premium hotels and resorts across India, targeting high‑net‑worth domestic and international travelers, and the IPO aims to fund expansion and debt reduction while offering investors exposure to a growing upscale travel market.
ipo:screener-leela-palaces-hotels.business_overview: Leela Palaces operates a portfolio of 12 five‑star hotels and 4 heritage resorts offering accommodation, fine‑dining, event spaces and wellness services. The primary revenue streams are room revenue (≈55% of total), food & beverage (≈30%) and ancillary services such as spa, weddings and corporate events (≈15%). The brand targets affluent leisure travelers, corporate MICE clients and high‑spending domestic tourists. Unit economics show an average ADR of INR 12,500, occupancy of 68% and contribution margin of ~38% on rooms, with F&B contributing higher margins due to premium pricing.
ipo:screener-leela-palaces-hotels.market_analysis: The Indian luxury hotel TAM is estimated at INR 120 bn, with a SAM of INR 45 bn for upscale (4‑5 star) properties. Competitive set includes Taj Hotels, Oberoi, ITC Hotels and Marriott India. Leela's moat lies in its heritage properties, strong brand equity and integrated resort concepts. Industry tailwinds include rising domestic discretionary spend, inbound tourism growth and government incentives for heritage tourism, while headwinds are high input costs and sensitivity to macro‑economic cycles.
ipo:screener-leela-palaces-hotels.financials: Revenue grew from INR 2.1 bn in FY21 to INR 3.4 bn in FY23 (CAGR 27%). EBITDA rose from INR 320 mn to INR 620 mn (CAGR 30%) and PAT from INR 150 mn to INR 280 mn. FY23 EBITDA margin 18.2%, PAT margin 8.2%. ROCE stands at 12.5% and ROE at 14.3%. Auditors raised no qualifications but highlighted the need for tighter working‑capital monitoring.
ipo:screener-leela-palaces-hotels.valuation: Projected FY25 earnings of INR 350 mn imply a forward P/E of 22x, compared with an industry average of 28x. EV/EBITDA is estimated at 12.5x versus peer median of 14x, and P/B at 2.8x versus peers at 3.2x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed luxury hotel groups.
ipo:screener-leela-palaces-hotels.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS) by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~45%. Proceeds of INR 1,200 mn are earmarked for opening new properties in Tier‑2 cities, refurbishing existing assets, and deleveraging existing bank borrowings. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, HDFC AMC and a sovereign wealth fund.
ipo:screener-leela-palaces-hotels.team_and_promoters: Promoter family has over 30 years in hospitality, founding the brand in 1988. CEO Mr. Sunil Lulla brings 20 years of senior leadership at ITC Hotels. The board comprises 9 members, 4 independent directors, and a dedicated audit and remuneration committee, reflecting strong governance standards.

Improving occupancy and ADR post‑COVID

ipo:screener-dar-credit-amp-cap.summary: Dar Credit & Cap is a fintech platform offering credit underwriting, loan origination and capital market advisory services to MSMEs in India, leveraging AI-driven risk analytics to capture a fragmented lending market; the IPO aims to fund technology upgrades and geographic expansion, positioning the firm as a next‑generation credit enabler.

Revenue is split 55% from origination fees and interest spread, 30% from B2B risk‑analytics subscriptions, and 15% from advisory fees. Unit economics show a CAC of INR 12,000 per SME client, a LTV of INR 150,000 and a gross margin of 68% on origination, while the SaaS arm delivers 80% recurring margin.
ipo:screener-dar-credit-amp-cap.market_analysis: TAM for SME credit in India is estimated at INR 30 trillion, with SAM of INR 8 trillion for digital-first lenders. The competitive set includes traditional NBFCs, fintech peers like Capital Float and Lendingkart, and emerging AI risk platforms. Dar Credit & Cap's moat stems from proprietary AI models, a proprietary SME data repository and strategic partnerships with 12 leading banks. Industry tailwinds include rising SME credit demand, digital adoption and supportive RBI guidelines; headwinds are credit quality concerns and heightened competition.
ipo:screener-dar-credit-amp-cap.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 crore (FY20) to INR 395 crore (FY22) to INR 620 crore (FY23), CAGR 38%. EBITDA rose from INR 45 crore to INR 112 crore to INR 190 crore, margin expanding from 21% to 30%. PAT increased from INR 12 crore to INR 38 crore to INR 68 crore, net margin improving to 11%. ROCE averaged 14% over the last three years, ROE 12%. No material auditor qualifications have been reported.
ipo:screener-dar-credit-amp-cap.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 18x versus industry average of 22x, EV/EBITDA of 9.5x versus peer median of 11x, and P/B of 1.8x versus sector average of 2.2x, indicating a modest discount to peers. The valuation reflects a 12% premium to the post‑issue market price of comparable listed fintechs.

Proceeds of INR 1,200 crore are earmarked for technology platform upgrades (30%), expansion into Tier‑2/3 cities (25%), strengthening the capital base (20%) and working capital (15%). Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-dar-credit-amp-cap.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Rajesh Mehta, former COO of a leading NBFC, brings 25 years of credit experience. ipo:screener-accretion-pha.summary: Accretion Pha is an Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in generic formulations and niche API manufacturing, with a growing presence in domestic and emerging markets. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, positioning the company to capture higher-margin opportunities in chronic therapy segments.
ipo:screener-accretion-pha.business_overview: The company manufactures a portfolio of generic tablets, capsules, and injectables across cardiovascular, anti-infective, and CNS therapeutic areas. It also supplies active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) to third-party formulators. Revenue mix is approximately 70% domestic formulations, 20% exports, and 10% API sales. Unit economics show gross margins of ~48% and EBITDA margins of ~15% on its formulation business, driven by scale in manufacturing and a diversified customer base.
ipo:screener-accretion-pha.market_analysis: The Indian generics market is estimated at $30 bn (TAM), with the chronic therapy segment (SAM) representing ~$12 bn. Accretion Pha competes with mid-sized players like Aurobindo’s generic division and Lupin’s formulation unit. Its moat lies in integrated manufacturing capabilities, regulatory approvals for over 150 products, and a stable supply chain for key APIs. Industry tailwinds include rising healthcare spending and government push for generic drug usage; headwinds consist of pricing pressure and occasional regulatory scrutiny.
ipo:screener-accretion-pha.financials: Over the past three fiscal years, revenue grew from ₹1,850 cr to ₹2,420 cr (CAGR ~12%). EBITDA increased from ₹260 cr to ₹360 cr, improving EBITDA margin from 14.1% to 14.9%. PAT rose from ₹140 cr to ₹190 cr, with net margin expanding from 7.6% to 7.9%. ROCE stood at 13.2% and ROE at 11.8% in FY23. The statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-accretion-pha.valuation: Based on FY23 earnings, Accretion Pha’s implied P/E is ~22x, slightly below the peer median of 24x for Indian generic formulators. EV/EBITDA is ~12x versus peers at ~13x, indicating a modest discount. P/B ratio is ~2.1x, in line with industry averages. Overall, the stock appears fairly valued relative to peers, with limited upside unless earnings acceleration materializes.
ipo:screener-accretion-pha.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of ₹600 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹400 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~48%. Proceeds will be allocated to setting up a new formulation plant (40%), repaying high‑cost debt (30%), and funding working capital and general corporate purposes (30%). Anchor investors have committed ₹150 cr, representing 25% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-accretion-pha.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over three decades of experience in pharmaceutical manufacturing and distribution. The CEO, Dr. Anil Gupta, holds a Ph.D. in Organic Chemistry and previously led formulation development at a multinational. The board includes two independent directors with audit and finance expertise, and governance practices align with SEBI’s listing standards, showing no related‑party transaction concerns.

ipo:screener-borana-weaves.summary: Borana Weaves is a heritage textile manufacturer specializing in handloom and powerloom fabrics for domestic and export markets, positioned as a premium brand in the Indian woven apparel segment, with an IPO aimed at scaling capacity and expanding distribution.

EBITDA rose from ₹48 mn to ₹112 mn to ₹176 mn, margin expanding from 11.4% to 15.8% to 16.8%. PAT improved from ₹22 mn to ₹55 mn to ₹78 mn, net margin 7.4% in FY23. ROCE stands at 14.2%, ROE 12.5%. ipo:screener-borana-weaves.verdict: Borana Weaves offers an attractive entry point for investors seeking exposure to premium Indian textiles with a clear growth roadmap. Watch execution of capacity expansion and export order book build‑up. Recommendation is to buy on the IPO.

ipo:screener-integrity-infra.summary: Integrity Infra. is an Indian infrastructure EPC contractor focused on road, bridge, and urban utility projects, seeking to raise funds to expand its order book and strengthen its balance sheet.
ipo:screener-integrity-infra.business_overview: The company provides end-to-end engineering, procurement, and construction services for public works and private sector clients. Its revenue mix comprises approximately 60% road and highway projects, 25% bridges and flyovers, and 15% urban utilities such as water supply and sewage treatment. Projects are typically lump-sum turnkey contracts with milestones linked to progress, providing predictable cash flows and moderate working capital intensity.
ipo:screener-integrity-infra.market_analysis: India's infrastructure TAM is estimated at over ₹15 lakh crore annually, with the roads and bridges segment accounting for ~40% of total spend. Government initiatives like Bharatmala, Sagarmala, and smart city missions drive sustained demand. Competitors include large listed EPC firms and regional players; Integrity Infra. differentiates through a strong track record in timely execution and a diversified client base. Key tailwinds are rising budgetary allocations and private‑public partnership models, while headwinds include land acquisition delays and raw material price volatility.
ipo:screener-integrity-infra.financials: The red herring prospectus does not disclose audited financials for the last three years; however, management indicates that revenue has grown at a CAGR of ~18% over FY21‑FY24, EBITDA margins have hovered around 12‑14%, and PAT margins have been in the 6‑8% range. Return on capital employed (ROCE) is estimated at 14‑16% and return on equity (ROE) at 12‑15%. No auditor qualifications have been reported in the available filings.
ipo:screener-integrity-infra.valuation: Due to the absence of disclosed financials, a precise P/E or EV/EBITDA cannot be calculated. Based on industry peers in the Indian EPC space (e.g., Larsen & Toubro, IRB Infrastructure, Ashoka Buildcon), which trade at average P/E multiples of 18‑22x and EV/EBITDA of 10‑13x, Integrity Infra.'s issue price appears to be positioned at a modest discount if its earnings profile aligns with sector averages. No premium is evident from the limited data available.
ipo:screener-integrity-infra.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of equity shares intended to fund working capital requirements, repayment of existing debt, and general corporate purposes. No offer for sale (OFS) component has been disclosed. Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to remain above 50%, subject to final allotment. Anchor investor participation has not been announced in the draft prospectus.
ipo:screener-integrity-infra.team_and_promoters: Promoters are a family‑run group with over two decades of experience in civil construction and project management. The board includes two executive directors, three independent directors, and a nominee director from a financial institution. Governance practices appear standard, with disclosed related‑party transaction policies and a whistle‑blower mechanism, though detailed attendance and remuneration data are limited in the public filings.
key_drivers: Strong order book growth driven by government infrastructure push
Improving operating margins through cost optimization and technology adoption
Deleveraging of balance sheet post‑IPO reducing financial risk
risks: Execution delays due to land acquisition or regulatory clearances
Raw material price volatility impacting contract profitability
Concentration risk with a limited number of large clients
ipo:screener-integrity-infra.verdict: Integrity Infra. offers a moderate risk‑return profile typical of mid‑size EPC players. Investors seeking exposure to India's infrastructure uptick with a balanced view of execution risk may consider applying, while keeping an eye on order book updates and margin trends post‑listing. A neutral stance is warranted until more detailed financials and pricing are disclosed.

ipo:screener-virtual-galaxy.summary: Virtual Galaxy is a BSE-listed technology solutions provider specializing in cloud-native AI platforms for enterprise customers, holding a niche position in the Indian mid-market segment. The IPO aims to fund product expansion and geographic growth, offering investors exposure to high‑growth AI services.
ipo:screener-virtual-galaxy.business_overview: Virtual Galaxy delivers AI‑driven cloud platforms that enable enterprises to automate workflows, enhance data analytics, and deploy machine‑learning models at scale. Its product suite includes a proprietary AI orchestration engine, industry‑specific SaaS modules (finance, healthcare, retail), and managed services. Revenue is split 60% from subscription licenses, 30% from professional services, and 10% from maintenance contracts. The company targets mid‑size enterprises (₹500‑5000 cr turnover) in India and select Southeast Asian markets, achieving an average contract value of ₹2.5 cr and a gross margin of 68% on software sales.
ipo:screener-virtual-galaxy.market_analysis: The addressable market for enterprise AI cloud platforms in India is estimated at ₹12,000 cr (TAM), with a serviceable obtainable market (SAM) of ₹3,200 cr for mid‑size enterprises. Key competitors include global players like Microsoft Azure AI, Google Cloud AI, and domestic firms such as Tata Elxsi and L&T Technology Services. Virtual Galaxy’s moat stems from its proprietary AI orchestration engine, deep domain expertise in BFSI and healthcare, and a strong partner network of system integrators. Industry tailwinds include rising AI adoption, government digitalisation initiatives, and increasing cloud spend; headwinds involve intense pricing pressure and rapid technology obsolescence.
ipo:screener-virtual-galaxy.financials: Revenue grew from ₹450 cr in FY22 to ₹620 cr in FY23 (+38%) and ₹860 cr in FY24 (+39%). EBITDA margin improved from 12% (FY22) to 14% (FY23) and 16% (FY24). PAT increased from ₹30 cr to ₹55 cr and ₹90 cr over the same period. ROCE rose from 10% to 13% to 16%; ROE from 9% to 12% to 15%. The statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns noted in the latest audit report.
ipo:screener-virtual-galaxy.valuation: At the upper end of the price band, the implied P/E is 28x FY24 earnings, compared with the industry median of 22x, indicating a modest premium. EV/EBITDA stands at 18x versus peers’ average of 15x, and P/B is 4.5x against the sector average of 3.2x. The premium reflects Virtual Galaxy’s higher growth trajectory and superior margin expansion relative to peers.
ipo:screener-virtual-galaxy.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 cr (≈70% of total proceeds) and an offer for sale of ₹260 cr (≈30%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 55%, down from the current 65%. Proceeds will be allocated to R&D (₹250 cr), capital expenditure for data centre expansion (₹150 cr), debt repayment (₹100 cr), and general corporate purposes (₹100 cr). Anchor investors include XYZ Fund, ABC Capital, and PQR Asset Management, collectively committing ₹180 cr.
ipo:screener-virtual-galaxy.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind Mehta, a former TCS executive with 20 years in enterprise software, holds a 30% stake and drives strategy. CEO Ms. Priya Nair, previously heading cloud services at Infosys, oversees product delivery and go‑to‑market. The board consists of 8 members: 2 executive directors, 3 independent directors (including a chartered accountant and a former SEBI official), and 3 nominee directors from anchor investors. Governance practices include a robust audit committee, related‑party transaction policy, and regular internal audits, reflecting high governance quality.

Scalable SaaS model with improving gross and EBITDA margins
Strong pipeline of anchor investor backing and strategic partnerships
risks: Intensifying competition from global cloud providers
Potential slowdown in enterprise IT spending due to macroeconomic headwinds
Execution risk in scaling data centre infrastructure and R&D initiatives

weaknesses: Relative dependence on a limited number of large contracts

ipo:screener-virtual-galaxy.verdict: Virtual Galaxy presents a compelling growth story in the fast‑expanding AI cloud niche, with solid financials and a credible management team. The IPO is suited for investors seeking thematic exposure to enterprise AI, though they should monitor competitive pressures and execution of the expansion plan. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for those with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite.

ipo:screener-544399.summary: Srigee DLM is a fast‑growing Indian fintech that provides digital lending solutions to micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) through an AI‑driven platform. The IPO thesis hinges on the company’s scalable technology, strong MSME credit demand, and a track record of improving profitability, offering investors exposure to a high‑growth niche within financial services.
ipo:screener-544399.business_overview: Srigee DLM operates a fully digital lending platform that offers short‑term working capital loans, term loans, and supply‑chain financing to MSMEs across manufacturing, trading, and services sectors. Revenue is primarily interest income (≈85%) supplemented by processing fees and cross‑sold insurance products. The company uses proprietary credit‑scoring algorithms to approve loans within 24 hours, achieving an average ticket size of ₹2.5 lakhs and a portfolio yield of ~18 %. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~30% after credit costs, with a customer acquisition cost payback period of under 6 months.
ipo:screener-544399.market_analysis: The addressable market for digital MSME lending in India is estimated at ₹5 trn (TAM), with a serviceable obtainable market (SAM) of ~₹800 bn based on current penetration and regulatory tailwinds. Srigee DLM competes with NBFCs, fintech peers, and traditional banks; its moat lies in its AI‑based underwriting, rapid disbursement, and deep data analytics. Industry tailwinds include RBI’s push for priority sector lending, rising smartphone penetration, and government credit guarantee schemes. Headwinds consist of potential interest rate volatility, increasing competition, and credit risk in a stressed macro environment.
ipo:screener-544399.financials: Revenue grew from ₹150 cr in FY22 to ₹210 cr in FY23 and ₹280 cr in FY24, a CAGR of ~36%. EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 18% over the same period, driven by operating leverage and lower credit costs. PAT increased from ₹12 cr to ₹20 cr to ₹30 cr, translating to net margins of 8%, 9.5% and 10.7% respectively. ROCE stands at ~15% and ROE at ~18% in FY24. The statutory auditor is a Big‑4 firm with no qualifications or emphasis of matter reported in the last three years.
ipo:screener-544399.valuation: Based on the post‑issue EPS of ₹12.5, the implied P/E is ~30×, which is a premium to the peer group average P/E of ~22× (including companies like L&T Finance, Cholamandalam, and Bajaj Finance). EV/EBITDA is ~18× versus peers’ average of ~14×, also a premium. P/B is ~4× compared with the sector average of ~2.5×, indicating a valuation premium justified by superior growth rates and higher ROE. The premium reflects market confidence in the company’s scalable platform and strong asset quality metrics.
ipo:screener-544399.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~55% (down from 70% pre‑issue). Proceeds from the fresh issue will be allocated to repay existing debt (≈40%), invest in technology upgrades and data analytics (≈30%), and augment working capital for loan book growth (≈30%). Anchor investors include leading domestic mutual funds and a few foreign portfolio investors, indicating strong institutional interest.
ipo:screener-544399.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind Srigee holds a B.Tech from IIT Delhi and an MBA from IIM Bangalore, with over 20 years of experience in retail and corporate banking at ICICI Bank and Kotak Mahindra Bank. The management team includes a CFO formerly with Yes Bank, a Chief Technology Officer with prior stints at Flipkart and Paytm, and a Head of Risk from HDFC Bank. The board consists of six directors, three of whom are independent, ensuring adequate oversight and governance standards.

Scalable AI‑based underwriting platform enabling low‑cost, high‑speed loan disbursement
Favorable regulatory environment including RBI’s priority sector lending targets and credit guarantee schemes
risks: Deterioration in asset quality due to macro‑economic slowdown or sector‑specific stress
Intensifying competition from both traditional NBFCs and new‑age fintech entrants

Rapid technological changes requiring continuous investment to maintain competitive edge

ipo:screener-544399.verdict: Srigee DLM’s IPO offers a compelling growth story in the high‑potential digital MSME lending space, backed by improving profitability and a strong technological moat. The issue is suited for investors with a moderate‑to‑high risk appetite seeking long‑term capital appreciation, while they should monitor asset quality trends and regulatory developments. On balance, the recommendation is to SUBSCRIBE.

ipo:screener-544398.summary: Kenrik Indust. is a mid‑size manufacturer of specialty engineering plastics serving automotive and consumer‑goods sectors in India, with a growing export footprint. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to a niche polymer player with improving margins.
ipo:screener-544398.business_overview: Kenrik Indust. produces high‑performance engineering plastics such as polyamide‑6, polybutylene terephthalate (PBT) and custom compounds. Its products are used in under‑the‑hood automotive components, electrical connectors, and durable consumer goods. Revenue mix is approximately 55% automotive, 30% electrical & electronics, and 15% industrial applications. The company operates two manufacturing plants in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu with a combined capacity of 120 ktpa. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per tonne, supported by long‑term supply contracts with OEMs that provide pricing stability and low customer concentration risk.
ipo:screener-544398.market_analysis: The Indian engineering plastics market is estimated at USD 4.5 bn (TAM) and is projected to grow at 8‑9% CAGR driven by automotive lightweighting, EV adoption, and rising demand for durable consumer goods. Kenrik’s addressable market (SAM) for specialty compounds is about USD 1.2 bn. Key competitors include SRF Ltd., Plastiblends India Ltd., and global players like BASF and Covestro operating through subsidiaries. Kenrik’s moat lies in its proprietary compounding technology, strong OEM relationships, and a captive raw‑material sourcing arrangement for certain additives. Industry tailwinds include government PLI schemes for automobiles and plastics, while headwinds stem from volatile crude‑linked raw material prices and increasing environmental regulations on single‑use plastics.
ipo:screener-544398.financials: Over the last three fiscal years, revenue grew from INR 820 cr (FY21) to INR 1 050 cr (FY22) and INR 1 260 cr (FY23), a CAGR of ~24%. EBITDA increased from INR 95 cr to INR 130 cr to INR 165 cr, with EBITDA margins improving from 11.6% to 13.2% to 13.1%. PAT rose from INR 45 cr to INR 68 cr to INR 88 cr, reflecting PAT margin expansion from 5.5% to 6.5% to 7.0%. ROCE improved from 12% to 15% to 18%, while ROE moved from 10% to 13% to 16%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations in the latest audit report.
ipo:screener-544398.valuation: Based on the upper price band of INR 140 per share, the implied P/E (FY23) is ~15.9x, EV/EBITDA ~9.8x, and P/B ~2.3x. Compared with peers: SRF Ltd. trades at P/E 22x, EV/EBITDA 13x; Plastiblends India at P/E 18x, EV/EBITDA 11x. Kenrik appears reasonably valued at a modest discount to peers, reflecting its smaller scale but higher growth trajectory and improving margins.
ipo:screener-544398.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 350 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 235 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 72% to ~58%. Proceeds from the fresh issue will be allocated 45% to capacity expansion (new extrusion line), 30% to debt repayment, 15% to working capital, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 70 cr, representing 20% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-544398.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Agarwal family, with over three decades of experience in polymer manufacturing. Managing Director Mr. Rajesh Agarwal holds a B.Tech in Chemical Engineering and previously worked at Reliance Industries. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and corporate governance, and one nominee director from a private equity investor. Governance practices include regular audit committee meetings, related‑party transaction disclosures, and a whistle‑blower policy, indicating adequate governance quality.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising automotive lightweighting demand
Improving margins from higher‑value specialty compounds and operational efficiencies
Debt reduction leading to lower financial leverage and higher ROCE
risks: Raw material price volatility linked to crude oil and natural gas
Intensifying competition from global players expanding in India
Regulatory changes affecting plastic usage and recycling norms

Improving financial metrics with rising ROCE and ROE
weaknesses: Smaller scale compared to large integrated chemical players
Customer concentration in automotive segment (~55% of revenue)
