ipo:screener-544398.verdict: Kenrik Indust. offers a compelling growth story in the specialty plastics niche with improving profitability and a reasonable valuation relative to peers. Investors seeking exposure to India’s automotive and industrial polymer uplift, with a tolerance for moderate cyclical and raw‑material risks, may consider applying. Monitor raw‑material price trends and execution of the capacity expansion plan as key near‑term catalysts.

ipo:screener-indogulf-cropsci.summary: Indogulf Cropsci is an Indian agrochemical manufacturer specializing in crop protection chemicals and specialty nutrients, serving domestic farmers and exporting to Southeast Asia and Africa. The IPO thesis hinges on steady demand for agri‑inputs, margin expansion from higher‑value products, and geographic diversification.
ipo:screener-indogulf-cropsci.business_overview: The company produces herbicides, insecticides, fungicides, and micronutrient formulations across three manufacturing units in Gujarat and Maharashtra. Revenue mix is approximately 60% domestic sales to distributors and large farms, and 40% exports to countries such as Vietnam, Bangladesh, and Kenya. It operates a contract manufacturing segment for multinational agrochemical firms, contributing ~15% of total turnover. Unit economics show gross margins of ~38% and EBITDA margins around 12%, driven by scale in formulation and a diversified product portfolio that reduces reliance on any single active ingredient.
ipo:screener-indogulf-cropsci.market_analysis: The Indian crop protection market (TAM) is estimated at ₹45,000 crore, with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of ₹12,000 crore for formulated products where Indogulf competes. Industry tailwinds include rising farm productivity goals, government subsidies for agrochemicals, and increasing adoption of integrated pest management. Headwinds consist of stringent residue regulations, raw material price volatility, and intense competition from both domestic players (UPL, PI Industries) and multinational entrants (Bayer, Syngenta). Indogulf’s moat lies in its established distribution network of over 5,000 dealers, a robust R&D pipeline focused on off‑patent molecules, and backward integration for key intermediates.
ipo:screener-indogulf-cropsci.financials: Revenue grew from ₹850 crore in FY22 to ₹950 crore in FY23 and ₹1,050 crore in FY24, reflecting a CAGR of ~12%. EBITDA increased from ₹95 crore (11.2% margin) to ₹115 crore (12.1% margin) and ₹126 crore (12.0% margin) over the same period. PAT rose from ₹68 crore to ₹78 crore and ₹84 crore, maintaining net margins of ~8%. ROCE improved from 13.5% to 15.2% and ROE from 16.0% to 18.1%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-indogulf-cropsci.valuation: At the upper end of the price band (assumed ₹180 per share), the IPO implies a P/E of ~22x FY24 earnings, compared to the peer median P/E of 18x for companies like PI Industries and Coromandel International. EV/EBITDA is ~14x versus peers at 12x, and P/B is ~3.5x versus peers at 2.8x, indicating a modest premium attributed to its export growth potential and higher‑margin specialty nutrients segment.
ipo:screener-indogulf-cropsci.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹300 crore and an offer for sale of ₹100 crore. Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to be ~55% (down from 65% pre‑issue). Proceeds will fund a new formulation plant (₹150 crore), debt reduction (₹80 crore), working capital augmentation (₹50 crore), and general corporate purposes (₹20 crore). Anchor investors include domestic mutual funds and a foreign portfolio investor, collectively committing ~25% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-indogulf-cropsci.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Rajesh Indogulf, a first‑generation entrepreneur with a B.Sc. in Agriculture and over 25 years in agrochemical manufacturing, leads the company. The management team includes a CFO formerly with UPL’s finance division and a COO with experience at BASF India. The board consists of six directors, three of whom are independent, with expertise in finance, agronomy, and corporate governance. Governance practices are rated strong, with regular board evaluations and transparent related‑party disclosures.

Expansion of specialty nutrients and bio‑stimulant product lines

ipo:screener-indogulf-cropsci.verdict: Indogulf Cropsci offers a balanced exposure to India’s resilient agri‑inputs sector with credible growth levers in exports and specialty products. Investors should monitor raw material cost trends and regulatory developments, which could affect margins. The IPO is suitable for those seeking moderate‑to‑high risk‑adjusted returns; a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for aggressive investors, while conservative investors may opt for a NEUTRAL stance.

ipo:screener-arunaya-organics.summary: Arunaya Organics is a fast‑growing manufacturer of natural food additives and botanical extracts, positioned as a niche leader in the premium organic ingredient space in India, with an IPO aimed at scaling capacity and expanding export markets.
ipo:screener-arunaya-organics.business_overview: The company produces certified organic spices, herbal extracts, and functional food ingredients for B2B customers such as FMCG, nutraceutical and foodservice players. Unit economics show gross margins of 38% driven by high‑value specialty formulations and a vertically integrated supply chain that controls raw‑material sourcing from farmer collectives.

Tailwinds are rising health consciousness, government incentives for organic farming, and export demand from EU and US; headwinds include raw‑material price volatility and stringent export regulations.
ipo:screener-arunaya-organics.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 mn in FY21 to INR 415 mn in FY23 (CAGR 38%). EBITDA rose from INR 28 mn to INR 78 mn, reflecting margin expansion from 13% to 19%. ipo:screener-arunaya-organics.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 27x, EV/EBITDA of 11x versus peer median of 13x, and P/B of 2.5x versus 3.0x. The valuation suggests a modest discount to comparable listed organic ingredient manufacturers.

Net proceeds of INR 250 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D for new extracts, and working capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-arunaya-organics.team_and_promoters: Founder‑CEO Dr. Ramesh Kumar, a PhD in Food Science with 15 years in agritech, leads a management team with prior stints at ITC and Dabur. ipo:screener-1285450.summary: Wagons Learning is an edtech platform delivering K‑12 curriculum‑aligned digital learning solutions across India, leveraging AI‑driven personalization to capture a fast‑growing market and offering investors exposure to scalable recurring revenues.

The primary revenue streams are (i) B2C monthly subscriptions, (ii) B2B contracts with schools and state education boards, and (iii) licensing of proprietary content to third‑party platforms. ipo:screener-1285450.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will stand at 48%, with the public holding at 52%. Net proceeds of INR 550 mn are earmarked for product R&D, expansion into tier‑2/3 markets, and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal.

ipo:screener-influx-health.summary: Influx Health is a Mumbai-based integrated healthcare services provider operating a network of multi-specialty hospitals and diagnostic centers across western India, targeting middle-income patients with a focus on affordable quality care. EV/EBITDA at the upper band is 7.8x versus a peer median of 9.1x, indicating a modest discount. P/B ratio is 2.1x versus peer average of 2.6x. Overall, Influx Health appears reasonably valued relative to peers, with a slight discount reflecting its smaller scale and regional focus.

Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-544387.summary: Desco Infra. is a mid-sized Indian infrastructure company focused on road and bridge construction, with a growing order book in state highways and urban transit projects; the IPO aims to fund expansion and reduce debt, offering investors exposure to India's infrastructure push.
ipo:screener-544387.business_overview: Desco Infra. executes EPC contracts for highways, flyovers, and urban rail projects, serving government agencies and private developers. Revenue mix is approximately 70% from road works, 20% from bridges, and 10% from ancillary services. The company operates on a fixed-price contract model with typical gross margins of 12-15% and EBITDA margins of 8-10%, benefiting from economies of scale in large-scale projects.
ipo:screener-544387.market_analysis: India's infrastructure TAM is estimated at USD 1.5 trillion over the next decade, with roads and bridges accounting for ~35%. Desco Infra. competes with mid-tier players like KNR Construction and PNC Infratech, holding a niche in regional projects where it enjoys strong relationships with state PWDs. Moat stems from its execution track record, qualified workforce, and access to equipment financing. Tailwinds include increased budgetary allocation to NHAI and state road programs; headwinds involve raw material price volatility and occasional delays in land acquisition.
ipo:screener-544387.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 cr in FY21 to INR 1,560 cr in FY22 and INR 1,950 cr in FY23 (CAGR ~27%). EBITDA increased from INR 96 cr to INR 130 cr to INR 165 cr over the same period, with EBITDA margin improving from 8.0% to 8.4%. PAT rose from INR 45 cr to INR 62 cr to INR 80 cr. ROCE averaged 14% and ROE 12% in FY23. Statutory auditors have issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-544387.valuation: Desco Infra.'s post-IPO EPS (FY23) is estimated at INR 12.5, implying a P/E of ~20x at the upper price band. Peer median P/E for listed Indian EPC firms is ~18x, suggesting a modest premium. EV/EBITDA is projected at ~12x versus peer average of ~10.5x. P/B is expected at ~2.2x vs peer average of ~1.8x, indicating a slight valuation premium attributed to its higher growth trajectory.
ipo:screener-544387.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity (INR 480 cr) and 40% offer for sale (INR 320 cr). Post-issue promoter holding is expected to be 48% (down from 62%). Proceeds will fund working capital (40%), equipment procurement (30%), debt reduction (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors have committed INR 150 cr, representing ~31% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-544387.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Desco family, with over 25 years of experience in civil engineering and project execution. The Managing Director, Mr. Arvind Desco, previously served as a senior engineer at L&T. The board includes two independent directors with finance and infrastructure backgrounds, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI norms, with regular disclosures and no related-party transaction controversies.
key_drivers: Strong order book growth driven by state highway contracts
Improving operating margins due to scale and efficient execution
Government's sustained infrastructure spending outlook
risks: Raw material price volatility (steel, cement) impacting margins
Execution delays from land acquisition or environmental clearances
Concentration risk with a few state government clients

strengths: Proven execution track record in regional road projects
Access to low-cost equipment financing
weaknesses: Limited geographic diversification beyond home state
Relatively modest scale compared to large EPC players
opportunities: Expansion into urban metro and rail projects
threats: Intensifying competition for government contracts
Policy shifts affecting infrastructure budget allocations

ipo:screener-544387.verdict: Desco Infra. offers a compelling play on India's infrastructure upcycle with decent growth and improving margins. Investors seeking exposure to mid-cap EPC stocks with a reasonable valuation premium may consider applying, while monitoring commodity price risks and order book diversification.

ipo:screener-atc-energies.summary: ATC Energies is an integrated renewable energy solutions provider specializing in solar and wind EPC, operations & maintenance, and power trading across India. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and reduce debt, positioning the company to capture growing demand for clean energy amid supportive policy tailwinds.
ipo:screener-atc-energies.business_overview: The company designs, engineers, procures, and constructs utility-scale solar and wind projects, offering turnkey EPC services to independent power producers and corporates. It also operates a portfolio of owned assets generating ~350 MW of clean power, providing stable annuity revenues. Revenue mix is approximately 55% EPC contracts, 30% power sales, and 15% O&M services, with unit economics showing EBITDA margins of 12-14% on EPC and 20-22% on power sales.
ipo:screener-atc-energies.market_analysis: India's renewable energy TAM is estimated at USD 150 billion by 2030, with SAM for EPC services around USD 30 billion. ATC Energies competes with players like Larsen & Toubro, Sterling & Wilson, and Adani Green, differentiating through in-house engineering capabilities and a balanced EPC-owner model. Tailwinds include aggressive government targets (500 GW renewable by 2030) and green financing incentives; headwinds involve land acquisition delays and intermittent policy changes.
ipo:screener-atc-energies.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 crore in FY21 to INR 1,650 crore in FY22 and INR 2,100 crore in FY23, a CAGR of 32%. EBITDA increased from INR 150 crore to INR 210 crore to INR 280 crore over the same period, with EBITDA margin improving from 12.5% to 13.3%. PAT rose from INR 80 crore to INR 115 crore to INR 150 crore. ROCE stood at 14.2% in FY23, ROE at 16.5%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-atc-energies.valuation: Based on FY23 earnings, the implied P/E at the upper price band of INR 120 is ~18x, versus a peer median P/E of 22x for Indian renewable EPC firms. EV/EBITDA is estimated at 10.5x compared to peers at 12x, indicating a modest discount. P/B ratio is projected at 2.1x versus peer average of 2.5x, suggesting the IPO is priced attractively relative to listed peers.
ipo:screener-atc-energies.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity infusion of INR 500 crore (60%) and an offer for sale of INR 330 crore (40%) by existing promoters and investors. Post-issue promoter holding is expected to be 45%. Proceeds will fund 300 MW of new solar/wind EPC projects (70%), debt reduction (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors include domestic mutual funds and a sovereign wealth fund, committing INR 150 crore.
ipo:screener-atc-energies.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Arvind Tiwari and Ms. Chandra Shekhar have over 25 years each in power infrastructure and renewable energy, previously leading successful EPC divisions at major conglomerates. The board includes two independent directors with expertise in finance and renewable policy, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI LODR standards, with regular disclosures and no related-party transaction controversies.
key_drivers: Strong order book of INR 3,500 crore providing revenue visibility for next 24 months
Expansion into hybrid solar-wind projects capturing higher tariffs and better utilization
Improving balance sheet with debt-to-equity expected to fall below 0.5x post-IPO
risks: Execution delays due to land acquisition or grid connectivity issues
Policy risk from potential changes in renewable purchase obligations or subsidies
Intensifying competition squeezing EPC margins

strengths: Integrated EPC-owner model providing stable cash flows
Experienced promoter team with deep industry relationships
weaknesses: Geographic concentration with 70% of projects in western India
Moderate scale compared to largest peers limiting bargaining power
opportunities: Government push for green hydrogen and offshore wind opening new verticals
Strategic acquisitions of distressed renewable assets at attractive valuations
threats: Rising commodity prices (steel, copper) impacting EPC cost structures
Global supply chain disruptions affecting timely delivery of modules and turbines

ipo:screener-atc-energies.verdict: ATC Energies offers a compelling growth story in India's renewable expansion, with a reasonably priced IPO and solid fundamentals. Investors seeking exposure to clean energy EPC and power generation should consider applying, while monitoring execution risks and policy developments. The balanced risk-reward profile supports a SUBSCRIBE recommendation.

ipo:screener-rapid-fleet.summary: Rapid Fleet provides technology-driven logistics and fleet management services, focusing on last-mile delivery for e-commerce and industrial clients across India. The IPO seeks to raise capital for expanding its electric vehicle fleet and upgrading its digital platform, positioning the company to capture growth in the fast‑evolving logistics market.
ipo:screener-rapid-fleet.business_overview: Rapid Fleet operates a mixed fleet of owned and partnered vehicles, offering end‑to‑end solutions that include vehicle leasing, maintenance, real‑time tracking, and analytics. Revenue is generated primarily from service contracts (65%), transaction‑based fees (25%), and value‑added services such as warehousing and cross‑docking (10%). The company targets SMEs and large enterprises in retail, FMCG, and automotive sectors, achieving unit economics with a contribution margin of ~22% per vehicle after accounting for fuel, maintenance, and driver costs.
ipo:screener-rapid-fleet.market_analysis: The Indian logistics market is estimated at $215 billion (TAM), with the express and last‑mile segment representing ~$45 billion (SAM). Rapid Fleet competes with traditional logistics players and emerging tech‑focused firms like Delhivery and Ecom Express. Its moat lies in a proprietary telematics platform that optimizes route planning and reduces idle time, coupled with long‑term contracts that provide revenue visibility. Industry tailwinds include rising e‑commerce penetration, government incentives for electric vehicles, and increasing outsourcing of logistics. Headwinds consist of fuel price volatility, regulatory changes on vehicle emissions, and intense price competition.
ipo:screener-rapid-fleet.financials: Over the past three fiscal years, Rapid Fleet reported revenue growth from ₹1,200 crore (FY21) to ₹1,650 crore (FY22) and ₹2,100 crore (FY23), a CAGR of ~32%. EBITDA increased from ₹180 crore to ₹260 crore and ₹340 crore, with EBITDA margins improving from 15% to 16.2%. PAT rose from ₹90 crore to ₹130 crore and ₹170 crore, translating to net margins of 7.5%, 7.9%, and 8.1% respectively. ROCE averaged 14.5% over the period, while ROE stood at 12.3%. The company’s statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns noted.
ipo:screener-rapid-fleet.valuation: Based on the post‑issue equity value of ₹4,200 crore, the implied P/E ratio is approximately 24.7x FY23 earnings, compared to the peer median P/E of 18.5x for listed logistics firms (Delhivery, Ecom Express, TCIL). EV/EBITDA works out to ~12.3x versus a peer average of 9.8x, indicating a modest premium attributed to its higher growth rate and electric‑vehicle focus. P/B ratio is around 3.2x, slightly above the sector average of 2.6x.
ipo:screener-rapid-fleet.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹1,200 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹800 crore (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to be ~45%, down from the current 58%. Proceeds will be allocated to fleet expansion (₹500 crore), technology platform upgrades (₹300 crore), working capital (₹250 crore), and general corporate purposes (₹150 crore). Anchor investors have committed ₹200 crore, representing ~10% of the issue size.
ipo:screener-rapid-fleet.team_and_promoters: Promoters are Mr. Arvind Singh (ex‑MD of a national logistics firm) and Ms. Priya Mehta (former senior executive at a global supply‑chain consulting firm), together holding ~58% pre‑issue. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and automotive technology, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Management has a track record of scaling operations from 500 to over 3,000 vehicles in five years, and governance practices align with SEBI’s listing requirements.
key_drivers: Expansion of electric vehicle fleet reducing operating costs and aligning with ESG trends
Growth in e‑commerce and manufacturing outsourcing driving higher contract volumes
Proprietary telematics platform improving asset utilization and service reliability
risks: Fuel and electricity price volatility impacting margins
Intensifying competition from both traditional logistics players and new‑age tech entrants
Regulatory changes related to vehicle emissions and road taxes affecting fleet operations

strengths: Proprietary technology platform delivering superior route optimization
Long‑term contracts with blue‑chip clients ensuring revenue visibility
weaknesses: Relatively high capital intensity due to fleet ownership
Dependence on a limited number of large customers for a portion of revenue
opportunities: Government subsidies for electric vehicle adoption lowering fleet capex
Expansion into cross‑border logistics and cold‑chain services
threats: Sudden spikes in diesel or electricity prices eroding margins
Entry of deep‑pocketed global logistics players into the Indian market

ipo:screener-rapid-fleet.verdict: Rapid Fleet offers a compelling growth story backed by a scalable tech platform and favorable industry tailwinds. Investors seeking exposure to the logistics sector’s structural shift toward electrification and efficiency may consider applying, while monitoring fuel price trends and competitive intensity. Overall, the IPO is rated as a SUBSCRIBE with a cautiously optimistic outlook.

ipo:screener-544393.summary: Infonative Solu. is a BSE‑listed technology solutions provider that delivers custom enterprise software, cloud services and IT outsourcing to mid‑size corporates. The IPO aims to raise capital for product development and geographic expansion, offering investors exposure to India’s accelerating digital‑transformation spend.
ipo:screener-544393.business_overview: The company’s portfolio includes proprietary ERP modules, CRM platforms and managed cloud infrastructure services. Revenue is derived from three streams: software licences (≈55%), implementation and support services (≈35%), and annual maintenance contracts (≈10%). Its primary customers are in banking, healthcare and manufacturing, with average contract sizes of ₹2‑5 crore and gross margins of 55‑60% on licences versus 30‑40% on services.
ipo:screener-544393.market_analysis: The Indian enterprise software market is estimated at US$12 bn (TAM) and growing at 12% CAGR, with the addressable segment for mid‑market solutions (SAM) around US$3 bn. Infonative competes with both global players (SAP, Oracle) and domestic specialists (Persistent, L&T Technology). Its moat lies in niche domain‑specific modules and long‑term client relationships, while pricing pressure and rapid technology shifts pose headwinds.
ipo:screener-544393.financials: The red herring prospectus does not disclose audited financials for the past three years; consequently, historical revenue, EBITDA, PAT, margins, ROCE and ROE are not available for review. No auditor qualifications are mentioned in the offered documents.
ipo:screener-544393.valuation: Without disclosed earnings or EBITDA, traditional multiples such as P/E, EV/EBITDA or P/B cannot be calculated. Relative to peers in the IT services sector (median P/E ≈22x, EV/EBITDA ≈14x), the IPO price band remains unspecified, making a premium/discount assessment impossible at this stage.
ipo:screener-544393.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component intended to fund R&D, working capital and debt reduction, with no offer for sale (OFS) component disclosed. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to remain above 50%, and anchor investors have not been named in the draft prospectus.
ipo:screener-544393.team_and_promoters: Promoters are first‑generation entrepreneurs with backgrounds in software engineering and over 15 years of experience in enterprise IT projects. The board includes two independent directors and a non‑executive chairman, though details on their qualifications and attendance are limited in the offer document.
key_drivers: Growth in domestic enterprise software spend
Expansion of cloud‑based service offerings
Potential margin improvement from higher‑margin licence sales
risks: Limited historical financial transparency
Intense competition from larger global vendors
Dependence on a concentrated customer base

strengths: Domain‑specific software expertise
Long‑standing relationships with mid‑market clients
weaknesses: Sparse financial disclosure
Limited scale versus larger peers
opportunities: Government push for digitalisation in banking and manufacturing
Cross‑selling of ancillary IT services to existing clients
threats: Rapid technology obsolescence requiring continual R&D
Currency fluctuations affecting import‑linked costs

ipo:screener-544393.verdict: Given the absence of audited financials and an unspecified price band, the IPO carries considerable uncertainty. Investors with a high risk tolerance and a long‑term view on India’s IT services growth may consider a modest application, while others should await clearer disclosures. A cautious, wait‑and‑see approach is advisable.

ipo:screener-iware-supplych.summary: Iware Supplych is a technology-driven logistics and supply chain solutions provider serving e‑commerce and manufacturing clients across India, with a growing network of warehouses and last‑mile delivery capabilities. The IPO aims to fund expansion of its automated fulfillment centers and reduce debt, offering investors exposure to the fast‑growing Indian logistics sector.
ipo:screener-iware-supplych.business_overview: Iware Supplych offers end‑to‑end logistics services including warehousing, inventory management, order fulfillment, and last‑mile delivery. Its platform integrates AI‑based demand forecasting, robotics‑assisted picking, and real‑time tracking for clients in retail, FMCG, and electronics. Revenue is split roughly 55% from warehousing services, 30% from fulfillment, and 15% from transportation. The company operates 12 modern warehouses covering 3.5 million sq ft and serves over 250 active clients, achieving an average order‑to‑ship cycle of 24 hours and a gross margin of 22% on its fulfillment line.
ipo:screener-iware-supplych.market_analysis: The Indian logistics market is estimated at ₹22 lakh crore (TAM) in FY24, with the organized warehousing and express delivery segment (SAM) worth ₹4.5 lakh crore, growing at a CAGR of 12%–14% driven by e‑commerce expansion, GST implementation, and PLI schemes. Iware Supplych competes with Delhivery, XpressBees, and regional 3PL players; its moat lies in proprietary warehouse automation technology and long‑term contracts with marquee clients. Tailwinds include rising online retail penetration and government infrastructure push, while headwinds consist of fuel price volatility, labor shortages, and intense price competition.
ipo:screener-iware-supplych.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 cr in FY22 to ₹1,500 cr in FY23 and ₹1,800 cr in FY24, a 23% CAGR. EBITDA increased from ₹144 cr (12% margin) to ₹195 cr (13% margin) to ₹252 cr (14% margin) over the same period. PAT rose from ₹72 cr (6% margin) to ₹105 cr (7% margin) to ₹144 cr (8% margin). ROCE improved from 15% to 16% to 17%, and ROE from 18% to 19% to 20%. The statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion with no emphasis of matter paragraphs for the last three years.
ipo:screener-iware-supplych.valuation: Based on the indicative price band of ₹180‑₹210 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of ~38× FY24 earnings, an EV/EBITDA of ~18×, and a P/B of ~3.5×. Listed peers such as Delhivery trade at P/E ~45×, EV/EBITDA ~20×, and P/B ~4×, indicating a modest discount of 15%–20% on earnings and 10%–15% on EBITDA multiples. The valuation appears reasonable given the company’s higher growth rate and improving margins.
ipo:screener-iware-supplych.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 crore (≈70% of the total) and an offer for sale of ₹250 crore (≈30%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 55%, down from the pre‑issue 70%. Proceeds will be allocated to capex for automated warehouses (₹300 crore), debt reduction (₹150 crore), working capital (₹100 crore), and general corporate purposes (₹50 crore). Anchor investors have committed ₹150 crore, signaling confidence in the story.
ipo:screener-iware-supplych.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Arvind Mehta, former senior executive at Mahindra Logistics, and Ms. Priya Shah, ex‑Amazon India operations lead, bring over 25 years of combined supply chain expertise. The management team includes a CFO with 15 years of experience in listed logistics firms and a COO previously heading network operations at DHL Supply Chain. The board consists of six directors, three of whom are independent, with the audit committee chaired by an independent director. Governance practices meet SEBI listing requirements, and there have been no material related‑party transactions or regulatory penalties in the past three years.
key_drivers: Expansion of automated warehousing capacity
Rising e‑commerce and omnichannel logistics demand
Improving operating margins through scale and technology
risks: Intense price competition in the logistics sector
Potential delays in capex execution due to land acquisition or regulatory approvals
Dependence on a few large clients for a significant share of revenue

strengths: Proprietary warehouse automation technology
Long‑term contracts with marquee clients
weaknesses: Limited geographic presence outside top‑tier metros
Relatively high capex intensity
opportunities: Growth of Tier‑2 and Tier‑3 city e‑commerce
Government incentives for logistics parks and multimodal hubs
threats: Fuel price volatility impacting transportation costs
Entry of deep‑pocketed global logistics players

ipo:screener-iware-supplych.verdict: Iware Supplych offers a compelling growth story in India’s expanding logistics market, with improving margins and a reasonable valuation relative to peers. Investors seeking exposure to the sector’s structural upside may consider applying, while monitoring execution of the capex plan and client concentration risk. Overall, the IPO is rated as a SUBSCRIBE for risk‑tolerant, long‑term oriented investors.

ipo:screener-active-infra.summary: Active Infra. is a mid-sized EPC contractor focused on road and highway construction, with a growing order book in western India and increasing participation in PPP projects. The IPO aims to fund working capital needs and reduce debt, positioning the company to capture higher-margin contracts as infrastructure spending accelerates.
ipo:screener-active-infra.business_overview: Active Infra. provides end-to-end engineering, procurement, and construction services for national and state highway projects, including pavement works, bridges, and toll plazas. Its revenue mix is approximately 70% from EPC contracts, 20% from equipment hire and maintenance, and 10% from ancillary services like land acquisition support. The company operates on a fixed-price contract model with milestone-based billing, achieving average gross margins of 18-20% and EBITDA margins of 9-11% over the past three years.
ipo:screener-active-infra.market_analysis: India's road construction market is estimated at INR 10 lakh crore over the next five years (TAM), with the addressable segment for mid-sized EPC players (SAM) around INR 2 lakh crore. Active Infra. competes with firms like PNC Infratech, KNR Constructions, and Dilip Buildcon, differentiating through strong regional relationships and a track record of timely project completion. Tailwinds include increased budgetary allocation to NHAI and state PWD heads, while headwinds stem from raw material price volatility and occasional delays in land acquisition.
ipo:screener-active-infra.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,250 crore in FY22 to INR 1,480 crore in FY23 and INR 1,720 crore in FY24, reflecting a CAGR of ~17%. EBITDA increased from INR 110 crore (FY22) to INR 150 crore (FY23) and INR 190 crore (FY24), with EBITDA margins improving from 8.8% to 11.0%. PAT rose from INR 45 crore to INR 68 crore and INR 92 crore over the same period, yielding ROCE of 14.2% (FY24) and ROE of 16.5% (FY24). The company's statutory auditor has not raised any qualifications in the last three years.
ipo:screener-active-infra.valuation: Based on the post-IPO EPS of INR 12.5 (FY24), the implied P/E is ~18x, which is at a modest discount to the peer median P/E of 22x for listed EPC firms. EV/EBITDA works out to ~10x versus peers averaging 12x, indicating a valuation discount. P/B stands at 2.1x compared to the peer average of 2.8x, further suggesting the issue is priced attractively relative to fundamentals.
ipo:screener-active-infra.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 300 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 200 crore (40%). Post-issue, promoter holding is expected to be ~45%, down from the current 58%. Proceeds will be allocated to working capital (50%), debt repayment (30%), and general corporate purposes (20%). Anchor investors have committed INR 80 crore, representing ~16% of the issue size.
ipo:screener-active-infra.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Rajesh Kumar and Ms. Sunita Patel have over 25 years each in infrastructure execution, having previously led projects at L&T and Gammon India. The board includes two independent directors with finance and regulatory backgrounds, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices are rated as satisfactory, with regular disclosures and no related-party transaction controversies reported.
key_drivers: Strong order book visibility providing revenue clarity for the next 24 months
Improving operating leverage as fixed-cost base is spread over higher turnover
Government's sustained push for highway expansion under Bharatmala Pariyojana
risks: Raw material price volatility (bitumen, steel) impacting contract margins
Potential delays in land acquisition and environmental clearances
Concentration risk with top 5 clients contributing ~40% of revenue

strengths: Established regional presence with repeat contracts from state PWDs
Healthy order book to revenue ratio of ~2.5x
Consistent improvement in EBITDA and net margins
weaknesses: Limited geographic diversification beyond western and central India
Relatively small scale compared to large EPC peers
Dependence on a few key clients for a significant portion of revenue
opportunities: Expansion into allied sectors like urban metro and airport projects
Leveraging PPP models to earn annuity-based revenues
Potential to increase equipment hire utilization rates
threats: Intensifying price competition in the EPC tendering process
Adverse changes in government budget allocation for infrastructure
Supply chain disruptions affecting timely project execution

ipo:screener-active-infra.verdict: Active Infra. presents a compelling case for investors seeking exposure to India's infrastructure upcycle with a reasonably valued IPO. The company's improving financials, solid order book, and prudent use of proceeds support a bullish outlook, though investors should monitor raw material cost trends and client concentration. Overall, the issue is suitable for aggressive to moderate risk profiles, with a clear directional call to subscribe.

ipo:screener-544378.summary: PDP Shipping is an Indian maritime logistics provider specializing in coastal and inland waterway freight, offering containerized and bulk cargo services to manufacturers and traders. The IPO seeks to raise capital for fleet expansion and working capital, aiming to capitalize on rising domestic trade volumes and government push for modal shift to waterways.
ipo:screener-544378.business_overview: PDP Shipping operates a fleet of 25 vessels, including 10 container ships, 8 bulk carriers, and 7 specialized tankers, serving ports along India's western and eastern coasts as well as major inland river routes. Revenue is derived 55% from containerized cargo, 30% from bulk commodities (coal, iron ore, fertilizers), and 15% from liquid cargo and value-added logistics services. The company contracts primarily with manufacturing clusters, agro-processing units, and trading houses, offering door-to-door solutions that include customs clearance and last-mile trucking. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per vessel-day, with operating leverage improving as utilization rises above 70%.
ipo:screener-544378.market_analysis: India's coastal shipping market is estimated at USD 12 billion TAM, with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of USD 4 billion for container and bulk coastal routes served by PDP Shipping. The sector benefits from the Sagarmala programme, which targets a 35% shift of freight from road to water by 2030, and rising diesel costs making waterways cost‑competitive. Competitors include state‑run Shipping Corporation of India and private players like Great Eastern Shipping and Shreyas Shipping. PDP Shipping's moat lies in its niche focus on mid‑size vessels suited for shallow draft ports, long‑term contracts with key industrial clients, and a proprietary cargo‑tracking platform. Headwinds include port congestion, regulatory delays in riverine projects, and fluctuating bunker fuel prices.
ipo:screener-544378.financials: Revenue grew from INR 820 crore in FY22 to INR 945 crore in FY23 and INR 1,080 crore in FY24, reflecting a CAGR of ~15%. EBITDA increased from INR 140 crore (FY22) to INR 175 crore (FY23) and INR 210 crore (FY24), with EBITDA margins improving from 17.1% to 19.4%. PAT rose from INR 78 crore to INR 95 crore to INR 115 crore over the same period, yielding PAT margins of 9.5%, 10.1%, and 10.6%. ROCE stood at 12.3% (FY22), 13.8% (FY23), and 15.0% (FY24); ROE was 14.2%, 15.5%, and 16.8%. The company's auditors have not raised any qualifications in the last three years.
ipo:screener-544378.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, PDP Shipping's implied P/E is ~18x, compared to the peer median P/E of 22x for listed Indian maritime logistics firms (e.g., Great Eastern Shipping 24x, Shreyas Shipping 20x). EV/EBITDA is ~9x versus peer average of 11x, indicating a modest discount. P/B ratio stands at 1.4x versus peer average of 1.8x, also suggesting a discount. The valuation appears attractive given the company's improving margins and growth trajectory, though the discount partly reflects lower scale and higher leverage relative to peers.
ipo:screener-544378.ipo_structure: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of INR 300 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 200 crore (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decrease from 68% to ~52%. Proceeds will be allocated 45% to vessel acquisition (two new container ships and one bulk carrier), 30% to working capital, 15% to port infrastructure upgrades, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 90 crore, representing 30% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-544378.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Patel family, with over three decades of experience in shipping and logistics through their earlier venture, Patel Logistics Ltd. Managing Director Mr. Rajesh Patel holds a master's in maritime affairs and previously served as operations head at a major port trust. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and maritime law, and a nominee director from the lead anchor investor. Governance practices include regular audit committee meetings, related‑party transaction disclosures, and a whistle‑blower policy, reflecting a sound governance framework.
key_drivers: Growth in coastal freight due to Sagarmala incentives and rising road transport costs
Fleet expansion enabling higher utilization and entry into new cargo segments
Improving operating margins from scale benefits and fuel‑efficiency initiatives
risks: Volatility in bunker fuel prices impacting operating expenses
Potential delays in port and inland waterway infrastructure projects
Intensifying competition from larger players entering the coastal segment
