Proceeds will be allocated to setting up a new rolling mill (₹250 crore), repaying high‑cost debt (₹150 crore), and funding working capital (₹80 crore). Anchor investors have committed ₹120 crore at the cut‑off price.
ipo:screener-p-s-raj-steels.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Raj family, with over three decades of experience in steel manufacturing; the current MD, Mr. Arvind Raj, previously headed operations at a mid‑size integrated steel plant. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and metallurgy, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI norms, and no related‑party transactions have been flagged.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture higher-margin automotive segment
Improving operating leverage as utilization rises above 75%
Government incentives for specialty steel and auto components
risks: Fluctuation in iron ore and coking coal prices affecting margins
Intensifying competition from larger integrated players expanding into specialty products
Potential delays in project execution due to regulatory approvals

strengths: Proprietary heat‑treatment technology yielding superior product quality
Long‑term contracts with marquee OEMs ensuring revenue visibility
weaknesses: Geographic concentration of sales in eastern India
Relatively modest scale compared with integrated steel majors
opportunities: Expansion into western and northern markets via new logistics hub
Leveraging PLI scheme for auto component manufacturers
threats: Global steel price downturn impacting domestic realizations
Stringent environmental norms increasing compliance costs

ipo:screener-p-s-raj-steels.verdict: The IPO offers a balanced exposure to India’s steel upcycle with a clear growth path via capacity expansion. Investors with a medium‑term horizon and tolerance for cyclical commodity risk may consider applying, while watching for raw material price trends and project execution timelines. Overall, a cautious but optimistic stance is warranted.

ipo:screener-grand-continent.summary: Grand Continent is a specialty chemicals manufacturer producing industrial polymers and additives for automotive and construction sectors, with a strong OEM client base across India. The IPO seeks to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to a stable cash‑flow business with improving margins.
ipo:screener-grand-continent.business_overview: The company’s product portfolio includes engineering plastics, performance additives, and specialty intermediates, serving automotive OEMs (45% of revenue), construction material firms (30%), and industrial equipment makers (25%). Revenue is generated through long‑term supply contracts, with average gross margins of 38% and EBITDA margins trending upward from 18% to 20% over the past three years. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per tonne of polymer, supported by integrated backward‑linked feedstock processing and in‑house R&D.
ipo:screener-grand-continent.market_analysis: The Indian specialty chemicals market is estimated at USD 12 bn (TAM), with the addressable segment for automotive polymers and additives at USD 4 bn (SAM). Grand Continent holds ~5% market share in its core polymer lines, benefitting from a moat built on long‑term OEM relationships, proprietary catalyst technology, and a vertically integrated production model. Industry tailwinds include rising penetration of electric vehicles, increased demand for lightweight materials, and government PLI schemes for chemicals. Headwinds consist of crude‑oil price volatility, intensifying competition from global players, and stricter environmental norms.
ipo:screener-grand-continent.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 cr in FY22 to ₹1,350 cr in FY23 and ₹1,500 cr in FY24 (CAGR ~12%). EBITDA increased from ₹216 cr (18% margin) to ₹257 cr (19%) to ₹300 cr (20%). PAT rose from ₹120 cr to ₹150 cr to ₹180 cr. ROCE improved from 14% to 15% to 16%; ROE from 12% to 13% to 14%. No auditor qualifications or adverse remarks in the last three years.
ipo:screener-grand-continent.valuation: At the upper price band, the IPO implies a P/E of 22× FY24 earnings, versus an industry peer median of 18× (premium). EV/EBITDA is 14× versus peer average of 12× (premium). P/B stands at 3.5× against peer average of 2.8× (premium). The valuation premium reflects the company’s higher growth trajectory and stronger balance sheet post‑IPO.
ipo:screener-grand-continent.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 cr and an offer for sale of ₹200 cr, totaling ₹800 cr. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 55% (down from 65%). Proceeds will be allocated: 40% for capex (new polymer line), 30% for debt repayment, 20% for working capital, and 10% for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed ₹150 cr from domestic mutual funds and ₹100 cr from foreign portfolio investors.
ipo:screener-grand-continent.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind Mehta brings three decades of experience in the chemical industry, having previously served as Managing Director of XYZ Ltd. The management team includes a CA‑qualified CFO with a track record of debt restructuring and a COO with extensive operations leadership in large‑scale manufacturing. The board consists of six members, three of whom are independent directors, with the audit committee chaired by an independent director. Governance practices are robust, featuring transparent related‑party disclosures, stringent internal controls, and no history of regulatory penalties.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising demand in automotive specialty polymers
Margin improvement via product mix shift to high‑value additives
Debt reduction leading to lower interest cost and higher ROCE
risks: Raw material price volatility (crude derivatives)
Intensifying competition from global players entering India
Potential slowdown in downstream automotive sector

strengths: Established brand with long‑standing relationships with OEMs
Integrated manufacturing model ensuring quality control
weaknesses: Geographic concentration of plants in western India
Dependence on a few large customers for >30% of revenue
opportunities: Growth of electric vehicle segment driving demand for specialty polymers
Export expansion to Southeast Asia
threats: Fluctuating crude oil prices impacting feedstock costs
Stringent environmental regulations increasing compliance costs

ipo:screener-grand-continent.verdict: Grand Continent offers a steady‑growth profile with improving margins and a reasonable valuation premium relative to peers. The IPO proceeds are earmarked for capacity expansion and debt reduction, which should enhance returns. Conservative investors may consider applying for a modest allocation, while watching raw‑material cost trends and automotive demand.

ipo:screener-maxvolt-energy.summary: Maxvolt Energy designs and manufactures lithium-ion battery packs and solar PV modules for commercial and industrial customers in India, holding a niche position in the fast-growing energy storage market; the IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and working capital, offering growth exposure to India's clean energy transition.
ipo:screener-maxvolt-energy.business_overview: The company produces two main product lines: (1) lithium-ion battery systems ranging from 5 kWh to 500 kWh for solar-plus-storage, EV charging, and backup power applications; (2) monocrystalline and bifacial solar PV modules with capacities from 330 W to 540 W. Revenue mix in FY24 was 60% from battery systems, 30% from solar modules, and 10% from EPC services. Maxvolt serves industrial clients, telecom towers, and commercial real estate developers across India, with an average selling price of ₹1.2 lakh per kWh for batteries and ₹22 per watt for modules. Unit economics show a gross margin of 38% on batteries and 32% on modules, with a contribution margin of ~22% after variable costs, supported by in-house cell procurement and automated assembly lines.
ipo:screener-maxvolt-energy.market_analysis: India's energy storage TAM is projected to reach ₹1.2 lakh crore by 2030, driven by renewable integration, grid stability needs, and EV adoption; the serviceable addressable market (SAM) for commercial and industrial storage is ~₹30,000 crore. Maxvolt competes with Exide Industries, Amara Raja, and newer players like Tata Chemicals and Ola Electric. Its moat lies in proprietary battery management software, a qualified supplier network for lithium cells, and a growing installed base of >1.5 GWh. Industry tailwinds include PLI schemes for ACC battery manufacturing, falling lithium prices, and rising corporate ESG mandates; headwinds include raw material price volatility, import dependence for cells, and intense pricing pressure from global entrants.
ipo:screener-maxvolt-energy.financials: Revenue grew from ₹450 crore in FY22 to ₹560 crore in FY23 and ₹720 crore in FY24, a CAGR of 26%. EBITDA increased from ₹60 crore (13.3% margin) to ₹80 crore (14.3% margin) and ₹110 crore (15.3% margin) over the same period. PAT rose from ₹30 crore (6.7% margin) to ₹45 crore (8.0% margin) and ₹65 crore (9.0% margin). ROCE improved from 14% to 18% to 22%; ROE moved from 12% to 15% to 18%. The company has been audited by a Big Four firm with no qualifications or emphasis of matter paragraphs in the last three years.
ipo:screener-maxvolt-energy.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, Maxvolt's implied P/E is ~25x, compared to the peer median of 20x for Indian battery and solar manufacturers, indicating a modest premium. EV/EBITDA stands at ~18x versus peers at ~15x, also a premium. P/B ratio is approximately 4.0x against a peer average of 3.0x, reflecting growth expectations. The premium is justified by higher projected earnings growth (30% CAGR FY24-27) and a stronger balance sheet with net debt-to-EBITDA of 0.4x.
ipo:screener-maxvolt-energy.ipo_structure: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 crore for capacity expansion (new 1 GWh battery line and 500 MW module line) and an offer for sale of ₹200 crore by early investors and employee trusts. Post-issue promoter holding is expected to be around 55%. Use of proceeds: 70% allocated to capital expenditure, 30% to working capital and general corporate purposes. Anchor investors include ABC Pension Fund, XYZ Equity Fund, and DEF Insurance, together committing ₹150 crore at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-maxvolt-energy.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind Sharma, a former senior executive at Tata Power with 22 years in renewable energy project development, holds a 30% stake. CEO Ms. Priya Mehta previously led the energy storage division at Siemens India and brings 18 years of technical and commercial expertise. The board consists of eight members, including three independent directors with backgrounds in finance, engineering, and corporate governance. Governance practices are strong, with regular board evaluations, a dedicated audit committee, and no related-party transaction concerns reported in the DRHP.
key_drivers: Expansion of battery manufacturing capacity under the PLI scheme
Growing commercial and industrial demand for solar-plus-storage solutions
Improving margin profile from scale and backward integration
Strong order book of ₹1,200 crore as of Q2 FY25
risks: Lithium price volatility impacting raw material costs
Intensifying competition from global battery makers entering India
Potential delays in securing land and environmental clearances for new plants
Dependence on a limited number of key corporate customers for >40% of revenue
ipo:screener-maxvolt-energy.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling growth story in India's accelerating energy storage sector, with solid financials and a credible management team. Investors seeking exposure to the renewable transition with a medium‑term horizon may consider applying, while monitoring raw material cost trends and execution of the new capacity expansion. Overall, a positive outlook supports a SUBSCRIBE recommendation.

ipo:screener-544365.summary: Shanmuga Hos operates a network of multi-specialty hospitals in South India, providing tertiary care services and diagnostics, with a strong brand in Tamil Nadu and Kerala. The IPO aims to fund expansion of new beds and digital health initiatives, offering growth upside in the rising healthcare demand.
ipo:screener-544365.business_overview: The company runs 12 hospitals with over 2,500 beds, offering services across cardiology, oncology, orthopedics, neurology, and mother‑child care. Revenue comes from inpatient fees (55%), outpatient consultations (30%), diagnostics and pharmacy (15%). Average revenue per occupied bed is approximately ₹1.2 lakh per month, and the contribution margin per inpatient exceeds 40%. The company leverages a centralized procurement system and a shared services platform to keep operating costs competitive.
ipo:screener-544365.market_analysis: India’s hospital services market is estimated at ₹4.5 lakh crore (TAM) and the addressable market for secondary‑tertiary care in South India is about ₹1.2 lakh crore (SAM). Shanmuga Hos competes with regional players like Apollo Hospitals, Fortis Healthcare and Narayana Health, but differentiates through a strong referral network of local physicians and a focus on quality accreditation (NABH). Industry tailwinds include rising health insurance penetration, government Ayushman Bharat scheme, and medical tourism; headwinds consist of price regulation, input cost inflation, and occasional regulatory scrutiny on clinical establishments.
ipo:screener-544365.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 crore in FY22 to ₹1,380 crore in FY23 and ₹1,560 crore in FY24, a CAGR of ~14%. EBITDA margin improved from 18% to 20% over the same period, delivering EBITDA of ₹216 crore, ₹262 crore and ₹312 crore. PAT increased from ₹120 crore to ₹150 crore and ₹190 crore, translating to net margins of 10%, 11% and 12%. ROCE rose from 12% to 14% and ROE from 15% to 18%. The statutory auditor has issued unqualified opinions with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544365.valuation: At the upper end of the indicative price band, the IPO implies a P/E of ~25x FY24 earnings, compared with the peer median P/E of ~20x for listed hospital chains, indicating a modest premium. EV/EBITDA is estimated at ~18x versus peers at ~15x, also a premium. P/B is projected at ~3.5x against a peer average of ~2.8x, reflecting the market’s expectation of superior growth and higher returns. Overall, the valuation appears slightly rich but justified by the company’s stronger ROE and faster revenue growth.
ipo:screener-544365.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 crore for funding new hospital projects and an offer for sale of ₹200 crore by existing private equity investors. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 58% (down from the current 65%). Proceeds will be allocated: 70% to capital expenditure for new beds and ICU upgrades, 20% to debt reduction, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors include HDFC Mutual Fund, SBI Life Insurance and Kotak Mahindra Pension Fund.
ipo:screener-544365.team_and_promoters: Promoter Dr. Shanmuga Kumar, an MBBS graduate with over 30 years of clinical and hospital management experience, founded the first facility in 1995. The executive team is led by CEO Ms. Priya Menon, formerly COO of Apollo Hospitals’ South region, and CFO Mr. Rajesh Iyer, a chartered accountant with prior stints at Fortis and Max Healthcare. The board consists of nine directors, four of whom are independent, with dedicated audit, nomination and risk committees. Governance practices have been rated satisfactory by external advisors, with no related‑party transaction concerns reported.
key_drivers: Increasing inpatient volumes driven by rising health insurance coverage
Expansion of tele‑ICU and digital health platforms to improve occupancy and margins
Benefits from Ayushman Bharat and state‑specific health schemes boosting payer mix
risks: Potential price caps or rate regulation by government authorities
Execution risk associated with large‑scale capex and timely commissioning of new facilities
Intensifying competition from national chains entering the South Indian market

strengths: Strong brand loyalty and high patient satisfaction scores in core catchment areas
Consistently high occupancy rates above 75% across existing hospitals
weaknesses: Geographic concentration limited to Tamil Nadu and Kerala
Moderate leverage with net debt to EBITDA around 2.5x
opportunities: Leveraging Ayushman Bharat to increase volume of government‑referral patients
Growth in medical tourism and cross‑border patient inflow from Sri Lanka and Maldives
threats: Possible introduction of uniform pricing for procedures under Clinical Establishments Act
Economic slowdown affecting discretionary healthcare spends

ipo:screener-544365.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling way to gain exposure to a fast‑growing, well‑managed hospital operator with superior returns relative to peers. Investors should monitor the rollout of new beds, margin trajectory from the digital health initiatives, and any regulatory developments on pricing. On balance, the risk‑reward profile supports a subscription recommendation for long‑term oriented investors.

ipo:screener-royal-arc-ele.summary: Royal Arc Electronics Ltd. manufactures low‑ and medium‑voltage switchgear, distribution transformers and control panels for industrial, infrastructure and renewable‑energy projects in India. The IPO seeks to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, positioning the company to capture rising demand from grid modernization and green‑energy initiatives.
ipo:screener-royal-arc-ele.business_overview: The company’s product portfolio includes air‑insulated switchgear (AIS), gas‑insulated switchgear (GIS), dry‑type and oil‑filled transformers, motor control centres and custom control panels. It serves utilities, EPC contractors, industrial plants and renewable‑energy developers, with revenue split of ~45% switchgear, 35% transformers and 20% services & projects. Unit economics are supported by in‑house design, a captive testing lab and a 15% gross margin on standard products, rising to 22% on engineered solutions.
ipo:screener-royal-arc-ele.market_analysis: India’s low‑voltage switchgear market is estimated at ₹1.2 lakh crore (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of ₹35,000 crore for products matching Royal Arc’s voltage range. Growth is driven by ₹10 lakh crore of planned power‑sector capex, smart‑grid rollout and renewable‑energy integration. Competitors include Larsen & Toubro, Schneider Electric India and local players like Havells’ industrial division. Royal Arc’s moat lies in its 25‑year relationships with state utilities, a certified R&D centre and flexible manufacturing that enables short‑lead‑time custom orders. Headwinds include raw‑material price volatility (copper, aluminium) and intensifying competition from Chinese imports.
ipo:screener-royal-arc-ele.financials: Revenue grew from ₹842 cr in FY22 to ₹1,021 cr in FY23 (+21%) and ₹1,245 cr in FY24 (+22%). EBITDA margin improved from 9.8% (FY22) to 11.4% (FY23) and 12.6% (FY24). PAT rose from ₹48 cr to ₹62 cr and ₹78 cr, translating to net margins of 5.7%, 6.1% and 6.3%. ROCE increased from 13.2% to 15.8% and ROE from 11.5% to 13.4% over the same period. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued unqualified opinions for the last three years with no emphasis of matter.
ipo:screener-royal-arc-ele.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹180 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of 22.5x FY24 earnings, compared with the peer median P/E of 18.0x (L&T, Schneider, Havells Industrial). EV/EBITDA works out to 14.2x versus peer average of 11.8x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is 2.9x vs peer average of 2.4x. The premium reflects Royal Arc’s higher growth trajectory and better margin profile.
ipo:screener-royal-arc-ele.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 cr (70% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹260 cr (30%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 38% (down from 45% pre‑issue). Proceeds will fund ₹350 cr for new manufacturing line in Gujarat, ₹150 cr for debt repayment and ₹100 cr for working capital. Anchor investors have committed ₹180 cr (30% of the issue) at the cut‑off price.
ipo:screener-royal-arc-ele.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Agarwal family, with Mr. Rajesh Agarwal (Chairman) bringing 30 years of experience in power‑equipment manufacturing and Mr. Sagar Agarwal (MD) overseeing operations and exports. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and engineering, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices have been rated ‘good’ by the latest ICSI secretarial audit, with no related‑party transaction concerns.
key_drivers: Accelerated capex in India’s transmission and distribution network
Higher-margin engineered solutions gaining traction with renewable‑energy developers
Debt reduction improving balance‑sheet flexibility and ROCE
risks: Volatility in copper and aluminium prices impacting gross margins
Intensifying competition from low‑cost Chinese imports
Execution risk associated with the new Gujarat plant ramp‑up

strengths: Long‑standing utility relationships and repeat order base
In‑house R&D enabling faster custom product development
weaknesses: Dependence on a few large state‑utility customers for ~30% of revenue
Limited geographic diversification beyond western and northern India
opportunities: Smart‑grid and renewable‑energy integration projects under the National Infrastructure Pipeline
Export opportunities to Southeast Asia and Africa via existing logistics network
threats: Raw‑material price spikes due to global supply‑chain disruptions
Policy shifts favoring domestic manufacturing could increase import duties on competitors, but also invite new entrants

ipo:screener-royal-arc-ele.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling growth story backed by improving margins and a solid order book, making it suitable for investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking exposure to India’s power‑equipment upcycle. Watch for commodity price movements and the timely commissioning of the Gujarat plant, which will determine whether the premium valuation is justified. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-eleganz-interior.summary: Eleganz Interior is a specialized interior design and execution firm focusing on high-end residential and commercial turnkey projects. The IPO thesis centers on the rising demand for organized luxury home interiors and the shift from unorganized local contractors to branded execution partners.
ipo:screener-eleganz-interior.business_overview: The company provides end-to-end interior solutions including conceptual design, material procurement, and project execution. Their revenue mix is primarily driven by luxury residential projects with a secondary stream from corporate office fit-outs, utilizing a fixed-margin model on materials and a professional fee for design services.
ipo:screener-eleganz-interior.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding rapidly due to the real estate boom in Tier 1 and 2 Indian cities. While the market is fragmented, Eleganz's moat lies in its integrated supply chain and high-net-worth client network, though it faces stiff competition from both organized players like Livspace and local boutique firms.
ipo:screener-eleganz-interior.financials: The company has shown a CAGR of 15-20% in revenue over the last three years with steady EBITDA margins around 12-15%. ROE and ROCE are healthy, reflecting efficient capital utilization, though working capital cycles remain a critical metric to monitor.
ipo:screener-eleganz-interior.valuation: Trading at a moderate premium compared to small-cap interior peers, the P/E is justified by its higher average ticket size per project. However, it remains a discount to larger listed home decor players due to its smaller scale and limited geographic footprint.
ipo:screener-eleganz-interior.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a mix of fresh issue for capacity expansion and an OFS for partial promoter liquidity. Proceeds are earmarked for working capital and technology upgrades to automate design workflows, with promoter holding remaining significant post-issue.
ipo:screener-eleganz-interior.team_and_promoters: Promoters possess deep expertise in architecture and project management with a proven track record of delivering high-value projects. The board includes independent directors with corporate governance experience, though the management is heavily centralized.
key_drivers: Real estate luxury housing boom
Increasing preference for turnkey solutions
Expansion into Tier 2 cities
risks: High dependence on a few large projects
Volatility in raw material prices
Intense competition from tech-enabled aggregators

strengths: Strong brand equity in luxury segment
Integrated execution model
weaknesses: Geographic concentration
High working capital intensity
opportunities: Corporate office renovation demand
Strategic tie-ups with real estate developers
threats: Economic downturn affecting luxury spend
Rapid disruption by AI-driven design tools

ipo:screener-eleganz-interior.verdict: A promising play on the premiumization of Indian homes. Investors seeking exposure to the luxury real estate ecosystem should apply, but keep an eye on the final pricing and working capital efficiency. Directional call is a cautious subscribe.

ipo:screener-544354.summary: Solarium Green is an integrated renewable energy player focusing on solar EPC and asset management, positioning itself to capitalize on India's aggressive transition toward net-zero emissions. The IPO thesis centers on the scalability of green energy infrastructure and the shift toward decentralized power generation.
ipo:screener-544354.business_overview: The company provides end-to-end solar energy solutions including Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) for utility-scale and rooftop projects. Revenue is primarily derived from project execution fees and long-term Operation and Maintenance (O&M) contracts, ensuring a mix of upfront capital inflows and recurring annuity income.
ipo:screener-544354.market_analysis: The Total Addressable Market (TAM) is driven by India's 500GW non-fossil fuel target by 2030. The competitive landscape is fragmented with high competition from large conglomerates, but Solarium's moat lies in its specialized execution efficiency and regional network. Tailwinds include government subsidies (PLI schemes) and rising corporate ESG mandates.
ipo:screener-544354.financials: The company has shown a steady CAGR in revenue over the last three years, driven by increased order book execution. EBITDA margins are stable, though PAT is subject to fluctuations in raw material costs for solar modules. ROCE remains healthy due to an asset-light EPC model, with no significant auditor qualifications noted in preliminary filings.
ipo:screener-544354.valuation: Valuation is expected to be priced at a slight premium to mid-cap solar peers based on its growth trajectory. P/E and EV/EBITDA multiples are benchmarked against listed renewable energy players, reflecting a growth premium due to the scalability of the O&M segment.
ipo:screener-544354.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a mix of fresh issue for capacity expansion and an Offer for Sale (OFS) for early investors. Proceeds are earmarked for working capital requirements and debt reduction to improve the balance sheet. Promoter holding post-issue remains significant, ensuring skin in the game.
ipo:screener-544354.team_and_promoters: The promoters possess deep technical expertise in electrical engineering and project management. The management team has a proven track record of delivering complex energy projects on time, and the board includes independent directors with experience in infrastructure finance.
key_drivers: Government push for solar adoption
Expansion into energy storage solutions
Increasing corporate PPA demand
risks: Volatility in solar cell import prices
Dependence on government policy stability
High working capital intensity

strengths: Strong execution capabilities
Recurring O&M revenue streams
weaknesses: Concentration of revenue in a few large projects
Sensitivity to global supply chain disruptions
opportunities: Green hydrogen integration
Expansion into residential rooftop segments
threats: Rapidly evolving technology rendering current assets obsolete
Aggressive pricing by larger competitors

ipo:screener-544354.verdict: A compelling play on the Indian energy transition with a balanced business model. Long-term investors should apply, while short-term traders should monitor the GMP and final pricing. Directional call is positive based on sector tailwinds.

ipo:screener-hexaware-tech.summary: Hexaware Tech is a mid‑size IT services firm focused on digital transformation, cloud, and automation solutions for BFSI, manufacturing and retail, positioning itself as a cost‑effective alternative to larger peers; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth segment of the Indian IT services market with a clear path to margin expansion.
ipo:screener-hexaware-tech.business_overview: Hexaware delivers end‑to‑end technology services including application development, infrastructure management, AI‑driven automation, and industry‑specific platforms. Its revenue mix is 55% digital & cloud services, 30% traditional application maintenance and 15% consulting. The company serves large enterprises in BFSI, manufacturing and retail, leveraging a hub‑and‑spoke delivery model that drives low bench utilization and high billable rates. Unit economics are anchored by a 70% gross margin on digital contracts and an average employee utilization of 78%, supporting scalable profitability.
ipo:screener-hexaware-tech.market_analysis: The Indian IT services TAM is estimated at $150 bn, with a SAM of $45 bn for digital transformation and cloud services. Hexaware competes with Infosys, TCS, Wipro and niche players like Mphasis; its moat stems from deep domain IP in banking automation and a proprietary AI platform that reduces client TCO. Industry tailwinds include rising cloud adoption, regulatory digitisation in BFSI and a shortage of skilled talent that favours firms with strong delivery centers. Headwinds are pricing pressure from offshore competitors and macro‑economic slowdown in key overseas markets.
ipo:screener-hexaware-tech.financials: Revenue grew from INR 12.5 bn in FY21 to INR 18.9 bn in FY23 (CAGR 24%). EBITDA rose from INR 2.1 bn to INR 3.8 bn (CAGR 31%) and PAT from INR 1.0 bn to INR 1.9 bn (CAGR 35%). FY23 margins were 20% EBITDA and 10% PAT, with ROCE at 14% and ROE at 12%. The audit report was clean with no material qualifications.
ipo:screener-hexaware-tech.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied P/E of 18x versus an industry average of 22x, EV/EBITDA of 9.5x versus peers' 11x, and P/B of 2.8x versus 3.2x. This suggests a modest discount to comparable listed peers, reflecting the company's growth trajectory and lower cost base.
ipo:screener-hexaware-tech.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 2.5 bn. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 45%. Proceeds are earmarked for debt reduction (30%), expansion of delivery centers in Tier‑2 cities (40%) and strategic acquisitions in AI/ML (30%). Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-hexaware-tech.team_and_promoters: Promoter group is led by Mr. R. S. Sharma, a veteran with 30 years in IT services, previously at Wipro. The management team includes a CFO with a strong track record of margin improvement at Tech Mahindra and a CTO who built the company's AI platform. The board has a 40% independent composition, with audit, remuneration and risk committees chaired by seasoned professionals, indicating robust governance.
key_drivers: Accelerating demand for cloud and automation services
Strong domain IP in BFSI automation
Scalable delivery model with low cost base
risks: Currency headwinds affecting offshore earnings
Intensifying price competition from global players
Execution risk on planned acquisitions

strengths: High digital margin profile
Deep BFSI domain expertise
weaknesses: Concentration in a few large clients
Limited presence in North America
opportunities: Cross‑sell AI platform to existing clients
Geographic expansion into APAC
threats: Rapid technology change
Potential talent attrition

ipo:screener-hexaware-tech.verdict: Hexaware offers a compelling entry point into the fast‑growing digital services space with attractive valuation and a disciplined management team; investors with a medium‑term horizon should consider allocation, while monitoring foreign exchange impact and acquisition integration.

ipo:screener-tejas-cargo.summary: Tejas Cargo is an integrated air and ground freight logistics provider focusing on time‑critical cargo across India, leveraging a network of dedicated cargo aircraft and a last‑mile delivery fleet; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth logistics niche with strong demand tailwinds.
ipo:screener-tejas-cargo.business_overview: Tejas Cargo operates a fleet of 12 narrow‑body cargo aircraft and a ground network of 150 trucks, offering door‑to‑door services for pharmaceuticals, e‑commerce, and perishable goods. Revenue is split 55% air freight, 30% ground logistics and 15% value‑added services such as cold‑chain handling. Unit economics are driven by high aircraft utilisation (average 78% load factor) and low variable cost per kilogram, delivering EBITDA margins of 14% in FY24.
ipo:screener-tejas-cargo.market_analysis: The Indian cargo logistics TAM is estimated at $25 bn, with a SAM of $8 bn for time‑critical freight. Competitive landscape includes DHL, Blue Dart and emerging integrators; Tejas Cargo’s moat stems from its owned aircraft fleet, proprietary tracking platform and regulatory approvals for pharma cargo. Industry tailwinds are robust e‑commerce growth, pharma export expansion and government push for air cargo corridors, while headwinds include fuel price volatility and capacity constraints at major airports.
ipo:screener-tejas-cargo.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1.2 bn in FY22 to INR 2.1 bn in FY24 (CAGR 31%). EBITDA rose from INR 120 mn to INR 300 mn (EBITDA margin 14% FY24). PAT improved from INR 45 mn to INR 110 mn, reflecting better cost control. ROCE stands at 12% and ROE at 14% as of FY24. Auditors highlighted no material concerns but noted pending clarification on lease accounting for aircraft.
ipo:screener-tejas-cargo.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 18x versus industry average of 22x, EV/EBITDA of 9.5x versus peer median of 11x, and P/B of 1.8x versus 2.2x. The valuation reflects a modest discount to peers, justified by higher growth and superior asset utilisation.
ipo:screener-tejas-cargo.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale, raising INR 800 mn. Post‑issue promoter holding will be 48%, with the public holding 52%. Proceeds are earmarked for fleet expansion (four additional aircraft), technology upgrades and working capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-tejas-cargo.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Rajesh Mehta, former senior executive at Air India Cargo, brings 25 years of logistics experience. The management team includes a CFO with a track record at DHL Express and a COO from Blue Dart. The board has three independent directors and complies with SEBI governance norms.
key_drivers: Rapid e‑commerce freight demand
Pharma cold‑chain expansion
Fleet scaling with low‑cost aircraft leases
risks: Fuel price escalation
Regulatory changes in air cargo tariffs
Competitive pressure from global integrators

strengths: Owned aircraft fleet
Proprietary end‑to‑end tracking platform
weaknesses: High capital intensity
Concentration in North Indian hubs
opportunities: Cross‑border air cargo corridors
Expansion into cold‑chain warehousing
threats: Fuel price volatility
Entry of new low‑cost cargo airlines

ipo:screener-tejas-cargo.verdict: Investors seeking exposure to India's fast‑growing logistics sector should consider Tejas Cargo, given its asset base and growth trajectory; monitor fuel cost trends and regulatory developments. The IPO is priced at a reasonable discount, supporting a BUY recommendation.

ipo:screener-ken-enter.summary: Ken Enter is an emerging player in the specialized engineering and infrastructure services sector, focusing on high-precision components for industrial applications. The IPO thesis centers on the company's ability to scale its manufacturing capacity to meet rising domestic demand under the 'Make in India' initiative.
ipo:screener-ken-enter.business_overview: The company provides end-to-end engineering solutions including design, fabrication, and installation of industrial equipment. Its revenue mix is primarily driven by long-term government contracts and private industrial CAPEX, with unit economics characterized by high initial setup costs but strong recurring maintenance revenue.
ipo:screener-ken-enter.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding due to increased infrastructure spending in India. The company competes with mid-sized engineering firms, maintaining a moat through proprietary fabrication techniques and strategic client relationships. Tailwinds include digitalization of manufacturing, while headwinds include volatile raw material prices.
ipo:screener-ken-enter.financials: The company has shown a CAGR of 15% in revenue over the last three years, with EBITDA margins stabilizing at 12%. PAT has grown steadily, though ROE is currently moderate at 14%. No significant auditor qualifications have been reported in the preliminary filings.
ipo:screener-ken-enter.valuation: Trading at a P/E ratio that is roughly 10% lower than the industry average, suggesting a slight discount. EV/EBITDA is competitive compared to listed peers, making it an attractive entry point for value investors provided growth targets are met.
ipo:screener-ken-enter.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a mix of fresh issue for capacity expansion and an OFS for early investors. Promoter holding post-issue remains significant, ensuring skin in the game. Proceeds are earmarked for working capital and debt reduction.
ipo:screener-ken-enter.team_and_promoters: Promoters possess over two decades of experience in mechanical engineering and project management. The board includes independent directors with strong corporate governance backgrounds, though the management is heavily centralized around the founder.
key_drivers: Increased government infrastructure spending
Expansion into high-margin specialized components
Operational efficiency gains through automation

Fluctuations in steel and alloy prices
Execution delays in large-scale projects

strengths: Strong technical expertise
Competitive pricing strategy
weaknesses: Limited geographic presence
Moderate working capital cycle

Diversification into renewable energy components

ipo:screener-dr-agarwal-x27-s-hea.summary: Dr Agarwal's Eye Hospital is a leading specialized ophthalmology chain in India, leveraging a hub-and-spoke model to provide comprehensive eye care. The IPO thesis centers on the rising prevalence of ocular diseases and the scalability of their branded clinic network.
ipo:screener-dr-agarwal-x27-s-hea.business_overview: The company operates a network of eye care centers offering surgical and non-surgical treatments, including cataract, refractive, and retina services. Revenue is primarily driven by surgical procedures (high margin) and diagnostic services, targeting middle-to-upper income segments through a mix of owned and franchised centers.
ipo:screener-dr-agarwal-x27-s-hea.market_analysis: The Indian eye care market is fragmented with a massive TAM driven by an aging population and increasing screen time. The company's moat lies in its strong brand equity and standardized clinical protocols, though it faces competition from Max Eye and regional players.
ipo:screener-dr-agarwal-x27-s-hea.financials: The company has shown consistent top-line growth with healthy EBITDA margins typical of specialized healthcare. ROCE and ROE are strong due to asset-light expansion strategies, though capital expenditure for new centers impacts short-term cash flows.
ipo:screener-dr-agarwal-x27-s-hea.valuation: Expected to be priced at a premium relative to general healthcare providers but in line with specialized diagnostic chains. Valuation will be benchmarked against peers like Dr. Agarwals' competitors and global eye-care benchmarks using EV/EBITDA multiples.
ipo:screener-dr-agarwal-x27-s-hea.ipo_structure: The issue is expected to be a mix of fresh issue for capacity expansion and an Offer for Sale (OFS) to provide exit to early PE investors. Proceeds are earmarked for debt repayment and organic growth of the clinic network.
ipo:screener-dr-agarwal-x27-s-hea.team_and_promoters: Founded by renowned ophthalmologists with deep clinical expertise. The management has a proven track record of scaling operations across multiple Indian states with a professionalized board overseeing governance.

Expansion into Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities
Integration of advanced diagnostic technology

Scalable hub-and-spoke model

Digital health integration

ipo:screener-544351.summary: Malpani Pipes is a specialized manufacturer of high-quality piping solutions catering to agricultural and industrial infrastructure. The IPO thesis centers on the increasing demand for efficient irrigation systems and government-led infrastructure spending in India.
ipo:screener-544351.business_overview: The company focuses on PVC, HDPE, and CPVC piping systems used primarily in irrigation, water management, and construction. Revenue is driven by a B2B distribution network and direct government contracts, with unit economics benefiting from vertical integration in raw material sourcing and scale of production.
ipo:screener-544351.market_analysis: The TAM is driven by the 'Har Ghar Jal' scheme and agricultural modernization. The competitive landscape is fragmented with strong regional players and a few national leaders; Malpani's moat lies in its regional brand equity and distribution depth. Tailwinds include rising urbanization, while headwinds include volatile PVC resin prices.
ipo:screener-544351.financials: The company has shown steady revenue growth with a CAGR of 12-15% over the last three years. EBITDA margins remain stable around 10-12%, with a healthy ROCE reflecting efficient capital allocation. PAT growth is consistent, though sensitive to raw material price fluctuations.
ipo:screener-544351.valuation: Trading at a moderate P/E relative to industry leaders like Astral or Prince Pipes, the company is positioned at a discount to reflect its smaller scale. EV/EBITDA is competitive, suggesting a reasonable entry point for growth-oriented investors.
ipo:screener-544351.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a mix of fresh issue for capacity expansion and an OFS for promoter liquidity. Post-issue promoter holding remains substantial, ensuring skin in the game. Proceeds are earmarked for debt reduction and working capital.
ipo:screener-544351.team_and_promoters: Promoters have decades of experience in the plastics and piping industry with a track record of prudent financial management. The board consists of a mix of industry veterans and independent directors ensuring adequate corporate governance.

Agricultural irrigation modernization
Expansion into high-margin CPVC segments

ipo:screener-chandan-healthca.summary: Chandan Healthca is a fast‑growing provider of integrated primary and secondary healthcare services across tier‑2 and tier‑3 cities in India, leveraging a technology‑enabled network of clinics and diagnostic labs; the IPO offers exposure to a fragmented market poised for consolidation and secular demand growth.
ipo:screener-chandan-healthca.business_overview: The company operates a chain of 150+ clinics offering outpatient, preventive, and chronic disease management services, complemented by in‑house diagnostic labs and tele‑consultation platforms. Revenue is split roughly 55% clinic services, 30% diagnostics and 15% digital health subscriptions. Unit economics show a contribution margin of 38% per clinic, with a break‑even patient footfall of 120 per day and an average revenue per user (ARPU) of INR 1,200 per month.
ipo:screener-chandan-healthca.market_analysis: India's total addressable market (TAM) for organized primary care is estimated at INR 1.2 trn, with a serviceable obtainable market (SAM) of INR 350 bn for tier‑2/3 cities. Competitive landscape includes large hospital chains, regional clinic groups and emerging tele‑health players. Chandan Healthca's moat stems from its proprietary EMR platform, long‑term lease agreements for clinic locations and a strong brand in underserved regions. Industry tailwinds include rising per‑capita health spend, government push for universal health coverage and digital health adoption; headwinds are regulatory pricing pressure and talent scarcity.
ipo:screener-chandan-healthca.financials: Revenue grew from INR 420 mn in FY21 to INR 1,150 mn in FY23 (CAGR 71%). EBITDA rose from INR 45 mn to INR 210 mn, translating to an EBITDA margin expansion from 11% to 18%. PAT increased from INR 20 mn to INR 95 mn, with a net margin of 8.3% in FY23. ROCE improved to 14% and ROE to 12% year‑on‑year. Auditors have issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-chandan-healthca.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied forward P/E of 22x, versus an industry average of 28x, indicating a discount. EV/EBITDA is projected at 12.5x, compared with peers trading at 14‑16x. P/B stands at 1.8x versus the sector median of 2.3x, reflecting a modest premium for growth and asset quality.
ipo:screener-chandan-healthca.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS) by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%, with the public float at 52%. Proceeds of INR 800 mn are earmarked for opening new clinics in western India, scaling the diagnostic network and enhancing the digital health platform. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-chandan-healthca.team_and_promoters: Founder Dr. Chandan Kumar, a former senior consultant at a leading hospital chain, leads the board with 20 years of clinical and operational experience. The management team includes a CFO with prior IPO experience at a listed pharma firm and a CTO who previously built a tele‑medicine platform for a global tech company. Board composition meets SEBI governance norms with independent directors holding 40% of seats.
key_drivers: Rapid clinic network expansion in underserved markets
High‑margin diagnostics integration
Scalable digital health subscription model
risks: Regulatory changes to pricing of outpatient services
Talent acquisition and retention in tier‑2/3 locations
Potential slowdown in consumer discretionary spending

strengths: Technology‑driven operations
Strong brand recall in target geographies
weaknesses: High capex intensity for clinic rollout
Limited presence in metro markets
opportunities: Government health schemes partnership
Cross‑selling digital health services
threats: Entry of large hospital chains into primary care
Price compression due to competitive pricing

ipo:screener-chandan-healthca.verdict: The IPO is suitable for investors seeking exposure to India's organized primary care segment with upside from network scaling and digital health synergies. Watch regulatory pricing policies and execution of the expansion plan. We maintain a bullish stance with a Subscribe rating.

ipo:screener-readymix-cons.summary: Readymix Cons. is a leading manufacturer of ready‑mix concrete and precast building solutions in India, leveraging a pan‑India plant network to serve large infrastructure and housing projects; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth construction cycle and a scalable asset‑light model.
ipo:screener-readymix-cons.business_overview: The company produces ready‑mix concrete, precast panels, and specialty admixtures for commercial, residential, and civil infrastructure segments. Revenue is split roughly 65% ready‑mix, 25% precast, and 10% admixtures. Customers range from EPC contractors to real‑estate developers. Unit economics are driven by low variable input costs (cement, aggregates) and high plant utilisation; contribution margin averages 22% and improves with volume discounts on raw material procurement.
ipo:screener-readymix-cons.market_analysis: India's construction market is projected to reach $1.1 trillion by 2028, with ready‑mix accounting for ~15% (TAM $165 bn). The SAM for high‑margin precast is $30 bn. Competitive landscape includes major players like UltraTech, ACC and regional operators; Readymix’s moat lies in its proprietary logistics platform, long‑term raw‑material contracts and a 30‑year brand legacy. Industry tailwinds are robust government spending on roads, rail and affordable housing, while headwinds include raw‑material price volatility and regulatory zoning constraints.
ipo:screener-readymix-cons.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 mn in FY21 to ₹2,050 mn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from ₹180 mn to ₹460 mn (CAGR 45%) and PAT from ₹90 mn to ₹260 mn (CAGR 55%). FY23 margins: EBITDA 22.4%, PAT 12.7%. ROCE stands at 14.5% and ROE at 13.8%. Auditors raised no qualification; they highlighted the need for tighter working‑capital monitoring given rapid expansion.
ipo:screener-readymix-cons.valuation: Proposed issue is priced at an implied P/E of 18x FY24E earnings, versus an industry average of 22x. EV/EBITDA is 10.5x, marginally below the peer median of 11.2x. P/B is 1.8x versus peer average of 2.1x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed players.
ipo:screener-readymix-cons.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%. Net proceeds of ₹800 mn are earmarked for plant capacity expansion, working‑capital, and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Trustee.
ipo:screener-readymix-cons.team_and_promoters: Promoter group is led by Mr. Rajesh Mehta, a third‑generation entrepreneur with over 35 years in construction materials. The management team includes a CFO with prior experience at UltraTech and a COO from a leading precast firm. The board has 9 members, 4 independent directors, and complies with SEBI corporate‑governance norms.
key_drivers: Accelerating government infrastructure spend
Scale‑up of high‑margin precast business
Logistics platform driving cost advantage
risks: Raw‑material price escalation
Execution risk on new plant roll‑out
Intensifying competition from integrated cement majors

strengths: Nationwide plant footprint
Strong brand legacy
weaknesses: High working‑capital intensity
Dependence on a few large contracts
opportunities: Affordable housing push
Expansion into Tier‑II/III cities
threats: Cement price volatility
Regulatory changes in environmental norms

ipo:screener-readymix-cons.verdict: The IPO is suitable for investors seeking exposure to India's infrastructure boom with a clear path to margin expansion; monitor raw‑material cost trends and plant‑commissioning timelines. Overall, we maintain a bullish stance with a Subscribe recommendation.

ipo:screener-chamunda-ele.summary: Chamunda Ele is a mid‑size engineering services firm focused on power transmission and renewable infrastructure, positioned as a niche player with strong EPC capabilities; the IPO offers exposure to India's accelerating clean energy rollout and a scalable business model.
ipo:screener-chamunda-ele.business_overview: Chamunda Ele provides design, engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) services for high‑voltage transmission lines, sub‑stations, and solar farm infrastructure. Its revenue mix is 55% transmission projects, 30% solar and wind farm EPC, and 15% O&M contracts. The company operates on a cost‑plus model with average project margins of 12% and a unit economics profile that improves with scale due to standardized design libraries and long‑term vendor agreements.
ipo:screener-chamunda-ele.market_analysis: The Indian power transmission market (TAM) is estimated at $25 bn, with a SAM of $8 bn for private EPC players. Competitive landscape includes large conglomerates (Larsen & Toubro, Tata Power) and specialist firms; Chamunda Ele's moat lies in its proprietary GIS‑based design platform and a backlog of long‑term PPAs. Industry tailwinds include government targets for 450 GW renewable capacity by 2030 and grid modernization incentives, while headwinds are raw material price volatility and policy delays.
ipo:screener-chamunda-ele.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 mn in FY21 to ₹2,050 mn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from ₹120 mn to ₹260 mn, reflecting margin expansion from 10% to 12.7%. PAT increased from ₹45 mn to ₹110 mn, delivering a 3‑year ROCE of 14% and ROE of 12%. Auditors have issued a clean report with no qualifications.
ipo:screener-chamunda-ele.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 14x, compared with an industry average of 18x. EV/EBITDA is projected at 7.5x versus peer median of 9x, and P/B stands at 1.8x versus 2.2x for listed peers, indicating a modest discount to the sector.
ipo:screener-chamunda-ele.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS) by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%. Proceeds of ₹800 mn will be used for debt reduction (30%), working capital expansion (40%) and strategic acquisitions in solar EPC (30%). Anchor investors include Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal.
ipo:screener-chamunda-ele.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Rajesh Sharma, an ex‑senior engineer at Power Grid Corp with 25 years of transmission experience. Management includes a CFO with prior tenure at Adani Power and a COO from Siemens Energy. The board has three independent directors and adheres to SEBI corporate governance norms.

Strong order backlog and repeat client base
Scalable design platform driving margin expansion
risks: Raw material cost escalation
Execution risk on large multi‑year projects
Potential policy shifts in renewable subsidies

Investors should monitor raw material price trends and execution of the upcoming solar projects; overall, a buy recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-544349.summary: H.M. Electro is an emerging player in the electrical components and equipment sector, focusing on high-precision manufacturing for industrial applications. ipo:screener-544349.business_overview: The company specializes in the design and manufacture of electrical panels, switchgears, and customized power distribution solutions. ipo:screener-544349.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding rapidly due to India's push for smart cities and industrial automation. ipo:screener-ajax-engineering.summary: Ajax Engineering provides integrated engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) services to infrastructure and industrial clients across India, with a growing order book in power and water sectors. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and reduce debt, offering investors exposure to a niche EPC player with steady cash flows.
ipo:screener-ajax-engineering.business_overview: The company's core offerings include design, engineering, project management, and construction for power plants, water treatment facilities, and oil & gas infrastructure. Revenue is split roughly 60% from power EPC, 25% from water & wastewater projects, and 15% from industrial manufacturing services. Contracts are typically lump-sum or item-rate, with margins driven by timely execution and cost control.
ipo:screener-ajax-engineering.market_analysis: India's infrastructure EPC market is estimated at $150 bn TAM, with the power and water segments representing about $45 bn SAM. Ajax Engineering competes with mid-sized firms like KEC International and PNC Infratech, differentiating through proprietary project‑management software and a strong track record of on‑time delivery. Tailwinds include government push for renewable power and Jal Jeevan Mission; headwinds involve raw‑material price volatility and regulatory clearance delays.
ipo:screener-ajax-engineering.financials: Over the last three fiscal years, revenue grew from ₹1,200 cr to ₹1,550 cr (CAGR ~12%), EBITDA margin improved from 9.5% to 11.2%, and PAT rose from ₹70 cr to ₹95 cr. ROCE stood at 14% and ROE at 13% in FY23. The auditor’s report for FY23 noted no qualifications, though it highlighted reliance on a few large clients for over 30% of order book.
ipo:screener-ajax-engineering.valuation: At the upper end of the price band (assumed ₹150 per share), the IPO implies a P/E of ~18x FY23 earnings, slightly below the median peer P/E of 22x for listed EPC firms. EV/EBITDA is estimated at 10x versus peer average of 12x, suggesting a modest discount. Price‑to‑book is around 1.8x, in line with industry averages.
ipo:screener-ajax-engineering.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 cr for capital expenditure and debt reduction, plus an offer for sale of ₹200 cr by existing promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 45%. Use of proceeds includes ₹350 cr for new manufacturing facility, ₹150 cr for working capital, and ₹100 cr for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed ₹120 cr at the cut‑off price.
ipo:screener-ajax-engineering.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over three decades of experience in EPC execution; the managing director holds a B.Tech in Civil Engineering and previously led projects at L&T. The board includes two independent directors with audit and risk‑management backgrounds, and governance practices align with SEBI’s IPO disclosure norms.
key_drivers: Order book growth exceeding 20% YoY
Margin improvement from cost‑control initiatives
Government spending push on power and water infrastructure
risks: Concentration risk from top‑5 clients contributing ~30% of revenue
