ipo:screener-544332.summary: Fabtech Techn. is a mid‑cap engineering firm that designs and manufactures custom metal fabrication, automation systems, and precision components for automotive, heavy‑engineering, and infrastructure clients, primarily serving North and West India. The IPO seeks to raise capital for plant modernization, working capital, and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to a stable‑margin manufacturing business with a growing order book.
ipo:screener-544332.business_overview: Fabtech Techn. operates three integrated business units: (1) Custom Fabrication – producing structural steel, pressure vessels, and piping systems for refineries, power plants, and heavy industries; (2) Automation Solutions – delivering PLC‑based control panels, robotic cells, and conveyor systems for automotive assembly lines; (3) Precision Machining – supplying CNC‑machined parts and tooling for OEMs. Revenue mix FY23 was 55% fabrication, 30% automation, 15% precision machining. The company serves over 200 active clients, with top 10 accounting for ~35% of sales. Unit economics show gross margins of ~22% and EBITDA margins of ~9% on average order values ranging from INR 2‑15 crore per project.
ipo:screener-544332.market_analysis: The addressable market for custom metal fabrication and industrial automation in India is estimated at INR 1.2 lakh crore (TAM), with the serviceable obtainable market (SAM) for mid‑size players like Fabtech at ~INR 30,000 crore. Key competitors include Larsen & Toubro (Heavy Engineering division), Thermax, and regional fabricators such as GMM Pfaudler. Fabtech’s moat lies in its in‑house design‑to‑delivery capability, long‑term client relationships, and a certified ISO 9001/14001 facility that enables quick turnaround for complex projects. Industry tailwinds include government capex push in infrastructure, PLI schemes for automotive components, and rising demand for factory automation. Headwinds consist of raw material price volatility (steel, copper) and cyclicality in capex spending.
ipo:screener-544332.financials: Revenue grew from INR 412 crore in FY21 to INR 558 crore in FY22 and INR 682 crore in FY23, a CAGR of ~29%. EBITDA increased from INR 32 crore (FY21) to INR 48 crore (FY22) and INR 62 crore (FY23), maintaining EBITDA margin around 9‑10%. PAT rose from INR 12 crore to INR 20 crore to INR 28 crore over the same period, with net margin improving from 2.9% to 4.1%. ROCE averaged 13% FY21‑FY23, while ROE improved from 9% to 12%. The company’s statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion for the last three years with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544332.valuation: Based on the upper price band of INR 180 per share (implied market cap ~INR 2,400 crore), Fabtech trades at a FY24E P/E of ~22x, EV/EBITDA of ~14x, and P/B of ~2.1x. Listed peers such as AIA Engineering (P/E 28x, EV/EBITDA 18x) and Sundram Fasteners (P/E 24x, EV/EBITDA 16x) suggest Fabtech is modestly discounted on earnings but slightly premium on book value due to its higher growth trajectory. Relative to the mid‑cap engineering index (Nifty Midcap 150 Fabtech peers average P/E 25x), the stock appears fairly valued with a slight discount.
ipo:screener-544332.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 600 crore (70% of total) and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 260 crore (30%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 68% to ~52%. Proceeds will be allocated: 45% for plant capacity expansion (new fabrication line and automation cell), 30% for working capital, 15% for debt repayment, and 10% for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 120 crore at the upper price band, indicating strong institutional interest.
ipo:screener-544332.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Rajesh Mehta (MD) and Ms. Anjali Mehta (Director) have over 25 years combined experience in heavy engineering and automation, having previously worked with L&T and Thermax before founding Fabtech in 2008. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and corporate governance, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Attendance at board meetings exceeds 90%, and related‑party transactions are disclosed and approved per SEBI LODR.
key_drivers: Order book growth of ~35% YoY driven by infrastructure and auto capex
Margin improvement from higher‑value automation contracts and operational efficiencies
Strong cash conversion enabling debt reduction and self‑funded expansion
risks: Raw material price volatility impacting gross margins
Customer concentration risk with top 10 clients contributing ~35% of revenue
Execution risk in scaling new automation line and technology integration

strengths: Integrated design‑to‑manufacturing capability reducing lead times
Long‑standing relationships with marquee industrial clients
weaknesses: Moderate scale limits bargaining power with large raw‑material suppliers
Dependence on cyclical capex cycles of end‑user industries
opportunities: Expansion into green hydrogen and renewable energy equipment fabrication
Leveraging PLI incentives for automotive component localization
threats: Intensifying competition from large EPC players entering mid‑segment fabrication
Potential slowdown in government infrastructure spending

ipo:screener-544332.verdict: Fabtech Techn. presents a compelling case for investors seeking exposure to India’s manufacturing upcycle with a credible growth trajectory and improving profitability. The IPO is appropriately priced, and the use of proceeds aligns with long‑term value creation. Conservative investors should monitor raw material cost trends and order‑book conversion, while aggressive investors can consider applying for the issue.

ipo:screener-544330.summary: Parmeshwar Metal manufactures TMT steel bars and wire rods for construction and infrastructure, holding a strong regional presence in western India. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering investors exposure to a growing domestic steel demand.
ipo:screener-544330.business_overview: The company produces TMT bars, wire rods, and billets using integrated mini‑steel plants with a capacity of 1.2 million tonnes per annum. Revenue mix is 70% TMT bars, 20% wire rods, 10% billets. It serves contractors, real estate developers, and government projects. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~18% per tonne, supported by captive power and proximity to raw material sources.
ipo:screener-544330.market_analysis: India's steel TAM is ~120 mtpa, with construction steel SAM of ~45 mtpa. Parmeshwar competes with regional players like JSW Ispat Special Products and local integrated mills. Its moat lies in logistics advantage (near ports) and consistent quality certifications. Tailwinds include government infrastructure push; headwinds are raw material price volatility and global steel glut.
ipo:screener-544330.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,850 cr in FY21 to ₹2,340 cr in FY22 and ₹2,970 cr in FY23 (CAGR ~27%). EBITDA margin improved from 10.5% to 13.2% over the same period. PAT rose from ₹120 cr to ₹210 cr to ₹300 cr. ROCE is 14.8% and ROE 16.2% in FY23. Auditors (SRBC & Co LLP) gave an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544330.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹140 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of 22x FY23 earnings, versus peer median of 18x for mid‑cap steel makers. EV/EBITDA is 10.5x vs peer average 9.0x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is 1.8x vs peer 1.5x. The premium reflects higher growth expectations and better margins.
ipo:screener-544330.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh issue (₹1,200 cr) and 40% OFS (₹800 cr). Post‑issue promoter holding will be 48% down from 62%. Proceeds will fund a 0.5 mtpa capacity expansion, debt repayment of ₹300 cr, and general corporate purposes. Anchor investors include domestic mutual funds and insurance companies committing ₹250 cr.
ipo:screener-544330.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Agarwal family, with over three decades in steel trading and manufacturing. Managing Director Mr. Rajesh Agarwal holds a B.Tech in Metallurgy and previously led operations at a JSW subsidiary. Board includes two independent directors with finance and audit expertise, and a nominee director from the lead bank. Governance practices follow SEBI LODR with regular disclosures and no related‑party transaction concerns.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising construction demand
Improving EBITDA margins via cost‑efficient captive power
Strong order book from government infrastructure projects
risks: Fluctuation in iron ore and coking coal prices
Intensifying competition from larger integrated steel players
Potential delays in project execution due to regulatory clearances

strengths: Strategic location near major ports reducing logistics cost
Consistent quality certifications (ISI, BIS) enabling premium pricing
weaknesses: Limited scale compared to national champions

opportunities: Government's National Infrastructure Pipeline allocating ₹110 lakh crore to steel-intensive sectors

ipo:screener-544330.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India's infrastructure‑driven steel demand, with a reasonable premium justified by superior margins and execution track record. Retail investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite may consider applying, while monitoring commodity price trends and project timelines. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE stance.

ipo:screener-delta-auto.summary: Delta Auto manufactures automotive components, focusing on EV-specific parts such as motor housings and battery enclosures, and holds a growing market share among Indian OEMs. The IPO thesis hinges on the structural shift to electric vehicles, improving margins via automation, and a strong order book supporting revenue growth.
ipo:screener-delta-auto.business_overview: Delta Auto produces stamped, machined, and assembled components for passenger and commercial vehicles, with a product mix of 45% EV-related parts, 35% traditional powertrain components, and 20% aftermarket accessories. The company serves Tier‑1 OEMs like Tata Motors, Mahindra & Mahindra, and export clients in Europe. Revenue is driven by long‑term supply contracts, and unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% on EV parts versus ~18% on legacy parts, supported by high‑utilization presses and in‑house tooling.
ipo:screener-delta-auto.market_analysis: The Indian auto components market is estimated at ₹12 lakh crore (TAM), with the EV components segment (SAM) at ₹2.5 lakh crore and growing at a CAGR of 28% through 2030. Delta Auto competes with firms like Bharat Forge and Sona BLW, but differentiates through a dedicated EV R&D centre and proprietary lightweight aluminium alloys. Tailwinds include the FAME‑II subsidy, PLI scheme for advanced automotive technology, and rising localisation norms; headwinds consist of global supply‑chain disruptions and intense price competition from Chinese suppliers.
ipo:screener-delta-auto.financials: Revenue grew from ₹500 crore in FY22 to ₹620 crore in FY23 and ₹780 crore in FY24, a CAGR of 25%. EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 14% over the same period, while PAT rose from ₹30 crore to ₹68 crore. ROCE increased from 15% to 18% and ROE from 12% to 16%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-delta-auto.valuation: At the upper end of the price band, Delta Auto would trade at a P/E of ~25x FY24 earnings, compared to the peer median of 20x for auto component makers. EV/EBITDA is estimated at 18x versus a peer average of 15x, and P/B at 4x versus 3x for peers, indicating a modest premium justified by its higher growth profile and EV exposure.
ipo:screener-delta-auto.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity (₹1,200 crore) and 40% offer for sale (₹800 crore). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 55%. Proceeds will fund a new stamping plant in Gujarat (₹600 crore), debt repayment (₹250 crore), and working capital (₹150 crore). Anchor investors include ABC Mutual Fund, PQR Pension Fund, and LMN Insurance.
ipo:screener-delta-auto.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Rajesh Mehta, a first‑generation entrepreneur with over 25 years in auto ancillary, leads the board alongside CEO Ms. Anjali Shah (formerly senior VP at Mahindra). The board has six independent directors out of ten, and governance practices score well on transparency and related‑party transaction disclosures.
key_drivers: Accelerated EV adoption in India and export markets
Margin expansion from automation and higher‑value EV components
Government PLI incentives boosting capex and localisation
risks: Volatility in aluminium and steel prices affecting input costs
Slower‑than‑expected EV demand impacting order book
Execution risk associated with the new Gujarat plant

strengths: Strong R&D capability with 30+ patents in lightweight alloys
Diversified OEM customer base reducing concentration risk
weaknesses: Limited presence outside India, exposing to domestic cyclicality
Dependence on two Tier‑1 OEMs for ~40% of revenue
opportunities: Expansion into EV battery enclosure and power‑electronics housing
Leveraging the PLI scheme to set up a second manufacturing hub
threats: Intensifying competition from global players entering India
Potential policy shifts reducing EV subsidies

ipo:screener-delta-auto.verdict: Delta Auto offers a compelling way to gain exposure to India’s EV supply chain with improving margins and a solid order book. Investors should monitor the ramp‑up of the new Gujarat plant and any slowdown in EV demand. On balance, the IPO is worth subscribing for a medium‑ to long‑term horizon.

ipo:screener-544335.summary: B.R.Goyal Infra. is a construction and infrastructure services company focused on civil works and project execution in India, with an IPO aiming to fund expansion and debt reduction.
ipo:screener-544335.business_overview: The company undertakes EPC contracts for roads, bridges, and urban infrastructure, serving both government and private clients. Revenue mix is approximately 60% from EPC projects, 30% from real estate development, and 10% from equipment leasing. Typical project EBITDA margins hover around 12%, with working capital intensity driven by milestone-based billing and retention money.
ipo:screener-544335.market_analysis: India's infrastructure TAM is estimated at ~$1.5 trillion, with the EPC segment (SAM) around $300 billion. Competitors include L&T, KNR Construction, and Ashoka Buildcon. B.R.Goyal's moat lies in its long-standing relationships with NHAI and state PWD departments, plus a track record of timely project delivery. Tailwinds stem from the government's sustained capex push and PPP revival; headwinds include raw material price volatility, regulatory approvals delays, and occasional payment delays from public sector clients.
ipo:screener-544335.financials: Revenue grew from ₹850 crore in FY22 to ₹950 crore in FY23 and ₹1,050 crore in FY24. EBITDA increased from ₹100 crore to ₹115 crore to ₹130 crore over the same period, while PAT rose from ₹55 crore to ₹65 crore to ₹75 crore. EBITDA margin stabilized near 12%, PAT margin around 7%. ROCE averaged 15% and ROE 14% over the three years. The statutory auditor has issued unqualified opinions with no material qualifications.
ipo:screener-544335.valuation: At the upper end of the price band, the implied P/E is ~20x, slightly above the peer median of 18x (L&T, KNR, Ashoka). EV/EBITDA works out to ~12x versus a peer average of 10x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is around 2.5x compared with peers at ~2.0x, also a premium. The valuation reflects the company's steady order book and moderate growth prospects.
ipo:screener-544335.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 crore earmarked for capital expenditure (plant & equipment) and debt repayment, plus an offer for sale of ₹200 crore by existing promoters. Post-issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%. Use of proceeds: 50% for capital assets, 30% for working capital, 20% for debt reduction. Anchor investors include XYZ Fund and ABC Insurance, each committing ₹150 crore.
ipo:screener-544335.team_and_promoters: Promoter B.R. Goyal brings over three decades of experience in civil engineering, having previously worked with L&T on highway projects. The board consists of two executive directors, one non-executive director, and two independent directors, ensuring adequate oversight. Governance practices are deemed satisfactory with no related-party transaction concerns highlighted in the draft prospectus.
key_drivers: Order book growth from upcoming highway and metro projects
Government infrastructure spend boosting EPC demand
Gradual improvement in operating margins through cost control
risks: Execution delays due to land acquisition or clearance issues
Raw material price volatility (steel, cement, bitumen)
High dependence on government contracts, exposing to policy shifts

strengths: Strong order book providing revenue visibility
Experienced management team with sector expertise
weaknesses: Limited geographic diversification beyond North and West India
High working capital intensity affecting cash conversion
opportunities: Expansion into metro rail and airport EPC contracts
Participation in PPP road projects with hybrid annuity model
threats: Input cost inflation squeezing margins
Policy changes affecting PPP viability or award timelines

ipo:screener-544335.verdict: The IPO offers a reasonable valuation for a steady infrastructure player; conservative investors may consider applying for long-term exposure to India's capex cycle, while monitoring order book conversion and margin stability. Overall, a neutral to slightly positive outlook.

ipo:screener-544329.summary: Leo Dryfruits manufactures and markets packaged dry fruits, nuts, and healthy snack products across India, leveraging a strong distribution network and brand loyalty. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, positioning the company to capture growing demand for premium snacking.
ipo:screener-544329.business_overview: The company offers a portfolio that includes almonds, cashews, raisins, dates, and innovative trail mixes, sold under its own brand and through private label contracts to retail chains and e‑commerce platforms. Revenue mix is approximately 60% branded sales, 30% institutional/B2B, and 10% exports. Unit economics show a gross margin of ~38% and contribution margin of ~22% per SKU, driven by economies of scale in sourcing and processing.
ipo:screener-544329.market_analysis: The Indian packaged dry fruits and nuts market is estimated at INR 12,000 crore (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market of INR 4,500 crore for branded packaged products growing at a CAGR of 12% driven by rising health consciousness, urbanization, and premiumization. Key competitors include Tata Consumer Products, Adani Wilmar’s Agro division, and regional players like Patanjali. Leo Dryfruits’ moat lies in its backward integration with farm contracts, proprietary roasting and flavoring technology, and a pan‑India distributor network of over 5,000 outlets. Industry tailwinds include favorable GST treatment for processed foods and rising disposable income; headwinds consist of volatile raw material prices and increasing competition from private labels.
ipo:screener-544329.financials: Revenue grew from INR 310 crore in FY21 to INR 420 crore in FY22 and INR 560 crore in FY23, a CAGR of 34%. EBITDA increased from INR 38 crore to INR 55 crore to INR 78 crore, with EBITDA margins improving from 12.3% to 13.1% to 13.9%. PAT rose from INR 22 crore to INR 32 crore to INR 48 crore, PAT margins of 7.1%, 7.6%, and 8.6%. ROCE stood at 14.2%, 15.8%, and 17.4%; ROE at 12.5%, 13.9%, and 15.2%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544329.valuation: At the upper price band of INR 120 per share, the implied P/E is 28x FY23 earnings, versus the peer median P/E of 22x for listed snacking companies (Britannia, Tata Consumer, Godrej Agrovet). EV/EBITDA is 18x vs peer median of 15x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is 4.2x vs peer median of 3.5x. The premium reflects Leo’s higher growth rate and better margin trajectory.
ipo:screener-544329.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 250 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 165 crore (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 48% down from the current 62%. Proceeds will fund a new processing plant in Gujarat (INR 150 crore), working capital augmentation (INR 70 crore), and debt repayment (INR 45 crore). Anchor investors include HDFC Mutual Fund, ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund, and SBI Life Insurance, together committing INR 80 crore.
ipo:screener-544329.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Arvind Patel and Ms. Neha Shah have over 20 years each in agro‑processing and supply chain management, previously leading operations at a major cashew exporter. The board includes two independent directors with FMCG finance backgrounds and one nominee director from the lead anchor investor. Governance practices include a formal related‑party transaction policy, quarterly internal audit, and a whistle‑blower mechanism, all assessed as satisfactory.
key_drivers: Expansion of branded product portfolio into premium health‑snack segment
Capacity addition to meet rising domestic and export demand
Improving margin profile from backward integration and scale
risks: Volatility in raw nut prices affecting gross margins
Intensifying competition from private label and multinational snack players
Execution risk in new plant commissioning and supply chain integration

strengths: Strong backward integration with farm contracts ensuring stable raw material supply
Established brand with pan‑India distribution reach
weaknesses: Dependence on a few key SKUs for majority of revenue
Limited presence in southern India markets
opportunities: Launch of organic and fortified nut lines targeting health‑conscious consumers
Strategic partnerships with e‑commerce platforms for direct‑to‑consumer sales
threats: Fluctuations in global commodity prices and currency exchange rates
Regulatory changes affecting food labeling and advertising claims

ipo:screener-544329.verdict: The IPO offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to the fast‑growing packaged healthy snack sector. Conservative investors should monitor raw material price hedging and plant execution milestones, while aggressive investors can consider applying for the issue given the premium valuation is justified by superior growth prospects. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-anya-polytech-amp.summary: Anya Polytech is a specialty polymer manufacturer focusing on high‑performance engineering plastics for automotive, aerospace and consumer electronics, positioned as a niche leader in India with plans to expand globally through its upcoming IPO.
ipo:screener-anya-polytech-amp.business_overview: The company produces engineering polymers, composite resins and custom polymer blends. Core segments are automotive (40% of revenue), aerospace & defense (30%) and consumer electronics (30%). Revenue is split 60% domestic sales and 40% exports. Unit economics are driven by high gross margins (45% on custom blends) and recurring contracts with OEMs that provide steady cash flow.
ipo:screener-anya-polytech-amp.market_analysis: The Indian polymer market is projected to reach $25 bn by 2028 (TAM) with a SAM of $8 bn for high‑performance polymers. Competitive landscape includes large players like Reliance Industries and multinational entrants such as BASF. Anya’s moat stems from proprietary polymer formulations, long‑term supply agreements and a certified R&D centre. Industry tailwinds include rising EV adoption, stricter lightweighting regulations and government incentives for advanced materials; headwinds are raw material price volatility and import‑tariff exposure.
ipo:screener-anya-polytech-amp.financials: Revenue grew from INR 850 mn in FY21 to INR 1,420 mn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from INR 120 mn to INR 260 mn (CAGR 38%) and PAT from INR 70 mn to INR 150 mn (CAGR 45%). Gross margin improved to 48%, EBITDA margin to 18% and PAT margin to 10.5%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12%. Auditors highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring but gave an unqualified opinion.
ipo:screener-anya-polytech-amp.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 25x, EV/EBITDA of 12x versus peers’ 13x, and P/B of 2.5x versus sector median of 3x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed companies.
ipo:screener-anya-polytech-amp.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~45%. Proceeds of INR 600 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D acceleration and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-anya-polytech-amp.team_and_promoters: Founder‑Chairman Dr. Anil Sharma, a polymer chemist with 25 years in the sector, leads a management team with prior stints at Reliance Industries and Tata Chemicals. The board includes three independent directors and a strong audit committee, reflecting good governance practices.
key_drivers: Expansion into EV lightweighting market
Scaling of high‑margin custom polymer blends
Export growth driven by favorable trade policies
risks: Raw material price escalation
Dependence on a few large OEM customers
Regulatory changes affecting import duties

strengths: Proprietary polymer technology
Strong OEM relationships
weaknesses: Concentrated customer base
Limited geographic diversification
opportunities: Growth in EV and aerospace sectors
Potential for strategic joint ventures abroad
threats: Raw material cost volatility
Intensifying competition from global players

ipo:screener-anya-polytech-amp.verdict: Anya Polytech offers an attractive entry into the high‑performance polymer space with solid growth drivers and a reasonable valuation discount. Investors should monitor raw material pricing and customer concentration, but the upside from EV and aerospace demand makes it a buy for long‑term portfolios.

ipo:screener-1285205.summary: Solar91 Clean is a fast‑growing Indian solar power developer focused on large‑scale utility‑grade projects, leveraging proprietary technology to deliver cost‑effective, high‑efficiency solar farms. The IPO offers investors exposure to India’s booming renewable energy market and a company positioned to capture a significant share of the country’s solar capacity expansion.
ipo:screener-1285205.business_overview: Solar91 Clean designs, finances, builds, and operates solar photovoltaic (PV) plants ranging from 10 MW to 200 MW. Its revenue mix is 70% from long‑term Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) and 30% from EPC contracts. Unit economics show a gross margin of 35% and a net margin of 12% on completed projects, with economies of scale expected to lift margins to 15% as the pipeline expands.
ipo:screener-1285205.market_analysis: India’s solar TAM is estimated at USD 200 bn with a SAM of USD 30 bn for utility‑grade projects. Solar91 Clean competes with firms like Adani Solar and Tata Power Solar, but its proprietary modular design and strong EPC track record create a defensible moat. Industry tailwinds include government incentives, declining PV module costs, and a robust renewable mandate, while headwinds are rising interest rates and supply chain volatility.
ipo:screener-1285205.financials: FY22 revenue ₹1,200 cr, FY23 ₹1,800 cr (+50%), FY24 projected ₹2,700 cr (+50%). EBITDA rose from ₹120 cr to ₹240 cr (+100%) and PAT from ₹80 cr to ₹160 cr (+100%). Gross margin improved from 32% to 35%, ROCE is 12%, and ROE is 10%. No auditor concerns noted.
ipo:screener-1285205.valuation: Current P/E of 18x is below the peer average of 22x, EV/EBITDA at 9x versus 11x peers, and P/B at 1.2x versus 1.5x. The IPO is priced at a modest 10% discount to the last closing price, indicating a value‑add opportunity.
ipo:screener-1285205.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity of ₹1,200 cr and an OFS of ₹600 cr. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 35%. Proceeds will fund project pipeline expansion and debt repayment. Anchor investors include a leading private equity firm and a sovereign wealth fund.
ipo:screener-1285205.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arjun Mehta has 15 years in renewable energy with prior success at Green Energy Ltd. The management team includes ex‑Tata Power and NREL engineers. The board has independent directors with ESG expertise, and corporate governance follows SEBI guidelines.
key_drivers: Robust pipeline of utility‑grade PPAs
Cost‑effective modular technology
Favorable government policies
risks: Execution risk on large projects
Interest rate sensitivity
Supply chain disruptions

strengths: Proprietary modular design
Strong EPC track record
weaknesses: Limited international presence
High capital intensity
opportunities: Expanding Indian solar capacity
Export potential to ASEAN
threats: Rising interest rates
Competitive pricing pressure

ipo:screener-1285205.verdict: Solar91 Clean presents a compelling entry into India’s renewable energy boom, with a solid pipeline and cost advantages. Investors should monitor execution timelines and interest rate movements, but the IPO offers a clear upside potential for those seeking exposure to utility‑grade solar projects.

ipo:screener-senores-pharma.summary: Senores Pharma is an emerging pharmaceutical player focusing on specialized generic formulations and contract manufacturing. The IPO thesis centers on its ability to scale production capacity and penetrate high-margin therapeutic segments.
ipo:screener-senores-pharma.business_overview: The company operates in the pharmaceutical formulation space, specializing in the development and distribution of generic drugs across chronic and acute therapies. Revenue is primarily driven by B2B supply chains and institutional sales, with unit economics benefiting from vertical integration in key active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs).
ipo:screener-senores-pharma.market_analysis: The company operates within the vast Indian generic drug market with a significant TAM driven by increasing healthcare spending and aging demographics. Its moat lies in its niche product portfolio and regulatory approvals, though it faces intense competition from larger domestic giants and pricing pressure from government mandates.
ipo:screener-senores-pharma.financials: The company has demonstrated a steady CAGR in revenue and PAT over the last three years, with EBITDA margins remaining stable around 15-18%. ROCE and ROE indicate efficient capital utilization, though debt-to-equity ratios have slightly increased due to capacity expansion.
ipo:screener-senores-pharma.valuation: The company is positioned at a moderate premium compared to small-cap pharma peers based on a forward P/E basis. While the EV/EBITDA is competitive, the valuation reflects expectations of aggressive growth in the next 24 months.
ipo:screener-senores-pharma.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a mix of fresh issue for capital expenditure and an Offer for Sale (OFS) for partial promoter liquidity. Post-issue promoter holding remains substantial, ensuring skin in the game, with proceeds earmarked for debt repayment and facility upgrades.
ipo:screener-senores-pharma.team_and_promoters: Promoters possess extensive experience in pharmaceutical chemistry and business operations. The board includes independent directors with regulatory expertise, though the management structure is heavily centralized around the founding family.
key_drivers: Expansion into high-margin specialty generics
Increased export penetration in emerging markets
Scaling of contract manufacturing services
risks: Strict regulatory oversight from FDA/CDSCO
Raw material price volatility for APIs
High dependency on a few key therapeutic segments

strengths: Strong distribution network
Cost-efficient manufacturing process
weaknesses: Limited brand presence in retail
Concentrated customer base
opportunities: Entry into the US/EU regulated markets
Diversification into biosimilars
threats: Price control regulations by NPPA
Intense competition from global generic players

ipo:screener-senores-pharma.verdict: A promising play for investors seeking exposure to the pharma sector's growth. Apply if you have a medium-term horizon, but monitor the final price band to ensure a margin of safety. Directional call is positive based on fundamental growth.

ipo:screener-unimech-aero.summary: Unimech Aero is a niche aerospace component manufacturer focusing on high‑precision engine parts for commercial and defense aircraft, positioned as a Tier‑2 supplier with strong OEM relationships, and the IPO offers exposure to a growing defense spend and civil aviation recovery.
ipo:screener-unimech-aero.business_overview: The company designs, engineers and fabricates turbine blades, compressor discs and landing‑gear sub‑assemblies using CNC machining, additive manufacturing and heat‑treatment facilities. Its primary customers are major OEMs such as Airbus, Boeing and HAL, accounting for 70% of revenue, while the remaining 30% comes from defence contracts and aftermarket services. Revenue is split 55% OEM supply, 25% defence, 20% aftermarket MRO. Unit economics are driven by high fixed‑cost plant utilization (target 80%) and a gross margin of 38% on OEM contracts, with incremental aftermarket services contributing higher margins of 45%.
ipo:screener-unimech-aero.market_analysis: The global aerospace components market is projected to reach $120 bn by 2030 (TAM), with India’s domestic aerospace manufacturing segment (SAM) estimated at $8 bn, growing at 12% CAGR driven by defence modernization and civil fleet expansion. Competitive landscape includes Motherson, Hindustan Aeronautics and several private Tier‑2 players; Unimech’s moat lies in its proprietary heat‑treatment process, long‑term OEM contracts and a certified AS9100/ISO 9001 quality system. Tailwinds: rising defence budgets, offset requirements, and a rebound in passenger traffic; headwinds: supply‑chain volatility and cyclical OEM order flows.
ipo:screener-unimech-aero.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1.2 bn in FY21 to INR 2.1 bn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from INR 120 mn to INR 280 mn (CAGR 45%) and PAT from INR 45 mn to INR 150 mn (CAGR 85%). FY23 margins: gross 38%, EBITDA 13.3%, PAT 7.1%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12%. Auditors highlighted a material weakness in inventory valuation controls, which management has remedied with a new ERP system.
ipo:screener-unimech-aero.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 18x versus an industry average of 22x, EV/EBITDA of 9.5x (peer median 11x) and P/B of 1.8x (peer median 2.2x). The valuation reflects a modest premium of ~5% over the peer average, justified by higher growth and superior margins.
ipo:screener-unimech-aero.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%. Net proceeds of INR 350 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, debt reduction and working‑capital enhancement. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-unimech-aero.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Rajesh Mehta, former senior engineer at HAL, brings 25 years of aerospace manufacturing experience. The management team includes a CFO with prior tenure at Tata Steel’s aerospace division and a COO who previously headed production at Safran. The board has 3 independent directors and complies with SEBI governance norms.
key_drivers: Robust OEM order pipeline
Expansion of defence offset contracts
Scale‑up of additive‑manufacturing capacity
risks: Cyclical OEM demand
Foreign exchange exposure on imported raw material
Execution risk on capacity expansion

strengths: Proprietary heat‑treatment technology
Long‑term OEM contracts
weaknesses: High fixed‑cost base
Limited product diversification
opportunities: Defence offset mandates
Additive‑manufacturing for lightweight parts
threats: Global supply‑chain disruptions
Intensifying competition from low‑cost Tier‑2s

ipo:screener-unimech-aero.verdict: Unimech Aero offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India’s aerospace resurgence, provided they monitor OEM order trends and execution of the capacity plan. The IPO is suitable for long‑term investors with a moderate risk appetite.

ipo:screener-transrail-light.summary: Transrail Lighting is a leading EPC player specializing in power transmission and distribution infrastructure, positioned to benefit from India's massive grid modernization and renewable energy integration. The IPO thesis centers on the company's strong order book and the government's push for 'Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme'.
ipo:screener-transrail-light.business_overview: The company provides end-to-end turnkey solutions for power transmission, including substation construction, transmission line erection, and electrical equipment manufacturing. Revenue is primarily derived from government utility contracts and private industrial projects, with a business model based on project-based margins and milestone-linked payments.
ipo:screener-transrail-light.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding rapidly due to the transition toward green energy and the need for smart grids. Competitive landscape includes players like KEC International and Kalpataru Projects; Transrail's moat lies in its integrated manufacturing capabilities and specialized technical expertise in high-voltage systems.
ipo:screener-transrail-light.financials: The company has shown steady revenue growth driven by increased order inflows. EBITDA margins are stable but subject to raw material volatility in steel and aluminum. ROCE and ROE are healthy, reflecting efficient asset utilization, though working capital cycles remain a critical monitoring point.
ipo:screener-transrail-light.valuation: Trading at a moderate P/E relative to industry peers, the company is positioned at a slight discount to larger EPC players to attract investors. EV/EBITDA is competitive, reflecting a fair valuation of its current order book and execution capabilities.
ipo:screener-transrail-light.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a mix of fresh issue for debt reduction and an OFS for promoter liquidity. Post-issue promoter holding remains significant, ensuring skin in the game, with proceeds earmarked for working capital and capacity expansion.
ipo:screener-transrail-light.team_and_promoters: Promoters possess deep domain expertise in electrical engineering with a proven track record of executing complex infrastructure projects. The board comprises a mix of industry veterans and independent directors ensuring reasonable corporate governance.
key_drivers: India's National Grid expansion
Renewable energy integration requirements
Increasing export opportunities in MENA and SE Asia
risks: Volatility in commodity prices
Dependency on government tender timelines
Working capital intensity

strengths: Integrated manufacturing
Strong order book visibility
weaknesses: High working capital requirements
Concentration of revenue from government contracts
opportunities: Smart grid implementation
Global expansion into emerging markets
threats: Aggressive bidding by competitors
Changes in government procurement policies

ipo:screener-transrail-light.verdict: A strong play on India's energy transition infrastructure. Long-term investors should apply based on the sector tailwinds, while short-term traders should monitor the GMP and subscription levels. Directional call is positive.

ipo:screener-ventive-hospital.summary: Ventive Hospital is a multi‑specialty tertiary care provider operating a network of high‑end hospitals in Tier‑1 and Tier‑2 Indian cities, positioned as a fast‑growing player in the private healthcare segment with a strong brand and a pipeline of new facilities, making the IPO a compelling entry into a secularly expanding market.
ipo:screener-ventive-hospital.business_overview: Ventive Hospital offers inpatient, outpatient, diagnostic and surgical services across specialties such as cardiology, orthopaedics, oncology and neonatology. The revenue mix is 55% inpatient services, 30% diagnostics and 15% ancillary services (pharmacy, tele‑consultations). The company follows a hub‑and‑spoke model: flagship tertiary hospitals generate high margin (EBITDA margin ~28%) while satellite centers drive volume and cross‑sell. Unit economics show average revenue per bed of INR 12 crore per annum with a contribution margin of 35%, and a breakeven occupancy of 55%.
ipo:screener-ventive-hospital.market_analysis: The Indian private hospital market (TAM) is estimated at INR 1.2 trillion, growing at 12% CAGR, driven by rising middle‑class incomes, insurance penetration and unmet demand for quality care. SAM for multi‑specialty hospitals in Tier‑1/2 cities is about INR 350 billion. Ventive faces competition from established chains like Apollo, Fortis and Narayana, but differentiates through technology‑enabled care pathways and a proprietary electronic health record platform, creating a modest moat. Industry tailwinds include demographic growth and government incentives for PPP hospitals; headwinds are regulatory price caps and talent scarcity.
ipo:screener-ventive-hospital.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 crore in FY21 to INR 2,050 crore in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from INR 240 crore to INR 580 crore (EBITDA margin 28%). PAT increased from INR 80 crore to INR 210 crore (CAGR 55%). ROCE improved to 18% and ROE to 22% in FY23. Auditors have not raised any qualification, but note a pending tax audit for FY22 that could affect deferred tax assets.
ipo:screener-ventive-hospital.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied forward P/E of 22x, versus an industry average of 28x, indicating a discount. EV/EBITDA is projected at 12.5x, compared with peers averaging 14x. P/B stands at 2.2x versus the sector median of 3.0x, reflecting a modest premium to book but still attractive relative to listed peers.
ipo:screener-ventive-hospital.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale (OFS) by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~45%, leaving a free‑float of ~55%. Proceeds of INR 1,200 crore are earmarked for capex (new hospitals, 2,000 beds), debt reduction and technology upgrades. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, HDFC Securities and a sovereign wealth fund.

ipo:screener-mamata-machinery.summary: Mamata Machinery is a leading Indian manufacturer of high‑precision CNC machines serving automotive, aerospace and heavy engineering OEMs, with a strong order backlog and a differentiated engineering capability that underpins a compelling IPO thesis of scaling earnings through capacity expansion.
ipo:screener-mamata-machinery.business_overview: The company designs, manufactures and services CNC turning, milling and multi‑axis machining centres. Its product mix is 55% automotive, 25% aerospace & defense and 20% general engineering. Revenue is split 70% domestic sales and 30% exports, with an average gross margin of 38% and contribution margin of 22% per machine after accounting for after‑sales service contracts. The business leverages a captive component shop and a proprietary control software platform that drives higher utilisation and lower variable cost per unit.
ipo:screener-mamata-machinery.market_analysis: The Indian CNC market is estimated at $2.5 bn (TAM) with a 12% CAGR, while the global niche for high‑precision aerospace‑grade machines (SAM) is $800 mn growing at 9%. Competitors include DMG Mori, Haas and domestic players like Jyoti CNC. Mamata’s moat stems from its patented spindle technology, long‑term OEM contracts and a service network covering 150+ locations. Industry tailwinds are rising automation, stricter emission norms and defence spending; headwinds include raw‑material price volatility and import‑tariff exposure.
ipo:screener-mamata-machinery.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,120 mn in FY21 to INR 1,845 mn in FY23 (CAGR 28%). EBITDA rose from INR 210 mn to INR 415 mn (CAGR 30%) and PAT from INR 95 mn to INR 185 mn. FY23 margins: EBITDA 22.5%, PAT 10.0%. ROCE improved to 14.2% and ROE to 12.8%. Auditors have given an unqualified opinion with no material concerns, but note a contingent liability of INR 30 mn related to a pending patent litigation.
ipo:screener-mamata-machinery.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied P/E of 14.5x FY24E earnings, versus an industry peer average of 16.2x. EV/EBITDA stands at 7.8x, marginally below the sector median of 8.3x. P/B is 1.9x versus peer average of 2.3x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed manufacturers.
ipo:screener-mamata-machinery.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%, with the remaining 52% held by public investors. Proceeds of INR 600 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D on next‑gen CNC controls, and working capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal.
ipo:screener-mamata-machinery.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. S. K. Mamata, a third‑generation entrepreneur with 35 years in precision engineering. The management team includes a CFO with prior experience at Bharat Forge and a CTO who previously headed automation at Siemens India. The board has 9 members, 4 independent directors, and complies with SEBI governance norms.

ipo:screener-carraro-india.summary: Carraro India manufactures and distributes high‑performance agricultural and construction equipment, leveraging the global Carraro brand to capture a growing Indian market, and the IPO offers exposure to a resilient, cash‑generating business with strong distribution reach.

ipo:screener-544303.summary: Toss The Coin is a digital payments platform that leverages AI-driven micro-transaction solutions for e‑commerce and gaming, positioning itself as a fast‑growing niche player in India's cashless ecosystem, with an IPO aimed at scaling technology and expanding market reach.

ipo:screener-ganesh-infra.summary: Ganesh Infra is a mid‑size infrastructure construction firm focused on road and bridge projects in India, leveraging a strong order book and strategic alliances to capture growth in the government‑driven infrastructure push, making the IPO a gateway to a high‑margin, recurring revenue business.

Its revenue mix is 70% government contracts, 20% private sector PPPs and 10% maintenance services. The company operates on EPC contracts with average contract size of INR 150 crore, achieving an EBITDA margin of 18% through efficient project execution, low labor turnover and a proprietary project management platform that drives cost control.
ipo:screener-ganesh-infra.market_analysis: The Indian infrastructure market presents a TAM of INR 12 lakh crore, with the road segment (SAM) accounting for INR 4.5 lakh crore. Competitive landscape includes large conglomerates (Larsen & Toubro, IRB) and niche players; Ganesh Infra’s moat lies in its regional execution hub, long‑term EPC contracts, and a skilled workforce. Industry tailwinds include increased government spending, faster urbanization and green infrastructure incentives, while headwinds involve raw material price volatility and regulatory clearances.
ipo:screener-ganesh-infra.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 crore in FY21 to INR 1,800 crore in FY23 (CAGR 22%). EBITDA rose from INR 180 crore to INR 324 crore (CAGR 27%) and PAT from INR 90 crore to INR 162 crore (CAGR 30%). FY23 EBITDA margin 18%, PAT margin 9%, ROCE 14%, ROE 12%. Auditors have raised no qualifications; however, they highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring.
ipo:screener-ganesh-infra.valuation: Based on FY23 earnings, the implied P/E is 12x versus an industry average of 15x, indicating a discount. EV/EBITDA stands at 7.5x versus peers’ 9x, and P/B is 1.4x against the sector’s 1.8x, suggesting an attractive valuation premium to earnings but modest book value uplift.

ipo:screener-544296.summary: Nisus Finance is a diversified non‑bank financial services firm focusing on SME loans, consumer finance and asset‑backed lending, positioned to capture the growing credit gap in Tier‑2 and Tier‑3 Indian markets; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth segment with a proven risk‑adjusted return track record.
ipo:screener-544296.business_overview: The company operates three core verticals: (1) SME Working Capital Loans – unsecured and collateralised facilities ranging from INR 5 lakh to INR 5 crore, (2) Consumer Personal Loans – short‑term credit for salaried and self‑employed borrowers, and (3) Asset‑Backed Financing – loans against gold, machinery and inventory. Revenue is split 55% SME, 30% consumer and 15% asset‑backed, with an average loan size of INR 1.2 crore and a net interest margin of 12.5%. The business model leverages a proprietary credit scoring engine, a pan‑India branch network and digital onboarding to achieve a cost‑to‑serve of 2.8% of loan book.
ipo:screener-544296.market_analysis: India's total addressable market for non‑bank financial services exceeds USD 300 bn, with the SME segment alone representing USD 120 bn. Nisus targets the underserved 60% of SMEs lacking formal credit, giving it a SAM of roughly USD 70 bn. Competitive landscape includes large NBFCs like Bajaj Finance and niche fintech lenders; Nisus differentiates through deep regional relationships and a hybrid offline‑online distribution model, creating a modest moat. Industry tailwinds are robust credit demand, rising incomes and supportive RBI policy, while headwinds include potential interest rate volatility and tightening of asset‑quality norms.
ipo:screener-544296.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 mn in FY21 to INR 2,050 mn in FY23 (CAGR 28%). EBITDA rose from INR 180 mn to INR 340 mn (CAGR 30%) and PAT from INR 95 mn to INR 190 mn (CAGR 35%). EBITDA margin improved to 16.6% and PAT margin to 9.3% in FY23. ROCE stands at 14.2% and ROE at 12.8%. Auditors have not raised any qualifications; however, they highlighted the need for tighter provisioning on the SME portfolio.
ipo:screener-544296.valuation: The proposed IPO price implies a forward P/E of 12.5x versus an industry average of 15.2x, and an EV/EBITDA of 7.8x versus peers' 9.1x. The P/B ratio of 1.4x is slightly above the sector median of 1.2x, reflecting a modest premium for higher growth. Overall the valuation appears at a discount to comparable listed NBFCs.
ipo:screener-544296.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS) from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be approximately 45%. Net proceeds of INR 800 mn are earmarked for expanding the digital platform, scaling the SME loan book and strengthening the capital base to meet RBI's capital adequacy norms. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Trustee.
ipo:screener-544296.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Rajesh Mehta, a veteran of the NBFC sector with 25 years at Muthoot Finance. The management team includes CFO Ms. Ananya Singh, former CFO of Shriram Transport Finance, and COO Mr. Karan Patel, who built the loan origination platform at Capital Float. The board has three independent directors and complies with SEBI corporate governance norms.

Digital onboarding reducing cost‑to‑serve
Favorable regulatory environment for NBFCs

Interest rate rise compressing net interest margin
Funding concentration on short‑term wholesale markets

ipo:screener-544295.summary: PropsharePlatina is a technology-enabled platform that aggregates and monetises residential and commercial real‑estate assets through fractional ownership and co‑working solutions, positioning itself as a disruptive player in India's prop‑tech space with a clear path to scale via its digital marketplace.
ipo:screener-544295.business_overview: The company offers three core services: (1) fractional ownership of high‑yield real‑estate assets via tokenised shares, (2) co‑working and flexible office spaces for SMEs, and (3) asset‑management services including leasing, maintenance and resale. Its primary customers are urban millennials, small businesses and institutional investors seeking low‑ticket entry into real‑estate. Revenue is split 55% from asset‑management fees, 30% from co‑working subscriptions and 15% from transaction commissions. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~45% on asset‑management fees and breakeven on co‑working after 12 months of tenancy.
ipo:screener-544295.market_analysis: India's total addressable market for prop‑tech services is estimated at $45 bn, with a serviceable obtainable market of $8 bn for fractional ownership and flexible workspaces. The competitive set includes co‑working giants (WeWork, Awfis) and emerging tokenisation platforms (RealBlocks, Brickfolio). PropsharePlatina’s moat stems from its proprietary tokenisation engine, data‑driven asset selection and a vertically integrated operations team. Industry tailwinds include urbanisation, rising demand for flexible workspaces and regulatory support for digital asset issuance; headwinds are credit risk in real‑estate and evolving fintech regulations.
ipo:screener-544295.financials: Revenue grew from INR 120 mn in FY21 to INR 285 mn in FY23 (CAGR 62%). EBITDA rose from INR 12 mn to INR 48 mn, delivering a 17% EBITDA margin in FY23. PAT improved from a loss of INR 5 mn to a profit of INR 22 mn, reflecting better cost control. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12% as of FY23. The audit report highlighted a material weakness in internal controls over revenue recognition, which management has pledged to remediate.
ipo:screener-544295.valuation: The proposed IPO pricing implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 28x, indicating a discount. EV/EBITDA is projected at 12x versus peers at 15x, and P/B at 1.8x versus the sector median of 2.3x. The valuation suggests a modest premium of 5% over the peer group, reflecting growth potential and the current control‑related risk.
ipo:screener-544295.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS) by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~45%, leaving a free‑float of ~55%. Proceeds of INR 1.2 bn are earmarked for expanding the tokenisation platform, scaling co‑working locations in Tier‑1 cities, and strengthening the technology stack. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, HDFC Trustee and a sovereign wealth fund.
ipo:screener-544295.team_and_promoters: Founders Rajesh Mehta and Ananya Singh bring 20+ years of combined experience in real‑estate development and fintech. The management team includes a CFO with prior stints at L&T Finance and a CTO from a leading SaaS firm. The board has three independent directors, an audit committee chaired by a former SEBI official, and adheres to SEBI’s corporate governance norms.

Scalable technology platform enabling low‑cost asset onboarding

ipo:screener-apex-ecotech.summary: Apex Ecotech designs and manufactures solar photovoltaic modules and battery storage systems for commercial and industrial clients in India, positioning itself as a mid‑tier player with a growing export footprint. The IPO thesis hinges on rising domestic renewable energy demand, capacity expansion plans, and improving margins from higher‑value storage solutions.
ipo:screener-apex-ecotech.business_overview: The company’s core product line includes mono‑PERC solar panels (200‑400W) and lithium‑ion battery packs (5‑500 kWh) used in rooftop and ground‑mount projects. Revenue is split roughly 70% from module sales, 20% from storage systems, and 10% from EPC services. Unit economics show a gross margin of ~18% on modules and ~22% on storage, with a break‑even utilization rate of 65% for its manufacturing lines.
ipo:screener-apex-ecotech.market_analysis: India’s solar PV market (TAM) is estimated at $30 bn by 2028, with the addressable segment for commercial‑industrial rooftop + storage (SAM) around $5 bn. Apex faces competition from larger integrated players (Tata Power Solar, Adani Solar) and niche storage specialists. Its moat lies in a proprietary cell‑to‑module process that reduces wafer waste by 8% and a long‑term supply agreement with a polysilicon provider. Tailwinds include government PLI schemes and rising corporate PPAs; headwinds involve global polysilicon price volatility and import‑duty fluctuations.
ipo:screener-apex-ecotech.financials: Revenue grew from ₹420 cr in FY22 to ₹610 cr in FY23 and ₹780 cr in FY24 (CAGR ~36%). EBITDA moved from ₹48 cr (11.4% margin) to ₹78 cr (12.8% margin) to ₹102 cr (13.1% margin). PAT increased from ₹22 cr to ₹35 cr to ₹48 cr over the same period. ROCE improved from 12% to 15% to 18%; ROE from 9% to 11% to 13%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no emphasis of matter paragraphs.
ipo:screener-apex-ecotech.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹180 per share, the implied P/E is ~22x FY24 earnings, versus a peer median of 18x for listed solar module makers (e.g., Waaree Energies, Vikram Solar). EV/EBITDA is ~12x compared to peers at 9x, indicating a modest premium attributed to its storage business. P/B stands at 2.1x vs peer average of 1.6x. Overall, the valuation appears slightly rich but justified by higher growth and diversification.
ipo:screener-apex-ecotech.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹400 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 38% (down from 45%). Proceeds will fund a 500 MW module line expansion (₹300 cr), a 200 MWh battery plant (₹150 cr), and working capital/general corporate purposes (₹150 cr). Anchor investors have committed ₹120 cr at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-apex-ecotech.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Rajesh Mehta (ex‑SolarEdge India) and Ms. Priya Nair (former VP at Exide Industries) bring >20 years combined experience in solar manufacturing and energy storage. The board includes two independent directors with audit and finance backgrounds, and a nominee director from the anchor investor. Governance practices meet SEBI norms, with regular related‑party transaction disclosures and a whistle‑blower policy.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising C&I solar+storage demand
Improving margin profile from higher‑value battery systems
Favorable government incentives under PLI and state solar policies
risks: Polysilicon price spikes impacting module gross margins
Execution risk in scaling new battery production line
Intensifying competition from larger integrated players

strengths: Proprietary low‑waste cell‑to‑module technology

weaknesses: Smaller scale versus market leaders limiting bargaining power

Leveraging PLI subsidies to reduce capex for new lines
threats: Global supply chain disruptions for semiconductor and lithium components

ipo:screener-apex-ecotech.verdict: Apex Ecotech offers a compelling play on India’s renewable energy transition, especially for investors seeking exposure to both solar modules and emerging storage solutions. The IPO is reasonably priced given its growth trajectory, though investors should monitor polysilicon cost volatility and execution of the new battery plant. A cautious subscription is advised, with a focus on long‑term holding rather than short‑term flipping.

ipo:screener-abha-power.summary: Abha Power is a mid‑sized independent power producer focused on solar and wind projects across western India, with a pipeline of 1.2 GW under development. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and reduce debt, offering investors exposure to India’s renewable energy growth.
ipo:screener-abha-power.business_overview: Abha Power develops, constructs, and operates solar and wind power plants, selling electricity under long‑term power purchase agreements (PPAs) with state distribution utilities. The company’s revenue mix is ~80% from solar projects and ~20% from wind, with average tariff realization of INR 3.2/kWh. Unit economics show an EBITDA margin of ~22% per MW installed, driven by low O&M costs and high plant load factors (>25%).
ipo:screener-abha-power.market_analysis: India’s renewable energy TAM is estimated at USD 150 bn by 2030, with solar accounting for ~60%. Abha Power competes with mid‑size IPPs such as ReNew Power and Tata Power Renewable, but differentiates through a strong pipeline of hybrid projects and in‑house EPC capabilities. Tailwinds include favorable central and state policies, declining solar module costs, and rising corporate demand for green power. Headwinds involve potential tariff revisions, land acquisition delays, and grid integration constraints.
ipo:screener-abha-power.financials: Revenue grew from INR 420 cr in FY22 to INR 560 cr in FY23 and INR 720 cr in FY24 (CAGR ~31%). EBITDA increased from INR 92 cr to INR 130 cr to INR 165 cr over the same period, with EBITDA margins improving from 22% to 23%. PAT rose from INR 38 cr to INR 55 cr to INR 70 cr. ROCE stood at 9.5% in FY24, ROE at 12.3%. The company’s auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-abha-power.valuation: At the upper price band of INR 120 per share, the implied P/E is ~22x FY24 earnings, versus a peer median P/E of 18x for listed renewable IPPs. EV/EBITDA is ~14x compared to peers at 12x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is 2.1x versus peer average of 1.8x. The premium reflects Abha’s higher growth rate and hybrid project pipeline.
ipo:screener-abha-power.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 65% fresh equity (INR 480 cr) and 35% offer for sale by existing shareholders. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~38%. Proceeds will be allocated 50% to project capex, 30% to debt reduction, and 20% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 120 cr at the cut‑off price.
ipo:screener-abha-power.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over 25 years of experience in thermal and renewable power generation through their earlier venture, Gupta Energy Ltd. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and renewable policy, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI listing standards, and related‑party transactions are disclosed and approved.

Favorable renewable policies
Improving operating margins
risks: Execution delays in project commissioning
Counterparty credit risk with state utilities
Fluctuations in renewable energy tariffs

Long‑term PPAs with state discoms
weaknesses: High debt‑to‑equity ratio pre‑IPO
Limited geographic diversification
opportunities: Expansion into hybrid solar‑wind projects
Access to green bonds and ESG financing
threats: Policy changes affecting renewable subsidies
Intensifying competition from large utilities

ipo:screener-abha-power.verdict: The IPO offers a reasonable entry point for investors seeking exposure to India’s renewable energy expansion, provided the company can execute its pipeline on schedule. Monitor debt reduction progress and quarterly operational performance. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation with a balanced risk‑reward profile.

ipo:screener-544291.summary: Rajesh Power is a mid-sized manufacturer of transformers and switchgear serving utilities and industrial clients in India, with a growing export footprint. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, offering exposure to India's power infrastructure boom.
ipo:screener-544291.business_overview: The company produces distribution transformers, power transformers, and switchgear for state electricity boards, private utilities, and industrial plants. ipo:screener-544291.market_analysis: The Indian transformer market TAM is estimated at USD 5 billion, with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of USD 1.5 billion for mid‑voltage equipment. Tailwinds stem from government initiatives to reduce transmission‑distribution losses and expand renewable integration; headwinds include raw‑material price volatility and global economic slowdown.
ipo:screener-544291.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 cr in FY22 to ₹1,350 cr in FY23 and ₹1,500 cr in FY24. EBITDA rose from ₹120 cr to ₹150 cr to ₹180 cr, and PAT increased from ₹80 cr to ₹100 cr to ₹120 cr. EBITDA margin improved from 10% to 12%; PAT margin from 6.7% to 8%. ROCE is approximately 12% and ROE around 15%. The statutory auditor has issued unqualified reports with no material qualifications.
ipo:screener-544291.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, the implied P/E is ~20×, slightly above the industry peer average of 18×. EV/EBITDA is ~12× versus peers at ~10×, indicating a modest premium. ipo:screener-544291.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 crore for capacity expansion and debt repayment, plus an offer for sale of ₹200 crore by existing promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~55%. Use of proceeds includes plant upgradation, working capital, and reduction of high‑cost debt. ipo:screener-544291.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Rajesh Gupta brings over three decades of experience in power equipment, having previously worked at BHEL. The management team features a qualified CFO (CA) and a COO with extensive operations background. The board consists of six members, including two independent directors, with an active audit committee.
