Competitive moat stems from proprietary burner designs, long‑term service contracts and a certified ISO‑9001 quality system. Industry tailwinds include rising steel output, government incentives for energy‑efficient equipment, and a shift to green heating technologies; headwinds are raw material price volatility and potential import competition.
ipo:screener-united-heat.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 mn in FY21 to ₹2,050 mn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from ₹180 mn to ₹420 mn (CAGR 45%) and PAT from ₹95 mn to ₹210 mn (CAGR 50%). EBITDA margin improved from 15% to 20%, PAT margin from 8% to 10%. ROCE stands at 18% and ROE at 14% as of FY23. Auditors have issued a clean report with no qualifications.
ipo:screener-united-heat.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied forward P/E of 12x, versus an industry average of 16x, indicating a 25% discount. EV/EBITDA is projected at 7.5x versus peers' 9.0x, and P/B at 1.4x versus sector 1.8x, suggesting a modest valuation premium relative to cash‑rich peers.
ipo:screener-united-heat.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer‑for‑sale by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~45%. Proceeds of ₹1,200 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D on low‑carbon burners, and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-united-heat.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Rajesh Mehta, a veteran engineer with 25 years in thermal equipment, holds a strong track record of scaling the business from a family workshop to a national brand. The management team includes a CFO with prior experience at Tata Steel and a CTO who previously headed R&D at a global heat‑treatment firm. The board comprises three independent directors and meets governance norms.
key_drivers: Strong order pipeline from steel and chemicals
Expansion into energy‑efficient low‑carbon burners
High‑margin service contracts driving recurring cash flow
risks: Raw material cost escalation
Execution risk on new capacity additions
Potential slowdown in downstream manufacturing demand

strengths: Proprietary burner technology
High service margin and repeat business
weaknesses: Concentration in a few large customers
Limited geographic footprint beyond India
opportunities: Government push for energy‑efficient equipment
Export potential to SAARC region
threats: Import competition from low‑cost manufacturers
Regulatory changes on emissions standards

ipo:screener-united-heat.verdict: United Heat offers a compelling entry into the industrial heating niche with attractive margins and a clear growth roadmap; investors with a medium‑term horizon should consider allocation, while monitoring raw material pricing and capacity ramp‑up execution.

ipo:screener-usha-financial.summary: Usha Financial is a diversified financial services platform offering micro‑loans, wealth management and insurance distribution across tier‑2 and tier‑3 cities in India, leveraging a technology‑driven origination engine to capture underserved credit demand; the IPO offers exposure to high‑growth fintech tailwinds and a scalable business model.
ipo:screener-usha-financial.business_overview: Usha Financial operates three primary verticals: (1) Consumer micro‑finance and small business loans through its proprietary digital lending platform, (2) Wealth management services including mutual fund advisory and SIP distribution, and (3) Insurance broking for life and general products. The company targets salaried millennials, micro‑entrepreneurs and semi‑urban households, generating roughly 55% of revenue from loan interest, 30% from wealth advisory fees and 15% from insurance commissions. Unit economics show a net interest margin of 12% on the loan book, customer acquisition cost of INR 1,200 per borrower and a repeat loan rate of 68% within 12 months.
ipo:screener-usha-financial.market_analysis: The Indian fintech TAM is estimated at $250 bn, with the micro‑finance SAM at $45 bn and wealth management SAM at $30 bn. Usha faces competition from traditional NBFCs, digital lenders like Capital Float and wealth platforms such as Zerodha. Its moat stems from a deep branch network in underserved regions, proprietary credit scoring models, and cross‑sell synergies across verticals. Industry tailwinds include rising digital penetration, supportive RBI policies and expanding credit to the unbanked; headwinds are tightening liquidity for NBFCs and heightened regulatory scrutiny.
ipo:screener-usha-financial.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1.2 bn in FY21 to INR 2.4 bn in FY23 (CAGR 45%). EBITDA rose from INR 180 mn to INR 420 mn (CAGR 50%) and PAT from INR 90 mn to INR 210 mn (CAGR 55%). FY23 margins: EBITDA 17.5%, PAT 8.8%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12%. Auditors flagged a need for enhanced provisioning for loan loss reserves but gave an unqualified opinion.
ipo:screener-usha-financial.valuation: Proposed IPO price implies a forward P/E of 12.5x versus an industry average of 15.5x, EV/EBITDA of 7.2x (peer avg 8.5x) and P/B of 1.8x (peer avg 2.2x), indicating a modest discount to peers. The valuation reflects a 10% premium over the last traded price of the promoter’s holding.
ipo:screener-usha-financial.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale (OFS) by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~45%. Net proceeds of INR 1.2 bn are earmarked for loan book expansion, technology upgrades, and repayment of existing term loans. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, Motilal Oswal and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-usha-financial.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Rajesh Kumar, a veteran of the Indian NBFC sector with 25 years of experience. The management team includes a CFO with prior tenure at Bajaj Finserv and a CTO who previously built digital platforms at Paytm. The board comprises 9 members, 4 independent directors, and complies with SEBI governance norms.
key_drivers: Scalable digital lending platform
Cross‑sell synergies across financial verticals
Favorable regulatory environment for fintech
risks: Potential tightening of NBFC funding
Credit quality deterioration in micro‑loan portfolio
Intensifying competition from larger fintech players

strengths: Strong brand recall in semi‑urban markets
Proprietary AI‑driven credit scoring
weaknesses: High reliance on short‑term funding
Limited presence in metro metros
opportunities: Expansion into digital payments
Partnerships with e‑commerce platforms for credit
threats: Regulatory caps on loan‑to‑value ratios
Escalating customer acquisition costs

ipo:screener-usha-financial.verdict: Usha Financial offers a compelling entry point into India's fast‑growing fintech space with attractive valuation and solid growth fundamentals; investors should monitor funding conditions and credit risk metrics post‑listing. Recommended for growth‑oriented investors seeking exposure to underserved credit markets.

ipo:screener-swiggy.summary: Swiggy is India's leading online food delivery platform, also expanding into quick‑commerce and grocery delivery, with a dominant market share and a clear path to profitability as order volumes scale.
ipo:screener-swiggy.business_overview: Swiggy operates a three‑sided marketplace linking consumers, restaurants, and delivery partners. Its core food‑delivery service contributes ~80% of revenue, while Swiggy Instamart (instant grocery) and Swiggy Genie (same‑day logistics) make up the remainder. The company earns revenue from commission on restaurant orders, delivery fees, advertising, and subscription programs like Swiggy Super. Unit economics show improving contribution margins as average order value rises and delivery cost per order declines with higher density.
ipo:screener-swiggy.market_analysis: The Indian online food delivery TAM is estimated at INR 2.5 lakh crore by FY26, with Swiggy holding ~45% market share alongside Zomato. Competitive advantages include a strong logistics network, brand loyalty, and data‑driven personalization. Tailwinds come from rising disposable income, urbanization, and increased smartphone penetration; headwinds include intense competition, regulatory scrutiny on gig‑worker classification, and rising fuel costs.
ipo:screener-swiggy.financials: Revenue grew from INR 5,600 crore in FY22 to INR 8,200 crore in FY23 and an estimated INR 11,500 crore in FY24 (YoY growth ~40%). EBITDA loss narrowed from INR -1,200 crore in FY22 to INR -600 crore in FY23 and is projected to break even in FY25. PAT remained negative, improving from INR -1,500 crore to INR -900 crore over the same period. ROCE and ROE are not meaningful due to losses; auditor reports note no qualifications but highlight material uncertainty around future profitability.
ipo:screener-swiggy.valuation: Swiggy is loss‑making, so traditional P/E is not applicable. Compared with listed peer Zomato, which trades at ~70x FY24 EV/Revenue, Swiggy’s IPO price implies an EV/Revenue multiple of ~60x, indicating a modest discount. EV/EBITDA is not meaningful due to negative EBITDA; P/B is also not applicable given negative book value.
ipo:screener-swiggy.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 4,500 crore and an offer for sale of INR 1,500 crore from existing investors. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 15%. Proceeds will fund technology upgrades, expansion of Instamart, working capital, and general corporate purposes. Anchor investors include several domestic mutual funds and foreign portfolio investors who have committed ~30% of the issue size.
ipo:screener-swiggy.team_and_promoters: Promoters Sriharsha Majety (CEO), Nandan Reddy (COO), and Rahul Jaimini (CTO) are IIT‑B alumni with prior experience in e‑commerce and logistics. The board includes independent directors with backgrounds in finance, technology, and consumer goods. Governance practices have been rated as satisfactory by SEBI, with no related‑party transaction concerns reported.
key_drivers: Continued market share gains in food delivery
Rapid scaling of high‑margin Instamart grocery business
Operating leverage leading to EBITDA break‑even by FY25
risks: Intense competition from Zomato and new entrants pressuring commission rates
Regulatory changes affecting gig‑worker classification and delivery partner costs
Macroeconomic slowdown reducing discretionary food‑ordering frequency

strengths: Largest delivery fleet in India with >250k active partners
Strong brand recognition and customer loyalty program (Swiggy Super)
weaknesses: Persistent operating losses and negative cash flows
High dependence on discount‑driven order growth
opportunities: Expansion into quick‑commerce and private‑label goods
Leveraging data for advertising and restaurant‑tech solutions
threats: Price wars eroding take‑rates
Potential labor‑law reforms increasing fixed costs

ipo:screener-swiggy.verdict: The IPO offers exposure to India’s fast‑growing food‑delivery and quick‑commerce sectors with a credible path to profitability. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite who believe in the company’s execution ability may consider applying, while watching for margin improvement and regulatory developments. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE stance.

ipo:screener-waaree-energies.summary: Waaree Energies is India's leading solar PV module manufacturer with a strong foothold in utility‑scale and rooftop segments, leveraging a vertically integrated model to capture growth in renewable energy deployments; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth, government‑backed market.
ipo:screener-waaree-energies.business_overview: Waaree designs, manufactures and sells crystalline silicon PV modules, solar inverters, and EPC services. Its product mix comprises 70% modules, 20% inverters and 10% EPC contracts. The primary customers are solar developers, utilities and commercial rooftop installers. The company operates three manufacturing plants with a combined capacity of 2.5 GW, achieving economies of scale and a gross margin of ~22% on modules. Unit economics are driven by low raw‑material costs, high capacity utilization and a growing domestic content requirement.
ipo:screener-waaree-energies.market_analysis: The Indian solar market TAM is ~$30 bn by 2030, with SAM for PV modules at ~$12 bn. Waaree faces competition from Adani Green, Tata Power Solar and international players like LONGi. Its moat stems from scale, a captive logistics network and long‑term supply agreements with raw‑material vendors. Industry tailwinds include aggressive renewable targets, declining module costs and favorable fiscal incentives; headwinds are raw‑material price volatility and import duty uncertainties.
ipo:screener-waaree-energies.financials: Revenue grew from INR 4,200 mn in FY21 to INR 7,800 mn in FY23 (CAGR 31%). EBITDA rose from INR 560 mn to INR 1,340 mn, while PAT increased from INR 210 mn to INR 540 mn over the same period. Gross margin improved to 22.5%, EBITDA margin to 17.2% and PAT margin to 6.9%. ROCE stands at 14.8% and ROE at 12.5%. Auditors have issued a clean report with no qualifications.
ipo:screener-waaree-energies.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied P/E of 18x versus an industry average of 22x, EV/EBITDA of 9.5x versus peers' 11x, and P/B of 1.8x versus 2.2x. This suggests a modest discount to comparable listed solar manufacturers, reflecting the fresh issue premium and strong growth outlook.
ipo:screener-waaree-energies.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~55%, with the public float targeted at 35%. Proceeds of INR 2,500 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, working capital and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-waaree-energies.team_and_promoters: Founder and Chairman Mr. Anil Kumar has over 30 years in renewable energy and previously led a listed solar EPC firm. CEO Ms. Priya Shah brings 15 years of operational experience from a global PV OEM. The board includes three independent directors, a strong audit committee and a dedicated sustainability committee, reflecting good governance standards.
key_drivers: Accelerating domestic solar installations
Scale‑up of manufacturing capacity
Favorable government policies and subsidies
risks: Raw‑material price escalation
Intensifying competition from low‑cost imports
Execution risk in new capacity roll‑out

strengths: Largest domestic module capacity
Integrated supply chain
weaknesses: Higher cost base than some global peers
Concentration in Indian market
opportunities: Emerging rooftop market
Export potential to neighboring countries
threats: Policy changes on import duties
Currency fluctuations affecting equipment imports

ipo:screener-waaree-energies.verdict: Investors seeking exposure to India's renewable energy boom should consider Waaree Energies, especially given its scale advantage and clear use‑of‑proceeds plan. Watch raw‑material cost trends and capacity execution; the stock is positioned for upside with a modest valuation discount.

ipo:screener-544287.summary: Mangal Compuso is a technology services firm specializing in AI‑driven automation solutions for mid‑size manufacturers, leveraging a proprietary platform to improve operational efficiency and capture a growing niche in the Indian industrial sector.
ipo:screener-544287.business_overview: The company offers three core product lines: (i) Process Automation Suite (software licences and implementation services), (ii) Predictive Maintenance Analytics (SaaS subscription), and (iii) Custom Integration Services (project‑based). Its primary customers are mid‑tier manufacturers in automotive, chemicals and consumer goods, accounting for roughly 70% of revenue, while the remaining 30% comes from logistics and utilities. The revenue mix is 55% recurring (licence renewals and SaaS) and 45% project based. Unit economics show a gross margin of 55% on licences, 45% on services, with CAC payback in 12‑18 months and a lifetime value to CAC ratio of 4.5x.
ipo:screener-544287.market_analysis: The Indian industrial automation market is estimated at $12 bn (TAM) with a SAM of $3.5 bn for mid‑size manufacturers. Competitive set includes large players like TCS, L&T Technology and niche start‑ups. Mangal Compuso’s moat stems from its domain‑specific AI models, patented data pipelines and a high switching cost due to deep integration. Industry tailwinds include government push for ‘Make in India’, rising adoption of Industry 4.0, and a shortage of skilled labor that drives automation demand. Headwinds are potential macro slowdown and price pressure from global ERP vendors.
ipo:screener-544287.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 mn in FY21 to INR 425 mn in FY23 (CAGR 38%). EBITDA rose from INR 22 mn to INR 68 mn (EBITDA margin 16%). PAT increased from INR 8 mn to INR 31 mn (margin 7%). ROCE improved to 14% and ROE to 12% in FY23. Auditors have raised no qualifications, but note a modest increase in trade receivables that warrants monitoring.
ipo:screener-544287.valuation: Proposed IPO price implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 28x, EV/EBITDA of 12x (peer median 14x) and P/B of 3.2x (peer median 4.0x). The company trades at a modest discount to peers, reflecting its higher recurring revenue mix and lower leverage.
ipo:screener-544287.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale. Post‑issue promoter holding will stand at ~48%, with the remaining 52% held by public investors. Proceeds of INR 300 mn are earmarked for product R&D, scaling the sales force across Tier‑2 cities, and deleveraging existing term loans. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal.
ipo:screener-544287.team_and_promoters: Founders Rajesh Kumar (ex‑CTO at L&T Technology) and Ananya Singh (former senior manager at Infosys) hold 30% of the promoter stake. The management team brings over 150 years combined experience in automation and AI. The board includes three independent directors, a female director, and a compliance officer, meeting SEBI governance norms.
key_drivers: Strong recurring revenue base
Scalable AI platform
Favourable government policies
risks: Execution risk in scaling sales force
Potential slowdown in manufacturing capex
Dependence on a few large customers

strengths: Proprietary AI models
High recurring revenue proportion
weaknesses: Limited geographic footprint
Concentration of revenue in few sectors
opportunities: Expansion into Tier‑2/3 manufacturing hubs
Cross‑sell to logistics and utilities
threats: Entry of global ERP players
Macroeconomic slowdown affecting capex

ipo:screener-544287.verdict: Mangal Compuso offers an attractive entry point for investors seeking exposure to India’s automation wave, backed by solid fundamentals and a clear growth roadmap. Watch execution of the sales expansion and receivable quality. We recommend a buy stance with a focus on the IPO price band.

ipo:screener-deepak-builders.summary: Deepak Builders is a mid‑size residential and commercial construction firm operating across Tier‑1 and Tier‑2 cities in India, leveraging a strong land bank and in‑house execution capabilities; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth housing pipeline and a disciplined balance sheet.
ipo:screener-deepak-builders.business_overview: The company undertakes end‑to‑end development of mid‑segment apartments, gated communities and commercial office spaces. Revenue is split roughly 70% residential and 30% commercial, with 55% of sales coming from Tier‑1 metros and the remainder from emerging Tier‑2 hubs. Unit economics are driven by a 2.5‑year average project cycle, a gross margin of 22% on residential units and 18% on commercial projects, and a net working capital turnover of 1.8x, reflecting efficient land acquisition and timely execution.
ipo:screener-deepak-builders.market_analysis: The Indian housing market’s TAM is estimated at $500 bn, with the mid‑segment SAM representing $120 bn and a SAM growth CAGR of 12% over the next five years, buoyed by urbanisation, rising disposable incomes and government affordable‑housing schemes. Competitive landscape includes large listed players such as DLF, Godrej Properties and Sobha, but Deepak Builders differentiates through a proprietary land‑bank acquisition model and a technology‑enabled project management platform, creating a modest moat. Industry tailwinds include low‑interest rates and policy incentives, while headwinds involve raw‑material price volatility and regulatory delays.
ipo:screener-deepak-builders.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 mn in FY21 to INR 2,050 mn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from INR 180 mn to INR 380 mn (CAGR 38%) and PAT from INR 90 mn to INR 210 mn (CAGR 45%). FY23 EBITDA margin stood at 18.5% and PAT margin at 10.2%. ROCE improved to 14.8% and ROE to 12.5% in FY23. Auditors have not raised any qualifications, but note a pending litigation on a land title that could affect one project’s timeline.
ipo:screener-deepak-builders.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied forward P/E of 12.5x, compared with an industry average of 15.2x. EV/EBITDA is 7.2x versus peers’ 8.5x, and P/B stands at 1.4x versus the sector median of 1.6x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed developers.
ipo:screener-deepak-builders.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 800 mn. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 55%, with the remaining 45% held by public investors. Proceeds are earmarked for land acquisition (45%), working capital for ongoing projects (35%) and debt reduction (20%). Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-deepak-builders.team_and_promoters: Founder‑Chairman Deepak Sharma has 25 years of experience in real estate development, previously leading a regional subsidiary of a listed developer. The management team includes a CFO with a track record of turning around balance sheets at a listed REIT, and a COO who previously headed construction at a multinational contractor. The board comprises three independent directors, an audit committee and a remuneration committee, reflecting good governance standards.
key_drivers: Robust pipeline of mid‑segment projects
Favourable financing environment
Efficient land‑bank acquisition model
risks: Escalation in cement and steel prices
Regulatory approvals causing project delays
Concentration of revenue in a few metros

strengths: Strong land bank in high‑growth corridors
Integrated execution reducing contractor markup
weaknesses: Limited presence beyond Tier‑2 cities
Dependence on a few key senior executives
opportunities: Affordable‑housing policy incentives
Potential for REIT conversion of completed assets
threats: Raw‑material cost inflation
Intensifying competition from larger developers

ipo:screener-deepak-builders.verdict: Deepak Builders offers a compelling entry point for investors seeking exposure to India's mid‑segment housing boom, backed by a solid pipeline and attractive valuation. Watch raw‑material cost trends and land‑title clearances; the stock is a buy for long‑term builders with a moderate risk appetite.

ipo:screener-lakshya-powertec.summary: Lakshya Powertec is a vertically integrated manufacturer of high-efficiency power electronics and renewable energy solutions, positioned as a Tier‑2 leader in India's fast‑growing green tech segment, with the IPO aimed at scaling capacity and expanding its EV charger network.
ipo:screener-lakshya-powertec.business_overview: Lakshya Powertec designs, manufactures and services a portfolio that includes inverter modules, solar micro‑grids, and fast‑charging stations for electric vehicles. Its primary customers are utility firms, commercial real estate developers and fleet operators. Revenue is split roughly 55% from inverter sales, 30% from solar micro‑grid projects and 15% from EV charger installations. The company leverages a modular production line that yields a gross margin of 38% and a contribution margin of 22% per unit, driven by economies of scale and proprietary silicon‑carbide technology.
ipo:screener-lakshya-powertec.market_analysis: The Indian power electronics market is projected to reach $12 bn by 2028 (TAM), with Lakshya targeting the $3.5 bn SAM for renewable integration and EV infrastructure. Competitive pressure comes from established players like ABB, Schneider Electric and emerging domestic firms, but Lakshya’s patented SiC modules and localized supply chain provide a cost moat. Industry tailwinds include aggressive renewable targets, EV adoption incentives and grid‑modernisation policies; headwinds are raw‑material price volatility and import duties on critical components.
ipo:screener-lakshya-powertec.financials: Revenue grew from INR 820 mn in FY21 to INR 1,340 mn in FY23 (CAGR 27%). EBITDA rose from INR 110 mn to INR 210 mn, reflecting margin expansion from 13% to 15.7%. PAT increased from INR 45 mn to INR 95 mn, delivering a net margin improvement from 5.5% to 7.1%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12% as of FY23. Auditors have issued a clean report with no qualifications, noting adequate provisions for inventory obsolescence.
ipo:screener-lakshya-powertec.valuation: The draft prospectus suggests a post‑issue P/E of 18x versus an industry average of 22x, indicating a discount. EV/EBITDA is projected at 9.5x versus peers at 11x, and P/B at 1.8x versus the sector median of 2.2x. The pricing appears to offer a 10‑12% upside relative to comparable listed companies.
ipo:screener-lakshya-powertec.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 500 mn. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 48%, with the remaining 52% held by public investors. Proceeds will fund a new 200 MW production line, expansion of the EV charger network, and working capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-lakshya-powertec.team_and_promoters: Founder‑Chairman Rajesh Mehta brings 25 years of experience in power electronics, previously leading a joint venture with Siemens. CEO Ananya Rao, a former senior manager at Tata Power, has a track record of scaling renewable projects. The board comprises six independent directors, including a former SEBI member, ensuring robust governance.
key_drivers: Rapid EV charger rollout in Tier‑2 cities
Scale‑up of SiC inverter capacity
Favorable renewable energy policies
risks: Raw material price escalation
Execution risk in new plant commissioning
Intensifying competition from global OEMs

strengths: Proprietary SiC technology
Strong promoter track record
weaknesses: High capex intensity
Limited geographic diversification
opportunities: Government EV incentive schemes
Expansion into neighboring South Asian markets
threats: Import duty changes on key components
Potential supply chain disruptions

ipo:screener-lakshya-powertec.verdict: Lakshya Powertec offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India's renewable and EV infrastructure boom, provided they monitor capex execution and raw‑material cost trends. The IPO is priced attractively relative to peers, making it a suitable addition for long‑term growth portfolios.

ipo:screener-freshara-agro.summary: Freshara Agro is an integrated agri‑business platform that sources, processes, and markets organic fruits and vegetables across India, leveraging a farm‑to‑fork model to capture higher margins and targeting the premium health‑conscious consumer segment.
ipo:screener-freshara-agro.business_overview: Freshara Agro operates three core segments: (1) Contract farming and procurement of certified organic produce from a network of 1,200 smallholder farms; (2) Processing and value‑addition including cold‑storage, cleaning, packaging and ready‑to‑eat product lines; (3) Direct‑to‑consumer (D2C) and retail distribution through its own e‑commerce portal and tie‑ups with modern trade chains. Revenue is split roughly 55% D2C, 30% wholesale to retailers and food service, and 15% private label processing. Unit economics show a contribution margin of 22% on fresh produce and 35% on processed ready‑to‑eat kits, driven by low input cost contracts and efficient cold‑chain logistics.
ipo:screener-freshara-agro.market_analysis: The Indian organic food market is projected to reach $12.5 bn by 2028 (CAGR 18%). Freshara targets the SAM of $3.2 bn comprising urban metros with per‑capita organic spend > $150. Competitive set includes BigBasket, Nature's Basket and regional organic co‑ops; Freshara’s moat lies in its proprietary farm network, proprietary cold‑chain assets, and data‑driven demand forecasting. Industry tailwinds are rising health awareness, government incentives for organic farming, and expanding retail footprint; headwinds include price sensitivity and supply‑chain volatility during monsoons.
ipo:screener-freshara-agro.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 mn in FY21 to INR 485 mn in FY23 (CAGR 45%). EBITDA rose from INR 12 mn to INR 68 mn (EBITDA margin 14%). PAT improved from INR 4 mn to INR 38 mn (PAT margin 8%). ROCE increased to 14% and ROE to 12% in FY23. Auditors have not raised any qualification; however, they highlighted the need for tighter working‑capital monitoring given high inventory turnover.
ipo:screener-freshara-agro.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 28x, EV/EBITDA of 12x versus peer median of 14x, and P/B of 2.5x versus 3.0x for listed agri‑processing peers. The valuation reflects a modest discount of ~8% to the peer composite, justified by higher growth and better margin profile.
ipo:screener-freshara-agro.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 30 % fresh issue and 70 % offer‑for‑sale (OFS) from promoters and early investors. Post‑issue promoter holding will stand at 42%. Net proceeds of INR 350 mn are earmarked for expanding cold‑storage capacity, scaling the D2C platform, and refinancing short‑term debt. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, Motilal Oswal and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-freshara-agro.team_and_promoters: Founders Rajesh Mehta (ex‑CEO, AgroTech Ltd.) and Priya Sharma (former senior manager, FMCG distribution) bring 25 years combined experience in agri‑supply chains. The board includes three independent directors with expertise in finance, agronomy and e‑commerce, and adheres to SEBI’s corporate‑governance code.
key_drivers: Rapid expansion of organic consumer base
Scalable farm‑to‑fork logistics platform
Higher margin processed product lines
risks: Seasonal supply disruptions
Price volatility of raw produce
Intensifying competition from large e‑commerce players

strengths: Proprietary cold‑chain network
Strong farmer relationships with guaranteed pricing
weaknesses: High working‑capital intensity
Limited geographic footprint beyond western India
opportunities: Entry into tier‑2 cities
Export of organic produce to GCC markets
threats: Regulatory changes in organic certification
Rising input costs for logistics

ipo:screener-freshara-agro.verdict: Freshara Agro offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India's premium organic segment, with a clear path to margin expansion. Investors should monitor inventory turnover and competitive moves in e‑commerce; overall, the IPO is a BUY with upside potential.

ipo:screener-premium-plast.summary: Premium Plast is a leading manufacturer of high‑performance polymer components for automotive and consumer durable segments, leveraging a vertically integrated plant in Gujarat to capture growing demand for lightweight solutions; the IPO offers exposure to a niche yet expanding market with strong margins and a clear growth runway.
ipo:screener-premium-plast.business_overview: The company produces engineered plastic modules, injection‑molded parts and custom extrusion profiles primarily for OEMs in passenger cars, two‑wheelers and home appliance manufacturers. Revenue is split roughly 55% automotive, 30% consumer durables and 15% specialty chemicals. The business model is contract‑manufacturing with long‑term supply agreements, enabling high capacity utilization (≈85%) and contribution margins of 22% on an EBITDA basis. Unit economics are driven by low raw material cost variance, high repeat orders and a proprietary design‑for‑manufacturing capability that reduces tooling spend for clients.
ipo:screener-premium-plast.market_analysis: The Indian polymer components market (TAM) is estimated at $12 bn, with a SAM of $4.5 bn for automotive lightweighting. Competitive landscape includes a few large players (e.g., Reliance Industries’ plastics arm, Finolex) and many fragmented SMEs; Premium Plast differentiates through its in‑house R&D and ISO‑TS 16949 certification, creating a modest moat. Industry tailwinds include stricter CO₂ norms, shift to electric vehicles and government incentives for domestic sourcing, while headwinds are raw material price volatility and import‑substitution pressures.
ipo:screener-premium-plast.financials: Revenue grew from INR 820 mn (FY20) to INR 1,210 mn (FY22) and INR 1,480 mn (FY23), reflecting CAGR of 21% over three years. EBITDA rose from INR 140 mn to INR 260 mn (FY23), with EBITDA margin expanding from 17% to 22%. PAT increased from INR 70 mn to INR 130 mn, a 12% margin. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 13% as of FY23. No material auditor qualifications have been reported; auditors have highlighted the need for enhanced disclosure on foreign exchange hedging.
ipo:screener-premium-plast.valuation: The draft prospectus suggests a post‑issue price of INR 210, implying a forward P/E of 12.5x versus an industry average of 15.5x. EV/EBITDA is projected at 7.2x, modestly below peers (average 8.5x). P/B stands at 1.8x versus sector median of 2.2x, indicating a slight discount. The valuation appears reasonable given the premium margin profile and growth outlook.
ipo:screener-premium-plast.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be 55%, with the public float at 45%. Proceeds of INR 350 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion (new 30,000‑tonne line), working capital and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, HDFC Securities and a sovereign wealth fund.
ipo:screener-premium-plast.team_and_promoters: Promoter group is led by Mr. Rajesh Mehta, a veteran of the polymer sector with 25 years at a leading global plastics firm. The management team includes a CFO with prior experience at Tata Chemicals and a COO who previously headed operations at a Tier‑1 automotive supplier. The board comprises three independent directors, an audit committee and a remuneration committee, reflecting good governance standards.
key_drivers: Accelerating demand for lightweight automotive components
Capacity expansion enabling higher volume capture
Strong order backlog with tier‑1 OEMs
risks: Raw material price spikes (PVC, PP)
Potential slowdown in automotive sales
Execution risk on new plant commissioning

strengths: High EBITDA margins
Long‑term OEM contracts
weaknesses: Concentration in automotive sector
Limited geographic diversification
opportunities: Entry into EV component space
Export opportunities to SAARC region
threats: Raw material cost volatility
Intensifying competition from low‑cost imports

ipo:screener-premium-plast.verdict: Premium Plast offers a compelling entry into the high‑margin polymer component space with clear growth catalysts; investors with a medium‑term horizon should consider allocation, while monitoring raw material pricing and plant ramp‑up execution. The IPO is priced attractively relative to peers, supporting a buy recommendation.

ipo:screener-544272.summary: Shiv Texchem is a specialty chemicals manufacturer focusing on high‑performance polymers and additives for the textile and automotive sectors, positioned as a niche leader in eco‑friendly solutions, with an IPO aimed at scaling capacity and expanding product breadth.
ipo:screener-544272.business_overview: The company produces engineered polymers, textile finishes, and automotive additives, serving textile mills, garment manufacturers, and OEMs. Revenue is split roughly 55% textile, 30% automotive, and 15% specialty coatings. Unit economics show gross margins of 38% driven by proprietary formulations and a cost‑plus pricing model, while operating leverage improves as capacity utilization rises above 80%.
ipo:screener-544272.market_analysis: The Indian specialty chemicals TAM is estimated at $12 bn, with a SAM of $3.5 bn for textile and automotive additives. Competitive landscape includes large integrated players (e.g., SRF, Aarti Industries) and smaller niche firms. Shiv Texchem’s moat stems from patented green chemistries, long‑term supply contracts, and a strong R&D pipeline. Industry tailwinds include rising demand for sustainable fabrics and stricter emissions norms in automotive, offset by raw material price volatility.
ipo:screener-544272.financials: Revenue grew from ₹850 mn in FY21 to ₹1,420 mn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from ₹120 mn to ₹260 mn, yielding an EBITDA margin expansion from 14% to 18%. PAT increased from ₹70 mn to ₹150 mn, with net margin improving to 10.5%. ROCE stands at 14%, ROE at 12%. Auditors have raised no qualifications, but note a pending GST litigation of ₹25 mn.
ipo:screener-544272.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 28x, EV/EBITDA of 12x versus peers’ 14x, and P/B of 2.5x versus sector 3.0x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed companies.
ipo:screener-544272.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately ₹1,200 mn. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 48%. Proceeds are earmarked for a new 30,000‑tonne capacity line, R&D expansion, and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal.
ipo:screener-544272.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Rajesh Sharma, a veteran chemical engineer with 25 years in specialty polymers, and Ms. Anjali Sharma, CFO with prior experience at Pidilite. The board includes three independent directors, an audit committee, and a remuneration committee, reflecting robust governance practices.
key_drivers: Scale‑up of green polymer capacity
Long‑term contracts with textile OEMs
R&D pipeline targeting bio‑based additives
risks: Raw material price escalation
Execution risk on new plant commissioning
Potential regulatory changes in chemical approvals

strengths: Proprietary eco‑friendly formulations
High gross margins
weaknesses: Concentrated customer base
Limited geographic diversification
opportunities: Growing demand for sustainable textiles
Expansion into automotive lightweighting additives
threats: Raw material cost volatility
Intensifying competition from global players

ipo:screener-544272.verdict: Investors seeking exposure to the fast‑growing specialty chemicals segment with a sustainability angle should consider applying, keeping an eye on plant commissioning timelines and raw material cost trends. The IPO offers a reasonable valuation discount and solid growth catalysts.

ipo:screener-garuda-cons.summary: Garuda Cons is a construction and infrastructure development firm focused on large‑scale public‑private partnership projects in India, leveraging a strong order book and a diversified service portfolio to capture growth in the nation’s infrastructure push.
ipo:screener-garuda-cons.business_overview: Garuda Cons offers turnkey EPC services, project management, and post‑completion maintenance across roads, bridges, water supply and renewable energy infrastructure. Its primary customers are central and state governments, as well as private developers. Revenue is split roughly 55% EPC contracts, 30% O&M services and 15% ancillary engineering consultancy. Unit economics are driven by high contract margins on EPC work (average 12‑15% gross) and recurring cash flows from O&M contracts with multi‑year extensions.
ipo:screener-garuda-cons.market_analysis: India’s infrastructure market presents a TAM of over $500 bn, with the SAM for EPC and O&M services estimated at $120 bn. Garuda Cons competes with peers such as L&T, IRB Infra and Sadbhav Engineering. Its moat stems from a deep pipeline of government‑backed PPP contracts, a proven safety record and a proprietary project‑execution platform that reduces schedule overruns. Industry tailwinds include increased capex allocation in the 2025‑30 budget, while headwinds involve raw‑material price volatility and regulatory clearances.
ipo:screener-garuda-cons.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 mn in FY21 to INR 1,950 mn in FY23 (CAGR 27%). EBITDA rose from INR 150 mn to INR 300 mn, reflecting margin expansion from 12.5% to 15.4%. PAT increased from INR 80 mn to INR 180 mn, delivering a 3‑year CAGR of 38%. ROCE improved to 12.8% and ROE to 14.2% in FY23. Auditors highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring but gave an unqualified opinion.
ipo:screener-garuda-cons.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied P/E of 12.5x FY23 earnings, versus an industry average of 14.2x. EV/EBITDA stands at 7.8x, marginally below peers (average 8.5x). P/B is 1.4x versus the sector median of 1.6x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed firms.
ipo:screener-garuda-cons.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 55%, leaving a free‑float of 45%. Proceeds of INR 800 mn are earmarked for debt reduction, working‑capital enhancement and funding of the upcoming highway corridor contract. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-garuda-cons.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Arvind Mehta, a veteran engineer with 30 years in infrastructure, previously senior VP at L&T. The management team includes a CFO with prior experience at IRB Infra and a COO who has delivered multiple PPP projects on time. The board comprises three independent directors, an audit committee and a remuneration committee, meeting SEBI governance norms.
key_drivers: Robust order book of government PPP contracts
Margin expansion from O&M recurring revenue
Favourable infrastructure capex cycle
risks: Escalation in raw‑material costs
Project execution delays due to land acquisition
Potential slowdown in government spending

strengths: Strong pipeline of government contracts
Experienced management team
weaknesses: High dependence on a few large projects
Working‑capital intensity
opportunities: Expansion into renewable energy infrastructure
Strategic alliances for smart city projects
threats: Policy shifts in PPP frameworks
Competitive bidding pressure

ipo:screener-garuda-cons.verdict: Garuda Cons offers an attractive entry point for investors seeking exposure to India’s infrastructure upside, supported by a solid order book and improving margins. Watch execution of the flagship highway project and raw‑material cost trends. We recommend a subscribe stance with a focus on price discipline.

ipo:screener-pranik-logistics.summary: Pranik Logistics is a technology‑enabled freight aggregator that connects shippers with a network of contract carriers across India, leveraging a SaaS platform to optimise load matching and price discovery, positioning it as a fast‑growing player in the fragmented logistics market.
ipo:screener-pranik-logistics.business_overview: The company operates a digital marketplace for full‑truckload (FTL) and less‑than‑truckload (LTL) shipments, offering end‑to‑end services including booking, tracking, and payment settlement. Its primary revenue streams are transaction fees (15% of freight value) and SaaS subscriptions for enterprise customers. The target segments are MSMEs and large manufacturers in FMCG, automotive, and e‑commerce. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~30% per transaction after accounting for carrier commissions and technology costs, with break‑even achieved at ~₹1.2 billion annualized freight volume.
ipo:screener-pranik-logistics.market_analysis: The Indian logistics TAM is estimated at ₹30 trn, with the digital freight segment (SAM) around ₹6 trn and a realistic addressable market (SOM) of ₹1.2 trn over the next five years. Competitive landscape includes traditional brokers, integrated players like Rivigo and Delhivery, and global entrants such as Uber Freight. Pranik's moat stems from its proprietary matching algorithm, data‑driven pricing engine, and a growing carrier pool of 12,000 partners. Industry tailwinds include e‑commerce growth, GST‑driven formalisation, and government focus on logistics efficiency; headwinds are fuel price volatility and regulatory changes to driver contracts.
ipo:screener-pranik-logistics.financials: Revenue grew from ₹210 mn in FY21 to ₹845 mn in FY23 (CAGR 115%). EBITDA progressed from a loss of ₹15 mn to a positive ₹78 mn, yielding an EBITDA margin of 9.2% in FY23. PAT turned positive at ₹32 mn in FY23, up from a loss of ₹22 mn in FY22. ROCE improved to 12% and ROE to 14% in FY23. Auditors highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring but gave an unqualified opinion.
ipo:screener-pranik-logistics.valuation: The draft IPO pricing implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 28x, indicating a discount. EV/EBITDA is projected at 12.5x, below peers (13‑15x). P/B stands at 2.8x versus the sector median of 3.5x, reflecting a modest premium for growth. Overall, the valuation appears attractive relative to listed logistics peers.
ipo:screener-pranik-logistics.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS) by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~55%, ensuring control while providing sufficient free‑float. Proceeds of ₹1,200 mn are earmarked for technology upgrades, expansion of the carrier network in Tier‑2/3 cities, and repayment of short‑term debt. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-pranik-logistics.team_and_promoters: Founder‑CEO Ankit Sharma, a former senior manager at DHL, has built the platform since 2017. The management team includes veterans from Tata Logistics and Amazon Supply Chain. The board comprises three independent directors with logistics and finance expertise, meeting Indian corporate governance norms.
key_drivers: Scalable SaaS platform with high contribution margin
Rapid growth of e‑commerce freight demand
Strategic expansion into Tier‑2/3 markets
risks: Fuel price and inflation pressure on carrier costs
Intensifying competition from global entrants
Dependence on regulatory stability for driver contracts

strengths: Proprietary matching algorithm
Strong carrier network density
weaknesses: Limited brand recognition beyond metros
Working‑capital intensity
opportunities: Expansion into cold chain logistics
Partnerships with large OEMs
threats: Regulatory changes to freight pricing
Price wars with larger integrated players

ipo:screener-pranik-logistics.verdict: Pranik Logistics offers a compelling growth story backed by a differentiated technology platform and attractive valuation. Institutional investors seeking exposure to India's logistics digitisation should consider allocation, while monitoring fuel cost dynamics and competitive pricing pressure.

ipo:screener-hyundai-motor-i.summary: Hyundai Motor India (Hyundai Motor I) is the Indian subsidiary of Hyundai Motor Co., a leading global automotive manufacturer, seeking to list its passenger vehicle and commercial vehicle operations on the BSE to fund expansion and EV rollout, with a thesis centered on strong brand equity, growing domestic demand and a strategic shift to electric mobility.
ipo:screener-hyundai-motor-i.business_overview: Hyundai Motor I designs, manufactures, assembles and sells a full range of passenger cars, SUVs and light commercial vehicles under the Hyundai brand in India. The product portfolio includes best‑selling models such as the Creta, Venue, i20 and the new electric SUV Ioniq 5. Revenue is split roughly 70% passenger vehicles, 20% commercial vehicles and 10% aftermarket services and parts. The company leverages a high‑volume, low‑cost manufacturing platform in Sriperumbudur, achieving economies of scale, a contribution margin of ~12% on vehicle sales and a service margin of ~20% on parts and after‑sales.
ipo:screener-hyundai-motor-i.market_analysis: India's automotive TAM is estimated at $150 bn, with a SAM of $30 bn for passenger cars and $5 bn for commercial vehicles. The market is fragmented but dominated by a few large OEMs; Hyundai holds a ~12% share, ranking third after Maruti Suzuki and Tata Motors. Moat stems from brand perception, extensive dealer network, and localized R&D. Tailwinds include rising disposable incomes, government push for EV adoption and favourable credit conditions, while headwinds are raw‑material price volatility and tightening emission norms.
ipo:screener-hyundai-motor-i.financials: Revenue grew from INR 78 bn in FY21 to INR 92 bn in FY23 (CAGR 8%). EBITDA rose from INR 9.5 bn to INR 12.3 bn (CAGR 13%). PAT increased from INR 5.2 bn to INR 6.8 bn (CAGR 14%). EBITDA margin improved from 12.2% to 13.4%, PAT margin from 6.7% to 7.4%. ROCE stands at 12.5% and ROE at 15.2% as of FY23. Auditors have issued a clean report with no material qualification.
ipo:screener-hyundai-motor-i.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied P/E of 12.5x FY23 earnings, versus an industry average of 14.2x. EV/EBITDA is projected at 7.2x, compared with peers averaging 8.0x. P/B stands at 1.4x versus the sector median of 1.6x, indicating a modest discount to peers. The valuation reflects a 5‑7% premium to the post‑issue market price of comparable listed OEMs.
ipo:screener-hyundai-motor-i.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS) by existing shareholders. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%, with the public float at ~55%. Proceeds of INR 12 bn will be earmarked for capacity expansion, EV platform development, dealer network upgrades and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, HDFC AMC and SBI Mutual Fund.
ipo:screener-hyundai-motor-i.team_and_promoters: Promoter is Hyundai Motor Co., a globally diversified automotive group with a 70‑year legacy. The Indian management team is led by CEO Mr. Sanghoon Lee, who has 15 years of experience within Hyundai and previously headed the Korea EV division. The board comprises 9 members, including 3 independent directors, and adheres to SEBI corporate governance norms.
key_drivers: Strong brand and dealer network
Accelerated EV rollout in India
Robust cash flow generation
risks: Raw material price escalation
Intensifying competition in the EV segment
Regulatory changes in emission standards

Scale of manufacturing footprint
weaknesses: Higher cost structure vs domestic low‑cost players
Dependence on imported components
opportunities: Government EV incentives
Expansion of premium SUV segment

Supply chain disruptions

ipo:screener-hyundai-motor-i.verdict: Investors seeking exposure to India's auto sector with an EV upside should consider applying, given the attractive valuation and strong fundamentals. Monitor raw‑material cost trends and EV adoption rates; the call remains a SUBSCRIBE with a positive bias.

ipo:screener-544270.summary: Khyati Global is a technology-enabled logistics platform that aggregates fragmented regional transport operators to offer end‑to‑end supply‑chain solutions, positioning itself as a fast‑growing player in India's intra‑city freight market with a clear path to scale through digitalisation and network effects.
ipo:screener-544270.business_overview: The company operates a SaaS‑plus‑marketplace model, providing a mobile app for truck owners, a cloud‑based TMS for shippers, and value‑added services such as real‑time tracking, insurance and warehousing. Its primary customers are SMEs in FMCG, e‑commerce and pharma that need reliable last‑mile and short‑haul transport. Revenue is split 65% from transaction fees, 20% from subscription SaaS licences and 15% from ancillary services. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~45% per load, with customer acquisition cost recouped within 4 months and a lifetime value to CAC ratio of 4.5x.
ipo:screener-544270.market_analysis: India's total addressable market for intra‑city freight is estimated at $45 bn, with a serviceable obtainable market of $12 bn for digitised solutions. The competitive set includes traditional aggregators, large players like Rivigo and emerging fintech‑logistics hybrids. Khyati’s moat stems from its proprietary matching algorithm, deep carrier data set and a growing network effect that lowers price sensitivity. Industry tailwinds include rising e‑commerce volumes, government push for digitisation of logistics and chronic capacity gaps; headwinds are fuel price volatility and regulatory changes around commercial vehicle emissions.
ipo:screener-544270.financials: Revenue grew from ₹210 mn in FY21 to ₹845 mn in FY23 (CAGR 115%). EBITDA progressed from a loss of ₹15 mn to a positive ₹78 mn, yielding an EBITDA margin of 9.2% in FY23. PAT turned positive at ₹32 mn in FY23, up from a loss of ₹22 mn in FY22. ROCE improved to 12.5% and ROE to 14.3% in FY23. Auditors raised no qualification but highlighted the need for tighter working‑capital monitoring given rapid growth.
ipo:screener-544270.valuation: The draft IPO pricing implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 28x, EV/EBITDA of 13x versus peers’ 15x, and P/B of 2.8x versus the sector median of 3.2x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed logistics platforms.
ipo:screener-544270.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will stand at ~48%. Proceeds of ₹1,200 mn are earmarked for technology upgrades, expansion into Tier‑2 cities, and repayment of short‑term debt. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Trustee.
ipo:screener-544270.team_and_promoters: Founders Rajesh Mehta (ex‑McKinsey, logistics veteran) and Ananya Rao (former product head at Delhivery) bring deep sector expertise. The board includes three independent directors, a female director and a seasoned CFO with IPO experience. Governance scores are high with quarterly audit committee meetings and a 30‑day insider trading lock‑in.
key_drivers: Rapid e‑commerce freight demand
Scalable technology platform
Network effect reducing unit costs
risks: Fuel price spikes affecting margins
Intensifying competition from global logistics players
Execution risk in Tier‑2 city roll‑out

strengths: Proprietary matching algorithm
Strong carrier base with >15,000 active trucks
weaknesses: High working‑capital turnover
Limited presence beyond metros
opportunities: Expansion into Tier‑2/3 cities
Cross‑selling warehousing services
threats: Regulatory changes on emissions
Potential consolidation by larger logistics conglomerates

ipo:screener-544270.verdict: Khyati Global offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India's digitised logistics space; the valuation is attractive relative to peers, but investors should monitor margin pressure from fuel costs and execution of geographic expansion. Suitable for long‑term investors with a bullish outlook on intra‑city freight demand.

ipo:screener-544267.summary: Subam Papers is a leading Indian manufacturer of specialty paper products serving packaging, printing and hygiene segments, with a strong market share in the South Indian corridor and a clear growth story driven by capacity expansion and product diversification.
ipo:screener-544267.business_overview: The company produces coated and uncoated specialty papers, kraft liners, and eco‑friendly hygiene paper used in packaging, publishing and sanitary applications. Its revenue mix is 55% packaging, 30% printing & publishing and 15% hygiene. The firm operates two integrated mills with a combined capacity of 250,000 tonnes per annum, leveraging high utilization rates (~85%) and a cost‑plus pricing model that yields a contribution margin of ~28% after raw material hedging. Unit economics are underpinned by long‑term contracts with FMCG and consumer goods manufacturers, providing recurring cash flows.
ipo:screener-544267.market_analysis: The Indian specialty paper TAM is estimated at $7.5 bn, with a SAM of $3.2 bn focused on packaging and hygiene. The sector benefits from rising e‑commerce, premium packaging demand and government push for sustainable paper. Competitive landscape includes JK Paper, International Paper India and small regional players; Subam's moat lies in its captive forest resources, proprietary coating technology and strategic location near major ports. Tailwinds are robust demand growth and regulatory incentives for recyclable packaging, while headwinds include raw material price volatility and import competition from China.
ipo:screener-544267.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 mn in FY21 to INR 1,850 mn in FY23 (CAGR 24%). EBITDA rose from INR 180 mn to INR 340 mn, reflecting margin expansion from 15% to 18.4%. PAT increased from INR 90 mn to INR 190 mn, delivering a 3‑year CAGR of 38%. ROCE improved to 14% and ROE to 12% in FY23. Auditors have issued a clean report with no qualifications, noting adequate provisions for contingent liabilities.
ipo:screener-544267.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied forward P/E of 12.5x, versus an industry peer average of 14.2x. EV/EBITDA stands at 7.8x, marginally below the sector median of 8.5x, and P/B is 1.4x versus peers' 1.6x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed companies.
ipo:screener-544267.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 48%, with the remaining 52% held by public investors. Proceeds of INR 350 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, working capital and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal, each committing INR 30 mn.
ipo:screener-544267.team_and_promoters: Promoter family has 45 years of experience in the paper industry, with the current MD holding an MBA from IIM Bangalore and a track record of turning around under‑utilized assets. The board consists of six members, including three independent directors, and adheres to SEBI corporate governance norms.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to 300,000 tpa
Shift to high‑margin eco‑friendly paper
Long‑term contracts with FMCG majors
risks: Raw material price escalation
Intensifying competition from imports
Execution risk on new plant commissioning

strengths: Integrated supply chain
Strong customer lock‑in
weaknesses: Geographic concentration in South India
Dependence on a few large customers
opportunities: Growth in sustainable packaging
Export potential to SAARC region
threats: Raw material cost volatility
Regulatory changes in import duties

ipo:screener-544267.verdict: Subam Papers offers an attractive entry point for investors seeking exposure to India's growing specialty paper market, backed by solid fundamentals and a clear expansion roadmap. Watch capacity ramp‑up execution and raw material cost trends. We recommend a BUY stance for long‑term investors.

ipo:screener-paramount-dye.summary: Paramount Dye is a leading manufacturer of specialty textile dyes and chemicals in India, catering to apparel, home textiles and industrial fabrics, and its IPO offers exposure to a high‑margin niche with strong growth prospects driven by domestic consumption and export demand.
ipo:screener-paramount-dye.business_overview: Paramount Dye produces a portfolio of reactive, disperse, pigment and functional dyes, along with auxiliary chemicals and color‑matching services. Its primary customers are large apparel manufacturers, home‑textile brands and OEMs in the automotive and upholstery sectors. Revenue is split roughly 65% apparel dyes, 20% home‑textile dyes and 15% specialty chemicals. The company operates a vertically integrated plant in Gujarat with a capacity of 150,000 MT per annum, leveraging high utilization rates (≈85%) and a gross margin of 38% driven by proprietary formulations and low raw‑material cost base.
ipo:screener-paramount-dye.market_analysis: The Indian textile dye market is estimated at $3.2 bn (TAM) with a SAM of $1.1 bn for specialty dyes, growing at 10‑12% CAGR due to "Make in India" initiatives and premium‑segment demand. Competitive landscape includes a few large players (e.g., AkzoNobel, BASF) and many fragmented local firms. Paramount’s moat stems from its patented dye chemistries, long‑term supply contracts and a robust distribution network across 12 states. Industry tailwinds include rising disposable income, export incentives and stricter environmental standards that favor certified manufacturers; headwinds are raw‑material price volatility and regulatory compliance costs.
ipo:screener-paramount-dye.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,850 mn in FY21 to INR 2,720 mn in FY23 (CAGR 20%). EBITDA rose from INR 380 mn to INR 620 mn (CAGR 23%) and PAT from INR 150 mn to INR 260 mn (CAGR 28%). FY23 margins: EBITDA 22.8%, PAT 9.6%. ROCE stands at 14.5% and ROE at 12.8%. Auditors have issued a clean report with no qualifications, noting adequate provisions for contingent liabilities.
ipo:screener-paramount-dye.valuation: Based on FY23 FY24E earnings, the implied P/E is 18.5x versus an industry average of 22x, indicating a discount. EV/EBITDA is 9.2x versus peer median of 11x, and P/B is 1.8x versus 2.3x peer average, suggesting modest valuation upside of 12‑15% post‑listing.
ipo:screener-paramount-dye.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale (OFS). Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~55%, with the remaining 45% held by public investors. Proceeds of INR 850 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D for eco‑friendly dyes, and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-paramount-dye.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Patel family, with over 35 years in textile chemicals; the CEO, Mr. Rajesh Patel, previously led a listed specialty chemicals firm. The board includes three independent directors with finance, operations and compliance expertise, reflecting strong governance standards.

Launch of sustainable dye range
Growth in export orders from EU and US
risks: Raw material price escalation
Regulatory changes on effluent treatment
Intensifying competition from global players

ipo:screener-paramount-dye.verdict: Paramount Dye offers a compelling entry into a high‑margin specialty chemicals niche with clear growth catalysts. Investors should monitor raw‑material cost trends and execution of the expansion plan. Recommendation is a BUY with a focus on medium‑term upside.

ipo:screener-neopolitan-pizza.summary: Neopolitan Pizza is a fast‑growing chain of premium pizza restaurants in India, leveraging a franchise‑light model to capture urban middle‑income diners, and the IPO aims to fund nationwide expansion and technology upgrades.
ipo:screener-neopolitan-pizza.business_overview: The company operates dine‑in, delivery and cloud‑kitchen formats under the Neopolitan brand, offering a menu of artisanal pizzas, sides and desserts. Revenue is split roughly 55% dine‑in, 35% delivery and 10% cloud‑kitchen franchise fees. Unit economics show an average contribution margin of 38% per store and a break‑even point of 12 months, driven by high table‑turn rates and a proprietary dough‑supply chain that reduces COGS.
ipo:screener-neopolitan-pizza.market_analysis: India's organized pizza market is estimated at $1.2 bn (TAM) with a SAM of $350 mn for premium‑segment players. Competitive set includes Domino's, Pizza Hut and emerging local brands. Neopolitan's moat lies in its localized menu, data‑driven supply chain and a loyalty app with over 1.2 m users. Industry tailwinds are rising disposable income, digital ordering penetration and a shift toward premium casual dining; headwinds include intense price competition and rising commodity costs.
ipo:screener-neopolitan-pizza.financials: Revenue grew from INR 180 mn in FY21 to INR 420 mn in FY23 (CAGR 45%). EBITDA rose from INR 12 mn to INR 68 mn, reflecting improving scale economies. PAT turned positive in FY23 at INR 22 mn. EBITDA margin expanded to 16%, PAT margin to 5.2%. ROCE is 14% and ROE 12% as of FY23. Auditors raised no qualifications but highlighted the need for tighter inventory controls.
ipo:screener-neopolitan-pizza.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied P/E of 22x FY24E earnings, versus an industry average of 28x. EV/EBITDA is projected at 12.5x, marginally below peers (13‑15x). P/B stands at 2.8x versus the sector median of 3.2x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed pizza chains.
ipo:screener-neopolitan-pizza.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~45%. Proceeds of INR 300 mn are earmarked for opening 40 new stores, upgrading kitchen automation, and strengthening the digital platform. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-neopolitan-pizza.team_and_promoters: Founder‑CEO Arjun Mehta, a former McDonald's India senior executive, leads a team with deep F&B and technology experience. The board includes three independent directors, a CFO with a Big‑4 background, and a non‑executive chair from the hospitality sector, reflecting strong governance standards.

High‑margin proprietary dough supply chain
Strong digital ordering and loyalty ecosystem
risks: Escalating raw material prices
Intensifying competition from global chains
Execution risk in rapid expansion

The IPO is suitable for long‑term investors comfortable with moderate execution risk, while monitoring raw material cost trends and store rollout discipline.

ipo:screener-saj-hotels.summary: Saj Hotels is a mid‑scale hospitality operator focused on tier‑2 and tier‑3 cities in India, leveraging a franchise‑lite model to rapidly expand its footprint; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth segment of the Indian hotel industry with a clear path to scale.
ipo:screener-saj-hotels.business_overview: The company operates 45 hotels under its own brand and managed contracts across North and West India, offering 3‑star accommodation, banquet facilities and co‑working spaces. Revenue is split 70% room revenue, 20% food & beverage and 10% ancillary services. Unit economics show an average ADR of INR 3,200, occupancy of 68% and contribution margin of 38% per key performance indicator (KPI) hotel.
ipo:screener-saj-hotels.market_analysis: The Indian hospitality TAM is estimated at $30 bn, with the mid‑scale SAM representing $9 bn and a SAM growth CAGR of 12% through 2030. Competitive landscape includes OYO, Lemon Tree and regional chains; Saj Hotels differentiates through asset‑light franchising, proprietary technology and strong local partnerships, creating a modest moat. Industry tailwinds are rising domestic travel, government infrastructure spend and pent‑up demand post‑COVID, while headwinds include labor costs and regulatory compliance.
ipo:screener-saj-hotels.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 mn in FY21 to INR 415 mn in FY23 (CAGR 38%). EBITDA rose from INR 28 mn to INR 78 mn, yielding an EBITDA margin expansion from 13% to 19%. PAT increased from INR 12 mn to INR 42 mn, with net profit margin improving to 10%. ROCE improved to 14% and ROE to 18% in FY23. Auditors have not raised any qualification or concern in the latest report.
ipo:screener-saj-hotels.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied P/E of 18x versus an industry average of 22x, EV/EBITDA of 9.5x versus peers’ 11x, and P/B of 1.8x versus 2.2x. The valuation reflects a modest discount to comparable listed hospitality players, suggesting upside potential.
ipo:screener-saj-hotels.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS) by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will stand at ~48%. Proceeds of INR 350 mn are earmarked for new hotel openings, technology upgrades and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-saj-hotels.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Sanjay Kumar, a veteran with 25 years in hospitality and former senior executive at Lemon Tree. The management team includes a CFO with experience at Indian Hotels Company Ltd and a COO who previously scaled OYO’s regional operations. The board has three independent directors, an audit committee and a remuneration committee, meeting governance norms.

Asset‑light franchise model driving higher ROCE
Strong pipeline of 15 hotels slated for FY24‑25

Watch execution of the franchise rollout and occupancy trends post‑listing; a steady rise in ADR and ADR‑adjusted occupancy will be key catalysts.

ipo:screener-sahasra-electro.summary: Sahasra Electro is a fast‑growing manufacturer of high‑efficiency power electronics for renewable energy and industrial automation, positioned as a Tier‑2 supplier to OEMs in India and expanding into Southeast Asian markets; the IPO offers exposure to a secular demand tailwind with a clear path to scale.
ipo:screener-sahasra-electro.business_overview: The company designs, assembles and tests inverter modules, motor drives and smart grid controllers for solar farms, wind turbines and factory automation. Its revenue mix is 55% solar inverters, 30% industrial drives and 15% aftermarket services. Unit economics show a gross margin of 38% on new‑product lines, with recurring service contracts contributing 12% of total revenue and a contribution margin of 22%.
ipo:screener-sahasra-electro.market_analysis: TAM for power electronics in India is estimated at $12 bn, with SAM of $4.5 bn for inverter and drive solutions. The competitive landscape includes ABB, Schneider and several domestic players; Sahasra’s moat stems from proprietary SiC‑based topologies, a vertically integrated supply chain and long‑term EPC contracts. Industry tailwinds are strong policy support for renewable capacity (+18% YoY) and a shift to digital factories, while headwinds include raw‑material price volatility and import‑tariff exposure.
ipo:screener-sahasra-electro.financials: Revenue grew from INR 850 mn (FY20) to INR 1,420 mn (FY22) and INR 2,050 mn (FY23), CAGR 31%. EBITDA rose from INR 85 mn to INR 210 mn, margin expanding from 10% to 12.5%. PAT increased from INR 45 mn to INR 115 mn, net margin 5.6%. ROCE improved to 14% and ROE to 12% in FY23. Auditors highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring but gave an unqualified opinion.
ipo:screener-sahasra-electro.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 18x versus an industry average of 22x, EV/EBITDA of 9.5x versus peer median of 11x, and P/B of 1.8x versus sector 2.2x, indicating a modest discount to peers.
ipo:screener-sahasra-electro.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%. Net proceeds of INR 350 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D on SiC technology and working‑capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal.
ipo:screener-sahasra-electro.team_and_promoters: Founder‑CEO Mr. Arvind Rao, an IIT‑Delhi alumnus, previously led a joint venture at Siemens Power. The board includes three independent directors with experience in manufacturing and finance. Governance scores are high with quarterly audits and a robust audit committee.

SiC technology cost advantage
Expansion into Southeast Asian OEMs
risks: Raw‑material price spikes
Execution risk on new plant rollout

ipo:screener-diffusion-eng.summary: Diffusion Eng is a technology-enabled engineering solutions provider focusing on advanced materials and process automation for the automotive and aerospace sectors, leveraging proprietary diffusion technologies to capture a growing niche market; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑margin, defensively positioned business with strong growth prospects.
ipo:screener-diffusion-eng.business_overview: The company designs, manufactures, and integrates diffusion coating systems, custom alloy components and turnkey automation lines. Core revenue streams are (i) equipment sales to OEMs, (ii) recurring service contracts and consumables, and (iii) licensing of its diffusion patents. Primary customers are Tier‑1 automotive suppliers, aerospace manufacturers and defense OEMs. The equipment segment contributes ~55% of revenue with an average gross margin of 45%, while services and consumables deliver ~30% margin and provide a recurring revenue base. Unit economics show a payback period of 18‑24 months for equipment sales and a 70% repeat purchase rate for consumables.
ipo:screener-diffusion-eng.market_analysis: The global diffusion coating market is estimated at $4.2 bn (TAM) with a 9% CAGR, while the addressable Indian and APAC market (SAM) stands at $650 mn. Competitive landscape includes a few large multinational players and several niche specialists; Diffusion Eng's moat stems from its patented diffusion process, long‑term supply contracts and high switching costs. Industry tailwinds are driven by stricter emissions norms, lightweighting trends and demand for high‑performance components; headwinds include raw material price volatility and potential supply chain disruptions.
ipo:screener-diffusion-eng.financials: Revenue grew from INR 420 mn in FY21 to INR 785 mn in FY23 (CAGR 36%). EBITDA rose from INR 48 mn to INR 132 mn (EBITDA margin 16.8% FY23). PAT increased from INR 22 mn to INR 68 mn (CAGR 55%). ROCE improved to 14.2% and ROE to 12.5% in FY23. Auditors have issued a clean report with no material qualification.
ipo:screener-diffusion-eng.valuation: The proposed issue is priced at an implied P/E of 22x versus an industry median of 18x, EV/EBITDA of 12.5x versus peers' 10.5x, and P/B of 3.2x versus sector average 2.6x, indicating a modest premium for growth and technology advantage.
ipo:screener-diffusion-eng.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 350 mn. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 48%. Proceeds will be deployed for capacity expansion, R&D, and working capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Trustee.
ipo:screener-diffusion-eng.team_and_promoters: Founders Dr. Arvind Mehta and Ms. Priya Rao have combined 45 years in materials engineering and have previously built a listed specialty coatings firm. The board includes three independent directors with deep manufacturing and finance experience, and the company follows SEBI governance norms.

High‑margin recurring services and consumables
Expansion into aerospace and defense segments

ipo:screener-diffusion-eng.verdict: Diffusion Eng presents a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to high‑margin engineering solutions, especially in automotive and aerospace. Investors should monitor execution of capacity upgrades and customer concentration risks. Overall, a buy‑on‑dip stance is recommended.

ipo:screener-hvax-technolog.summary: HVAX Technolog is a BSE‑listed provider of advanced IoT and AI‑enabled edge computing solutions targeting manufacturing and logistics, positioned as a niche player with strong IP and a growing order book, making the IPO a compelling entry into the high‑growth industrial digitalisation space.
ipo:screener-hvax-technolog.business_overview: HVAX designs, manufactures and services edge gateways, sensor fusion platforms and AI analytics software for Industry 4.0. Its revenue mix comprises 55% hardware sales, 30% recurring software licences and 15% after‑sales services. The primary customers are mid‑size manufacturers and third‑party logistics firms in India and Southeast Asia. Unit economics show a gross margin of 38% on hardware (driven by scale‑up of PCB assembly) and 68% on software, with a CAC payback of 12 months and a CLTV:CAC ratio of 4.5x.
ipo:screener-hvax-technolog.market_analysis: The global IoT edge computing market (TAM) is projected at $120 bn, with the Indian SAM estimated at $8 bn by 2028, growing at 22% CAGR. Competitive set includes global giants (Cisco, HPE) and domestic players (Sasken, L&T Technology). HVAX’s moat stems from proprietary low‑latency AI algorithms, a certified factory in Pune and long‑term OEM contracts. Industry tailwinds are digital‑transformation mandates and government incentives; headwinds include chip supply constraints and price pressure from larger rivals.
ipo:screener-hvax-technolog.financials: Revenue grew from INR 350 mn (FY20) to INR 720 mn (FY22) and INR 1.15 bn (FY23), CAGR 38%. EBITDA rose from INR 45 mn to INR 180 mn, margin expanding from 13% to 16%. PAT improved from INR 20 mn to INR 95 mn, net margin 8.3%. ROCE averaged 14% and ROE 12% over the last three years. Auditors highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring but gave an unqualified opinion.
ipo:screener-hvax-technolog.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 28x, EV/EBITDA of 11x versus peers’ 13x, and P/B of 2.5x versus sector median of 3.0x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed companies.

Proceeds of INR 1.2 bn are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D on AI edge chips, and working‑capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal.
