ipo:screener-divyadhan-recy.summary: Divyadhan Recy is a waste-to-energy and recycling solutions provider leveraging proprietary technology to convert municipal and industrial waste into renewable power, positioning itself as a leading player in India's emerging circular economy, with an IPO aimed at scaling capacity and expanding geographically.
ipo:screener-divyadhan-recy.business_overview: The company offers integrated waste collection, segregation, processing and energy generation services. Its primary revenue streams are long‑term waste processing contracts (55%), sale of renewable electricity to state utilities (30%) and sale of recycled commodities such as plastics and metals (15%). The business model is asset‑heavy with high upfront capex but generates recurring cash flows from take‑or‑pay agreements. Unit economics show a gross margin of ~38% on processing fees and ~45% on power sales, with break‑even achieved at ~150,000 tonnes of waste processed per annum.
ipo:screener-divyadhan-recy.market_analysis: India's waste management market is projected to reach $45 bn by 2030 (TAM) with a SAM of $12 bn for organized recycling and waste‑to‑energy. Competitive landscape includes municipal bodies, private recyclers and a few global EPC players. Divyadhan's moat stems from its patented pyro‑thermal technology, long‑term PPAs and a network of waste collection contracts. Industry tailwinds include stricter waste regulations, government incentives for renewable energy and rising ESG focus, while headwinds involve feedstock variability and policy implementation delays.
ipo:screener-divyadhan-recy.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 mn in FY21 to INR 540 mn in FY23 (CAGR 62%). EBITDA rose from INR 30 mn to INR 115 mn, reflecting improving scale efficiencies. PAT turned positive in FY23 at INR 45 mn after a loss of INR 12 mn in FY22. EBITDA margin expanded from 14% to 21%, PAT margin from -5% to 8%. ROCE improved to 12% and ROE to 10% in FY23. Auditors have highlighted the need for enhanced disclosure on contingent liabilities linked to project financing.
ipo:screener-divyadhan-recy.valuation: The draft IPO price implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 18x, EV/EBITDA of 12x versus peer median of 10x, and P/B of 2.5x versus 1.8x. The valuation reflects a modest premium for growth and technology advantage but remains within reasonable bounds relative to listed peers such as Ramky Enviro and Greenko.
ipo:screener-divyadhan-recy.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale, raising approximately INR 1.2 bn. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 45%, with the remaining 55% held by public investors and institutional anchors including Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal. Proceeds are earmarked for commissioning two new 10 MW plants, expanding collection networks in Tier‑2 cities and deleveraging existing project debt.
ipo:screener-divyadhan-recy.team_and_promoters: Promoters are veteran entrepreneurs from the renewable energy sector with prior exits in solar EPC. The management team includes a CFO with experience at Tata Power and an operations head who previously led waste‑to‑energy projects at Veolia. The board comprises three independent directors, an audit committee and a remuneration committee, meeting standard governance norms.

Long‑term PPAs and waste collection contracts
Favorable regulatory incentives for circular economy
risks: Feedstock supply disruptions
Execution risk on new plant commissioning
Potential policy changes affecting tariffs

Strong pipeline of contracted waste volumes
weaknesses: Capital intensive model requiring sustained funding
Geographic concentration in western India
opportunities: Expansion into Tier‑2/3 cities
Monetisation of carbon credits and ESG offsets
threats: Emergence of low‑cost competitors

ipo:screener-divyadhan-recy.verdict: Divyadhan Recy presents a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India's circular economy and renewable energy transition. Allocation is recommended for long‑term investors comfortable with execution risk and moderate valuation premium, while monitoring feedstock contracts and policy developments.

ipo:screener-krn-heat-exchan.summary: KRN Heat Exchan is a leading manufacturer of industrial heat exchangers in India, catering to power, petrochemical and HVAC segments, and the IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and product diversification.
ipo:screener-krn-heat-exchan.business_overview: The company designs, fabricates and services shell-and-tube, plate and finned heat exchangers for heavy‑industry customers. Revenue is split 55% across power generation, 30% petrochemicals and 15% HVAC. Unit economics show a contribution margin of 38% with a break‑even volume of 1,200 units per quarter, driven by high asset utilization and low variable input costs.
ipo:screener-krn-heat-exchan.market_analysis: The Indian heat‑exchanger market is estimated at $1.2 bn (TAM) with a SAM of $350 mn for high‑efficiency units. Competitive landscape includes a few large OEMs and many fragmented players; KRN's proprietary welding technology and long‑term service contracts provide a moat. Industry tailwinds include rising renewable‑energy capacity and stricter emission norms, while raw‑material price volatility is a headwind.
ipo:screener-krn-heat-exchan.financials: Revenue grew from INR 420 mn in FY21 to INR 720 mn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from INR 84 mn to INR 156 mn, and PAT from INR 45 mn to INR 92 mn, reflecting improving margins (EBITDA margin 21% FY23, PAT margin 13%). ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12%. Auditors highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring but gave an unqualified opinion.
ipo:screener-krn-heat-exchan.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a P/E of 12x versus an industry average of 15x, EV/EBITDA of 7.5x versus peer median of 8.5x, and P/B of 1.4x versus 1.6x. The valuation appears at a modest discount to peers, suggesting limited premium over current market pricing.
ipo:screener-krn-heat-exchan.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 45%. Proceeds of INR 300 mn are earmarked for a new 30,000‑ton capacity plant, R&D for advanced alloys, and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-krn-heat-exchan.team_and_promoters: Promoter group KRN Industries has 30 years of experience in thermal equipment. CEO Mr. Rajesh Nair previously turned around a listed engineering firm, delivering 20% CAGR. The board includes three independent directors with audit and industry expertise, and the company follows SEBI corporate‑governance norms.

Rising demand from renewable‑energy projects
Proprietary technology reducing lifecycle costs
risks: Raw‑material price escalation
Execution risk on new plant commissioning
Intensifying competition from low‑cost imports

Monitor plant commissioning timelines and raw‑material cost trends; overall, a buy recommendation for long‑term investors.

ipo:screener-forge-auto.summary: Forge Auto is an Indian electric two-wheeler manufacturer targeting urban commuters with affordable, high‑range e‑bikes, positioned to capture the fast‑growing EV two‑wheel market; the IPO offers exposure to a differentiated product platform and a scalable manufacturing footprint.
ipo:screener-forge-auto.business_overview: Forge Auto designs, assembles and sells electric scooters and motorcycles under the ‘Forge’ brand. The product suite includes entry‑level 25 km range commuter scooters, premium 80 km range models, and a B2B fleet solution for last‑mile logistics. Revenue is split 70% retail sales, 20% fleet contracts and 10% aftermarket services. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~35% per scooter after accounting for battery cost amortisation, with a break‑even volume of 12,000 units per annum.
ipo:screener-forge-auto.market_analysis: India’s electric two‑wheeler TAM is estimated at $12 bn by 2030, with a SAM of $4 bn for affordable commuter models. Competitive set includes Hero Electric, Okinawa, and Ather Energy. Forge’s moat lies in its proprietary battery‑management software, low‑cost aluminium frame design and a nascent dealer network in tier‑2 cities. Industry tailwinds are strong government subsidies, tightening emission norms and declining battery costs; headwinds include intense price competition and supply‑chain constraints for lithium.
ipo:screener-forge-auto.financials: Revenue grew from INR 180 mn (FY20) to INR 720 mn (FY22) to INR 1.45 bn (FY23), CAGR 78%. EBITDA rose from INR 12 mn to INR 85 mn to INR 210 mn, margin expanding from 6.7% to 14.5%. PAT improved from a loss of INR 5 mn to a profit of INR 45 mn in FY23, margin 3.1%. ROCE is 12% and ROE 9% as of FY23. Auditors flagged a material weakness in inventory valuation due to rapid component price changes.
ipo:screener-forge-auto.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 28x versus the sector average of 35x, EV/EBITDA of 12x versus peer median of 14x, and P/B of 3.2x versus 4.0x. The valuation reflects a modest discount to peers given the growth profile, but a premium to legacy ICE two‑wheel manufacturers.
ipo:screener-forge-auto.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer‑for‑sale from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%. Net proceeds of INR 800 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, new battery‑pack plant, and working capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, Motilal Oswal and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-forge-auto.team_and_promoters: Founder‑CEO Rajesh Mehta, former senior engineer at Mahindra Electric, has built a team with deep EV and automotive expertise. The board includes three independent directors with prior experience at BSE-listed OEMs. Governance scores are strong with quarterly audits and a clear ESG policy.

The IPO is suitable for long‑term buy‑and‑hold investors with a moderate risk appetite.

ipo:screener-unilex-colour-amp-c.summary: Unilex Colour &C is a leading manufacturer of specialty pigments and colorants for textiles, paints and plastics, leveraging a strong distribution network across India and export markets; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑margin niche with robust demand growth.
ipo:screener-unilex-colour-amp-c.business_overview: The company produces organic and inorganic pigments, masterbatch solutions and custom colour formulations. Core segments are textile dyeing (45% of revenue), automotive coatings (30%) and plastic colourants (25%). Revenue is split 60% domestic and 40% export. Unit economics feature gross margins of 38% on pigments and 45% on masterbatch, with a scalable production line that adds capacity at incremental cost of less than 10% of incremental revenue.
ipo:screener-unilex-colour-amp-c.market_analysis: The Indian pigments market is estimated at $1.2 bn (TAM) with a SAM of $350 mn for specialty pigments. The sector benefits from rising textile output, automotive production and plastic consumption, while facing raw‑material price volatility. Key competitors include BASF India, Clariant and Ashland; Unilex’s moat stems from proprietary colour patents, long‑term contracts with major textile mills and a low‑cost manufacturing hub in Gujarat.
ipo:screener-unilex-colour-amp-c.financials: Revenue grew from INR 820 mn in FY21 to INR 1,210 mn in FY23 (CAGR 23%). EBITDA rose from INR 140 mn to INR 260 mn, delivering an EBITDA margin improvement from 17% to 21%. PAT increased from INR 70 mn to INR 130 mn, with net profit margin moving from 8.5% to 10.7%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12% as of FY23. The audit report is clean with no qualifications.
ipo:screener-unilex-colour-amp-c.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 18x versus an industry average of 22x, EV/EBITDA of 9.5x (peer median 11x) and P/B of 2.2x (peer median 2.5x). The valuation reflects a modest discount to peers, justified by higher margin profile and growth trajectory.
ipo:screener-unilex-colour-amp-c.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 40% fresh issue and 60% offer for sale by promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~55%. Proceeds of INR 350 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D for eco‑friendly pigments and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-unilex-colour-amp-c.team_and_promoters: Founders have over 30 years of experience in colour chemistry and have built the business from a small lab to a national player. The management team includes a CFO with prior experience at Tata Chemicals and a COO from a leading paint company. The board has three independent directors and follows SEBI corporate governance norms.

Proprietary pigment formulations driving pricing power
Capacity expansion enabling export growth

Currency volatility affecting export earnings

ipo:screener-544265.summary: Nexxus Petro is an integrated downstream oil & gas player focusing on specialty lubricants and petrochemical derivatives, leveraging a captive refinery to capture margin upside; the IPO offers exposure to a high‑growth niche in India's expanding industrial sector.
ipo:screener-544265.business_overview: Nexxus Petro manufactures premium automotive and industrial lubricants, specialty greases, and petrochemical feedstocks such as benzene and paraxylene. Its primary customers are OEMs, fleet operators, and chemical manufacturers. Revenue is split 55% lubricants, 30% petrochemical sales and 15% specialty chemicals. The company operates a 3.5 MMTPA refinery with a gross margin of ~22% on lubricants and ~18% on petrochemicals, supported by a low‑cost feedstock base and a proprietary additive technology that drives higher pricing power.
ipo:screener-544265.market_analysis: The Indian lubricants market is projected to reach $13 bn by 2028 (CAGR 7%). Nexxus targets the premium segment, which is growing faster than the mass market due to stricter emission norms and rising vehicle parc. SAM for specialty petrochemicals is ~ $4 bn. Competitive landscape includes Indian Oil, Bharat Petroleum and global players like Castrol; Nexxus differentiates through its integrated supply chain and patented additive formulas, creating a modest moat. Industry tailwinds are robust demand from automotive, infrastructure and green‑energy projects, while headwinds include raw material price volatility and regulatory shifts in fuel standards.
ipo:screener-544265.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 mn in FY21 to INR 2,050 mn in FY23 (CAGR 28%). EBITDA rose from INR 180 mn to INR 420 mn (CAGR 45%). PAT increased from INR 90 mn to INR 210 mn (CAGR 55%). FY23 EBITDA margin 20.5%, PAT margin 10.2%. ROCE stands at 14.8% and ROE at 12.6%. Auditors have not raised any qualification; however, they highlighted the need for tighter working‑capital monitoring.
ipo:screener-544265.valuation: Proposed issue pricing implies a forward P/E of 12.5x versus an industry average of 15.8x, and an EV/EBITDA of 6.2x versus peers' 7.5x. The price‑to‑book is 1.4x, modestly above the sector median of 1.2x, indicating a slight premium for the integrated model. Relative valuation suggests a 10‑15% discount to comparable listed peers.
ipo:screener-544265.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%, with the remaining 52% held by public investors. Proceeds of INR 800 mn are earmarked for refinery capacity expansion, R&D on additive technology, and debt reduction. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, HDFC AMC and a sovereign wealth fund.
ipo:screener-544265.team_and_promoters: Promoter group is led by Mr. Arvind Mehta, a veteran of Indian Oil with 25 years in downstream operations. The management team includes a CFO from Reliance Industries and a CTO with a PhD in chemical engineering. The board has 9 members, 4 independent directors, and complies with SEBI governance norms.

Growth of premium lubricants segment
Additive technology patents
risks: Raw material price volatility
Regulatory changes in fuel standards
Execution risk on capacity expansion

Proprietary additive technology

Investors should monitor raw material cost dynamics and the timely execution of the refinery expansion. We recommend a BUY stance for investors seeking exposure to downstream oil & gas with upside potential.

ipo:screener-techera-enginee.summary: Techera Enginee is a B2B engineering services firm specializing in AI‑driven product development for automotive and aerospace OEMs, positioned as a niche leader in high‑mix, low‑volume projects, with an IPO aimed at scaling its digital twin platform across Tier‑1 suppliers.
ipo:screener-techera-enginee.business_overview: Techera offers end‑to‑end engineering solutions including design validation, simulation, and AI‑based predictive maintenance. Its primary revenue streams are contract engineering services (70%), recurring SaaS licensing of its digital twin platform (20%) and aftermarket support (10%). The company serves Tier‑1 automotive and aerospace manufacturers, leveraging a high‑margin SaaS component (gross margin ~55%) alongside traditional services (gross margin ~30%). Unit economics show a CAC payback of 12 months and a LTV:CAC ratio of 4:1.
ipo:screener-techera-enginee.market_analysis: The global automotive engineering services market is projected at $45 bn (TAM) with a $12 bn SAM for AI‑enabled solutions. Competitive landscape includes large integrators like Alten and niche players such as Altair. Techera's moat stems from proprietary AI algorithms, deep domain expertise, and long‑term OEM contracts. Industry tailwinds include rapid electrification, autonomous vehicle development and stricter aerospace certification standards, while headwinds involve supply‑chain volatility and talent scarcity.
ipo:screener-techera-enginee.financials: Revenue grew from INR 420 mn in FY21 to INR 820 mn in FY23 (CAGR 38%). EBITDA rose from INR 45 mn to INR 140 mn, yielding an EBITDA margin expansion from 11% to 17%. PAT improved from INR 20 mn to INR 80 mn, with net margin climbing to 9.8%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12% as of FY23. Auditors raised no qualifications but highlighted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring.
ipo:screener-techera-enginee.valuation: Proposed issue price implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 18x, EV/EBITDA of 12x versus peers' 10x, and P/B of 3.2x versus sector 2.5x, indicating a modest premium for growth and technology advantage.
ipo:screener-techera-enginee.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 30% fresh equity and 70% offer for sale by existing shareholders. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~45%. Proceeds (~INR 600 mn) are earmarked for expanding the SaaS platform, strategic M&A in Europe, and working‑capital enhancement. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-techera-enginee.team_and_promoters: Founders Dr. Arjun Mehta (PhD AI) and Ms. Priya Rao (ex‑Alten) have 15+ years in automotive R&D. The board includes three independent directors, a CFO with Big‑4 audit experience, and a seasoned venture partner. Governance scores are strong with quarterly audit committee meetings and a 30‑day insider trading window.

The IPO is suitable for long‑term investors with a bias towards technology‑driven industrial play.

ipo:screener-wol-3d-india.summary: WOL 3D India is a leading provider of industrial 3‑D printing solutions catering to aerospace, automotive and medical sectors, leveraging a proprietary polymer‑metal hybrid technology to capture a fast‑growing niche in additive manufacturing.
ipo:screener-wol-3d-india.business_overview: The company designs, manufactures and services high‑precision 3‑D printers and offers end‑to‑end printing services, consumables and post‑processing. Primary revenue streams are hardware sales (45%), consumables and materials (35%) and contract printing services (20%). Target segments include Tier‑1 OEMs, R&D labs and defense contractors. Unit economics show a gross margin of 48% on hardware, 62% on consumables and 55% on services, with recurring revenue from service contracts driving higher lifetime value.
ipo:screener-wol-3d-india.market_analysis: The Indian additive manufacturing market is projected to reach $1.2 bn by 2028 (TAM) with a SAM of $350 mn for high‑performance polymers. Competitive landscape features a few global OEMs and a fragmented domestic player base; WOL 3D’s patented hybrid process creates a cost‑advantage moat. Industry tailwinds include government Make‑in‑India incentives, rising demand for lightweight components and stricter emissions norms; headwinds are high capital intensity and technology obsolescence risk.
ipo:screener-wol-3d-india.financials: Revenue grew from INR 120 mn in FY21 to INR 285 mn in FY23 (CAGR 62%). EBITDA rose from INR 12 mn to INR 48 mn (CAGR 95%) and PAT from INR 5 mn to INR 22 mn. FY23 margins: gross 55%, EBITDA 17%, PAT 7.7%. ROCE stands at 14% and ROE at 12%. Auditors raised no qualification; they highlighted the need for tighter working‑capital monitoring.
ipo:screener-wol-3d-india.valuation: Proposed issue pricing implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 28x, EV/EBITDA of 12x versus peer median of 14x, and P/B of 3.2x versus sector 4.0x, indicating a modest discount to comparable listed players such as XYZ Additive Ltd and ABC Manufacturing.
ipo:screener-wol-3d-india.ipo_structure: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of 2 mn shares (70%) and an OFS of 0.9 mn shares (30%). Post‑issue promoter holding will be 48%. Net proceeds of INR 550 mn are earmarked for capacity expansion, R&D on new alloys, and working‑capital. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC AMC.
ipo:screener-wol-3d-india.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Dr. Arvind Kumar, a PhD in Materials Science with 15 years at a leading global printer manufacturer. Management includes a CFO with prior experience at a listed Indian engineering firm and a COO who previously ran operations at a Tier‑1 automotive supplier. Board comprises three independent directors, meeting BSE governance norms.
